process: Unit Tests (Dart/Flutter, plus Swift/JUnit on the native repos) — Dart unit tests for Flutter app business logic, models, and services in test/unit/. Executed today by hand via `make test-unit` (`flutter test`). automatable_score 95. Grounded reality found in the repos: Scheduler (Flutter) has 46 Dart test files in test/unit/ with 11 generated mockito mocks (build_runner codegen dependency — a common failure/flake source); scheduler-ios uses `make test` -> `swift test`; scheduler-android uses `make test` -> `./gradlew test`. Critical gap discovered: NO CI workflow actually runs unit tests on PRs (only gate.yml, code-analysis.yml, codeql exist; the only `flutter test` reference is in firestore-migration.yml). So unit tests are effectively human-triggered and easy to skip. v1 agent value = a managed LangGraph deployment that, on a webhook/cron trigger, executes the test suite on a remote runner, parses results, uses Claude to classify failures (genuine regression vs flaky vs stale mock vs env) and triage, and — when a fix is safe and small (e.g. regenerate mocks, update a brittle assertion) — drafts a patch + PR that is HELD at a human-in-the-loop interrupt before any push/PR/comment/merge.
trigger: Primary: GitHub webhook (pull_request opened/synchronize, push to main) -> LangGraph Platform webhook endpoint, which creates a run on the `unit-test-sentinel` graph. Secondary: nightly LangGraph cron (e.g. 02:00) to catch main-branch drift and flaky tests without a PR. Manual: on-demand run via LangGraph SDK / Studio with {repo, ref, platform} input. Webhook secret and GitHub App creds injected via env; no trigger performs writes on its own — all writes pass through the approval interrupt.
graph_steps: ingest_event: validate webhook/cron/manual payload (HMAC-verify GitHub signature), normalize into RunState {repo, ref, platform in {flutter,ios,android}, pr_number?, trigger_source}; short-circuit & END if event is irrelevant (no test-path changes / draft PR).

prepare_workspace: on a REMOTE runner (Stratus ARC runner or GAL Swarm job — NEVER local Mac subprocess; per MEMORY 'orchestrate-local-execute-cluster'), shallow-clone repo@ref into an isolated worktree, restore tool/pub caches. No model training/distillation anywhere — orchestration of shell + Claude reasoning only.

resolve_test_plan: pick command by platform: flutter -> `make test-unit` (flutter test, with `dart run build_runner build` first if any *.mocks.dart is stale/missing); ios -> `make test` (swift test); android -> `make test` (./gradlew test). Scope to test/unit when changed-files allow; capture machine-readable output (flutter test --machine / JSON reporter).

execute_tests: run the suite on the remote runner with a hard timeout and retry-once-on-infra-error policy; collect pass/fail/skip counts, per-test durations, stack traces, and the raw reporter JSON as artifacts. Read-only step — no writes.

parse_and_classify: deterministically parse reporter output, then call Claude to label each failure: {genuine_regression, flaky/nondeterministic, stale_or_missing_mock (build_runner), env/infra, assertion_drift}. Produce a structured triage with confidence + evidence (file:line, trace).

decide_action (conditional branch): if all green -> report_status (success) -> END. If failures are env/infra -> open or comment-draft an infra issue (still approval-gated). If genuine_regression -> draft a failing-test report + (optional) draft fix. If stale_mock/assertion_drift and fix is small & high-confidence -> draft_patch. Anything ambiguous -> escalate to human with summary only.

draft_patch (optional, only for safe classes): regenerate mocks (build_runner) or apply a minimal, scoped diff to fix a brittle assertion / stale mock; re-run ONLY the affected tests on the remote runner to prove green; produce a unified diff + rationale. Still no push.

approval_gate (interrupt): HUMAN-IN-THE-LOOP `interrupt()` — pause the run and present {triage summary, proposed PR title/body, the exact diff, the GitHub action to be taken}. The run blocks until a human resumes with approve / edit / reject via LangGraph SDK or Studio. Aligns with MEMORY 'no-send-without-approval' + 'no-delete-without-approval'. Reject -> report_status(noted) -> END.

execute_approved_action: ONLY after explicit approval — create branch, push commit, open/update PR via GitHub App (per project git rules: branch-name-prefixed commit, `[branch] ...` message, Co-Authored-By trailer), or post the approved PR comment / open the approved issue. Idempotent (dedupe by content hash to avoid duplicate PRs on webhook re-delivery).
report_status: write back a non-mutating status summary to the run + persist triage to the LangGraph store (long-term memory) keyed by repo+test-id for flaky-test trend tracking; emit metrics. END.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS, api.smith.langchain.com) — auto-provisioned Postgres checkpointer + store; do NOT pass custom checkpointer/store to .compile()
GitHub App (REST/GraphQL via Octokit/PyGithub) — webhook receiver (custom http.app in langgraph.json), HMAC signature verify, branch/commit/PR/issue/comment writes (approval-gated)
Remote test runner: Stratus ARC self-hosted runner OR GAL Swarm job (execute `make test-unit`/`swift test`/`gradlew test` off the local Mac) — invoked over SSH/API, never local subprocess
Flutter/Dart toolchain: flutter test (--machine JSON reporter), dart run build_runner build (mockito codegen)
Swift toolchain (scheduler-ios): swift test; Gradle/JUnit (scheduler-android): ./gradlew test --console=plain
Anthropic Claude API (Opus/Sonnet) for failure classification + patch drafting — orchestration only, with prompt caching; NO model training/distillation (Anthropic Consumer Terms guardrail)
LangGraph BaseStore (semantic index: openai:text-embedding-3-small / 1536) for flaky-test history + de-dupe
Optional: Slack (slack_send_message_draft, approval-gated) for triage notifications; Sentry for correlating regressions to runtime errors
langgraph CLI / LangGraph SDK + Studio for deploy, manual runs, and resuming the human interrupt
impact: Closes a real gap: unit tests currently run only by hand (`make test-unit`) and there is NO PR CI that runs them, so regressions in app business logic/models/services can merge unnoticed — directly risking the live, revenue-generating Scheduler app. Automating to 95% removes a manual QA step, gives every PR a deterministic green/red gate, and surfaces flaky vs genuine failures with Claude-classified evidence and a ready-to-approve fix. The HIL interrupt keeps every push/PR/merge under explicit human control (per workspace approval rules), so the agent accelerates QA without ever taking an irreversible action autonomously. Trend memory in the store also makes chronic flaky tests visible over time. Multi-repo (Flutter + iOS + Android) coverage from one deployment.
approval_gates: HARD human-in-the-loop interrupt() at `approval_gate` before ANY write or irreversible action. Gated actions: (1) creating/pushing a branch or commit; (2) opening/updating a PR; (3) posting a PR/issue comment; (4) opening an issue; (5) any merge — merge is never auto, even on green, and is left to the existing branch-protection + human reviewer. The run blocks (durable, via the managed checkpointer) until a human resumes with approve / edit-diff / reject through LangGraph SDK or Studio. Reading repo state, cloning, running tests, parsing, and classifying are the only un-gated (read-only) steps. Deletes (e.g. stale branches) are NOT in v1 scope; if ever added they get their own explicit gate. Webhook re-delivery is de-duplicated by content hash so an approval can't be replayed into duplicate PRs.
secrets_needed: REMOTE_RUNNER_SSH_KEY_OR_TOKEN (Stratus ARC / GAL Swarm dispatch credential)
OPENAI_API_KEY (only if store semantic index is enabled for flaky-test search)
deploy_notes: Repo layout for the deployment (new repo, e.g. Scheduler-Systems/qa-unit-test-sentinel): langgraph.json -> {dependencies:["."], graphs:{"unit_test_sentinel":"./agent/graph.py:graph"}, env:".env", python_version:"3.12", image_distro:"wolfi", pip_installer:"uv", http:{app:"./agent/webhook.py:app", cors:{allow_origins:["https://github.com"]}}, store:{index:{embed:"openai:text-embedding-3-small",dims:1536}, ttl:{default_ttl:43200}}, checkpointer:{ttl:{strategy:"delete",default_ttl:43200}}}. graph.py builds a StateGraph(RunState), adds the nodes above, uses add_conditional_edges at decide_action, and exposes module-level `graph = builder.compile()` — DO NOT pass checkpointer=/store= (the managed platform injects Postgres). The human pause uses langgraph `interrupt()` at approval_gate; resume via Command(resume=...). webhook.py is a FastAPI/Starlette app mounted via http.app that HMAC-verifies the GitHub signature then calls the SDK to create a run (keeps secrets server-side, not in graph input). Deploy: `langgraph deploy` (CLI) or connect the GitHub repo in LangSmith Deployments UI; inject all secrets via the Deployments env UI/API — never commit .env with real values (security rule: never hardcode secrets). CRITICAL constraint compliance: the graph orchestrates shell + Claude calls only — it does NOT train/fine-tune/distill any model (Anthropic Consumer Terms; gal-model guardrail). Heavy test execution runs on remote Stratus ARC / GAL Swarm runners, never as local Mac subprocesses (MEMORY: orchestrate-local-execute-cluster — local fleet wedged the Mac before). Configure GitHub App with least-privilege perms (contents:write, pull_requests:write, issues:write, checks:read) and subscribe to pull_request + push webhooks pointing at the deployment's http endpoint. Set a per-run timeout and retry-once-on-infra policy on execute_tests. Before go-live, file/attach a tracking GitHub issue (workspace rule: tie work to a documented issue). Suggested first milestone: Flutter-only (test/unit + build_runner mock regen), then add ios/android branches once the Flutter path is proven green end-to-end behind the interrupt.

process: Flutter Widget Tests (QA) — widget tests under Scheduler-Systems/Scheduler test/widget/ that verify UI component state, user interactions, and rendering as part of the curated `flutter test` P0/P1 CI suites. The repo has 23 widget test files (e.g. scheduler_loading_widget_test.dart, profile_settings_widget_test.dart, export_shifts_widget_test.dart, plus views/ and accessibility/ subdirs), curated into run_p0_flutter_tests.sh and run_p1_flutter_tests.sh, executed by .github/workflows/code-analysis.yml on PRs to main (Flutter 3.32.0, Dart SDK >=3.7.0). The agent is a deployable LangGraph Cloud (LangSmith Deployments / Agent Server) graph that acts as a widget-test guardian/triage assistant: given a PR or pushed branch (or a failing CI run), it (a) discovers which widget tests are affected by changed UI/widget source, (b) runs the curated widget test suite in a sandboxed executor and parses pass/fail/flake, (c) on failures, uses Claude to diagnose root cause and DRAFT a fix or a new/updated widget test, then (d) gates every write/irreversible action (committing a test, pushing a branch, opening/commenting on a PR, re-running CI, posting Slack) behind a human-in-the-loop interrupt for approval. It is orchestration that CALLS Claude for reasoning/diagnosis only — it never trains, fine-tunes, distills, or evaluates any ML model.
trigger: Primary: GitHub webhook on pull_request (opened/synchronize/reopened) and on workflow_run completed=failure for the "Code Analysis" workflow on Scheduler-Systems/Scheduler — wired via langgraph.json `webhooks` / a custom `http` app route that creates a run on the `widget_test_guardian` graph. Secondary: manual invocation (LangGraph SDK / Studio) passing {repo, ref/PR number, optional failing_run_id}. Optional scheduled cron (LangGraph assistant cron) for a nightly full widget-suite health sweep on main. No auto-trigger performs any write — all writes pause at the approval interrupt.
graph_steps: ingest_event: normalize the trigger (PR webhook / workflow_run failure / manual / cron) into State{repo, base_sha, head_sha, pr_number, changed_files, failing_run_id?}. Validate it targets Scheduler-Systems/Scheduler; reject anything else (guard).

scope_changes: read-only git/GitHub diff (base..head) to list changed Dart UI/widget sources under lib/ and existing tests under test/widget/. Map changed widgets -> candidate widget tests (curated P0/P1 lists in scripts/test/run_p0|p1_flutter_tests.sh + import-graph heuristic). Produces affected_tests[].

run_widget_tests: in a sandboxed executor (LangGraph deployment Docker image with Flutter 3.32.0 pinned, OR a tool that dispatches to a self-hosted ARC runner / `flutter test test/widget/... --reporter=json` per the orchestrate-local-execute-cluster rule — NOT the local Mac), run the affected widget tests with machine-readable JSON output. Capture per-test status, duration, stderr. Re-run failures once to flag flakes.
parse_results: deterministic parse of the JSON reporter into structured results{passed, failed[], flaky[], errors[]}. If all green -> route to summarize (no writes needed).

diagnose (Claude): for each failure, send the test source + failing widget source + stack/assertion output to Claude (claude-opus/sonnet via langchain-anthropic) to classify root cause (real regression vs. stale test vs. flaky vs. missing coverage) and produce a proposed unified diff (fix the widget OR fix/add the widget test) WITHOUT applying it. Orchestration-only LLM call.

build_proposal: assemble a human-readable triage report + the candidate diff(s) + suggested action (comment-only / draft test commit / push branch / re-run CI). Persist to state + long-term store (BaseStore) for audit.

APPROVAL GATE (interrupt): call interrupt() to pause and surface {report, diffs, proposed_action} to a human (LangGraph Studio inbox / Agent Inbox / Slack-with-resume). No commit, push, PR comment, branch creation, or CI re-run happens before resume with an explicit approve/edit/reject decision.

apply_action (post-approval, conditional): only on approve — perform the single approved write via GitHub tool: post PR review comment, OR commit the approved test/fix diff to a new branch `widget-test-guardian/<pr>-<slug>` and open/update a PR (never push to main; respects branch-name commit convention), OR trigger workflow_dispatch re-run. Each write is idempotent and logged.
summarize_and_persist: write final outcome to the checkpointer-backed thread + store, emit a concise status (and optional approved Slack/PR note). End. On reject -> record decision, no writes, end.
impact: Protects the curated P0/P1 widget-test gate that every PR to Scheduler (the live, RevenueCat-revenue app) must pass, so it directly defends release velocity and prevents UI regressions from reaching paying users. Automatable_score 90 reflects that discovery+run+parse is fully deterministic and diagnosis is a bounded Claude reasoning task; the only human touch is the approval of any code/test write. Cuts manual triage time on red CI, surfaces flaky widget tests, and proposes coverage for newly added widgets — all without an engineer hand-running `flutter test`. Net effect: faster green PRs, fewer escaped UI bugs, auditable trail of every proposed test change. Risk is contained because no write is autonomous (HITL interrupt) and scope is hard-pinned to one repo's widget tests.
tools_integrations: langgraph (StateGraph, interrupt/Command for HITL, Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore auto-provisioned on LangGraph Platform)
langchain-anthropic ChatAnthropic (Claude — diagnosis/diff generation ONLY; no model training/distillation)
GitHub REST/GraphQL via PyGithub or gh (read diffs/PR; gated writes: branch, commit, PR open/comment, workflow_dispatch re-run)
GitHub webhooks -> langgraph.json `webhooks` + custom `http` Starlette app route (signature-verified) to create runs
Flutter SDK 3.32.0 toolchain in the deployment image (or a dispatch tool to a self-hosted ARC runner) running `flutter test test/widget/... --reporter=json`
scripts/test/run_p0_flutter_tests.sh & run_p1_flutter_tests.sh (curated suite source of truth)
Slack (optional, approval/notify — send is approval-gated)
LangSmith tracing/observability for the deployment
LangGraph cron (optional nightly main-branch widget health sweep)
secrets_needed: GITHUB_APP_ID / GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY (or scoped GITHUB_TOKEN with repo + workflow scope) for Scheduler-Systems/Scheduler
SLACK_BOT_TOKEN (optional, only if Slack notify/approve enabled)
approval_gates: ["Single mandatory HITL interrupt before ANY write/irreversible action: committing a generated widget test or source fix, creating/pushing a branch, opening or commenting on a PR, or re-triggering CI. Human resumes with approve / edit-diff / reject; reject and timeout default to no-write.", "Hard repo scope guard at ingest: only Scheduler-Systems/Scheduler events proceed; everything else is dropped.", "Never push to `main` and never force-push — writes go to a `widget-test-guardian/*` branch and an opened PR for normal human review/CI on top of the approval.", "Slack/PR-comment sends are themselves approval-gated (no message leaves the agent without explicit human OK, per workspace no-send-without-approval rule).", "Anthropic-Terms guard: agent must NOT develop/train/fine-tune/distill/evaluate any ML model (incl. gal-model); it only orchestrates Claude calls for diagnosis. Any task drifting toward model work halts.", "Secrets only via LangGraph Platform env injection — never committed in langgraph.json/.env; .compile() must NOT pass a custom checkpointer/store (platform injects Postgres)."]
deploy_notes: LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments / managed Cloud SaaS, api.smith.langchain.com). langgraph.json at repo root: {"dependencies":["."],"graphs":{"widget_test_guardian":"./agent/graph.py:graph"},"env":".env","python_version":"3.12","image_distro":"wolfi","http":{"app":"./agent/app.py:app"},"checkpointer":{"ttl":{"strategy":"delete","default_ttl":43200}},"store":{"ttl":{"default_ttl":10080,"sweep_interval_minutes":60}},"dockerfile_lines":["RUN <install Flutter 3.32.0 SDK>"] }. Critical: do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= to .compile() — the platform injects production Postgres. The graph variable `graph` must be a compiled StateGraph using interrupt() for the approval node so it survives suspension on the managed checkpointer (resumable via Command(resume=...)). Deploy via `langgraph deploy` CLI or Studio Git integration. Webhook route in app.py must verify GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET (HMAC) before creating runs. Heavy `flutter test` execution should run on a self-hosted ARC runner via a dispatch tool (per the local-Mac-is-conductor-only memory) rather than blocking the Agent Server worker; if run in-image, bump deployment resources and set a per-test timeout. Tie work to a GitHub issue per workspace policy. Run a v1 in comment-only/dry-run mode (proposal + report, no code writes) before enabling the commit/PR action path.

trigger:
Three production triggers, all landing on the same compiled graph via the LangGraph Platform Agent Server:
1. GitHub webhook -> thin Cloudflare Worker / FastAPI shim -> POST /runs on the deployment. Fires on (a) PR opened/synchronize touching test/integration/**, test/runners/run_integration_tests.sh, or lib/**, and (b) push to main. Webhook payload (repo, sha, PR#, changed paths) becomes graph input. The shim is just signature-verify + forward; no logic.
2. Scheduled nightly regression: LangGraph Platform native Cron (CronCreate, e.g. 0 6 * * *) kicks a full-suite run of all 18 legacy files in test/integration/ to catch transition-drift while the suite is being retired in favor of Patrol.
3. Manual / on-demand: a human POSTs to /runs (or via LangGraph Studio) with {sha, suite: smoke|full, files?:[...]} to re-triage a specific failure. Resume of an interrupted run (post-approval) is POST /runs/{thread_id} with the Command(resume=...) payload.
This agent NEVER executes flutter drive itself (the managed container has no Flutter SDK, no adb, no emulator/FTL). It is an orchestrator: it dispatches the real run to existing GitHub Actions self-hosted runners (ci-android-runc-x64, Chrome, FTL) via workflow_dispatch and polls results back. This is the same "orchestrate centrally, execute on runners" split already used in the workspace.
process: Legacy Flutter integration tests (QA, automatable 85). 18 end-to-end test files under Scheduler-Systems/Scheduler/test/integration/ (auth, onboarding, billing, calendar, plus a mocked/ subdir), executed by test/runners/run_integration_tests.sh which wraps `flutter drive --driver=test_driver/integration_test.dart --target=<file> --device-id=<chrome|emulator-XXXX|FTL> --dart-define=INTEGRATION_TEST=true`. The suite is "active but transitioning" — Patrol (integration_test/patrol/**, flutter_web_e2e.yml) is the successor. The recurring human toil is NOT writing tests; it is: on every relevant PR / nightly, kick the legacy suite, then sift the output to decide per-failure whether it is (a) a genuine regression that must block, (b) a known-flaky test (network/emulator/Firebase timeout — the runner itself has 5-retry network checks and pm-clear churn, classic flake sources), or (c) a test that is dead weight in the transition and should be quarantined/migrated to Patrol. The agent automates that orchestrate-collect-classify-recommend loop and posts a structured verdict, leaving every state-changing or merge-affecting action behind a human approval gate.
graph_steps: ingest_trigger: normalize webhook/cron/manual payload into State (repo=Scheduler-Systems/Scheduler, base_sha, head_sha, pr_number?, changed_paths, suite=smoke|full, requested_files?). Compile-time note: graph = builder.compile() with NO checkpointer=/store= args — the Platform injects Postgres persistence; passing a saver is local-only.

scope_suite: deterministic node (no LLM). Map changed_paths -> affected legacy test files in test/integration/. PR default suite=smoke (auth/billing happy-path subset), push-to-main and cron default suite=full (all 18). Emit selected_files[]. If empty and not full -> short-circuit to END with 'no legacy integration coverage impacted'.

dispatch_runner [APPROVAL GATE 1]: interrupt() to surface the exact GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch (ref=head_sha, inputs={suite, files, platform}) before triggering, because a dispatch consumes self-hosted runner/FTL minutes (cost) and is externally visible. On resume(approve) call the dispatch tool; capture run_id. interrupt is the canonical LangGraph human-in-the-loop primitive and survives the managed Postgres checkpointer across the pause.

poll_results: long-poll GitHub Actions run status + download artifacts (junit/console logs, screenshots) for the dispatched run. Bounded ret(max wall-clock, backoff). Pure read. Parse per-file pass/fail/error + failure messages, stack traces, and timing.

classify_failures: LLM reasoning node (Claude via langchain ChatAnthropic — orchestration only, NO model training/distillation). For each failing test classify regression | flaky | transition-dead-weight, with evidence: cross-reference failure signature against (i) the runner's known flake surfaces (adb network ping retries, Firebase/RevenueCat/Intercom timeouts, pm clear race), (ii) git blame / whether head_sha actually touched the exercised code path, (iii) flake history pulled from the long-term BaseStore (semantic index keyed by test name + normalized error). Produce per-test verdict + confidence.

draft_verdict: assemble a single Markdown report — table of test -> status -> classification -> recommended action (block-merge / re-run-flaky / quarantine-or-migrate-to-Patrol) — plus an overall gate recommendation. No side effects yet. Persist flake observations to BaseStore for future runs (store.put under (namespace='flaky', key=test_name)).

post_actions [APPROVAL GATE 2]: interrupt() presenting the drafted verdict AND the concrete irreversible actions it would take, each individually toggleable: (a) post/update the PR review comment, (b) re-dispatch only the flaky-classified tests for a confirmation re-run, (c) open a quarantine PR / GitHub issue to migrate a dead-weight test to Patrol, (d) Slack #qa notification. Human approves a subset. ONLY approved actions execute on resume; the rest are dropped. END.
tools_integrations: GitHub REST/Actions API (PyGithub or httpx): workflow_dispatch the existing flutter_web_e2e.yml / a thin legacy-integration dispatch workflow on ref=head_sha; poll run status; download run artifacts (junit XML, console logs, screenshots); post/update PR review comment; open quarantine PR or issue. NOTE origin push is DISABLED in this repo — agent must operate via PR/dispatch, never direct push.
Cloudflare Worker or FastAPI shim mounted via langgraph.json http.app: verifies the GitHub webhook HMAC signature and forwards to POST /runs. Keeps webhook secret out of the graph.

Anthropic Claude (langchain-anthropic ChatAnthropic) for the classify_failures + draft_verdict reasoning — inference/orchestration only; explicitly NOT used to train, fine-tune, or distill any model (workspace Anthropic-terms guardrail).
LangGraph Platform managed Postgres checkpointer (auto-injected) for interrupt/resume durability across the two approval gates.
LangGraph BaseStore with semantic index (store.index embed=openai:text-embedding-3-small, dims=1536) as the cross-run flake-history memory.
LangGraph Platform native Cron (CronCreate) for the nightly full-suite trigger.
Slack Web API (chat.postMessage to #qa) — optional, approval-gated notification only.
Read-only access to repo metadata (changed paths, git blame) via the GitHub API; the agent does NOT clone or run the Flutter toolchain.
approval_gates:
Two mandatory human-in-the-loop interrupt() gates; the agent takes ZERO irreversible action autonomously.
GATE 1 (dispatch_runner): before triggering any GitHub Actions run / FTL job, interrupt() shows the exact dispatch (workflow, ref=head_sha, suite, file list, target platform, est. runner cost). Resume payload = approve | edit-scope | deny. Rationale: a dispatch is externally visible and burns self-hosted runner + Firebase Test Lab minutes.
GATE 2 (post_actions): before ANY write — PR comment, flaky re-dispatch, quarantine PR/issue creation, Slack post — interrupt() presents the full drafted verdict plus a checklist of proposed actions; the human approves a per-action subset; only approved items run on resume, the rest are discarded.
All gates implemented with langgraph interrupt() + Command(resume=...), durable via the managed Postgres checkpointer so a run can sit paused for hours/days awaiting a human. Pure read/analysis nodes (scope_suite, poll_results, classify_failures, draft_verdict) run without gates. No secrets in state; merge itself is never performed by the agent — it only recommends a gate verdict for the existing branch-protection/Gate workflow to enforce.
secrets_needed: GITHUB_APP_ID / GITHUB_PRIVATE_KEY (or GITHUB_TOKEN) — scoped to Scheduler-Systems/Scheduler: actions:write (dispatch), contents:read, pull_requests:write, issues:write
GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET — HMAC verification in the http.app shim
OPENAI_API_KEY — only for the BaseStore embedding index (text-embedding-3-small); drop if using a non-OpenAI embed or no semantic flake memory
LANGSMITH_API_KEY — platform auth / tracing (set in the deployment, not committed)
deploy_notes:
Target: LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments, managed Cloud SaaS tier, api.smith.langchain.com). Single compiled graph, deployed via `langgraph deploy` or GitHub-integration build from repo root.

langgraph.json (repo-root):
{
  "$schema": "https://langgra.ph/schema.json",
  "dependencies": ["."],
  "graphs": { "qa_integration_triage": "./agent/graph.py:graph" },
  "env": ".env",
  "python_version": "3.12",
  "image_distro": "wolfi",
  "pip_installer": "uv",
  "http": { "app": "./agent/webhook.py:app", "cors": { "allow_origins": [] } },
  "store": { "index": { "embed": "openai:text-embedding-3-small", "dims": 1536 } },
  "checkpointer": { "ttl": { "strategy": "delete", "default_ttl": 43200 } }
}

CRITICAL build rules:
1. graph = StateGraph(State).compile() with NO checkpointer=/store= passed — the managed Platform injects production Postgres persistence + store; a custom/InMemorySaver is local-only and is ignored/overridden in the hosted runtime. interrupt()/Command(resume=...) depend on that injected checkpointer.
2. All secrets injected via the Deployment UI/API env, NEVER in the committed .env (only non-secret defaults there). Honors workspace 'never hardcode secrets' rule.
3. The graph does inference/orchestration ONLY. It must not contain or invoke any model training/eval/distillation path (Anthropic consumer-terms guardrail; this is QA orchestration, which is permitted).
4. Reality check baked into design: the managed container has NO Flutter SDK / adb / emulator / FTL, so the agent cannot and must not try to run flutter drive or test/runners/run_integration_tests.sh in-process — it dispatches to the repo's existing self-hosted GitHub Actions runners (ci-android-runc-x64, ci-medium, FTL) and reads artifacts back. Add (or reuse) a workflow_dispatch-able workflow that wraps run_integration_tests.sh for the legacy suite; flutter_web_e2e.yml already exposes workflow_dispatch + suite selection to model after.
5. Idempotency: key threads by head_sha so re-deliveries of the same webhook resume the existing thread rather than spawning duplicate dispatches.
6. Rollout: start with GATE 1 + GATE 2 both manual and Slack/PR-comment in dry-run (draft only) for the first ~2 weeks while the BaseStore accumulates flake history; only then enable the flaky-rerun and quarantine-PR actions. The suite is being retired in favor of Patrol, so scope agent investment accordingly — this is a transition-period safety net, hence P2.
v1 cut line: ship steps ingest/scope/dispatch(GATE1)/poll/classify/draft/post(GATE2) with PR-comment + Slack actions only. Defer auto-quarantine-PR and the semantic flake store to v1.1 if they prove their weight.
impact: Removes the recurring manual toil of a QA/dev reading raw flutter drive output on every relevant PR and every nightly run, and deciding per-failure regression-vs-flaky-vs-dead-weight. Concretely for this repo: 18 legacy E2E files whose runner already has heavy flake surface (5-retry adb network checks, Firebase/RevenueCat/Intercom timeouts, pm-clear races) — exactly the noise a human currently triages by hand. The agent produces a consistent, evidence-backed verdict in minutes, reduces wasted re-runs (re-dispatches only the genuinely-flaky subset, gated), and shortens the safe retirement of the legacy suite by flagging which tests are dead weight to migrate to Patrol vs. which still guard live behavior (billing/auth, which protect paying-user flows). Human stays in control of every external/irreversible action via two interrupt gates, so blast radius is zero. Right-sized as P2: high toil-reduction value but on a transitioning, soon-to-shrink test suite, so capped investment with a defer-to-v1.1 cut line.

trigger:
Three entry points, all landing on the same graph (one deployment, multiple assistants/configs):
1. GitHub webhook -> custom HTTP route on the Agent Server (langgraph.json `http.app` mounts a FastAPI app at e.g. POST /webhooks/github). Fires on PR opened/synchronize touching `integration_test/patrol/**`, `patrol_test/**`, `pubspec.yaml` (patrol 4.2.0 pin), or `scripts/test/patrol_*.sh`. The route validates the GitHub HMAC signature, then creates a run on the graph via the SDK.
2. Scheduled nightly full-regression: a LangGraph Cloud Cron (cron schedule on the deployment) kicks a run with config {scope: "full", platforms: ["android","ios","web"]} at 02:00.
3. Manual/on-demand: human invokes the assistant from LangGraph Studio or `langgraph` SDK with {scope, platforms, target_globs, pr_number} to retest a specific area (e.g. only `integration_test/patrol/billing/`).
Hard constraint: the agent NEVER executes Patrol itself (no Flutter SDK / Android emulator / iOS simulator / Xcode 26 in the managed container). It DISPATCHES the existing self-hosted GitHub Actions workflows (android-build.yml, ios-build.yml, flutter_web_e2e.yml) which carry `workflow_dispatch` + `run_patrol_tests` boolean and `PATROL_TARGET` inputs and run on runners `ci-android-runc-x64` / `bare-high-arm64` (macOS). Mirrors the "orchestrate local, execute on cluster" rule.
process:
Patrol E2E Tests (Flutter Mobile) — QA. Patrol 4.2.0 integration testing for the Scheduler Flutter app across Android/iOS/web. Today a human runs `make patrol-android/ios/web TARGET=<test>` (-> scripts/test/patrol_unified.sh -> patrol_test.sh / patrol_web_test.sh), or dispatches the GitHub Actions workflows with run_patrol_tests=true. Tests live in integration_test/patrol/ across ~16 suites (auth, billing, schedule, calendar, chat, employee, export, priorities, performance, edge_cases, bugs, navigation, onboarding, profile, gemini). The unified runner already handles iOS Xcode-26 quirks (UDID resolution, dyld cache pre-gen, 600s timeout + 1 retry on hang exit 124/137/143) and Android health-check wrapping. Artifacts land in build/app/reports/androidTests/.../index.html and are uploaded as `patrol-android-artifacts`/iOS equivalents; JUnit/log output is the parse surface.
The agent's job is the surrounding ORCHESTRATION + TRIAGE brain that a human does manually: (a) decide which suites/platforms to run for the change, (b) dispatch the right CI workflow(s), (c) wait for completion, (d) pull and parse artifacts/logs into pass/fail per test, (e) triage each failure as likely-flaky (matches the known iOS XCTest hang / testmanagerd / Firebase-emulator-timeout / Google-SignIn signatures, or fails only intermittently) vs likely-real-regression, (f) for flaky-suspected failures propose ONE bounded re-dispatch of just the failed targets (the runner already does an in-run retry; the agent adds at most one suite-level retry), (g) summarize and draft the human-facing outputs (PR check summary comment, Slack QA message, GitHub issue for a real regression). Every dispatch, comment, Slack post, and issue creation is an irreversible/side-effecting action and is APPROVAL-GATED via a LangGraph human-in-the-loop interrupt() before execution. The LLM (Claude) is used only for triage reasoning and drafting prose — no model training/distillation. NOTE re Anthropic terms: this orchestrates Claude for QA triage; it does NOT build/train/eval any ML model.
graph_steps: ingest_and_normalize (entry node): accept the trigger payload (webhook/cron/manual), normalize into RunRequest {scope, platforms[], pr_number?, head_sha?, changed_files[]}. For webhook path, changed_files come from the GitHub PR files API.

select_targets (Claude reasoning + deterministic map): map changed_files -> affected Patrol suites using a path->suite table (e.g. lib/billing -> integration_test/patrol/billing/, lib/auth -> auth/). scope=full -> all suites; scope=smoke -> auth/00_minimal + 01_login + billing premium-parity (mirrors flutter_web_e2e.yml smoke list). Output: per-platform PATROL_TARGET strings. Cap total dispatched targets to a configurable budget.
plan_dispatch: build the concrete list of workflow_dispatch calls {workflow_file, ref, inputs:{run_patrol_tests:true, PATROL_TARGET, platform}}. Compute a human-readable plan.

INTERRUPT approve_dispatch (human-in-the-loop): interrupt() surfacing the dispatch plan (which workflows, which targets, which ref, est. runner cost). Human approves / edits target list / rejects. Resume via Command(resume=...).

dispatch_ci: on approval, call GitHub Actions REST 'create workflow dispatch' for each planned workflow (android-build.yml / ios-build.yml / flutter_web_e2e.yml). Record run dispatch handles (workflow + ref + a correlation marker).

poll_runs: poll GitHub Actions run status until each dispatched run reaches a terminal conclusion or a max-wait budget. Implemented as a node that re-enters itself with backoff (interrupt-free wait) and persists progress in checkpointed state so the long wait survives.

collect_artifacts: for each finished run, download the Patrol artifacts (patrol-*-artifacts: JUnit XML / index.html report / run log). Parse into TestResult[] {suite, test, status, duration, failure_message, log_excerpt}.

triage_failures (Claude reasoning): classify each failure as FLAKY-SUSPECT (matches known signatures: iOS XCTest hang exit 124/137/143, testmanagerd/RunnerUITests stale connection, dyld/sim-boot, Firebase emulator network timeout, Google Sign-In 'server temporarily unavailable'/SHA-1, 'Continue with Email button not found' load race) or REAL-REGRESSION (deterministic assertion failure, new error not in signature list). Produce a structured triage report + confidence.
conditional retry_gate: if any FLAKY-SUSPECT failures AND retry budget (<=1 suite-level retry) remains -> route to INTERRUPT approve_retry; else -> finalize.

INTERRUPT approve_retry (human-in-the-loop): interrupt() proposing a single bounded re-dispatch of ONLY the failed/flaky targets. Human approves/declines. On approve, loop back to dispatch_ci (retry counter incremented); on decline, go to finalize.

synthesize_outputs (Claude drafting): write the artifacts a human reviews — (1) PR check-summary markdown, (2) Slack QA digest, (3) GitHub issue body for each REAL-REGRESSION (title, failing test, log excerpt, suspected change/commit). Nothing is sent yet.

INTERRUPT approve_publish (human-in-the-loop): interrupt() showing all drafted outputs. Human selects which to send (PR comment / Slack / file issue), edits text, or sends none. Resume with the approved set.

publish: execute ONLY the approved sends via GitHub (issue/comment) and Slack MCP. Each send is idempotent (dedupe by head_sha + suite). END with a persisted RunReport in state/store for audit + nightly trend.
tools_integrations: GitHub Actions REST API — create-workflow-dispatch (android-build.yml, ios-build.yml, flutter_web_e2e.yml) with inputs {run_patrol_tests, PATROL_TARGET, platform/ref}; list/get workflow runs; download run artifacts (patrol-*-artifacts). Primary execution lever.
GitHub REST/GraphQL — PR files (changed_files), create issue, create/upsert PR comment (check-summary), commit/PR metadata for blame on regressions.
JUnit XML + Patrol HTML report parser (in-container Python: lxml/junitparser) — turn build/app/reports artifacts into TestResult[].
Slack MCP (mcp__plugin_slack_slack__slack_send_message / slack_send_message_draft) — QA digest to the QA/eng channel; draft-first then approved send.
Anthropic Claude (LLM via langgraph/anthropic SDK) — target selection reasoning, failure triage classification, and prose drafting only. NO model training/eval.
LangGraph BaseStore (managed Postgres store) — known-flaky signature library + per-test historical flake rate (updated each run) to sharpen triage; nightly trend.
LangGraph Postgres checkpointer (managed, auto-injected) — durable interrupt/resume across the long CI poll and the 3 approval gates.
Optional Linear/Jira (SMR project) instead of GitHub issues for regression tickets — gated identically; default GitHub issue.
approval_gates: ["GATE 1 approve_dispatch — before ANY CI run is triggered (spends self-hosted runner time + money). Human sees: workflows, PATROL_TARGET per platform, git ref, target count vs budget. Can edit targets or reject. Implemented with interrupt(); resume via Command(resume).", "GATE 2 approve_retry — before the single bounded re-dispatch of flaky-suspect targets. Prevents retry loops; max 1 suite-level retry beyond the runner's own in-run retry. Skipped entirely if no flaky-suspects.", "GATE 3 approve_publish — before sending ANY PR comment, Slack message, or filing ANY GitHub/Jira issue. Human picks which outputs to send and edits text. Matches global rules: no send without approval, no irreversible action without human sign-off.", "Auth on side-effecting HTTP routes: langgraph.json `auth.path` -> custom Auth verifying caller identity; the /webhooks/github route additionally verifies the GitHub HMAC signature before creating a run. All gates use LangGraph durable interrupt()/Command(resume) so a phone/Studio approval can land minutes-to-hours later (survives via the managed checkpointer)."]
secrets_needed: GITHUB_APP_ID / GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY (or GITHUB_TOKEN) — scoped to actions:write (workflow_dispatch), actions:read+contents:read (runs/artifacts), issues:write, pull_requests:write on Scheduler-Systems/Scheduler
GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET — HMAC verification for the inbound PR webhook
SLACK_BOT_TOKEN (or Slack MCP OAuth) — post to QA channel
LANGGRAPH_AUTH_SECRET / signing key for the custom Auth on HTTP routes
(optional) LINEAR_API_KEY or JIRA_API_TOKEN+JIRA_EMAIL if filing regressions to SMR instead of GitHub issues
deploy_notes:
langgraph.json at repo root of a NEW small Python service (suggested: Scheduler-Systems/scheduler-qa-agent, NOT inside the Flutter app repo): {"dependencies":["."],"graphs":{"patrol_orchestrator":"./app/graph.py:graph"},"env":".env","python_version":"3.12","image_distro":"wolfi","pip_installer":"uv","http":{"app":"./app/http.py:app"},"auth":{"path":"./app/auth.py:auth"},"store":{"index":{"embed":"openai:text-embedding-3-small","dims":1536}},"checkpointer":{"ttl":{"strategy":"delete","default_ttl":43200}}}.
Build the StateGraph in app/graph.py and export module-level `graph = builder.compile()` — do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= (managed Postgres is auto-injected; passing InMemorySaver is local-only and ignored in the hosted runtime). The 3 approval gates use langgraph.types.interrupt(); resume with Command(resume=...). The long CI wait is a self-re-entering poll node with backoff (state persisted by the checkpointer) — do not block a thread for hours.
Deploy: `langgraph deploy` (Cloud SaaS, region us via api.smith.langchain.com) or via LangSmith Deployments UI from the repo. Inject all secrets through the Deployments UI/API env, never in the committed .env (security rule: never hardcode secrets).
Set the GitHub webhook (PR events) to POST the mounted /webhooks/github route on the deployment URL; configure the nightly Cron in the Deployments UI for the full-regression run.
SCOPE GUARDRAIL: the agent only dispatches/polls/parses/drafts. It contains NO Flutter/Android/iOS toolchain and never runs Patrol in-container — device execution stays on the existing self-hosted runners (android `ci-android-runc-x64`, iOS `bare-high-arm64`). v1 platform default: android+web (fast, cheap); iOS opt-in per run due to runner cost/Xcode-26 flakiness. Anthropic-terms note: Claude is used for QA triage/drafting orchestration only — no AI/ML model is developed, trained, evaluated, or distilled.
impact: Replaces the manual loop a human runs today (pick targets -> dispatch CI -> wait -> open HTML reports -> eyeball flaky vs real -> retry -> write up results -> ping the team / file a bug). Process automatable_score is 85; the orchestration/triage/drafting layer is fully automatable, while the 3 things that genuinely need judgment or are irreversible (spend runner time, retry, publish/file) stay human-gated. Concrete wins: (1) faster PR feedback — auto-selects the minimal affected suites instead of full-suite-or-nothing; (2) consistent flaky-vs-regression triage using the codified iOS-XCTest/Firebase/Google-SignIn signature library + historical flake rates, reducing false-alarm bug tickets; (3) bounded retries (<=1 suite-level on top of the runner's in-run retry) instead of ad-hoc human re-runs; (4) audit trail of every QA run in the store for nightly trend. Protects the live revenue app (Scheduler has paying RevenueCat users) by catching real regressions in auth/billing/schedule before release, without burning self-hosted-runner budget on needless full runs. v1 is intentionally android+web default with iOS opt-in; success metric = % of PRs where the agent's triage matches a human's, and reduction in manually-dispatched patrol runs.

area: QA — Flutter mobile runtime testing (Marionette MCP)
process:
Marionette MCP Runtime Testing (Flutter Mobile). AI-driven interactive UI testing of the Scheduler Flutter app. Claude connects to a running debug build over the Dart VM service WebSocket via Marionette MCP, inspects widgets, taps, enters text, scrolls, screenshots, and reads logs across six playbooks (01 app launch → 06 full regression), then emits a pass/fail report with evidence.

ARCHITECTURE (the load-bearing decision): The hosted LangGraph Cloud graph CANNOT run Marionette directly — the managed runtime has no Flutter SDK, no Android emulator / iOS simulator, and no route to a localhost ws://127.0.0.1 VM service. So this is a SPLIT deployment, NOT a single hosted agent:
  • ORCHESTRATOR (LangGraph Cloud, managed): holds the plan, state, checkpointer, HITL interrupts, retries, and the final report. Calls Claude for reasoning/triage/report synthesis only — never trains/fine-tunes/distills any model (Anthropic-terms compliant).
  • EXECUTOR (self-hosted runner = the dev Mac OR a Stratus ARC runner with the mobile toolchain): a thin long-poll worker the orchestrator drives over a SINGLE custom HTTP tool surface. The runner is the ONLY thing that runs `make marionette-health`, `make marionette-android|ios` (via run_marionette.sh → JSON {vm_service_uri,pid,device,log}), and the Marionette MCP tool calls (connect / get_interactive_elements / tap / enter_text / scroll_to / take_screenshots / get_logs / disconnect) against the live VM URI. The runner mediates ALL device access; LangGraph Cloud never holds the ws:// URI or device handles itself.

This matches the workspace 'orchestrate local, execute on cluster' rule and keeps the heavy device work off the hosted control plane.
trigger: Primary: webhook / API run-create on a deploy-verify event — GitHub Actions (or the SDLC step 05 deploy-verify gate) POSTs to the LangGraph deployment when a Flutter PR build is ready, with input {platform: android|ios, playbook: smoke|full|01..05, branch, runner_id}. Secondary: scheduled cron (LangGraph Platform cron / launchd nightly) for a nightly android+ios full-regression smoke. Manual: a developer kicks a run from the LangGraph Studio UI or `langgraph` SDK mirroring the existing `/marionette-test <platform> <playbook>` slash command. A live executor runner MUST have first registered/heartbeated (runner online) or the graph short-circuits to a 'no runner available' terminal state.
graph_steps: START → ingest_request: validate input (platform in {android,ios}; web is rejected up front — Marionette has no VM service on web, route to Chrome/Playwright out of band); resolve playbook list (smoke=[01]; full=[01,02,03,04,05]; or a single NN); load test creds + bundle id from env/secrets (NEVER hardcode). Seed State.

select_runner: confirm a self-hosted executor runner is online via its heartbeat (runner registry). If none online → set status=NO_RUNNER and jump to report. (Hosted graph cannot itself reach a device.)

preflight_health: instruct runner to run `make marionette-health PLATFORM=<platform>`. Parse PASS/WARN/FAIL counts. On any FAIL (Flutter/marionette_mcp/emulator/simulator missing) → fail-fast to report with the missing-prereq list. Firebase-emulator WARNs (auth on 9099, firestore on 8080) are recorded but non-blocking.

launch_app: instruct runner to run `make marionette-<platform>` (run_marionette.sh) in background; poll its log for the VM service URI (timeout 300s, matches MARIONETTE_URI_TIMEOUT). Capture JSON {vm_service_uri, device, pid, log}. On uri_timeout (exit 2) → retry once, then fail to report with the flutter_run log path. Store the run pid so cleanup can stop it. The ws:// URI lives only on the runner side.

connect_session: runner calls Marionette connect(uri) then get_interactive_elements to verify the session is live + take_screenshots for a baseline. On connect failure → one reconnect attempt (URI may have expired), else fail to report.

run_playbook_loop: for each playbook in the list, deterministically drive Marionette per the markdown steps (01 launch-controls present / 02 auth: tap email→enter test email→tap password→enter test password→tap login→wait→verify dashboard / 03 navigate each tab / 04 settings catalog + toggle / 05 logout returns to login). Pattern per step: get_interactive_elements → tap/enter_text/scroll_to (prefer ValueKey over text) → wait → take_screenshots at milestones → get_logs after every transition. Claude classifies each step PASS/FAIL/BLOCKED and scans logs for unhandled exceptions. NOTE: every action here is on an ephemeral debug build = read-mostly + reversible (clean app state per run), so the loop itself is NOT approval-gated — it runs autonomously.

triage_failures: if any step FAILED/BLOCKED, Claude analyzes the screenshot + logs to bucket the cause: infra/tooling, auth/test-data dependency, or genuine app/UI regression (per playbook 06 failure-handling). Produces a structured findings list with repro context. No external side effects.

disconnect_cleanup: runner calls Marionette disconnect and (per policy) stops the launched flutter pid / clears app state so the runner is left clean for the next run. Always runs, even on failure paths (finally-style).

synthesize_report: Claude builds the test report (platform, device, playbook, duration, per-step PASS/FAIL/BLOCKED, screenshots-captured manifest, log excerpts, triaged issues) in the existing slash-command report format. Persist evidence pointers to the store.

HITL APPROVAL GATE (interrupt) — publish_results: pause via LangGraph interrupt BEFORE any irreversible/outbound action: (a) posting a status check / comment back to the GitHub PR, (b) opening/updating a GitHub issue for a confirmed regression (referencing SMR/known blockers), (c) flipping the SDLC deploy-verify gate to pass/fail, (d) Slack/notification to the team. Human reviews the rendered report + proposed actions and resumes with approve/edit/reject. Read-only outcomes (storing evidence, returning the report payload to the caller) do NOT require approval; only sends/publishes/gate-flips do.

execute_approved_actions: only after human resume, perform the approved subset (GitHub status/comment/issue, gate flip, Slack) through their tool integrations. → END with terminal status PASSED | FAILED | BLOCKED | NO_RUNNER | PREFLIGHT_FAILED.
impact: Turns the manual, human-driven `/marionette-test` ritual into a repeatable, resumable, evidence-producing QA gate that protects Scheduler — the live revenue app. v1 value: catches login/navigation/settings/logout regressions on real debug builds (android + ios) before release, on every PR build and nightly, without a human babysitting the VM-service dance. Because it is a deploy-verify gate it directly reduces the risk of shipping a broken auth/home flow to paying users. automatable_score 70: the device/emulator orchestration and toolchain prerequisites are the genuine friction, which is exactly why the executor-on-runner split (not a pure hosted agent) is the only honest v1 — the hosted graph buys repeatability, state, retries, HITL, and reporting, while the runner keeps the irreducible hardware coupling.
approval_gates: Human-in-the-loop interrupt() before ALL irreducible/outbound actions, never before read/observe steps. Gated (require explicit human approve/edit/reject on resume): (1) posting a GitHub PR status check or comment, (2) opening or updating a GitHub issue for a confirmed regression, (3) flipping the SDLC step-05 deploy-verify gate pass/fail, (4) any Slack/team notification. NOT gated (autonomous): health check, launch app, connect, the entire Marionette tap/text/scroll/screenshot/log playbook loop (ephemeral reversible debug build, app state cleared each run), failure triage, disconnect/cleanup, evidence storage, and returning the report payload to the run caller. State is checkpointed at the interrupt so a run survives the wait for human review and resumes exactly where it paused.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS, api.smith.langchain.com) — hosted orchestrator with auto-provisioned Postgres checkpointer + store; do NOT pass a custom checkpointer/store to .compile()

Anthropic Claude (Opus/Sonnet via langchain-anthropic) — reasoning, step classification, failure triage, report synthesis ONLY; with prompt caching; NO model training/fine-tuning/distillation (Anthropic Consumer Terms)

Custom HTTP executor-runner protocol (the agent's primary tool surface): register/heartbeat, run_make (health/launch), marionette_call (connect/get_interactive_elements/tap/enter_text/scroll_to/take_screenshots/get_logs/disconnect), upload_evidence — implemented as LangGraph @tool wrappers calling the runner endpoint

Self-hosted executor runner on the dev Mac or a Stratus ARC runner (Flutter SDK, marionette_mcp, marionette_flutter, Android emulator + adb, iOS simulator + xcrun, Firebase emulators) running run_marionette.sh / check_marionette_health.sh
Marionette MCP (leancodepl/marionette_mcp) over the Dart VM service WebSocket — invoked ONLY from the runner, never from the hosted graph
GitHub (gh / REST) for PR status checks, comments, and regression issues — behind the HITL gate
Slack — optional team notification of results — behind the HITL gate
LangGraph store/object storage for screenshot + log evidence artifacts
LangGraph Platform cron (or launchd) for the nightly full-regression schedule
secrets_needed: RUNNER_SHARED_SECRET (HMAC/bearer auth between LangGraph orchestrator and the self-hosted executor runner)
MARIONETTE_TEST_EMAIL (Firebase-emulator test user; injected as env, not hardcoded in playbooks)
GITHUB_TOKEN (PR status/comment/issue — used only after HITL approval)
SLACK_BOT_TOKEN (optional notifications — used only after HITL approval)
deploy_notes: langgraph.json at repo root (e.g. Scheduler-Systems/Scheduler/agents/marionette-qa/): {dependencies:["."], graphs:{"marionette_qa":"./marionette_qa/agent.py:graph"}, env:".env", python_version:"3.12", image_distro:"wolfi", store:{ttl:{default_ttl:10080}}, checkpointer:{ttl:{strategy:"delete",default_ttl:43200}}}. Build the StateGraph, .compile() to a module-level `graph` — do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= (the platform injects Postgres ones; an InMemorySaver would be ignored in the hosted runtime). State (TypedDict): platform, playbooks[], runner_id, vm_service_uri, run_pid, flutter_log_path, step_results[], evidence_refs[], findings[], proposed_actions[], status. Use interrupt() at the publish_results node for HITL; the auto Postgres checkpointer makes the pause durable across the human wait. Deploy via `langgraph` CLI / LangSmith Deployments UI; inject all secrets through the platform secrets UI/API (never in env file committed to git). The web platform is intentionally out of scope (Marionette has no VM-service on web — that path stays on Chrome/Playwright MCP). v1 ships with ONE executor runner (the dev Mac registered via tunnel); a Stratus ARC runner is the natural next step to move device execution fully off the local Mac per the 'orchestrate local, execute on cluster' rule. CRITICAL caveat to flag to the human: this is genuinely a split system — without a registered, heartbeating executor runner the hosted graph can do nothing but report NO_RUNNER, because LangGraph Cloud has no Flutter/emulator/VM-service of its own.

process: E2E Tests - Playwright (Scheduler Web). A LangGraph Platform (managed Cloud SaaS / LangSmith Deployments) agent that orchestrates Playwright browser-automation E2E runs for the scheduler-web Next.js app, then triages the results. It does NOT run browsers itself: real Chromium execution stays on GitHub Actions runners (per the workspace "orchestrate local, execute on cluster" rule). The agent is the conductor: it decides which suite/environment to run, dispatches the GitHub Actions workflow, polls to completion, pulls logs + the failure artifact (test-results/), uses Claude to classify each failure (real regression vs flake vs auth/env drift vs anti-pattern blind-pass), and proposes a next action. Every irreversible or external action (dispatching a CI run, re-running, commenting on a PR, opening/closing a GitHub issue, posting to Slack) is held behind a human-in-the-loop interrupt and only executes after explicit approval. The agent is grounded in the REAL repo surface, which has drifted from the process card: there is no scripts/test.sh and no e2e-*.yml; the live surface is .github/workflows/gate.yml (a gate job + an e2e job) plus npm run test:e2e against e2e/*.spec.ts, default base URL https://scheduler-web-next.web.app, projects chromium (excludes accessibility.spec.ts) and accessibility. The agent reconciles requested-vs-actual on every run. Claude/LLM is used ONLY for orchestration and triage reasoning — no model training, fine-tuning, distillation, or eval-data generation (Anthropic Consumer Terms guardrail in AGENTS.md).
trigger: Three entry points, all landing on the same compiled StateGraph (one assistant): (1) Scheduled cron via LangGraph Platform Crons API — nightly smoke + weekly accessibility run against https://scheduler-web-next.web.app; (2) Webhook/API thread create from GitHub (PR opened/synchronized or the gate.yml e2e job failing) posted to the deployment's /runs endpoint, carrying repo, ref/SHA, PR number, suite, environment; (3) Manual ad-hoc invocation from LangGraph Studio / SDK with a natural-language request ("run the premium emulator suite against dev-local and tell me what broke"). Note: Dev-Local (localhost:3000) cannot be reached from managed Cloud SaaS, so Dev-Local requests are routed to the GitHub Actions e2e job (which can spin its own server) or rejected with guidance — the agent never assumes localhost is reachable from the cloud runtime.
graph_steps: parse_request: normalize trigger payload into RunPlan {repo: Scheduler-Systems/scheduler-web, ref/SHA, pr_number?, suite ∈ {smoke, accessibility, authenticated, emulator-premium, emulator-features, public-routes, full}, environment ∈ {dev-ci(prod-API read-only test acct), production}, base_url, project ∈ {chromium, accessibility}}. Use Claude to map NL requests to a structured RunPlan.

reconcile_repo_state: read the live repo surface (playwright.config.ts, package.json scripts, .github/workflows/gate.yml) via a read-only GitHub tool. Detect drift from the process card (no scripts/test.sh, no e2e-*.yml; only gate.yml). Confirm the requested suite maps to a real spec in e2e/ and the requested project exists. If Dev-Local requested → flag unreachable-from-cloud and propose CI route.

guard_environment: enforce the e2e-testing.md rule — Dev-CI/Production hit the PRODUCTION API with isolated TEST_USER_* accounts and MUST be read-only/idempotent (no create/modify/delete of prod data). If the requested suite contains destructive or non-test-namespaced steps, refuse or downgrade and record why. Confirm secrets are referenced via env, never inlined.

approval_gate__dispatch (INTERRUPT): present the resolved RunPlan + chosen mechanism (gh workflow dispatch of the gate/e2e job on the target ref, with E2E_BASE_URL) and pause via interrupt() for human approval. No CI minutes spent without sign-off.
dispatch_run: on approval, trigger the GitHub Actions e2e job for the ref (workflow_dispatch / re-run), capturing the run_id. All creds come from injected env (GH_TOKEN), never hardcoded.

poll_until_complete: poll the Actions run status with bounded backoff until success/failure/timeout; surface progress to the thread. This is the long-running step that benefits from the platform's durable checkpointer.
collect_evidence: on completion, fetch job logs and download the failure artifact (test-results/ — traces, screenshots-only-on-failure) plus the Playwright reporter output. Read-only.

triage_failures: use Claude to classify each failed test → {real regression, flake (passed on CI retry / retries:2), auth-or-env drift (redirected to /login, JWT secret mismatch, wrong API), anti-pattern blind-pass (OR-logic, networkidle, waitForTimeout, CSS :has-text), infra/timeout}. Cross-reference e2e-testing.md anti-patterns and the login-redirect guard. Produce per-test verdict + suspected owner + minimal repro command.

decide_next_action: synthesize a recommendation — re-run flakes once, refresh cached auth (.auth/storage-state.json) for auth drift, open a GitHub issue for a real regression (tied to a documented issue per workspace rule), comment a triage summary on the PR, or post a Slack digest. Default = report only.

approval_gate__publish (INTERRUPT): for ANY outward/irreversible action (PR comment, issue open/close, Slack post, CI re-run) present the exact drafted payload and pause via interrupt() for approval. Honors the workspace 'no send without approval' rule.

execute_or_report: on approval, perform the single approved action via the matching tool; otherwise emit the structured triage report to the thread and exit. Persist a run summary to the long-term BaseStore for trend/flake-rate memory across runs.
impact: Protects the only revenue-bearing web surface (scheduler-web, the paywalled premium funnel) by making E2E triage continuous and decision-ready instead of a manual log-reading chore. Automatable score 90. Converts the drifted/manual process ('gh workflow run e2e-*.yml', './scripts/test.sh') into a single governed entry point that reconciles request-vs-reality, classifies flake-vs-regression (the dominant time sink), and keeps a cross-run flake-rate memory. Keeps humans in control of every CI spend and every outward message, satisfying the workspace approval and read-only-prod-data guardrails. Low blast radius: the agent itself executes nothing destructive — heavy browser work stays on GitHub runners.
approval_gates: Two HITL interrupt() gates, both mandatory. GATE 1 (pre-dispatch): blocks spending CI minutes / triggering any workflow run or re-run until the human approves the resolved RunPlan (suite, environment, base_url, ref). GATE 2 (pre-publish): blocks EVERY outward or irreversible action — PR comment, GitHub issue open/close/label, Slack/digest post, CI re-run — showing the exact drafted payload for approval first (workspace 'no send without approval' + 'no delete without approval'). Read-only steps (read repo files, read logs, download artifacts, classify) run without a gate. Implemented with langgraph interrupt() so the managed Postgres checkpointer durably suspends the thread; resume via Command(resume=...) from Studio/SDK. Environment guard is a hard refusal, not an approval: any request that would write/modify/delete production data, or that targets non-test-namespaced resources, is rejected regardless of approval.
tools_integrations: GitHub Actions (workflow_dispatch + re-run of gate.yml e2e job; run status polling; job-log + test-results/ artifact download) via gh CLI / GitHub REST with injected GH_TOKEN
GitHub repo read (playwright.config.ts, package.json, .github/workflows/gate.yml, e2e/ spec listing) for drift reconciliation
GitHub Issues + PR comments (write — gated)
Playwright reporter/trace artifact parsing (read-only, from downloaded test-results/)
Anthropic Claude via langchain (orchestration + triage reasoning ONLY — no model training/eval/distillation)
LangGraph Platform Crons API (scheduled nightly smoke + weekly accessibility)
LangGraph managed Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore (durable interrupts + cross-run flake-rate memory)
Slack (optional digest post — gated)
secrets_needed: GH_TOKEN (fine-scoped PAT or GitHub App installation token: actions:read+write, contents:read, issues:write, pull_requests:write on Scheduler-Systems/scheduler-web)
LANGSMITH_API_KEY (deploy/auth to LangSmith Deployments control plane)
TEST_USER_GITHUB_ID / TEST_USER_USERNAME (consumed by the CI e2e job's auth, not by the agent runtime — referenced so the agent can verify CI has them, never inlined)
deploy_notes: Target = LangGraph Platform managed Cloud SaaS (LangSmith Deployments / api.smith.langchain.com). langgraph.json at scheduler-web/agents/e2e-orchestrator/: {"$schema":"https://langgra.ph/schema.json","dependencies":["."],"graphs":{"e2e_orchestrator":"./graph.py:graph"},"env":".env","python_version":"3.12","image_distro":"wolfi","pip_installer":"uv","checkpointer":{"ttl":{"strategy":"delete","default_ttl":43200}},"store":{"index":{"embed":"openai:text-embedding-3-small","dims":1536},"ttl":{"default_ttl":10080,"sweep_interval_minutes":60}}}. graph.py exports a module-level compiled `graph` (graph = builder.compile()) — do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= at compile time; the platform injects managed Postgres + store (passing InMemorySaver would be overridden). Use interrupt() at both approval gates; resume with Command(resume=...). Real secrets are injected via the LangSmith Deployments UI/API, never committed to .env. Deploy via `langgraph deploy` CLI (or GitHub-integrated build). CRITICAL boundaries: (1) Browser execution stays on GitHub Actions runners — the agent orchestrates, it does not run Chromium in the managed container (matches 'orchestrate local/conductor, execute on cluster' memory). (2) Dev-Local (localhost:3000) is unreachable from Cloud SaaS — always route Dev-Local to the CI e2e job. (3) Per AGENTS.md, this is orchestration/QA tooling, NOT ML model dev — compliant with Anthropic Consumer Terms. (4) Tie every regression to a documented GitHub issue (workspace rule) before any code-fix follow-up. Process card is stale (scripts/test.sh, e2e-*.yml absent); the reconcile_repo_state node is load-bearing — keep it even after the card is corrected.

trigger: GitHub webhook (App or Actions-dispatched POST to the deployment's /webhooks endpoint) on `check_run`/`workflow_run` = completed+failure for the `gate` job, plus `pull_request` (opened/synchronize), on the Vitest-capable repos. A thin GitHub Action step in gate.yml fires the webhook with {repo, pr_number, sha, run_id, job} on Vitest/coverage failure. Also supports manual invocation (LangGraph SDK `/runs`) for ad-hoc "triage this PR" and a nightly CronCreate sweep of open PRs with red Vitest gates. Hard repo allowlist + per-repo capability probe (must have vitest.config.ts and a `test` script) so non-Vitest repos like gal-website are skipped; gal-swarm is restricted to test/coverage files only (never model/training code per Anthropic-terms guardrail).
process: Vitest unit-test / coverage CI gatekeeper for React/TypeScript web repos. When the `gate` CI job's Vitest step goes red on a PR, the agent reproduces and diagnoses the failure, classifies it (failing assertion/runtime error vs. coverage-threshold shortfall vs. flaky/non-deterministic vs. infra/setup), and produces a concrete, scoped fix proposal (updated/added co-located *.test.tsx, or a justified vitest.config.ts threshold/exclude note). It NEVER commits, pushes, opens, or merges anything autonomously: every code change and every PR comment is rendered as a draft and held at a human-in-the-loop interrupt for shay@ to approve, edit, or reject. On approval it posts the review comment / opens a draft PR via the GitHub tool; the merge itself stays human-only. The agent only orchestrates Claude for reasoning over test output and source — it does no model training/eval/distillation, satisfying the workspace Anthropic-terms guardrail.
graph_steps: ingest_event: parse webhook/manual payload; resolve {repo, pr_number, head_sha, run_id, failed_job}; enforce repo allowlist + GAL-restriction scope; short-circuit (END) if repo has no vitest.config.ts / `test` script (capability probe)

fetch_context: via GitHub tool pull failed-run logs, the PR diff, changed files, vitest.config.ts (coverage thresholds + include/exclude), and any uploaded coverage-summary.json / json-summary artifact; load into state

classify_failure: Claude node categorizes -> {assertion_failure, runtime_error, coverage_shortfall, flaky_nondeterministic, infra_setup}; extract the exact failing test names / uncovered files+lines (e.g. schedule-detail-client.tsx uncovered stmts) and statements/branches/functions/lines deltas vs 85/75/85/85

plan_fix: Claude proposes the minimal scoped change set — add/repair co-located *.test.tsx for uncovered logic, fix a broken assertion, de-flake (fake timers / deterministic seeds), or (only with explicit rationale) a config exclude for genuinely-untestable SDK-bootstrap shells; emits a unified diff + plain-English explanation, no writes yet

validate_proposal (optional/best-effort): if a sandbox runner is wired, run `npm run test:coverage` on the proposed diff in an ephemeral sandbox and attach pass/fail + new coverage numbers; otherwise mark as 'unverified, needs local run' in the proposal

human_approval_gate [interrupt]: present {classification, diff, coverage before/after, risk notes} and BLOCK via LangGraph interrupt(); resume with approve / edit / reject — no irreversible action crosses this line

execute_approved_action: on approve, use the GitHub tool to post the diagnosis as a PR review comment and/or open a DRAFT PR with the test changes against the contributor's branch; record run_id + decision to the long-term store; merge is never performed by the agent
finalize: write outcome (proposed/approved/rejected, links) to BaseStore for the nightly sweep + audit trail; END
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS, api.smith.langchain.com) — Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore auto-injected; do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= to .compile()
Claude via langchain-anthropic (ChatAnthropic, claude-opus/sonnet) — reasoning over test logs + source ONLY; no model training/eval/distillation
GitHub REST/GraphQL (PyGithub or httpx) as a LangChain tool — read run logs, PR diff, files, artifacts; write = post review comment + open draft PR (gated)
GitHub Action shim in each repo's gate.yml that POSTs the failure webhook to the deployment
Vitest + @vitest/coverage-v8 output parser; coverage-summary.json (json-summary reporter) parser keyed to thresholds 85/75/85/85
LangGraph custom http app (/webhooks) for inbound GitHub events; langgraph.json `auth` for webhook HMAC + caller authz
CronCreate-style scheduled run (LangGraph cron) for the nightly open-PR red-gate sweep
Optional: ephemeral Node sandbox runner (e.g. Vercel Sandbox / ARC runner) for validate_proposal — heavy work off the local Mac per workspace policy
LangSmith tracing/observability for every run
approval_gates: Single mandatory human-in-the-loop interrupt() at human_approval_gate before ANY irreversible/external action. Gated actions: posting a PR review comment, opening/updating a draft PR, any git write. Non-gated (safe, read-only): fetching logs/diffs/config/artifacts, classification, and generating the proposal diff in-memory. Merging to main is OUT OF SCOPE for the agent entirely (human-only, protected by branch protection). The sandbox validate step is read-only (ephemeral, no repo writes) so it runs pre-gate. Approver = shay@scheduler-systems.com; resume payload carries approve|edit(diff)|reject + reason, persisted to the store for audit. Aligns with workspace rules: no send/commit/merge/delete without explicit approval.
secrets_needed: APPROVAL_NOTIFY_TOKEN (optional: Slack/Gmail bot creds to ping the approver when an interrupt is waiting)
SANDBOX_RUNNER_TOKEN (optional: only if validate_proposal sandbox is enabled)
deploy_notes: Deploy as a managed LangGraph Platform deployment. Repo root holds langgraph.json: {"dependencies":["."],"graphs":{"vitest_gatekeeper":"./agent/graph.py:graph"},"env":".env","python_version":"3.12","image_distro":"wolfi","pip_installer":"uv","auth":{"path":"./agent/auth.py:auth"},"http":{"app":"./agent/app.py:app"},"store":{"ttl":{"default_ttl":43200,"sweep_interval_minutes":60}}}. graph.py: StateGraph compiled WITHOUT a custom checkpointer/store (platform injects Postgres) and assigned to module-level `graph`; the human_approval_gate uses interrupt() so state persists across the wait. app.py mounts a custom /webhooks route (FastAPI) verifying the GitHub HMAC; auth.py enforces caller authz. Secrets injected via the LangSmith UI/API, never committed — env file is placeholders only (security rule: never hardcode secrets). Deploy via `langgraph deploy` CLI or Git-connected build. v1 repo allowlist: Scheduler-Systems/scheduler-web (primary, live-revenue, strict gate — highest care), gal-run/platform/gal-chrome-extension, gal-run/platform/gal-swarm (test/coverage files ONLY — model code off-limits). Exclude gal-website (no Vitest). Wire CI by adding a webhook-fire step to each repo's gate.yml on the Vitest step failure. Heavy validation (npm run test:coverage) must run in the cloud sandbox/ARC runner, not locally. Tie work to a tracked GitHub issue before the agent opens any PR (workspace rule).
impact: Protects the scheduler-web CI gate that guards live RevenueCat revenue: keeps the 85/75/85/85 Vitest coverage floor green so regressions and untested new pages can't silently ship, and turns the most common red-gate (a new app/** page added without its co-located test, which the config is explicitly designed to catch) into an instant, reviewable fix proposal instead of a manual back-and-forth. Cuts contributor/maintainer time-to-green on the highest-frequency, highest-automatable (score 95) QA failure class, while keeping a human in control of every code change and every merge.

process: Node.js Unit Tests - Firebase Cloud Functions / API backend (Scheduler-Systems/scheduler-api). Authors, repairs, and triages unit tests for route handlers, middleware, Firebase integration, contracts, and custom functions. Ground-truth correction vs the intake brief: the repo does NOT use Vitest/Jest — it uses the native node:test runner (node --test), driven by package.json scripts ("test": "node --test", "test:e2e", "test:coverage" via c8) and test/run.mjs (node:test programmatic runner + spec reporter, concurrency:true, timeout 30000). Two npm workspaces: root API (test/, ~33 *.test.mjs specs, ESM) and functions/ (functions/test/, 4 *.test.js specs, c8 coverage, firebase-functions-test). The "6358 test files" figure is inflated by node_modules — actual hand-authored specs are ~215, of which ~37 are the core unit specs the agent targets. CI of record is .github/workflows/gate.yml (PR + push to main): npm install -> npm run typecheck -> npm run lint -> npm test, plus a separate e2e job (npm run test:e2e). The agent is a LangGraph Platform (managed / LangSmith Deployments) deployment whose graph orchestrates Claude to: detect failing/missing/flaky unit tests, run the REAL repo runner in a sandbox, generate or repair test code, and propose changes — but every irreversible action (git push, branch creation, PR open, PR/issue comment, CI re-trigger) is held behind a human-in-the-loop interrupt. NO ML model development/training/distillation anywhere; orchestration calls Claude only as the reasoning LLM. Secrets (GITHUB_TOKEN, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, optional FIREBASE emulator config) come from LangGraph Platform env injection, never hardcoded or committed.
trigger: Three entry points, all mapped to LangGraph Platform assistant runs / threads: (1) Webhook from GitHub Actions gate.yml on a red unit-test job (workflow_run: completed + conclusion=failure on the gate job for scheduler-api) -> POST to the deployment's runs endpoint with {repo, sha, branch, failing_job_url}. (2) Scheduled cron (LangGraph Platform Crons / assistant schedule) nightly to sweep for coverage gaps and quarantined/flaky specs on main. (3) On-demand human invocation from the LangGraph Studio / SDK ("write unit tests for src/routes/<x>.mjs" or "fix the failing route-handlers spec") passing a thread_id for resumable HIL. All three converge on the same StateGraph; the cron and webhook paths still stop at the approval interrupt before any push/PR.
graph_steps: ingest_request: normalize trigger payload (webhook | cron | manual) into State {repo, ref, target_paths, mode: repair|author|triage, correlation_id}; clone/checkout scheduler-api at the given sha into an ephemeral sandbox workspace (shallow). No git writes yet.

detect_runner: read package.json + functions/package.json + test/run.mjs to CONFIRM the runner is node:test (not Vitest/Jest) and which workspace (root vs functions/) the target lives in; pick the exact command (npm test / node --test test/<f> / functions: npm --prefix functions test). Fail-closed to 'manual review' if an unexpected runner is found.

run_baseline_tests: execute the real repo runner in the sandbox (node --test, 30s timeout, concurrency) capturing pass/fail/flaky + stderr; for functions workspace run c8 coverage. This is a READ/idempotent action, no interrupt. Parse node:test TAP/spec output into structured failures.

classify (conditional router): branch on mode -> 'repair' (failing/regressed spec), 'author' (missing coverage for a route handler/middleware/contract/custom function), or 'triage' (flaky/quarantine candidate). Routes to the matching Claude node.

claude_analyze_and_draft: Claude (orchestration only) reads the relevant src module + sibling specs, the failure output, and project conventions (ESM, node:assert/strict, createXRoutes + createMemoryStore patterns, actor/tenant fixtures) and DRAFTS test file edits as a unified diff. Does NOT touch the working tree's tracked history — writes to a scratch branch in the sandbox only.

apply_in_sandbox_and_verify: apply the drafted diff to the sandbox checkout, re-run the targeted specs + a focused subset of the suite; loop back to claude_analyze_and_draft up to N=3 times if still failing (bounded self-correction). Reversible — sandbox only.

summarize_and_propose: build a human-readable proposal (diff, before/after test results, coverage delta, risk notes, which CI job this satisfies). Persist to LangGraph store/checkpointer so the thread is resumable.

APPROVAL_GATE (interrupt): call interrupt() with the proposal payload and STOP. The graph yields a human-in-the-loop interrupt; nothing is pushed. A human resumes the thread with Command(resume={approve|reject|edit_instructions}).

act_on_approval (conditional, post-resume): ONLY if approved -> create branch test/<slug>, commit (message prefixed with branch name per repo git-workflow rule), git push origin <branch>, open a PR against scheduler-api via GITHUB_TOKEN, optionally post a summary comment. On 'edit_instructions' loop back to claude_analyze_and_draft; on 'reject' end cleanly and record outcome. Every action here is the gated irreversible set.
finalize: emit run result (PR url or 'no-op'/'rejected'), write learnings note to store, clean up sandbox; END.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments / managed Cloud SaaS) — Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore auto-provisioned; do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= to .compile()
Anthropic Claude (via langchain-anthropic) as the sole reasoning LLM — orchestration only, no training/distillation
GitHub REST/GraphQL (gh CLI or PyGithub) for branch/PR/comment — gated
Git (sandbox checkout of github.com/Scheduler-Systems/scheduler-api) — read free, write gated
node:test runner via Node 20 + npm (root workspace) and functions/ workspace with c8 coverage
GitHub Actions gate.yml (webhook source via workflow_run) — read of CI result, optional re-trigger gated
Firebase emulators (auth:9099, firestore:8088, functions:5001) — optional, only if integration-flavored unit tests require them
LangGraph Crons / scheduled assistant runs for the nightly coverage+flaky sweep
langgraph CLI + langgraph.json (graphs: {"guardian": "./agent/graph.py:graph"}, python_version 3.12, image_distro wolfi, env injected) for deploy
