approval_gates: Single mandatory human-in-the-loop interrupt() at the APPROVAL_GATE node, before ANY irreversible/state-mutating action. Held behind the gate: git branch creation, commit, git push, PR open, PR/issue comments, and any CI re-trigger. Allowed WITHOUT approval (read-only/idempotent, sandbox-confined): cloning/checkout, reading source + CI results, running the real node:test suite, applying diffs in the ephemeral sandbox, computing coverage. Resume contract: human replies via Command(resume={'decision':'approve'|'reject'|'edit', 'notes':...}); 'edit' loops back to drafting, 'reject' ends as no-op, only 'approve' enters act_on_approval. The bounded self-correction loop (max 3 sandbox iterations) cannot bypass the gate. State is checkpointed so the thread is fully resumable after the interrupt (durable across the managed Postgres checkpointer).
deploy_notes: Deploy as a Python graph to LangGraph Platform (managed). langgraph.json at repo root of a NEW agent repo (do NOT add Python deps into scheduler-api): {"dependencies":["."],"graphs":{"guardian":"./agent/graph.py:graph"},"env":".env","python_version":"3.12","image_distro":"wolfi","pip_installer":"uv"}. The sandbox node needs Node 20 + npm available in the image to run node:test — add via dockerfile_lines (e.g. install Node 20 + a writable workdir) since the base wolfi image is Python-only; alternatively run the test execution in a separate provisioned runner. graph.py builds a StateGraph, compiles WITHOUT custom checkpointer/store (platform injects Postgres). Use interrupt()/Command for HIL (LangGraph durable interrupts). Set up a GitHub webhook (workflow_run failure) -> deployment runs endpoint, secured with GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET via the http.app custom route or a thin verifier. Inject ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / GITHUB_TOKEN through the LangGraph Platform secrets UI, never in langgraph.json or .env-in-git. CRITICAL CORRECTIONS vs intake brief baked into the agent: (1) runner is node:test, not Vitest/Jest — detect_runner fail-closes if it sees otherwise; (2) treat the '6358 files' as node_modules noise — scope to test/ + functions/test/ + test/e2e/ only; (3) two npm workspaces with different runner invocations (root vs functions/, the latter with c8). Compliance: this agent performs ordinary app/test/IaC work and orchestrates Claude only — it does NOT develop, train, evaluate, or distill any ML model, consistent with the workspace Anthropic-terms guardrail. Tie work to a documented GitHub issue and never send/open anything without the approval gate firing (matches workspace 'no send/no delete without approval' rules).
impact: automatable_score 95/100 is justified for the mechanical core (run real suite, classify node:test failures, draft conforming ESM specs, verify in sandbox), but the irreversible 5% (push/PR/comment) is deliberately kept human-gated. Targets the ~37 core unit specs across the two workspaces guarding route handlers, middleware-auth, contracts, firebase integration, revenuecat-sync, and custom functions — the suite that gate.yml blocks every PR on. Value: keeps the red->green loop fast, raises functions/ coverage (currently the thinner workspace), and quarantines flaky specs without a human writing boilerplate, while never auto-merging or auto-pushing.

process: Unit Tests - Swift/iOS (XCTest). Triages CI test results, diagnoses failures, detects flaky tests, finds coverage gaps across the 6 XCTest files (AuthViewModelTests, ModelsTests, ScheduleViewModelCreateTests, ServiceTests, ViewModelServiceTests, AppViewsTests), and drafts new/fixed tests + GitHub issues/PRs for Scheduler-Systems/scheduler-ios. Every write or dispatch action is human-approval-gated.
trigger: Primary: GitHub webhook into the deployment's custom HTTP route (http.app in langgraph.json) on the `gate` workflow's check_suite/workflow_run completed event for pull_request and push-to-main. Secondary: manual run via LangGraph SDK/Studio with {repo, ref, run_id}. Tertiary: a daily cron (08:00 local) that scans for newly-merged Sources/SchedulerApp ViewModels/Services lacking matching tests. The agent itself does NOT run swift test (Linux container, no Xcode) — it dispatches/reads the macOS-14 GitHub Actions runner.
graph_steps: ingest: parse trigger payload (repo, ref, PR#, workflow_run_id) into State; load thread by ref so reruns resume the same checkpoint

fetch_results: read CI artifacts via GitHub API + read-only git clone — gate.yml logs, swift test xUnit/JSON output, per-test pass/fail/duration, changed files in Sources/SchedulerApp/{ViewModels,Services,Models}
classify: Claude routes State into {all_green, real_failure, suspected_flaky, coverage_gap} (conditional edges)

diagnose_failure: for real_failure, Claude reads the failing XCTest + the SUT (e.g. ScheduleViewModel.createSchedule dup-name logic) and produces a root-cause hypothesis + minimal fix candidate (test bug vs product bug)
detect_flaky: for suspected_flaky, request N reruns of just the failing tests on the macOS runner via workflow_dispatch (gated), compare pass ratio, label flaky vs deterministic

coverage_gap: map changed Sources symbols to existing test classes, list untested ViewModels/Services/business-logic branches, draft XCTest cases in repo style (MainActor, mock *Protocol services, Result-based stubs)
synthesize_artifacts: assemble proposed git diff (new/edited test file under Tests/SchedulerAppTests/), draft GitHub issue body, and PR title/description — held in State, nothing sent yet
HUMAN_APPROVAL (interrupt): surface proposed diff/issue/PR + rerun-dispatch for explicit approve/edit/reject before any write or workflow dispatch
act: on approval only — open branch+PR or comment on PR or file issue via GitHub API (commit trailer: Co-Authored-By Claude); on reject, record rationale to BaseStore memory
report: write run summary + decisions to long-term store keyed by repo/ref, post concise status comment (gated), END
tools_integrations: GitHub REST/GraphQL API (Scheduler-Systems/scheduler-ios): read check_suite/workflow_run, artifacts, PR diffs; gated create-branch/commit/PR/issue/comment
GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch to re-run gate.yml on macos-14 runner (gated) — the ONLY way to actually execute swift test/XCTest
Read-only git clone/sparse-checkout of Sources/SchedulerApp + Tests/SchedulerAppTests for SUT-vs-test reasoning
Anthropic Claude (claude-opus / sonnet via ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) for classify/diagnose/test-synthesis — orchestration only, NO model training/distillation
LangGraph Platform managed Postgres checkpointer (resume per-ref) + BaseStore (flaky history, prior reject rationales, coverage map)
Custom HTTP route (Starlette app in langgraph.json http.app) for the GitHub webhook receiver with signature verification
Optional Slack notify of approval-pending interrupts (gated send)
approval_gates: HUMAN-IN-THE-LOOP via LangGraph interrupt() before EVERY irreversible/external write: (1) opening branch/PR, (2) committing test files, (3) PR/issue comments, (4) filing issues, (5) dispatching workflow_dispatch reruns (consumes macOS-runner minutes), (6) any Slack send. The interrupt payload shows the exact diff/body/dispatch params; resume requires approve|edit|reject. all_green path with no proposed action requires NO approval (read-only). Reject reasons persisted to store to tune future runs.

CRITICAL: graph.compile() with NO checkpointer=/store= args — the managed platform injects production Postgres; passing InMemorySaver would be ignored/overridden. Secrets injected via LangSmith Deployments UI/API, never committed (.env only references names). Deploy via `langgraph deploy` (CLI) or LangSmith UI to api.smith.langchain.com. interrupt() pauses persist on the managed checkpointer so an approval can arrive hours later and resume the same thread. Hard guardrail (workspace AGENTS.md / Anthropic Terms): orchestration calls Claude only — NO training/fine-tuning/distillation/eval of any ML model; this agent must never bundle or run gal-model. It also never executes XCTest itself (no Xcode on Linux) — it strictly orchestrates the existing macos-14 gate.yml runner. Register a GitHub App (not PAT) scoped to scheduler-ios for least-privilege gated writes. Wire webhook to the deployment's custom HTTP route URL.
impact: Cuts manual XCTest triage on every scheduler-ios PR: auto-diagnoses red gate.yml runs (test-bug vs product-bug), quarantines flaky tests with rerun evidence instead of blind retries, and continuously closes coverage gaps on the revenue-critical ViewModels/Services (e.g. ScheduleViewModel.createSchedule dup-name validation, AuthViewModel flows). Protects the live iOS release pipeline (gate.yml blocks merge; merge feeds TestFlight via release.yml) while keeping a human in control of every code/issue/PR write. automatable_score 90 supports near-full automation of the read+diagnose+draft loop, with humans only approving the final irreversible step.

process: Automates triage of the Scheduler-Android JUnit/MockK unit-test gate (./gradlew test, 35 test files under app/src/test/java covering ViewModels, domain scheduling, DTOs, UI state). When the gate.yml unit-test job fails on a PR or push to main, the agent ingests the JUnit XML (app/build/test-results/testDebugUnitTest) and GitHub Actions run logs, parses failed cases, uses Claude to classify each failure as real-regression vs flaky/infra (timeouts, MockK stub mismatch, coroutine timing, env), and produces a structured diagnosis. For real regressions it drafts a minimal candidate fix (test or source) and a PR comment summarizing root cause + suggested patch. All writes (PR comment, opening/pushing a fix branch) are blocked behind a human approval interrupt; nothing is sent or pushed without explicit human resume. LLM is used only for reasoning/triage over artifacts — no model training, no gradle execution by the agent. v1 is read-mostly: ingest -> parse -> triage -> classify -> draft -> APPROVAL GATE -> act-or-park.
trigger: Primary: GitHub Actions webhook on workflow_run completed for the 'gate' workflow's unit-test job with conclusion=failure (PR or push to main). Secondary: manual invocation via LangGraph SDK/Studio passing {owner, repo, run_id or sha, pr_number?} for on-demand triage of a known failing run. Optional nightly cron pass to flag accumulating flaky tests. The agent is webhook/event-driven, not a poller; managed Postgres checkpointer holds the interrupted thread until a human resumes the approval.
graph_steps: ingest_trigger: validate event payload {owner, repo, run_id/sha, pr_number}; reject if not the unit-test gate job or not a failure; seed State
fetch_artifacts: GitHub API + tools — download JUnit XML (testDebugUnitTest), failed-step logs, and changed-files diff for the PR/commit (read-only)
parse_failures: deterministically parse JUnit XML into structured failed-case list (class, method, message, stacktrace, type); no LLM
triage_failures: Claude reasons over each failure + relevant changed-files context to produce root-cause hypothesis and confidence
classify_real_vs_flaky: Claude tags each failure real-regression | flaky/infra (MockK stub drift, coroutine/Turbine timing, timeout, env) with rationale
route_decision: conditional edge — if all flaky -> park_and_report (annotate, no fix); if any real -> draft_fix
draft_fix: Claude drafts a minimal candidate patch (diff) + PR-comment body explaining cause and proposed change; nothing applied yet
human_approval_gate: interrupt() surfaces {classification, diagnosis, proposed comment, proposed diff} to a human; pause thread until Command(resume={approve|edit|reject})
apply_action: on approve only — post PR comment and/or create branch + commit fix-PR via GitHub API; on reject -> park_and_report with reason
park_and_report: write structured triage summary (LangSmith trace + optional GH check-run annotation) and END
impact: Cuts time-to-diagnosis on red unit-test gates for the live revenue app (scheduler-android), reduces wasted re-runs from flaky tests by classifying them before a human looks, and keeps the test suite trustworthy as ViewModels/scheduling logic evolve. Read-mostly + approval-gated, so zero risk of unreviewed code reaching the repo. Not directly revenue-generating, so P1 rather than P0 — it protects shipping velocity for a paying product without blocking it.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS, Postgres checkpointer + store auto-provisioned)
Anthropic Claude via langchain-anthropic (triage/classification/draft reasoning only)
GitHub REST/GraphQL API (read: workflow_run, artifacts/logs, PR diff; write: PR comment, create branch/commit — gated)
GitHub Actions webhook (workflow_run completed) as the deployment trigger
JUnit XML parser (stdlib, deterministic)
LangSmith tracing/observability for run inspection
langgraph.json deploy config (python_version 3.11, image_distro wolfi, dependencies ['.'], graphs.agent -> ./agent/graph.py:graph)
approval_gates: human_approval_gate node uses LangGraph dynamic interrupt(): execution pauses before ANY irreversible action (posting a PR comment, creating a branch/commit, opening a fix-PR). The interrupt payload presents the failure classification, root-cause diagnosis, proposed PR-comment text, and the proposed diff. A human resumes via Command(resume={'decision':'approve'|'edit'|'reject', 'edited_diff?':...}). Only 'approve' (or 'edit'+approve) reaches apply_action; 'reject' routes to park_and_report. State survives the pause via the managed Postgres checkpointer so approval can arrive minutes/hours later through a separate API call. No auto-merge, no force-push, no destructive git ops anywhere.
deploy_notes: Deploy as a new repo (e.g. Scheduler-Systems/qa-junit-triage-agent) targeting LangSmith Deployments / LangGraph Platform managed Cloud SaaS (api.smith.langchain.com). langgraph.json: {python_version:'3.11', image_distro:'wolfi', dependencies:['.'], graphs:{agent:'./agent/graph.py:graph'}, env:'.env'(local only)}. Build graph as StateGraph(State).compile() exported as module-level `graph`; do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= (platform injects production Postgres). Inject all secrets via the platform UI/API, never commit. Wire a GitHub Actions webhook (workflow_run, verified with GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET) to the deployment's runs endpoint to start a thread per failing gate run; resume the interrupt via the LangGraph SDK/Studio or a thin approval UI. GITHUB_TOKEN must be a least-privilege fine-grained PAT/app scoped to scheduler-android with pull_request:write + contents:write (for gated fix branches) and actions:read. Test locally with `langgraph dev` against a sample failing run before promoting. Constraint compliance: agent only orchestrates Claude over test artifacts (no training/distillation, no gradle run), all sends/pushes are interrupt-gated, secrets are env-injected.

process: Instrumented Tests - Android (Espresso/Compose) failure triage and fix-proposal for scheduler-android. Wraps the existing `gate.yml` `connectedDebugAndroidTest` job (reactivecircus/android-emulator-runner, API 34, Hilt + espresso-core 3.5.1 + compose ui-test-junit4, 25 instrumented test classes under app/src/androidTest/java/com/schedulersystems/scheduler/). The agent does NOT run the emulator itself (heavy GPU/KVM work stays on GitHub-hosted/ARC CI per the "orchestrate local, execute on cluster" rule); it ingests CI artifacts (JUnit XML at app/build/outputs/androidTest-results/connected/*.xml + HTML at app/build/reports/androidTests/connected/ + logcat) from a failed gate.yml run, parses per-test outcomes, classifies each failure as real-regression vs flaky/emulator-infra (timeout, ANR, INSTALL_FAILED, emulator boot, Hilt inject failure, Compose idling-resource timeout), uses Claude purely for root-cause reasoning + diff-proposal on the failing test/source, and produces a triage report. Every irreversible/outbound action (PR comment, GitHub issue, applying a `flaky-retry`/`ci-rerun` label, dispatching a workflow re-run, opening a fix branch/PR) is held behind a human-in-the-loop interrupt. v1 is orchestration-only (calls Claude for reasoning; NO model training/distillation, NO gal-model).
trigger: Primary: GitHub webhook (workflow_run completed with conclusion=failure for workflow "gate", job android-test) routed to the LangGraph Platform deployment's webhook endpoint -> creates a run on the `triage` graph with {repo, pr_number, run_id, head_sha}. Secondary: manual invocation via LangGraph SDK / `langgraph dev` with the same payload for replay of a historical failed run. Also supports a nightly CronCreate-scheduled sweep that scans the last N gate.yml runs on main for newly-flaky tests. No auto-trigger on push to avoid noise; only on failed instrumented-test jobs.
graph_steps: ingest_artifacts: download the failed gate.yml run's artifacts via GitHub API (Octokit/REST) using GITHUB_TOKEN from env -- JUnit XML (app/build/outputs/androidTest-results/connected/*.xml), HTML report dir, and logcat; store raw blobs in the injected BaseStore keyed by run_id. Read-only.

parse_results: parse the JUnit XML into a structured list of {test_class, test_method, status, failure_message, stacktrace, duration_ms}; diff against the last known-green baseline for head_sha's branch (also from store) to isolate NEWLY-failing tests vs pre-existing.

classify_failures: deterministic rule pass first (regex/heuristics on stacktrace+logcat: emulator-boot/INSTALL_FAILED/ANR/idling-timeout/HiltAndroidRule inject error => infra/flaky; assertion/Compose onNodeWith* not-found/NPE in app code => candidate-regression). Ambiguous cases escalate to a Claude classify call. Output a label per failure.

root_cause_analysis: for each candidate-regression, fetch the failing test file + the production source it exercises (e.g. HomeScreenTest.kt + HomeScreen/HomeViewModel) via GitHub contents API, send to Claude with the stacktrace to get a root-cause hypothesis and a minimal proposed unified diff. Bounded fan-out (cap concurrent Claude calls).

build_triage_report: assemble a single markdown report: newly-failing vs flaky table, per-regression root-cause + proposed diff, suggested CI action (rerun for flaky, fix-PR for regression). Persist to store.

human_review_interrupt: interrupt() with the full report + an explicit list of proposed irreversible actions (post PR comment / open issue / apply label / dispatch rerun / open fix branch). Human approves, edits, or rejects each action. NOTHING outbound happens before resume.

execute_approved_actions: on resume, perform ONLY the approved actions via GitHub API (idempotent: dedupe PR comments by marker, dedupe issues by title+head_sha). Pushing a fix branch opens a draft PR only -- never merges, never force-pushes, never re-runs destructively.
record_outcome: write the decision + executed actions + updated flaky-test scoreboard back to the store for the nightly sweep and audit trail; END.
tools_integrations: GitHub REST/GraphQL API (octokit) - read workflow_run artifacts, repo contents, PR metadata; write PR comments, issues, labels, draft fix-PRs (all approval-gated)
GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch - trigger gate.yml re-run for confirmed-flaky jobs (approval-gated)
Anthropic Claude API (orchestration/reasoning only) - failure classification of ambiguous cases + root-cause + diff proposals; prompt-cache the static rubric/test-context
LangGraph Platform managed Postgres checkpointer (auto-injected) - durable interrupt/resume across human review
LangGraph BaseStore (auto-injected, store config in langgraph.json) - raw artifact blobs, green-baseline-per-branch, flaky-test scoreboard, dedupe markers
LangGraph Platform webhook endpoint - receives workflow_run failure events
LangGraph CronCreate/scheduled run - nightly flaky-test sweep over recent main gate.yml runs

(Reference only, NOT in v1 runtime) Gradle connectedDebugAndroidTest + reactivecircus/android-emulator-runner API 34 - the CI job whose artifacts are consumed; the agent never boots the emulator itself
approval_gates: HARD human-in-the-loop via LangGraph interrupt() at human_review_interrupt before ANY outbound/irreversible action. Gated actions: (1) posting/updating a PR comment, (2) opening a GitHub issue, (3) applying/removing labels (flaky-retry, ci-rerun, needs-triage), (4) dispatching a gate.yml workflow re-run, (5) creating a fix branch + draft PR. Read-only steps (ingest, parse, classify, root-cause, report) run unattended. Resume carries an explicit per-action approve/edit/reject decision; default on timeout/no-decision = reject (fail-closed). Hard prohibitions enforced in code regardless of approval: never merge a PR, never force-push, never push to main/protected branches, never close another author's PR, never delete branches/issues/comments, never rewrite history. Aligns with workspace 'no send without approval' + 'no delete without approval' + 'always a documented GitHub issue' rules. Branch protection on scheduler-android still independently blocks merges.
deploy_notes: Target: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (Cloud SaaS, managed), region api.smith.langchain.com. langgraph.json at repo root of a NEW small Python service repo (Scheduler-Systems/scheduler-qa-triage-agent or a subdir of scheduler-android tooling -- keep it out of the Kotlin app module): {"$schema":"https://langgra.ph/schema.json","dependencies":["."],"graphs":{"triage":"./agent/graph.py:graph"},"env":".env","python_version":"3.12","image_distro":"wolfi","pip_installer":"uv","store":{"index":{"embed":"openai:text-embedding-3-small","dims":1536}},"checkpointer":{"ttl":{"strategy":"delete","default_ttl":43200}},"http":{"app":"./agent/webhook.py:app"},"auth":{"path":"./agent/auth.py:auth"}}. CRITICAL platform rules followed: graph is module-level `graph = builder.compile()` with NO checkpointer=/store= passed (platform injects managed Postgres + store); custom Starlette app via http.app mounts the /github/webhook route (HMAC-verify with GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET) that maps workflow_run failures to SDK run creates; custom Auth in auth.py guards the deployment. Secrets injected via LangSmith Deployments UI/API, never committed (.env holds only non-secret defaults). Prefer a GitHub App (App ID + private key + installation) over a PAT for least-privilege, scoped to scheduler-android: contents:read, pull_requests:write, issues:write, actions:write, checks:read. Deploy via `langgraph deploy` (CLI) or Git-integration auto-build. Compliance: this is orchestration calling Claude for reasoning only -- NO ML model training/eval/distillation, gal-model untouched, satisfies the Anthropic-terms guardrail in AGENTS.md. Pre-req cleanup flagged: scheduler-android/.github/workflows/android-build.yml is STALE (references Flutter/pubspec on a now-Kotlin repo and will never match its lib/** paths) -- the live instrumented-test gate is gate.yml's android-test job; the agent keys off gate.yml only. Local dev/test the graph with `langgraph dev` replaying ci-logs/android-test-log.txt before first cloud deploy.
impact: Cuts manual instrumented-test triage on every failed PR gate. Today a human must open the gate.yml android-test job, read logcat + JUnit XML across up to 25 Espresso/Compose/Hilt test classes, decide flaky-vs-real, and either re-run or file a fix. The agent does the parse/classify/root-cause unattended and presents a one-screen approve/reject report, turning ~15-30 min of log-reading per failure into a few seconds of review. Protects the revenue-bearing Scheduler app's release gate (faster green PRs, less flaky-rerun roulette) while keeping a hard human gate on anything that touches the repo. automatable_score 85 is consistent: ingestion/parsing/classification/reporting fully automate; only the act-on-repo decisions stay human, by design.

trigger: Three trigger surfaces, all landing on the same compiled graph (one assistant, multiple input shapes): (1) Scheduled cron via LangGraph Platform Crons API — a recurring "health sweep" thread that runs `emulators-status --json`, diffs against expected port/service inventory, and only escalates (notifies + optionally proposes a restart) when a service is down or drifted; default cadence every 30 min during work hours, idle otherwise. (2) Webhook / API run — GitHub Actions or a developer hits the deployment's `/runs` endpoint (e.g. before an integration-test job, or on a `make emulators-*` failure) with an intent payload {action: status|start|restart|reset, repo, reason}. (3) Slack slash-command / ChatOps — `/emulators status|reset` routed to the same endpoint. The agent is orchestration-only: it never spawns emulators inside the LangGraph container (the Mac is a conductor per workspace memory); it calls out to a configured executor (an ARC self-hosted runner or an SSH-reachable dev host) that actually runs the `make emulators-*` targets, then reasons over the returned JSON/exit codes.
process: Firebase Emulator Suite (Local Development) — QA. Docker/Firebase-CLI emulator environment for Scheduler: Auth(9099), Firestore(8081), Functions(5001), Hosting(5003 per firebase.json / 5000 per docker-compose — a real config drift), UI(4000), Storage(9199). Lifecycle via `make emulators-start | emulators-status | emulators-reset | emulators-stop`, backed by scripts/emulators/{start_emulators,status,reset,stop}.sh + docker-compose.emulators.yml. status.sh emits --json/--simple; reset.sh does `rm -rf` on emulator data dirs and supports --backup. automatable_score 95.
graph_steps: START -> classify_intent: normalize the trigger payload (cron sweep | api/webhook | slack) into a typed EmulatorRequest {action, repo, reason, requested_by, dry_run}. An LLM node (Claude via langchain-anthropic, orchestration only) maps free-text reasons to one of {status, start, restart, reset, stop} and extracts/validates the target repo against an allowlist.

classify_intent -> probe_health: ALWAYS run read-only `make emulators-status` (prefer status.sh --json) on the configured executor (ARC runner/SSH host) via a tool node. Parse JSON into a ServiceInventory and compare against the canonical port map from firebase.json. This grounds every downstream decision in live state and never mutates anything.

probe_health -> diagnose (LLM): Claude reasons over the inventory + exit codes + recent log tail to produce a structured Diagnosis {overall: healthy|degraded|down, per_service status, detected_drift (e.g. Hosting 5003 vs 5000), root_cause_hypothesis, recommended_action, destructive: bool}. Pure analysis, no side effects.
diagnose -> route: conditional edge on (request.action, diagnosis.recommended_action, diagnosis.destructive). Routes: read_only_report | safe_remediation | approval_gate.

route -> safe_remediation (non-destructive only): for action in {start, restart, stop} the tool node invokes the matching `make emulators-*` target on the executor. These are recoverable, so they run without interrupt but ARE rate-limited and logged. After running, loop back to probe_health once to confirm convergence (bounded to 1 retry).

route -> approval_gate (DESTRUCTIVE: reset, OR any action diagnose flags destructive: bool=true): call langgraph.types.interrupt() with a rich payload — what will run (`make emulators-reset`, which `rm -rf`s emulator data), whether --backup will be added, repo, requester, current data footprint, and the diagnosis. Execution PAUSES here. The Postgres checkpointer (auto-provisioned) persists the thread until a human resumes via Command(resume=...) from Slack/UI. No reset, no data deletion, ever, without an explicit human approve.

approval_gate -> apply_destructive: on resume with {approved: true}, FORCE a `--backup` first (mutate the command to `reset.sh --backup`) then run `make emulators-reset` on the executor; on {approved: false} short-circuit to report with status 'declined_by_human'. Records approver identity + decision into state.

{read_only_report, safe_remediation(converged), apply_destructive, declined} -> notify: post a concise outcome card (before/after inventory, action taken, approver, drift warnings) to Slack and/or the API response. Sends are themselves treated as the only outbound action and are templated, not free-form.
notify -> persist_memory: write a run summary to the long-term BaseStore (namespaced by repo) so the cron sweep can detect recurring flaps / chronic drift across runs and tune escalation. -> END.
tools_integrations: langgraph (StateGraph, interrupt/Command, Crons API for the scheduled sweep)
LangGraph Platform managed Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore (auto-provisioned — do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= to .compile())
langchain-anthropic ChatAnthropic (Claude) for classify_intent + diagnose — LLM orchestration/reasoning ONLY, no model training/fine-tuning/distillation

Executor transport (one of): ARC self-hosted GitHub Actions runner via workflow_dispatch+gh API, OR paramiko/asyncssh to an SSH-reachable dev host — runs the actual `make emulators-start|status|reset|stop` so nothing heavy runs in the LangGraph container
Firebase CLI / emulator scripts on the executor: status.sh --json, start_emulators.sh, reset.sh --backup, stop_emulators.sh
Slack (incoming webhook for notify + slash-command/interactive endpoint for the approval resume)
GitHub Actions webhook -> deployment /runs endpoint (pre-integration-test trigger)
Custom Auth (langgraph.json auth.path) so only the Slack signing secret / CI token / dev SSO can create runs
Optional: status.sh --json parsed against firebase.json canonical port map for drift detection
approval_gates: HARD human-in-the-loop interrupt on EVERY irreversible action. Specifically: (1) `make emulators-reset` — reset.sh runs `rm -rf` on emulator data dirs, so it ALWAYS hits approval_gate via langgraph interrupt(); on resume the command is force-rewritten to include `--backup` before deletion. (2) `--all` / env/config-touching reset variants are blocked outright in v1 (not exposed). (3) Any action the diagnose node tags destructive:true (e.g. stop during an active integration-test run) also routes to the gate. (4) Outbound Slack/notify sends use fixed templates, but a v1 toggle requires approval before posting to any channel other than the dev-internal one. Non-destructive start/restart/status run without interrupt but are rate-limited (cooldown in state + store) and fully logged. The graph CANNOT delete emulator data on a path that doesn't pass through interrupt() — verified structurally by the conditional edges. Resume is via Command(resume={approved, approver}) from Slack interactive buttons or the LangSmith Deployments UI; the Postgres checkpointer holds the paused thread indefinitely.
secrets_needed: EXECUTOR_SSH_HOST + EXECUTOR_SSH_USER + EXECUTOR_SSH_KEY (PEM) — if SSH executor
GH_RUNNER_TOKEN / GITHUB_TOKEN with workflow_dispatch scope — if ARC-runner executor
AGENT_API_AUTH_TOKEN (shared secret for the custom langgraph.json auth, used by GitHub Actions/webhook callers)
REPO_ALLOWLIST (config, not a secret — defaults to Scheduler-Systems/Scheduler)
deploy_notes: Deploy to LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS, US region api.smith.langchain.com). langgraph.json at repo root: {"dependencies":["."],"graphs":{"emulator_warden":"./agent/graph.py:graph"},"env":".env","python_version":"3.12","image_distro":"wolfi","auth":{"path":"./agent/auth.py:warden_auth"},"store":{"ttl":{"default_ttl":43200,"sweep_interval_minutes":60}},"checkpointer":{"ttl":{"strategy":"delete","default_ttl":43200}},"pip_installer":"uv"}. CRITICAL: graph.py must `graph = builder.compile()` with NO checkpointer=/store= args — the platform injects production Postgres for both; passing InMemorySaver would be ignored in-cluster. Deps in pyproject.toml: langgraph, langchain-anthropic, asyncssh (or PyGithub), slack_sdk, httpx, pydantic. Secrets injected via the Deployments UI/API, never committed (.env is for local langgraph dev only). Schedule the health sweep with the Crons API after first deploy (POST /assistants/{id}/crons, cron "*/30 9-19 * * 1-5", input {action:"status"}). The agent runs NO emulators itself — it must reach an executor (ARC runner in the cluster or an SSH dev host); confirm network egress from the managed deployment to that executor before go-live. Compliance: this is application/infra orchestration that only CALLS Claude for routing/diagnosis — it does NOT develop, train, or operate any ML model, so it is permitted under the workspace Anthropic-terms guardrail. Surface the firebase.json(5003) vs docker-compose(5000) Hosting-port drift in the first diagnose run as a known finding to fix.
impact: automatable_score 95 → realistic v1 coverage ~80% of the lifecycle toil: removes manual ad-hoc `make emulators-status` checks and silent emulator-down states that block local QA and CI integration tests. Proactive 30-min sweeps catch a downed/flapping emulator before a developer or a GitHub Actions integration-test job wastes a cycle on it; auto safe-restart fixes the common "emulator died overnight" case without a human. The reset path — the one genuinely dangerous, data-destroying operation — gets a consistent, audited, backup-forced, human-approved workflow instead of a developer running `reset.sh` by hand and losing seed data. Bonus: drift detection (port map, service inventory) and cross-run memory turn recurring instability into a tracked signal instead of tribal knowledge. Low blast radius (read-by-default, destructive-gated), which is why this is a safe P2 automation rather than a risky one.

process: Test Data & Fixtures (Dart/Flutter) — keep mockito-generated mock classes and Firebase/Firestore test helpers in sync with the code they mock. Today this is manual: a developer edits a class under test, re-runs `flutter pub run build_runner build --delete-conflicting-outputs`, eyes the regenerated `.mocks.dart`, and commits. The 62 committed `.mocks.dart` files (driven by 30 `@GenerateMocks`/`@GenerateNiceMocks`-annotated test files) silently drift when an annotated source class changes signature but nobody regenerates — tests then fail to compile or, worse, pass against stale mocks. The agent (fixture-sentinel) is a LangGraph Platform / LangGraph Cloud (managed, SaaS tier) deployment that continuously detects fixture drift in Scheduler-Systems/Scheduler, regenerates mocks + analyzes the diff with Claude, validates that the test suite still compiles/passes, then PROPOSES a fix as a draft PR. Every irreversible action (branch push, PR open, commit) is held behind a human-in-the-loop interrupt. The agent ONLY orchestrates and calls Claude for reasoning/diff-summarization — it does NOT train, fine-tune, distill, or evaluate any ML model (Anthropic Consumer Terms compliance, per workspace AGENTS.md). Emulator test credentials (shaypanuilov@gmail.com / 123456) and the GitHub token are read from env/secrets, never hardcoded — matching the workspace security rules.
trigger: Three trigger modes, all landing on the same compiled graph (assistant `fixture_sentinel` in langgraph.json -> ./agent/graph.py:graph): (1) Webhook from GitHub on push/PR to Scheduler-Systems/Scheduler touching `lib/**`, `test/**/*_test.dart`, `test/helpers/**`, `test/custom_code/actions/test_data.dart`, or `pubspec.yaml` (mockito/build_runner pins) — POSTs to the deployment's Runs API. (2) Scheduled Cron (LangGraph Platform native cron, e.g. nightly 02:00 UTC) as a safety net to catch drift introduced outside CI. (3) Manual run from LangGraph Studio / Runs API with an explicit `{repo, ref}` payload for on-demand audits. The graph is thread-scoped per (repo, ref) so checkpointer state resumes a paused human-review interrupt across days.
graph_steps: START -> ingest_trigger: normalize the webhook/cron/manual payload into State {repo, ref, base_sha, head_sha, changed_paths, run_mode}; short-circuit to END if no fixture-relevant path changed (cheap filter, no Claude/no clone).

checkout_workspace: shallow-clone or fetch Scheduler-Systems/Scheduler at head_sha into an ephemeral workdir using the env GITHUB_TOKEN; pin mockito/build_runner versions from pubspec.yaml. (read-only, no model work)

scan_drift: deterministic node — enumerate all 30 @GenerateMocks/@GenerateNiceMocks annotation sites, map each to its target source class + committed .mocks.dart, and run `dart run build_runner build --delete-conflicting-outputs` into a SCRATCH output dir; diff scratch mocks vs committed mocks. Emit a structured drift_report (per-file: clean | stale | missing | orphaned). No mutation of the repo.

classify_with_claude: call Claude (Sonnet) to summarize the drift_report into a human-readable changelog and a risk label (low/medium/high) per file — purely text reasoning over diffs; NO model training/eval. If drift_report is empty -> route to report_clean.

regenerate_fixtures: apply the validated regeneration into the working tree (commit candidate, NOT pushed); also reconcile test/helpers/*_mocks.dart and test/custom_code/actions/test_data.dart only when an annotation/source change requires it. Stage diff in State.

validate_suite: run `flutter analyze` + `flutter test` against the regenerated tree to confirm it COMPILES and PASSES; Firebase emulator creds injected from env (FIREBASE_EMULATOR_EMAIL / FIREBASE_EMULATOR_PASSWORD), never hardcoded. On failure -> route to needs_human_repair with logs.

interrupt__await_approval: HUMAN-IN-THE-LOOP. Use LangGraph `interrupt()` to pause and surface {drift changelog, regenerated diff, test results, risk label}. The run blocks (checkpointer persists) until a reviewer resumes with Command(resume={approve|reject|edit}). NOTHING is pushed before this gate.

propose_pr: ONLY on approve — create a branch `fixtures/sync-<head_sha>`, commit the regenerated fixtures with message `[fixtures/sync] regenerate mocks for <changed classes>`, push, and open a DRAFT GitHub PR linking the changelog. (irreversible; reachable only past the interrupt). On reject -> record decision, no writes.
report_clean / report_done -> END: post a status summary back (PR URL or 'no drift'); persist outcome to State for the thread.
impact: Eliminates a silent, high-frequency QA failure mode: stale committed mocks that either break CI test compilation or let suites pass against outdated mocks (false-green). With 62 mock files and 30 annotation sites in an actively-changing repo, manual regeneration is error-prone and skipped under deadline pressure. Automating detect+regenerate+validate (automatable_score 90) recovers developer time and protects test-suite trustworthiness. Bounded blast radius: the agent only ever opens a DRAFT PR behind human approval — it cannot merge, cannot touch production, and cannot deploy. Caveat: Scheduler is the ARCHIVED Flutter repo (native rebuilds — scheduler-ios/android/web/api — are where new work goes per workspace memory), so ROI is maintenance-tier, not revenue-tier; value is keeping the legacy suite green for regression coverage and any backport. Revenue-first lens: this is hygiene, not a money lever — keep it cheap (nightly cron + webhook, Sonnet not Opus) and do not expand scope into the live native repos without a separate decision.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangGraph Cloud (managed SaaS, api.smith.langchain.com) — Postgres checkpointer + store auto-provisioned (do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= to .compile())
GitHub REST/GraphQL API via `gh` or octokit (clone, branch, push, draft PR) — read for scan, write only past interrupt
GitHub webhook -> Runs API (push/PR trigger)
LangGraph native Cron (scheduled nightly audit)
Dart/Flutter toolchain in the build image: flutter, dart, `dart run build_runner build`, mockito ^5.4.4, build_runner ^2.4.8 (added via langgraph.json dockerfile_lines / base image)
Firebase Local Emulator Suite (for validate_suite test run)
Anthropic Claude API (Sonnet) for drift changelog + risk classification ONLY — no model training/eval/distillation
LangGraph Studio + Agent Inbox (to action the human-in-the-loop interrupt approvals)
Slack (optional) notification of pending-approval and PR-opened events
secrets_needed: GITHUB_TOKEN (repo-scoped PAT or GitHub App installation token for Scheduler-Systems/Scheduler — read + branch/PR write)
GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET (validate inbound webhook signatures in the custom http app)
LANGSMITH_API_KEY (deployment auth to LangGraph Platform control plane)
FIREBASE_EMULATOR_EMAIL (test credential, injected at runtime — NOT committed; documented value shaypanuilov@gmail.com belongs in the secret store)
FIREBASE_EMULATOR_PASSWORD (test credential — injected at runtime, never hardcoded)
approval_gates: Single mandatory human-in-the-loop gate at `interrupt__await_approval`, implemented with LangGraph `interrupt()` so the run suspends (state persisted by the managed Postgres checkpointer) until a reviewer resumes via `Command(resume=...)` from LangGraph Studio / Agent Inbox / Slack action. NOTHING irreversible runs before it: no branch push, no commit, no PR. The reviewer sees the drift changelog, the full regenerated diff, the `flutter analyze`/`flutter test` results, and Claude's per-file risk label, then chooses approve / reject / edit. Approve -> propose_pr (draft PR only — agent never merges and never deploys). Reject -> decision logged, zero writes. Secondary safety stop: validate_suite failures route to `needs_human_repair` (also a human touchpoint, surfaces logs) instead of proposing a PR. Hard guardrails: agent is forbidden from merging PRs, from writing to any repo other than Scheduler-Systems/Scheduler, and from any ML model train/eval/distill work (Anthropic Consumer Terms).
deploy_notes: langgraph.json at repo root: {"dependencies":["."],"graphs":{"fixture_sentinel":"./agent/graph.py:graph"},"env":".env","python_version":"3.12","image_distro":"wolfi","http":{"app":"./app.py:app"},"pip_installer":"uv","dockerfile_lines":["RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl git unzip xz-utils","RUN git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/flutter/flutter.git -b stable /opt/flutter","ENV PATH=$PATH:/opt/flutter/bin","RUN flutter precache && dart --version"]}. The compiled `graph` is a StateGraph(State).compile() with NO checkpointer/store args (managed platform injects production Postgres). State is a TypedDict {repo, ref, base_sha, head_sha, changed_paths, run_mode, drift_report, changelog, regenerated_diff, test_results, decision}. Deploy via `langgraph deploy` (CLI) or LangSmith UI -> set region (us or eu). Inject all secrets through the Deployments UI/API env, never in the committed .env. Webhook: mount custom Starlette route in app.py that HMAC-verifies GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET then POSTs to the Runs API with thread_id=`<repo>@<ref>`. Cron: register a LangGraph schedule (nightly) pointing at the fixture_sentinel assistant with run_mode='scheduled'. Build image must carry the Flutter SDK + run a one-time `flutter pub get` warm step; pin mockito ^5.4.4 / build_runner ^2.4.8 from the repo's pubspec to match committed mock generator output. Thread the run per (repo, ref) so a paused approval interrupt resumes correctly days later. Use Agent Inbox / LangGraph Studio for the human gate; optionally wire Slack for notify. Keep model = Claude Sonnet (cost) — this is hygiene-tier work on an archived repo.

process: Emulator-Based E2E Tests (Playwright) for scheduler-web — the 3 emulator-* suites (emulator-features, emulator-employer-journey, emulator-premium) that exercise the full authenticated employer journey, billing/paywall, and tier-gating against the local Firebase emulator suite (Auth :9099, Firestore :8088, Functions :5001) + the Next.js dev server with NEXT_PUBLIC_USE_FIREBASE_EMULATORS=true.
trigger: Three triggers, all into the same compiled graph (LangGraph Platform assistant): (1) Scheduled cron via LangGraph Platform Crons API — nightly + on a 4h cadence — since these suites currently test.skip in the live-deploy CI gate.yml and are otherwise only run by a human locally; (2) Webhook/HTTP from a GitHub Actions "push to main / PR labeled run-emulator-e2e" event hitting a custom http app route mounted via langgraph.json http.app; (3) Manual run from LangGraph Studio / SDK with input {ref, suites?, reason}. The agent does NOT run emulators or Playwright itself — it dispatches the heavy job to the existing scheduler-web GitHub Actions executor (workflow_dispatch on a new emulator-e2e.yml that runs `firebase emulators:exec` + dev server + `npm run test:e2e` with E2E_USE_EMULATORS=1 and E2E_BASE_URL=http://localhost:3000), then polls, parses, and triages. This honors the workspace "orchestrate local / execute on cluster" rule: LangGraph is the conductor, GitHub-hosted runners are the executor.
graph_steps: START -> normalize_input: validate {ref/sha, suites (default the 3 emulator-* specs), trigger_reason}; resolve the GitHub repo (Scheduler-Systems/scheduler-web) and default branch; load run config from BaseStore (last-known-green sha, flaky-test allowlist, recent run history).

dispatch_e2e_run (tool: github_workflow_dispatch): call Actions workflow_dispatch on emulator-e2e.yml with inputs {ref, suites, E2E_USE_EMULATORS=1, E2E_BASE_URL=http://localhost:3000, firebase project scheduler-systems-dev}. NON-destructive to product data (emulators are ephemeral) so it runs WITHOUT an interrupt, but it IS a write to CI; gate it behind the approval interrupt only when trigger=manual-with-force or when re-dispatching a 3rd+ time. Capture run_id.

poll_run (tool: github_get_run + Monitor-style loop with interrupt-safe checkpointing): poll Actions run status until completed/timeout (cap ~25 min). On each poll, persist progress to the Postgres checkpointer so a cold resume continues the same run rather than re-dispatching.
fetch_artifacts (tool: github_download_artifacts): pull the Playwright JSON/blob report + test-results/ (traces, screenshots-on-failure) for the run. Read-only.

parse_results (LLM + deterministic parser node): parse the Playwright report into structured {suite, test_title, status, duration, error_message, attachment_paths}. Deterministically classify each failure; the LLM only SUMMARIZES and proposes a root-cause hypothesis per failing test — it never edits product code or tests.

classify_failures (LLM reasoning node, Claude via langchain-anthropic): bucket failures into known-flaky (matches store allowlist e.g. RTL/locale-switch timing, RC-sandbox 404-mapping latency), infra (emulator port bind / dev-server boot / firebase project mismatch — the documented CORS/404 callable trap), or real regression (e.g. duplicate-name guard, free-tier station=1 paywall, Pro/Enterprise unlock). Compute a confidence score; cross-check the dup-name + paywall assertions against the spec text loaded into the prompt to avoid blind-pass misreads.

decide_action (conditional edge): if all-green -> record_green; if only known-flaky -> propose single retry (capped) ; if real regression or infra -> route to human_gate. Retry budget enforced from store (max 2 auto-retries, then mandatory human gate).

human_gate (interrupt() — human-in-the-loop, REQUIRED before any irreversible/outward action): present the structured triage (pass/fail matrix, root-cause hypotheses, links to traces/screenshots, proposed action) and BLOCK. Approvable actions: (a) re-dispatch the run, (b) open/append a GitHub issue with the failure bundle, (c) post a summary to the QA Slack channel, (d) comment on the triggering PR. Nothing in this set executes without explicit approve; reject/edit resumes the graph with the human's decision via Command(resume=...).

execute_approved_action (tools, only reachable post-resume): github_create_or_update_issue / github_pr_comment / slack_send_message / github_workflow_dispatch(retry). Each is idempotent (dedupe issues by a stable title key 'emulator-e2e regression: <suite>' to avoid duplicates across runs).
record_green / record_result: update BaseStore with new last-known-green sha (on all-green), append run to history, update flaky-test stats. END.
tools_integrations: GitHub Actions API — workflow_dispatch on a new .github/workflows/emulator-e2e.yml (runs firebase emulators + Next dev server + Playwright with E2E_USE_EMULATORS=1)
GitHub REST API — get workflow run status, download artifacts (Playwright report + test-results traces/screenshots), create/update issues, comment on PRs
Anthropic Claude via langchain-anthropic (orchestration/reasoning only — summarize + classify failures; NO model training/distillation)
Slack (chat.postMessage) for QA-channel triage summaries — approval-gated
LangGraph Platform Crons API (scheduled triggers, nightly + 4h)
LangGraph BaseStore (last-known-green sha, flaky-test allowlist, run history) + Postgres checkpointer (provisioned by the platform — do NOT pass custom checkpointer/store to .compile())
Custom Starlette/FastAPI http.app mounted via langgraph.json for the GitHub webhook ingress
LangSmith tracing/eval for the orchestration (built-in on the platform)
approval_gates: human_gate is a hard interrupt() that BLOCKS before every outward/irreversible action: opening or updating a GitHub issue, commenting on a PR, posting to Slack, and any re-dispatch beyond the auto-retry budget. The all-green path and the read-only path (dispatch ephemeral CI run, poll, download artifacts, parse, classify) proceed without a gate because they touch no product data and no human-visible surface — emulators are ephemeral and the Firestore/Auth state is thrown away. Resume is via Command(resume={'action':..., 'edits':...}); a reject ends with no side effects recorded. Idempotency (stable issue-title dedupe key, capped retry budget in store) prevents an approve-loop from spamming. Secrets (GITHUB_TOKEN, SLACK_BOT_TOKEN, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) are injected via the LangGraph Platform env/secrets UI — never committed in langgraph.json env or hardcoded.
secrets_needed: GITHUB_TOKEN (repo scope: actions:write for workflow_dispatch, contents:read, issues:write, pull-requests:write on Scheduler-Systems/scheduler-web)
GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET (verify HMAC on the http.app webhook ingress)
impact: Closes a real, verified automation gap: the 3 emulator-* suites are gated by onEmulators (E2E_USE_EMULATORS + localhost base URL) and therefore test.skip in the existing gate.yml e2e job (which only hits the live deploy scheduler-web-next.web.app). Today they only run when a human manually does `make emulators` + `make dev` + Playwright locally — so the full authenticated employer journey, the duplicate-name guard, the free-tier station=1 paywall, and Pro/Enterprise tier-unlock are NOT continuously protected. The orchestrator makes them run on a schedule + on main/labeled-PR, triages failures into flaky/infra/regression with trace+screenshot evidence, and surfaces regressions to a human for an approval-gated issue/Slack/PR-comment — protecting Scheduler's live RevenueCat paywall path (the revenue-bearing tier gates) from silent regression. automatable_score 90 is justified: dispatch+poll+parse+classify is fully mechanical; only the act-on-failure step needs the human gate. Note: requires authoring the missing emulator-e2e.yml workflow in scheduler-web (the executor) as a one-time prerequisite — the LangGraph agent is the conductor, not the emulator host.
deploy_notes: langgraph.json at repo root of a NEW small Python orchestrator repo (NOT inside scheduler-web): {dependencies:['.'], graphs:{'e2e_orchestrator':'./agent/graph.py:graph'}, env:'.env' (local only), python_version:'3.12', image_distro:'wolfi', http:{app:'./agent/webhook.py:app'} for the GitHub webhook ingress, store:{} (default, used as KV for green-sha + flaky allowlist + run history), checkpointer:{ttl:{strategy:'delete',default_ttl:43200}}}. graph.py builds a StateGraph(State) with the nodes above, compiles with builder.compile() and assigns module-level `graph` — do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= (the platform injects production Postgres + store). human_gate uses interrupt() so the platform persists the paused thread; resume via SDK Command(resume=...) or Studio. Deploy via `langgraph` CLI / LangSmith Deployments (Cloud SaaS tier, api.smith.langchain.com). Schedule the nightly+4h runs with the Crons API after deploy. PREREQUISITE: add .github/workflows/emulator-e2e.yml to Scheduler-Systems/scheduler-web that boots firebase emulators (project scheduler-systems-dev, Auth/Firestore/Functions) + `next dev` with NEXT_PUBLIC_USE_FIREBASE_EMULATORS=true and runs `npm run test:e2e` with E2E_USE_EMULATORS=1 E2E_BASE_URL=http://localhost:3000, uploading the Playwright JSON report + test-results/. Per workspace rules: build this in a worktree (StratusCloudLabs or a new gal-run/Scheduler-Systems orchestrator repo), tie work to a GitHub issue first, and never run/build an ML model — this is pure orchestration calling Claude.

trigger: Primary: GitHub webhook (pull_request opened/synchronize and push to main) on the ~15 gal-run sub-projects that carry vitest suites, plus a nightly cron over all suites. Secondary: manual /threads run via the LangGraph SDK/Studio with an explicit list of changed packages. The webhook handler mounts as a custom Starlette route through langgraph.json `http.app` and creates one thread per (repo, sha). The agent ORCHESTRATES test runs and triages results by calling Claude; it never trains/evaluates an ML model, and it must skip the gal-model / governance eval-worker suites entirely.
process:
Deployable LangGraph Platform (managed / LangSmith Deployments, Cloud SaaS, region us api.smith.langchain.com) agent that orchestrates the gal-run Vitest unit-test process and turns raw results into a reviewed, human-approved triage outcome.

WHAT IT DOES (v1):
1. Resolves which vitest projects are affected by a change (path -> owning package via the 15 known vitest.config.ts locations), or the full matrix on the nightly cron.
2. Dispatches the actual test execution to the cluster, NOT to the LangGraph container: it calls a single tool `dispatch_vitest_run` that triggers the existing GitHub Actions CI (workflow_dispatch on ci.yml) or a GAL Swarm burst job, per the workspace rule "orchestrate local, execute on cluster." LangGraph holds the plan and the state; the runners run `npm ci && npm run type-check && vitest run --reporter=json`.
3. Polls for completion and ingests the structured JSON reporter output (passed/failed/flaky counts, failing file:test, error message, duration) plus type-check output.
4. Uses Claude (Opus/Sonnet via langchain-anthropic) to classify each failure: real regression vs. flaky vs. infra/env (e.g. registry IPv6 black-hole, DNS roam — known fleet outage modes) vs. type-error, and to draft a concise triage summary + suggested owner.
5. Persists per-failure evidence to the long-term BaseStore (semantic index on) so repeat/flaky signatures are recognized across runs — mirrors the swarm "evidence ledger" concept the suite itself tests.
6. HUMAN-IN-THE-LOOP GATE: before ANY external write, the graph hits an `interrupt()` and surfaces the proposed action(s) for approval. Only after a human resumes with approve does it call the gated tool.
7. On approval, performs at most: post a single PR review comment with the triage table, set a non-blocking commit status/check ("vitest-qa: N failed, classified"), and optionally open/za-update ONE GitHub issue per confirmed regression. Merging is never performed by the agent.

EXPLICIT NON-GOALS: no model training/eval/distillation; does not run the governance eval-worker or gal-model suites; does not auto-merge; does not auto-rerun CI without approval; does not write to Firestore/prod.

DEPLOYMENT SHAPE: managed Postgres checkpointer + store injected by the platform — graph is compiled with NO custom checkpointer/store. `langgraph.json` (python_version 3.13, image_distro wolfi, pip_installer uv) points graphs.qa -> ./agent/graph.py:graph; store.index uses openai:text-embedding-3-small dims 1536 with ttl sweep; checkpointer ttl strategy delete; http.app -> ./agent/webhook.py:app mounts the GitHub webhook + signature verification; auth.path -> ./agent/auth.py:auth validates LangSmith API keys / a shared HMAC for the webhook. Secrets (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY/token, GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET, optional GAL_SWARM_TRIGGER_TOKEN) injected via the platform UI/API, never committed; .env only holds non-secret defaults.
graph_steps: ingest_trigger: parse webhook/cron/manual payload into State (repo, base_sha, head_sha, changed_paths, mode=pr|nightly|manual); idempotency-key on (repo, head_sha) so re-deliveries reuse the existing thread.

resolve_affected_projects: map changed_paths -> owning vitest projects using the 15 known vitest.config.ts roots; nightly mode selects all; HARD-FILTER OUT gal-model / governance eval-worker suites (ML guardrail). Emits run_plan[].

dispatch_vitest_run (TOOL, non-destructive trigger): for each project, invoke GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch on ci.yml (or GAL Swarm burst) to run `npm ci && type-check && vitest run --reporter=json` ON THE CLUSTER. Records run_id per project. (This is a CI trigger, not a prod write; it is conditionally gated — see approval_gates.)

poll_runs (Command-loop / interrupt-light): poll run status with bounded retries+backoff; tolerate the known IPv6-registry/DNS-roam flake by marking infra-suspect rather than failing the node; collect json artifacts when complete.
parse_results: normalize each project's vitest JSON + type-check into Failure[] {project, file, test, message, durationMs, status}; compute totals, new-vs-known via Store lookup.

classify_failures (LLM, langchain-anthropic, structured output): label each failure regression|flaky|infra|type-error, assign suspected owner/area, write evidence records to BaseStore (semantic index) for cross-run dedup; produce a human-readable triage table.
plan_external_actions: assemble the proposed write set — exactly one PR comment, one commit status, and 0..N issue create/update intents — with full preview text. No side effects yet.

approval_gate (interrupt): emit interrupt({proposed_actions, triage_table}) and HALT. A human resumes via SDK/Studio with {decision: approve|approve_subset|reject, edits?}. Reject -> skip_writes node. (This is the mandatory human-in-the-loop checkpoint.)

execute_approved_writes (TOOL, gated): only runs post-approval; calls post_pr_comment / set_commit_status / upsert_github_issue for the approved subset using the GitHub App token. Each call is idempotent (dedupe by marker comment / issue title).
finalize: write run summary + evidence pointers to Store, set thread status, emit optional PushNotification/Slack draft (also approval-gated, draft-only). END.
tools_integrations: GitHub (App auth): trigger CI via Actions workflow_dispatch (ci.yml), read check/run status, download vitest JSON artifacts, post_pr_comment, set_commit_status, upsert_github_issue — all reads free, all writes approval-gated
GitHub webhook receiver mounted via langgraph.json http.app (Starlette) with HMAC signature verification
Anthropic Claude via langchain-anthropic (Opus/Sonnet) for failure classification + triage drafting — orchestration/LLM-call only, no model training
GAL Swarm / Stratus ARC runners (optional alternative executor to GitHub Actions) via a thin trigger token — keeps heavy test execution on the cluster, not in the LangGraph container
LangGraph managed Postgres checkpointer (auto-injected) for durable thread state across the interrupt
LangGraph BaseStore with semantic index (openai:text-embedding-3-small, dims 1536) as the cross-run flaky/evidence ledger
Vitest JSON reporter (`vitest run --reporter=json`) as the structured data source
Optional: Slack/PushNotification for draft-only, approval-gated notifications
secrets_needed: GAL_SWARM_TRIGGER_TOKEN (optional, if executing on Stratus/Swarm instead of GitHub Actions)
approval_gates: ["MANDATORY interrupt() before any external write — the approval_gate node halts the graph and requires a human resume with approve/approve_subset/reject. Nothing is posted, no issue is opened, no status is set without it.", "dispatch_vitest_run (CI trigger) is auto-allowed in pr/nightly mode (it only kicks off a read-only test run on the cluster) BUT is approval-gated in manual mode and whenever the plan would trigger >K runs (burst guard) to avoid runner stampedes / cost.", "post_pr_comment, set_commit_status, upsert_github_issue, and any Slack/push notification are ALL behind the single approval_gate; the human can approve a subset and edit text before send. Drafts only until approved (matches 'no send without approval').", "Hard guardrail node before resolve/dispatch: refuse to run if the target suite is gal-model or the governance eval-worker (Anthropic ML-dev terms); fail closed.", "No-merge invariant: the agent has no merge tool path at all; merging stays a human action."]
impact: Converts a 1200+ file, 15-project Vitest suite from a wall of CI logs into per-PR, Claude-classified triage (regression vs flaky vs known fleet-infra flake) with a cross-run evidence ledger, while keeping every external write behind a human approval. Cuts time-to-triage on red CI and reduces noise from the known IPv6-registry/DNS-roam infra flakes. Low direct revenue (internal QA), so P2 — it protects velocity on the GAL platform rather than shipping to paying users.
deploy_notes: Repo: new gal-run/gal-vitest-qa-orchestrator (or a langgraph/ subdir in gal-swarm). Deploy via `langgraph deploy` CLI or the LangSmith Deployments UI targeting Cloud SaaS (us). langgraph.json: $schema langgra.ph/schema.json; dependencies ['.']; graphs.qa=./agent/graph.py:graph; python_version 3.13; image_distro wolfi; pip_installer uv; env .env (non-secret defaults only); store.index {embed: openai:text-embedding-3-small, dims:1536} + store.ttl sweep 60m; checkpointer.ttl {strategy: delete, default_ttl: 43200}; http.app=./agent/webhook.py:app; auth.path=./agent/auth.py:auth. CRITICAL: graph = builder.compile() with NO checkpointer=/store= args — the platform injects production Postgres checkpointer + store; passing InMemorySaver is local-only and ignored in the hosted runtime. Real secrets injected through the platform UI/API, never in .env or code. Local dev: `langgraph dev` with a .env for the same vars. Webhook URL = the deployment URL + /webhooks/github, registered as a GitHub App webhook with the shared HMAC secret. Resume the interrupt via LangGraph SDK (client.runs.create with Command(resume=...)) or Studio. Pin: do not point this agent at gal-model/eval-worker suites; the guardrail node enforces it but the project allowlist should also exclude them.

process: Playwright Browser Tests (gal-run Web) — E2E test orchestration, failure triage, and regression gating for the gal-website (Next.js) and gal-chrome-extension web surfaces. A deployable LangGraph Platform / LangGraph Cloud (managed, LangSmith Deployments) agent that runs the existing Playwright suites against a deployed/preview URL or local build, classifies each failure as flake vs. real regression (routing break, contract regression, broken browser interaction) using Claude for reasoning over traces/screenshots/console output, dedupes against known issues, and proposes follow-up actions. Every write/irreversible action (opening or commenting on a GitHub issue, re-triggering a CI workflow, posting to Slack, blocking a deploy) is suspended on a human-in-the-loop interrupt and only executes after explicit approval. Read-only test execution and analysis run autonomously. Claude is used solely for orchestration/triage reasoning — never for model training, fine-tuning, or distillation.
trigger: Primary: deploy/preview event for either repo (gal-run/gal-website deploy-website.yml workflow_dispatch run completing, or a Vercel/preview deployment_status webhook) POSTs to the deployment's /runs (or /threads/{id}/runs) webhook endpoint with {repo, target_env, base_url, commit_sha}. Secondary: scheduled nightly cron (LangGraph Platform Crons / external cron hitting the run API) against production base_url for regression sweep. Tertiary: manual/on-demand run from LangGraph Studio or the SDK with an explicit base_url + suite selector. Because gal-website CI is workflow_dispatch-only today, the webhook is wired from the deploy job's post-deploy step.
graph_steps: ingest_trigger: parse webhook/cron/manual input into RunRequest state (repo, base_url, target_env, commit_sha, suite=[website|extension|both], reason). Validate base_url is set; if absent and target!=local, route to error.

select_suite: branch on repo/suite. website -> gal-run/web/gal-website (e2e/ smoke+integration, BASE_URL-driven, skips webServer when BASE_URL set, 5 browser projects). extension -> gal-run/platform/gal-chrome-extension (test:e2e, headless=new Chrome with unpacked --load-extension from ../dist, serial single-worker). Flag config drift: gal-website playwright.config.ts calls pnpm preview/build but package.json lacks @playwright/test + test:e2e script — emit a warning artifact, do not auto-fix.

run_playwright (tool node): execute the suite read-only against base_url in a sandboxed runner. website: BASE_URL=<url> npx playwright test (CI mode: 1 worker, 2 retries, 60s timeout, trace retain-on-failure, html+list reporters). extension: pnpm build then pnpm test:e2e (1 worker). Capture JSON reporter + trace zip + failure screenshots. NEVER mutate the repo or deploy.

collect_artifacts: gather per-test results, retry counts, trace files, screenshots, console/network logs from traces; normalize into structured FailureRecords (test id, project/browser, error class, retry-passed?, category-hint: routing|contract|interaction|infra).

triage_failures (Claude reasoning): for each failure, classify flake (passed on retry / network/timeout signature) vs real regression; sub-categorize routing break, contract regression (integration/forms-and-security), broken browser interaction, extension load/permission failure, or env/infra. Produce severity + confidence + human-readable root-cause summary. Orchestration-only LLM use.

dedupe_known_issues (tool node, read-only): query GitHub issues in the owning repo (gal-run/gal-website or gal-run/gal-chrome-extension) for existing open matches by signature; mark NEW vs DUPLICATE vs flake-only.

decide_actions: assemble proposed action list — e.g. open/comment GitHub issue for each NEW real regression, post Slack summary to QA channel, optionally re-trigger deploy workflow or recommend deploy-block. Pure result for all-green / flake-only runs needs no write action.

approval_gate (interrupt): if any write/irreversible action is proposed, interrupt() with the full proposed payload (issue title/body, comment text, Slack message, re-trigger target) for human review. Resume only on explicit approve/edit/reject Command. All-green and read-only-only paths skip the gate.

execute_approved_actions (tool node): perform ONLY human-approved actions via GitHub MCP/REST (create_issue, create_comment) and Slack (slack_send_message). Re-trigger CI only if explicitly approved. Each action idempotent + logged.

report: write final RunResult to LangGraph store/checkpointer and emit summary (counts: passed/failed/flaky, categories, actions taken vs proposed, links to traces). Surface the config-drift warning. Return structured output for the webhook caller / Studio.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed runtime, Postgres checkpointer + store auto-provisioned — do NOT pass custom checkpointer/store to .compile())
Anthropic Claude API (triage/classification reasoning only; orchestration, no model training/distillation)

Playwright Test runner (@playwright/test) executing existing configs: gal-website e2e/ (chromium/firefox/webkit/mobile-chrome/mobile-safari) and gal-chrome-extension e2e (headless=new Chrome unpacked extension)
Sandboxed Node/pnpm execution environment for the runner (Chromium browsers; for extension suite needs a built ../dist and X-capable headless Chrome)
GitHub API/MCP (read issues for dedupe; create/comment issues gated; optional workflow re-dispatch gated)
Slack API/MCP (QA channel summary, gated send)
Deploy/preview webhook source: gal-website deploy-website.yml post-deploy hook and/or Vercel deployment_status
LangGraph human-in-the-loop interrupt() + Command(resume) for approval gate
LangGraph store (BaseStore) for known-flake fingerprints and run history; checkpointer for resumable interrupts
impact: Catches routing breaks, contract regressions, and broken browser interactions on gal-run web surfaces before/just-after deploy, with automatic flake-vs-regression triage so humans only review real, deduped failures. Closes the current gap where gal-website deploy is workflow_dispatch-only with no CI-wired Playwright gate. Automatable score 90: execution + triage + dedupe are fully autonomous; only the small set of write/irreversible actions (issue filing, Slack, re-trigger, deploy-block) requires human approval. Reduces manual QA toil and mean-time-to-detect on the public-facing website and the Chrome extension. Non-revenue/QA-infra work (flag per revenue-first priority), but protects the gal-run web brand surface and unblocks safe automated regression coverage.
approval_gates: Human-in-the-loop interrupt() before ANY write or irreversible action. Specifically gated: (1) opening a GitHub issue for a NEW regression, (2) commenting on an existing issue, (3) sending the Slack QA summary, (4) re-triggering the deploy/CI workflow, (5) any recommend/execute deploy-block. The interrupt surfaces the exact payload (issue title+body, comment text, Slack message, re-trigger target+ref) and resumes only on an explicit approve/edit/reject Command. Autonomous (no gate): test execution, trace/screenshot/console collection, Claude triage classification, read-only GitHub issue dedupe queries, and the final report for all-green or flake-only runs. The agent never edits repo source, never auto-fixes the flagged config drift, and never deploys on its own.
secrets_needed: GITHUB_TOKEN (repo+issues scope for gal-run/gal-website and gal-run/gal-chrome-extension; workflow scope only if re-trigger enabled)
WEBHOOK_SHARED_SECRET (HMAC to authenticate inbound deploy/preview webhook)
PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL (per-run input, not a static secret — passed as BASE_URL)
VERCEL_WEBHOOK_SECRET (only if Vercel deployment_status is the trigger source)
deploy_notes: langgraph.json: {"$schema":"https://langgra.ph/schema.json","dependencies":["."],"graphs":{"e2e_sentinel":"./agent/graph.py:graph"},"env":".env","python_version":"3.13","image_distro":"wolfi","pip_installer":"uv","store":{"index":{"embed":"openai:text-embedding-3-small","dims":1536}}}. The graph file must export a module-level compiled `graph` (builder.compile() with NO custom checkpointer=/store= — the managed platform injects Postgres checkpointer + store; passing InMemorySaver is local-only and ignored in hosting). Inject ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/GITHUB_TOKEN/SLACK_BOT_TOKEN/WEBHOOK_SHARED_SECRET via the Deployments UI/API, never commit them (.env holds only non-secret defaults). Deploy via `langgraph deploy` CLI or the LangSmith Deployments UI targeting Cloud SaaS (api.smith.langchain.com; eu. for EU). HARD CONSTRAINT (Anthropic Consumer Terms / AGENTS.md): Claude is used for orchestration + triage reasoning ONLY — no model training, fine-tuning, eval-of-a-model, or distillation; do not touch gal-model. RUNNER CAVEAT: Playwright + Chromium (and the extension's headless=new Chrome with --load-extension from a freshly built ../dist) is heavyweight; the default managed Agent Server image may need dockerfile_lines to install Playwright browser deps (npx playwright install --with-deps) and Node/pnpm, OR run the actual browser execution on an external sandboxed runner (Stratus ARC runner / dedicated CI container) that the graph invokes — keep heavy execution off the conductor per the orchestrate-local/execute-on-cluster rule. PRE-DEPLOY FIX (flag, don't auto-apply): gal-website playwright.config.ts references pnpm preview/pnpm build but package.json (next-based) has no @playwright/test devDep and no test:e2e script — add @playwright/test + a `test:e2e` script and reconcile the webServer command (next build && next start, not pnpm preview) before the website suite can run green in the managed runner. Wire the trigger by adding a post-deploy step to deploy-website.yml that POSTs {repo,base_url,target_env,commit_sha} (HMAC-signed) to the deployment run endpoint. Use LangGraph Crons for the nightly production sweep. Set forbidOnly via CI=1 in the runner env so .only-marked tests fail the run.

trigger: Three entrypoints into the same compiled graph (assistant), all stateless/event-driven so nothing runs on a dev Mac: (1) GitHub webhook -> LangGraph Platform `/runs` (PR opened/synchronized, or `workflow_dispatch`/issue-comment `/qa run`) carrying {repo, sha, branch, pr_number, changed_files}; (2) cron via LangGraph Platform scheduled run (nightly full-matrix regression); (3) manual API/Studio invocation with an explicit run-request payload. The agent NEVER runs Patrol/shell locally — it decides scope and DISPATCHES execution to GitHub Actions (`workflow_dispatch` of flutter_web_e2e.yml + Patrol mobile workflows on self-hosted ARC runners), honoring the workspace "orchestrate local, execute on cluster" rule. The agent itself only calls Claude + read/dispatch/poll tools.
process: Test Execution Orchestration Scripts (patrol_unified.sh / patrol_test.sh / patrol_web_test.sh) — unified Patrol E2E across web/Android/iOS with local|prod env management, results in test-results/ + playwright-report/. v1 agent = the decision+dispatch brain over those scripts (executed remotely via GitHub Actions/ARC runners), not a reimplementation. Scope: pick platform/target/env from the change, dispatch the matching CI workflow, poll to completion, parse artifacts (JUnit/JSON + playwright HTML), triage pass/fail/flaky, post a structured summary back to the PR. Honors automatable_score 85: deterministic plan -> dispatch -> poll -> parse is fully automatable; the only human gate is irreversible side effects (the sibling CI scripts that ACTUALLY deploy to Firebase Hosting / distribute to Play Store, and any retry that would re-trigger such a workflow) — those pause on a LangGraph interrupt() until approved.
graph_steps: ingest_event (entrypoint node): normalize the 3 trigger shapes into a TestRunRequest state {repo, sha, branch, pr_number, changed_files[], requested_platforms?, requested_env, reason}; reject malformed payloads to END with a validation error.

plan_scope (Claude reasoning node): given changed_files + trigger, produce a TestPlan = list of {platform in web|android|ios, target path under integration_test/patrol/, env in local|prod, headless}. Heuristics: web-only diff -> web; *.dart/integration_test changes -> matrix; nightly cron -> full matrix prod headless; map changed dirs to the narrowest Patrol target dir to keep runs cheap. Output is structured (Pydantic) and logged to state.

classify_risk (router node): inspect the resolved workflow/ref each plan item would dispatch. If the target workflow or env implies an IRREVERSIBLE side effect (deploy-to-Hosting / Play Store distribution CI scripts, or env=prod write paths, or a re-dispatch retry of a deploying workflow) -> route to approval_gate; else route straight to dispatch_runs.

approval_gate (human-in-the-loop): call interrupt() with the exact {workflow, ref, inputs, blast_radius} payload; BLOCK until a human resumes with approve/deny via the Platform /threads/{id}/runs resume API. Deny -> skip that item and record reason. No deploy/distribution ever fires without this.

dispatch_runs (tool node): for each approved/safe plan item, call github_dispatch_workflow (workflow_dispatch on flutter_web_e2e.yml / patrol mobile workflow, ref=sha, inputs={platform,target,env,headless}) on the self-hosted ARC runners. Capture run_id per dispatch into state. Idempotency-keyed on (sha, platform, target) to avoid duplicate dispatches on webhook re-delivery.

poll_status (tool + loop node): poll github_get_run_status for each dispatched run with bounded backoff; loop edge back to itself until all runs are completed/timed-out (cap total wait, mark stragglers as timeout). LangGraph checkpointer persists progress so the run survives container restarts.
collect_artifacts (tool node): download JUnit XML / Patrol JSON + playwright-report HTML and the test-results/ summary from each finished run via github_download_artifact; load into state.

triage_results (Claude reasoning node): parse artifacts into a verdict per item {passed, failed_tests[], flaky_suspects[], duration, runner}. Apply flaky heuristic (timeout exit 124/137/143 seen in patrol_unified retry, or pass-on-rerun) -> label flaky not fail. Decide whether a single bounded auto-retry of a FLAKY (non-deploying) item is warranted -> if yes and item is side-effect-free, loop once to dispatch_runs; if the retry would touch a deploying workflow, route through approval_gate first.

report (tool node): assemble a structured Markdown summary (per-platform table, failures with artifact links, flaky list, total time) and post it via github_post_pr_comment / set a commit status check. PR comment is a reversible communication, but is still drafted-then-posted; if configured strict, it too can be gated. Update final state verdict.
END: emit final TestRunVerdict to the run output for the caller / Studio / downstream automation.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments) managed runtime — Postgres checkpointer + store auto-provisioned; do NOT pass checkpointer/store to .compile()
Anthropic Claude (claude-opus / sonnet via langchain-anthropic) for plan_scope + triage_results — ORCHESTRATION/reasoning only, no model training/distillation
GitHub REST/Actions API as LangGraph @tools: dispatch_workflow (workflow_dispatch), get_workflow_run, list_run_artifacts, download_artifact, create/update_pr_comment, create_commit_status
GitHub webhooks -> LangGraph Platform /runs (via the http custom-app mount in langgraph.json, signature-verified)

Target executors (invoked remotely, not by the agent): GitHub Actions workflows flutter_web_e2e.yml + Patrol mobile workflows on self-hosted ARC runners, which run patrol_unified.sh/patrol_web_test.sh
Artifact parsers: JUnit XML + Patrol/Playwright JSON/HTML report readers (pure-python deps in pyproject.toml)
LangGraph human-in-the-loop interrupt()/resume for the approval gate
LangSmith tracing for observability of every run/thread
approval_gates: HARD GATE via interrupt() before any IRREVERSIBLE action: (1) dispatching any CI workflow that actually deploys to Firebase Hosting or distributes to Play Store/Firebase App Distribution (the sibling test_*_ci_workflow.sh / deploy paths flagged in CI_WORKFLOW_TESTS.md as 'ACTUAL DEPLOYMENT'); (2) any plan item with env=prod that writes to the live priority-118da project; (3) any auto-retry that would re-trigger a deploying workflow. The interrupt payload shows {workflow, git ref, inputs, blast_radius}; execution blocks until a human resumes with approve/deny. Read-only/ephemeral E2E runs against emulators or non-deploying test workflows do NOT require approval (keeps the 85 automatable_score). PR comments are reversible and posted automatically by default, but a `strict_comment` flag can route them through the gate too. Deny is always safe: the item is skipped with a recorded reason, never silently forced.
