deploy_notes: Managed LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments) deploy. langgraph.json: dependencies ["."], graphs {"qa_orchestrator":"./agent/graph.py:graph"}, python_version "3.12", image_distro "wolfi", pip_installer "uv", http {"app":"./agent/app.py:app"} to mount the signature-verified GitHub-webhook receiver, and a checkpointer.ttl block (the platform auto-provisions Postgres checkpointer+store — never pass your own to .compile(), it is ignored/overridden in the hosted runtime). graph.py builds a StateGraph(TestRunState) with the nodes above, conditional edges for classify_risk->approval_gate|dispatch_runs and the poll/retry loops, and exports the compiled `graph` variable referenced by langgraph.json. Secrets injected via the Platform UI/API env (NOT committed; .env only for local `langgraph dev`). Webhook -> create thread + background run so the GitHub delivery returns fast; poll_status relies on the checkpointer to resume across the (possibly long) E2E wait. Nightly full-matrix via a Platform Cron scheduled run. Execution itself stays on the cluster: the agent only dispatches GitHub Actions on self-hosted ARC runners + reads artifacts, never spawning local Patrol/shell — consistent with the 'orchestrate local, execute on cluster' workspace rule. Use one stub repo (Scheduler-Systems/Scheduler) as the single configured target for v1; harden auth via a custom Auth instance on the Platform before exposing more repos. v1 cut line: single repo, web+android+ios plan heuristics, one bounded flaky auto-retry, PR-comment + commit-status reporting; defer cost-aware sharding, multi-repo fan-out, and Slack notification to v2.
impact: Removes the manual "decide-what-to-test -> kick off -> babysit -> read the report -> summarize on the PR" loop for every Scheduler PR and nightly regression. Right-sizes test scope automatically (narrowest Patrol target for the diff) so CI minutes on the self-hosted runners aren't burned on full-matrix runs for a web-only change. Flaky-vs-real-failure triage with one bounded auto-retry cuts false-red PRs. Crucially it keeps heavy execution OFF the dev Mac (dispatch-only) and puts a hard human gate in front of the few scripts that actually deploy/distribute — so automation speed never risks an unreviewed prod push. Net: faster, cheaper, lower-risk E2E signal on a revenue app (Scheduler) with humans only in the loop where it's irreversible.

area: QA / Security (Firestore security-rules validation for scheduler-api)
process: Firestore Rules Testing — security-rules validation for the Scheduler Firestore backend. v1 agent runs the rules test suite against the Firebase emulator, detects coverage gaps (critically: the current suite is a placeholder), classifies pass/fail/degraded, and drafts a remediation proposal. It is an orchestration agent only: it reasons with Claude and calls deterministic local tools (emulator runner, git, gh) — it does NOT train, fine-tune, distill, or evaluate any ML model. Every action that writes to a remote (PR comment, opening a GitHub issue, pushing a fix branch) is paused at a human-in-the-loop interrupt and only executes after explicit approval resumed via the LangGraph Platform threads API. Ground truth verified in repo: test/firestore-rules.test.js is currently all assert.ok(true) with mocked Firestore (no real @firebase/rules-unit-testing execution, dependency not even installed), runner is `node --test` not Vitest, and there is no firestore.rules file in scheduler-api (canonical rules live in Scheduler-Systems/Scheduler/firebase/firestore.rules). The agent surfaces exactly these gaps rather than reporting a false green.
trigger: Primary: GitHub webhook on pull_request and push-to-main for Scheduler-Systems/scheduler-api where the diff touches test/firestore-rules.test.js, firestore.rules, storage.rules, or firebase.json — mounted as a custom http.app route (POST /webhooks/github) on the Agent Server that creates/streams a run. Secondary: scheduled cron run (daily 07:00 Asia/Jerusalem) as a drift/regression sweep against main. Manual: direct run create via SDK/UI for ad-hoc validation. Webhook signature verified against GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET before a run is created.
graph_steps: ingest_signal: parse webhook/cron/manual input into RunState (repo, ref, pr_number, changed_files, trigger_kind). Validate it actually concerns rules/rules-tests; short-circuit to END if not.
checkout_repo: tool node — shallow-clone/checkout Scheduler-Systems/scheduler-api at the target ref into an ephemeral workdir using GH_TOKEN (read scope). Read-only; no writes.

locate_rules_and_tests: tool node — find firestore.rules/storage.rules and test/firestore-rules.test.js. Records the verified gap: rules file ABSENT in scheduler-api + suite is placeholder. Populates state.findings.preconditions.

run_rules_suite: tool node — run `firebase emulators:exec --only firestore,auth --project scheduler-api-test 'npm test'` (or `node --test test/firestore-rules.test.js`) in the sandbox. Capture exit code, per-test results, stdout/stderr, duration. Deterministic; no LLM.

analyze_coverage (LLM/Claude): reason over the run output + test source to detect blind-pass tests (assert.ok(true)), missing @firebase/rules-unit-testing wiring, uncovered collections vs the canonical rules, and false-green risk. Produce a structured coverage/gap report. Reasoning only — no model training.

classify (LLM/Claude): label outcome as PASS_REAL | PASS_BUT_PLACEHOLDER | FAIL_RULE_REGRESSION | INFRA_ERROR | NOT_APPLICABLE and set severity (P0/P1/P2). Decide which (if any) publish actions are warranted.
decide_actions: routing node — if NOT_APPLICABLE or clean PASS_REAL with no PR, go to END; otherwise route to draft_remediation.

draft_remediation (LLM/Claude): generate the proposed artifact(s): a PR review comment summarizing findings, OR a GitHub issue body for the placeholder/coverage gap, OR a patch (real rules-unit-testing test scaffold + firestore.rules relocation note). Nothing is sent yet.

human_review (interrupt): call interrupt() to pause. Surface the classification + drafted artifact + exact target (PR #, repo, branch) and ask approve/edit/reject. Run stays paused in Postgres checkpointer until resumed via threads API.
apply_decision: route on the resumed human command — approved -> publish_action; edited -> re-draft then re-interrupt; rejected -> record_outcome with no remote write.

publish_action: tool node — perform ONLY the approved write via gh/GitHub API (post PR comment, open issue, or push a fix branch + open PR). Idempotency key on (pr_number, run_id) to avoid duplicates. Uses a separate write-scoped token.
record_outcome: persist summary to BaseStore (history/dedup) and to LangSmith run metadata; emit final RunState. END.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS) — Agent Server runtime, auto-provisioned Postgres checkpointer + store
Anthropic Claude (Opus/Sonnet via langchain-anthropic) for analyze_coverage, classify, draft_remediation — orchestration/reasoning only, no model training/distillation
Firebase CLI + Firestore/Auth emulator (firebase emulators:exec) — deterministic rules test execution
node --test runner (the repo's actual test runner; NOT Vitest)
@firebase/rules-unit-testing — the library the real suite must use (agent flags its absence)
GitHub via gh CLI / REST API (octokit) — read clone, PR comments, issues, fix-branch PRs (write actions gated)
Custom Starlette/FastAPI app mounted via langgraph.json http.app for the GitHub webhook + HMAC signature verification
LangGraph human-in-the-loop interrupt() + threads API (resume with Command) for approval gating
Cron scheduler (LangGraph Platform cron / assistants schedule) for the daily drift sweep
approval_gates: Single mandatory human-in-the-loop interrupt at node human_review before ANY remote/irreversible action. Paused via LangGraph interrupt(); the run state persists in the managed Postgres checkpointer and resumes only on an explicit threads API resume carrying a typed Command (approve | edit | reject). Gated actions: posting a PR review comment, opening a GitHub issue, pushing a fix branch / opening a PR. Read-only actions (clone at ref, run emulator suite, analyze) require no gate. Reject path writes nothing remote and records the decision. Edit path loops back through draft_remediation and re-interrupts (no silent re-send). Idempotency keys on publish prevent duplicate comments/issues across retries. Aligns with workspace rule 'no send/publish without explicit human approval'.
secrets_needed: GH_TOKEN_WRITE (PR-comment/issue/push scope, separate least-privilege token, used only by publish_action)
FIREBASE_TOKEN_OR_ADC (CI service-account creds for emulator/CLI; emulator runs offline against scheduler-api-test project)
impact: Closes a real false-green security risk: the rules suite that gates every scheduler-api PR currently passes while asserting nothing, on a repo that has no firestore.rules file — exactly the surface where the live IDOR/Firestore breach lived. The agent turns 'rules tests are green' into a trustworthy signal by detecting placeholder/blind-pass tests, surfacing uncovered collections, and proposing the relocation of the canonical rules + a real @firebase/rules-unit-testing harness. Automatable_score 90 is justified for the run+analyze+draft loop; the publish step stays human-gated by policy. Protects the live, paying Scheduler product's access-control posture without any ML-model work, keeping it within Anthropic Consumer Terms.
deploy_notes: Managed LangGraph Platform deploy. langgraph.json at repo root: dependencies ['.'], graphs {'rules_qa': './agent/graph.py:graph'}, python_version '3.12', image_distro 'wolfi', http {'app':'./agent/app.py:app'} for the GitHub webhook, store with default TTL for run-history dedup. CRITICAL: compile the StateGraph WITHOUT passing a checkpointer/store — the managed platform injects production Postgres automatically; passing InMemorySaver is local-only and is ignored/overridden in the hosted runtime. interrupt() requires the durable checkpointer, so this works only on a persisted deployment, not a bare in-memory local run. The Firebase emulator must be available in the build image (add via dockerfile_lines: install firebase-tools + a JRE for the Firestore emulator, plus npm ci so node --test can run) — verify the hosted sandbox permits launching the emulator; if not, fall back to a self-hosted/standalone Agent Server runner or run the emulator as a sidecar. Secrets injected via the platform UI/API, never committed (env in langgraph.json points only to non-secret defaults). Configure the GitHub webhook URL to the deployment's /webhooks/github with the shared secret. Schedule the daily sweep via a platform cron on the rules_qa assistant. NOTE for implementers: the process metadata says 'Vitest' but the repo uses node --test and the data_sources path uses a capital 'scheduler-Systems' that does not exist — canonical path is /Users/scheduler-systems/Documents/scheduler-systems-ltd/Scheduler-Systems/scheduler-api/test/firestore-rules.test.js.

process: Test Result Aggregation & Reporting — collect heterogeneous test outputs (Playwright JSON from scheduler-web, JUnit XML from scheduler-android, Go test results from scheduler-api, XCResult/JUnit from scheduler-ios), normalize them into one schema, detect regressions/flaky/newly-failing tests against stored history, produce a single CI/CD-visibility report, and (approval-gated) fan it out to Slack and a GitHub regression issue.
trigger: GitHub Actions webhook on `workflow_run` completion of the `gate` (and `release`) workflows across all scheduler-* repos. The CI job uploads its raw results as a build artifact and POSTs a JSON event (repo, sha, branch, run_id, run_url, artifact urls, suite kind) to the LangGraph Platform deployment's `/runs` endpoint for the `aggregate` assistant (HMAC-signed; verified in auth.py). A nightly LangGraph Cron (`schedule`) run also sweeps the last 24h of runs to compute a rollup. Manual re-run supported via the LangGraph SDK with the same input schema.
graph_steps: ingest_event (entry node): validate the signed webhook payload against InputState schema; resolve which repos/run_ids/artifacts are in scope; load prior history for these suites from the BaseStore (semantic index keyed by repo+test-id). No mutations.

fetch_artifacts (tool node): use a GitHub-token-authenticated tool (token from env) to download the named CI artifacts — Playwright test-results/.last-run.json + per-test dirs, Android build/test-results/**/TEST-*.xml, Go gotestsum JSON, iOS junit. Read-only API calls. Conditional edge: if artifact missing/expired, route to degrade_node which records a partial-data flag instead of failing the run.

normalize_results: deterministic parsers (NOT the LLM) convert each format into a unified TestResult schema {repo, suite, test_id, name, status(pass/fail/skip/error), duration_ms, failure_message, sha, branch, run_url}. Playwright .last-run.json failedTests + JUnit testsuite/testcase attrs + Go/iOS mapped here. Counts (total/passed/failed/skipped/flaky) computed.

detect_regressions: diff current run against BaseStore history per test_id to classify newly-failing, fixed, still-failing, and flaky (pass/fail oscillation over last N runs). LLM (Claude, orchestration only) is used here ONLY to summarize/triage and draft human-readable root-cause hypotheses from failure messages — never to score/train a model.

build_report: assemble the unified Markdown + JSON CI/CD-visibility report (per-repo rollup, regression list, flaky list, trend vs last green). Persist normalized run to the Postgres checkpointer/state and upsert per-test history into the BaseStore.

decide_actions (conditional router): if no new regressions and no flaky escalations -> go straight to write_summary (no human needed for the read-only GitHub Step Summary / Check annotation, which is idempotent). If new regressions OR flaky escalation crossing threshold -> route to approval_gate.

approval_gate (HITL): call interrupt() to surface the drafted Slack message AND the drafted GitHub regression-issue body to a human reviewer (via LangGraph Platform inbox / Agent Inbox). The run is durably paused on the Postgres checkpointer until a human resumes with approve / edit / reject. NOTHING is sent before this returns approve.

execute_actions (tool node, runs ONLY on approve): post the approved message to Slack (slack send) and create/update the GitHub regression issue (gh API). Each send is wrapped so a reject/edit short-circuits it. Writes an audit record of who approved and what was sent into state.
write_summary (terminal): emit the GitHub Actions Step Summary text + Check annotation payload (returned to the CI caller in the run output) and the final aggregated report object. END.
impact: Gives a single cross-repo CI/CD test-health view that doesn't exist today (currently results are siloed per repo, Android XML isn't even published, web artifacts are upload-on-failure-only with 7-day retention, and there's no flaky-test or regression-trend detection). Reduces time engineers spend manually opening separate artifact downloads to understand a red build, and surfaces flaky tests that currently erode trust in the gate. Revenue-relevant indirectly: faster, more trustworthy gates on the live Scheduler apps (paying users) mean fewer bad releases slip through and less engineer time lost to triage. Not a direct money-maker, hence P2 — protective/efficiency value, not new revenue.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments / managed Cloud SaaS) — Agent Server runtime
GitHub Actions (workflow_run webhook trigger + artifact download + Checks/Step-Summary output)
GitHub REST API (gh) — read artifacts, create/update regression issue (approval-gated, irreversible)
Slack — post aggregated report / regression alert (approval-gated, irreversible)
Playwright JSON reporter (scheduler-web test-results/.last-run.json + per-test dirs)
JUnit XML (scheduler-android app/build/test-results/testDebugUnitTest/TEST-*.xml)
Go test / gotestsum JSON (scheduler-api)
iOS junit/xcresult (scheduler-ios)
Anthropic Claude API via langchain (orchestration: triage + summarize failures only — NOT model training/eval/distillation)
Postgres checkpointer (auto-provisioned by platform) for durable run state + HITL pause/resume
BaseStore with semantic index (openai:text-embedding-3-small) for per-test flaky/regression history
secrets_needed: GITHUB_TOKEN (repo-scoped PAT or GitHub App token: artifacts:read, issues:write, checks:write)
GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET (HMAC verification of inbound workflow_run events in auth.py)
OPENAI_API_KEY (embeddings for the store semantic index; or swap to a self-hosted embedder)
approval_gates: ["Human-in-the-loop interrupt() at approval_gate before ANY irreversible/outbound action — drafted Slack message and drafted GitHub regression-issue body are surfaced to the LangGraph Agent Inbox; the run is durably paused on the Postgres checkpointer until a human resumes with approve / edit / reject.", "execute_actions runs ONLY on an explicit approve resume value; reject or empty short-circuits and the run ends having sent nothing.", "Read-only outputs (GitHub Step Summary text + Check annotation returned to the CI caller, and persisting history to the store) are idempotent and do NOT require approval — only the Slack post and GitHub issue create/update are gated.", "Webhook authn gate: auth.py verifies the GitHub HMAC signature on every inbound event before a run is created, so untrusted callers cannot trigger the agent."]
deploy_notes: Managed LangGraph Platform deploy. langgraph.json: {dependencies:['.'], graphs:{aggregate:'./qa_aggregator/graph.py:graph'}, env:'.env', python_version:'3.12', image_distro:'wolfi', auth:{path:'./auth.py:gh_webhook_auth'}, store:{index:{embed:'openai:text-embedding-3-small',dims:1536}, ttl:{default_ttl:43200}}, checkpointer:{ttl:{strategy:'delete',default_ttl:43200}}, http:{app:'./app.py:app'}}. CRITICAL: compile the StateGraph with NO custom checkpointer=/store= — the platform injects production Postgres + store automatically; passing InMemorySaver is local-only and is ignored/overridden in the hosted runtime. Wire each scheduler-* gate.yml to (1) always upload test-results as an artifact (currently web is upload-on-failure-only and android publishes nothing — fix both so the agent has data on green runs too, needed for flaky/trend history) and (2) add a final job that POSTs the signed workflow_run payload to the deployment /runs endpoint for the aggregate assistant. Use LangGraph Cron for the nightly rollup. Secrets injected via the Platform UI/API, never committed (.env is for non-secret config only). HITL resume handled via the Agent Inbox UI or SDK client.threads.update_state. Anthropic Consumer Terms guardrail: this agent only orchestrates LLM calls for triage/summarization — it must never be extended to train, fine-tune, evaluate, score, or distill any ML model (e.g. gal-model); keep it strictly application/orchestration code.

process: Gate/Code Quality Checks (PR & Commit Validation) — automated quality gates (typecheck, eslint/prettier/yamllint/shellcheck, unit tests, build, optional CodeQL/Zenux security gate) on every PR and push to main across ~24 active repos. The LangGraph agent does NOT re-run or replace the GitHub Actions gate (the CI runners remain the source of truth for actually executing typecheck/lint/test/build). Instead it is a deployed-on-LangGraph-Platform triage + remediation + standardization orchestrator that sits on top of the existing gate.yml fleet: it ingests gate-failure / gate-drift signals, uses Claude purely for reasoning (root-cause diagnosis, fix synthesis, gate-config normalization — NO model training/distillation), and produces human-approval-gated outputs (a PR comment with a triage summary, a remediation branch+PR, a re-run request, or a standardized gate.yml proposal). All write/irreversible actions pause on a LangGraph human-in-the-loop interrupt and only execute after explicit approval.
trigger: Primary: GitHub webhook into the deployment's custom HTTP route (langgraph.json `http.app`) on `check_suite`/`workflow_run` completed:failure for the `gate` / `Gate` workflow, and on `pull_request` opened/synchronize. Secondary: a scheduled LangGraph cron (drift sweep) that periodically enumerates the ~24 repos' gate.yml files to detect divergence from the standard pattern. Manual: thread invocation from a /gate-triage slash command or direct API run with {repo, pr_number, run_id}. Webhook signature (X-Hub-Signature-256) verified in the HTTP app using GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET before a run is created.
graph_steps: ingest_event (HTTP app verifies HMAC webhook sig, normalizes payload to {repo, owner, pr_number, run_id, workflow_name, head_sha, trigger_kind}; START edge; node does NOT call an LLM)
classify_signal (Claude reasoning node: route to one of failure_triage | drift_sweep | pr_precheck based on trigger_kind; conditional edge fan-out)

fetch_gate_context (read-only GitHub tools: download failed job logs via Actions API, fetch the repo's gate.yml, fetch changed files in the PR diff; load per-repo gate baseline from BaseStore long-term memory; idempotency check against checkpointer so a re-delivered webhook is a no-op)

diagnose_root_cause (Claude reasoning: parse logs, attribute failure to a category — typecheck | eslint/prettier | yamllint/shellcheck | unit-test | build | codeql/zenux-security-gate | infra/runner-policy | flaky; produce structured Diagnosis with confidence + minimal proposed remediation; cite log lines)

draft_remediation (Claude reasoning: synthesize the concrete artifact — a triage comment, OR a code/gate.yml patch as a unified diff on a new branch name, OR a re-run recommendation for flaky; NOTHING is written to GitHub yet)

human_approval_gate (interrupt(): surface Diagnosis + proposed artifact + blast-radius to the reviewer; resume payload carries approve | edit | reject + optional reviewer edits; loops back to draft_remediation on edit)

execute_approved_action (ONLY runs post-approval: conditional branch -> post_pr_comment | open_remediation_pr | request_workflow_rerun | open_standardization_pr; each is a separate guarded node; never auto-merges)
record_outcome (write decision + artifact links to BaseStore for cross-repo learning and drift baseline update; emit run summary; END)
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS, Agent Server; Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore auto-provisioned — do NOT pass custom checkpointer/store to .compile())
Custom Starlette/FastAPI app mounted via langgraph.json http.app for GitHub webhook ingestion + HMAC verification

GitHub REST/GraphQL via a custom LangChain tool wrapping a GitHub App installation token (Actions: list/download run logs; Pulls: diff, create branch+PR, comment; Checks/Statuses: read; re-run workflow). NO admin/merge scope.
Anthropic Claude (claude-opus / sonnet) as the reasoning LLM ONLY — orchestration/diagnosis/codegen, never training/fine-tuning/distillation
Reads the existing gate.yml fleet as the standardization reference (scheduler-web/api Node pattern; legal/stratus runner-policy; Zenux external security-gate API)
Optional read-only Zenux security-gate API (https://security.gal.run/api/ecosystem/evaluate-gate) for security-classification context, fail-open
LangGraph cron for the scheduled drift sweep
langgraph.json auth.path custom Auth for run authz; LangSmith tracing for observability
approval_gates: Single mandatory human-in-the-loop interrupt() at human_approval_gate BEFORE any GitHub write. The agent reads freely (logs, diffs, gate.yml, security context) with zero gating, but EVERY irreversible/outbound action is blocked behind the interrupt: posting a PR comment, opening a remediation PR, opening a gate.yml standardization PR, and requesting a workflow re-run all require explicit approve. Reviewer can approve / edit (loops back to re-draft with their edits) / reject (records and ends). The agent NEVER merges PRs and NEVER pushes to protected branches — it only opens PRs for the existing human review + the real gate.yml CI to validate. Re-runs are treated as irreversible (consume CI minutes) and are gated too. Resume is via the Platform's interrupt-resume API carrying the reviewer decision.
secrets_needed: LANGGRAPH_AUTH_SECRET (custom Auth signing/verify for run authz)
deploy_notes: Repo layout: gate_sentinel/graph.py exporting module-level `graph = builder.compile()` (NO checkpointer=/store= args — Platform injects Postgres persistence in the hosted runtime); gate_sentinel/webhook_app.py exporting Starlette `app` for langgraph.json http.app (HMAC-verifies X-Hub-Signature-256, creates a run per event); gate_sentinel/tools/github.py (GitHub App-token tooling, read scopes + pulls:write + actions:write, NEVER contents:merge); gate_sentinel/auth.py for langgraph.json auth.path. langgraph.json: python_version 3.12, image_distro wolfi, dependencies ["."], graphs {"gate_sentinel":"./gate_sentinel/graph.py:graph"}, http.app "./gate_sentinel/webhook_app.py:app", store.index for cross-repo gate-pattern semantic recall, env via Platform-injected secrets (NEVER commit .env). Deploy via `langgraph deploy` (Cloud SaaS, region us or eu); inject all secrets through the Deployments UI/API not the repo. GitHub App: subscribe to check_suite, workflow_run, pull_request events; point webhook URL at the deployment's http app path. Add a LangGraph cron for the drift sweep. State schema must use add_messages reducer + a typed Diagnosis/Artifact field; mark interrupt-resuming so re-delivered webhooks are idempotent against the checkpointer (key on head_sha+run_id). Compliance: orchestration-only, no ML model development (honors workspace AGENTS.md Anthropic-terms guardrail); secrets strictly via env. Pilot scope: start on scheduler-web + scheduler-api (canonical Node gate), then expand to legal/stratus runner-policy and Zenux security-gate variants once approval throughput is proven.
impact: Tackles the stated pain point — divergent repo-specific gate implementations and inconsistent linting coverage across ~24 active repos (255 active workflow files, ~429 cited) — without touching the load-bearing CI execution. Cuts mean-time-to-green on PRs by auto-diagnosing gate failures and proposing the exact fix, and drives gate.yml standardization via human-approved normalization PRs. High automatable_score (9) fits the orchestration pattern. Risk is contained: read-heavy, every write is approval-gated, no auto-merge, no protected-branch pushes, fail-open security context, and the real gate.yml CI still validates every PR the agent opens. Revenue-relevant indirectly (faster, safer merges into scheduler-web/api/ios which carry live paying-user revenue) but it is a developer-productivity/QA bet, not a direct monetization lever — hence P1 not P0.

trigger: Primary: GitHub webhook -> LangGraph Platform webhook endpoint on `workflow_run.completed` and `check_suite.completed` for the gate/test workflows (gate.yml, test.yml, deploy-stratus.yml) across the 5 covered repos. Also `pull_request` (opened/synchronize) so the agent can attach a pending merge-gate verdict. Secondary: scheduled cron (LangGraph Platform Crons) hourly sweep to catch runs the webhook missed and to age out stale gate verdicts. Manual: `workflow_dispatch`-style invocation via the Assistants API for a named PR/run when a human wants an on-demand re-evaluation. The webhook handler is mounted via the `http.app` custom Starlette app in langgraph.json (validates the GitHub HMAC signature against GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET before creating a run), so unsigned/forged events never start a graph.
process: Unit & Integration Testing with Artifact Reporting (QA). A deployed LangGraph Platform (managed/Cloud SaaS) agent that orchestrates the EXISTING CI test pipelines rather than running tests itself. It listens for completed CI runs across gal-run/extensions/gal-code, Scheduler-Systems/scheduler-web, Scheduler-Systems/scheduler-api, StratusCloudLabs/stratus, and EscalateLabs/marketing; pulls the junit XML / test artifacts those workflows already produce (mikepenz/action-junit-report output, actions/upload-artifact bundles); normalizes them into one schema; classifies each failure as real-failure / known-flake / infra-error / skipped-on-branch using an LLM (Claude) reasoning node plus deterministic heuristics; and emits a single merge-gate verdict with a human-readable summary. It directly targets the documented pain points: inconsistent artifact retention (it records a durable normalized copy + retention metadata in the Store), test steps skipped on certain branches (it flags coverage gaps as a gate concern), E2E flakiness (it correlates the heartbeat-monitored Playwright suites and proposes selective re-runs instead of blind full re-runs), and untuned timeouts (it tracks per-suite duration vs the configured 20-45min timeouts and surfaces tuning recommendations). It is orchestration calling Claude/LLMs only -- NO model training, fine-tuning, distillation, or eval-data generation for any ML model (explicitly NOT gal-model). Every irreversible/outward action (posting or updating a GitHub Check Run, commenting on a PR, re-running a failed job, overriding or setting a required merge-blocking status) is paused at a LangGraph `interrupt()` human-in-the-loop gate and only executes after explicit human approval delivered via the platform's resume/Command API. Read-only ingestion and analysis run autonomously.
graph_steps: ingest_event (entry node): parse the GitHub webhook/cron payload from graph input, identify repo + PR/commit + workflow_run id; load prior gate state for this PR from the BaseStore (durable retention-aware copy); short-circuit to END if event is a duplicate or for an unsupported workflow.

fetch_artifacts (tool node, read-only): via a thin GitHub REST tool, list workflow_run artifacts and check-run annotations, download the junit XML bundles (packages/*/.artifacts/unit/junit.xml, e2e/junit-*.xml, scheduler test-results/) and the mikepenz junit summary; store raw blobs + retention-days metadata. Bounded by per-call timeouts; no writes to GitHub.

normalize_reports: deterministic parser that flattens heterogeneous junit XML (Bun/Turbo, jest/vitest, pytest, go test, Playwright) into one schema {repo, suite, testcase, status, duration_ms, failure_message, branch, skipped_reason}; compute per-suite duration vs configured timeout (20-45min) and detect branch-skipped suites.

classify_failures (LLM node, Claude via langchain_anthropic): for each failing/erroring testcase, classify real-failure vs known-flake (cross-referenced against a flake-history record in the Store and the gal-code E2E heartbeat signal) vs infra/timeout error vs branch-skip-gap. Pure reasoning over already-collected data -- no model training. Deterministic guardrails wrap the LLM output (schema-validated, defaults to 'real-failure' on low confidence so we never silently pass a true failure).

decide_gate: combine classifications into a merge-gate verdict (PASS / BLOCK / NEEDS_HUMAN) and assemble the GitHub-check summary + tuning recommendations (retention bumps, timeout adjustments, missing-coverage flags). Pure function, no side effects.

route_action (conditional edge): if verdict == PASS and the run was already green -> go to persist_and_end (no outward action needed). If any outward action is required (post/update Check Run, PR comment, re-run a flaky job, set required status) -> go to human_review.

human_review (interrupt / HITL): call interrupt() with the full proposed action payload (exact check-run body, target PR, any job re-run request, verdict). Execution pauses until a human resumes with Command(resume=...) carrying approve / edit / reject. Approve-with-edits replaces the payload; reject records the decision and skips the action.

execute_approved_action (tool node, gated): only reachable post-approval. Performs the single approved outward GitHub mutation (create/update Check Run, comment, or re-run job) via the GitHub tool with idempotency keys; never auto-merges. On API error, routes back to human_review with the error rather than retrying blindly.

persist_and_end: write the normalized verdict, flake-history update, retention metadata, and approval record to the BaseStore (durable, survives the 7-30 day CI artifact retention window); update PR gate state; emit final state and END.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS tier) as the runtime; Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore auto-provisioned by the platform (do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= to .compile())

GitHub REST/GraphQL API via a thin custom tool (Checks API, Actions artifacts/runs API, Pull Requests, Statuses) using a fine-grained PAT or GitHub App installation token -- read scopes used autonomously, write scopes only behind the HITL gate
langchain_anthropic ChatAnthropic (Claude) for the classify_failures reasoning node -- orchestration/inference only, no training/distillation
junit XML parsers for Bun/Turbo, jest/vitest, pytest, go test, and Playwright outputs (the formats the existing workflows already emit, incl. mikepenz/action-junit-report)
Custom Starlette/FastAPI app mounted via langgraph.json `http.app` to receive + HMAC-verify GitHub webhooks and forward to the runs API
LangGraph Platform Crons for the hourly missed-run sweep
LangSmith tracing/observability (built-in) for run inspection; optional Slack notification of a pending approval is itself treated as outward and stays read-only/notification-only
Assistants API / SDK for manual on-demand re-evaluation of a named PR or run
approval_gates: ["human_review interrupt() before posting or updating ANY GitHub Check Run (the merge-gate verdict that blocks merges)", "human_review interrupt() before commenting on a PR or setting/overriding a required merge-blocking status", "human_review interrupt() before re-running any failed/flaky CI job (re-runs consume runner minutes and are irreversible side effects)", "Approve / edit / reject is delivered via the platform resume Command API; reject is recorded and the action is skipped; low-confidence LLM classification defaults to BLOCK/NEEDS_HUMAN so a real failure is never auto-passed; the agent NEVER auto-merges a PR under any path"]
deploy_notes: langgraph.json at repo root: { dependencies:["."], graphs:{"qa_gate":"./qa_agent/graph.py:graph"}, env:".env", python_version:"3.12", image_distro:"wolfi", http:{app:"./qa_agent/webhook_app.py:app"}, store:{ttl:{default_ttl:43200,sweep_interval_minutes:60}}, checkpointer:{ttl:{strategy:"delete",default_ttl:43200}} }. Graph built with StateGraph(...).compile() exported as module-level `graph`; do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= (platform injects Postgres). Use interrupt() + Command(resume=...) for all HITL gates (not deprecated NodeInterrupt). Deploy via `langgraph deploy` CLI or LangSmith UI from GitHub; inject all secrets through the platform UI/API, never in committed .env. Set TTLs so the normalized verdict + flake history outlive the 7-30 day CI artifact retention (default_ttl 43200 min = 30 days). Configure the GitHub App webhook to point at the deployment's /webhooks/github route and subscribe to workflow_run, check_suite, pull_request. v1 scope: report+verdict+gated actions only -- explicitly excludes auto-merge and any ML/model work (Anthropic terms; not gal-model). Cost/latency control: classify_failures runs only on failing testcases, and prompt-cache the static repo/suite context per Claude API best practices.
impact: Automatable score 8/10. Eliminates manual triage of green/red CI across 5 repos and standardizes a single merge-gate verdict + summary, directly attacking the four named pain points: it records a durable normalized copy with retention metadata (fixes inconsistent 7-30 day artifact retention), flags branch-skipped test steps as gate gaps (fixes silently-skipped tests), correlates Playwright heartbeat signals to distinguish flake from real failure and proposes selective re-runs (fixes E2E flakiness churn), and tracks per-suite duration vs the 20-45min configured timeouts to recommend tuning. Humans stay in control of every merge-affecting and runner-consuming action via the interrupt gate, so the agent reduces toil without taking risky autonomous actions. Protects merge quality on revenue repos (scheduler-web/scheduler-api) while keeping orchestration strictly LLM-inference-only per Anthropic terms.

process: Pre-commit Hooks & Local Code Quality Enforcement
trigger: Three entry points, all hitting the same compiled LangGraph graph on LangGraph Platform (managed Cloud SaaS): (1) Cron via the platform's scheduled-runs / Crons API — weekly drift sweep across the StratusCloudLabs org (default Mon 09:00). (2) Webhook — GitHub `push`/`pull_request` on the canonical repo (StratusCloudLabs/stratus) touching `.pre-commit-config.yaml` or `.yamllint.yml`, so a hook-version bump (e.g. pre-commit-hooks v6.0.0 -> v6.1.0) immediately re-fans-out to detect newly-drifted repos. (3) Manual run via SDK/Studio with `{repos:[...], dry_run:bool}` to target a single repo. Threads are keyed by `thread_id = "precommit-sweep:<org>:<run-date>"` so a sweep is resumable and an interrupted approval can be picked back up.
graph_steps: load_canonical: read the source-of-truth `.pre-commit-config.yaml` + `.yamllint.yml` from StratusCloudLabs/stratus@main via GitHub contents API (read-only tool). Parse the 6 repo blocks / 9 hook ids (trailing-whitespace, end-of-file-fixer, check-yaml, check-json, check-merge-conflict, detect-private-key, yamllint, shellcheck, terraform_fmt, prettier) and their pinned `rev`s + the `^archive/` excludes. Store the canonical spec hash in the BaseStore (semantic index) keyed by content hash so repeat sweeps are diff-only.

enumerate_targets: list non-archived StratusCloudLabs repos via GitHub API (stratus-api, stratus-cli, stratus-dashboard, stratus-website, stratus-runtime, argocdgitops, fluxgitops, gitops, runner-images, ...), honoring an allow/deny list from config. Fan out one branch of work per repo (Send API / map step).

fetch_and_diff (per repo): read each target's existing `.pre-commit-config.yaml` (if any) read-only. Classify: MISSING (no config), DRIFTED (present but stale revs / missing hook ids / missing archive excludes), or COMPLIANT. Compute a structured diff + remediation patch. NO writes here.

classify_and_score: a Claude LLM node (orchestration only — NO model training/eval/distillation) reviews each diff to (a) confirm the patch is safe given repo language mix (e.g. skip terraform_fmt suggestion for a pure-Dart repo, keep secret-scanning + whitespace everywhere), (b) draft a human-readable PR title/body + a one-line risk note, (c) rank repos by risk (secret-detection gap = highest). Output is a typed proposal object; nothing is sent.

human_approval_gate (interrupt): `interrupt()` surfaces the batch of proposed PRs (repo, diff, rationale) to a reviewer in Studio / via the SDK. Reviewer can approve-all, approve-subset, edit a patch, or reject. Resume payload carries the approved set. This is the ONLY path to any write. Idempotency key per repo prevents duplicate PRs on resume.

open_prs (gated): for each APPROVED repo, create a branch `chore/precommit-enforce`, commit the remediated `.pre-commit-config.yaml` (+ `.yamllint.yml` when needed), and open a draft PR via GitHub API. Reversible by design (draft PR, never auto-merge, never pushes to default branch). Records PR URL + idempotency key in the store.

report: write a sweep summary (compliant / drifted / PR-opened counts, links) back as graph output and post an optional Slack digest to #qa behind the same approval flag. Persist run record to BaseStore for the next diff-only sweep.
tools_integrations: GitHub REST/GraphQL API (read: contents, repo list; write: branch create, commit, draft PR) via PyGithub or httpx wrapped as LangChain tools
Anthropic Claude (claude-opus / sonnet) via langchain-anthropic — orchestration/reasoning ONLY, no model training/eval/distillation
pre-commit + yamllint + shellcheck schema knowledge encoded as a deterministic parser/differ (no shelling out to git in the managed runtime)
LangGraph Platform managed Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore (auto-provisioned; do NOT pass custom checkpointer/store to .compile())
LangGraph Crons API (scheduled weekly sweep)
GitHub webhook -> LangGraph Platform webhooks/http app (langgraph.json `http.app`)
Slack Web API (optional digest, approval-gated) via env-injected bot token
approval_gates: Single mandatory human-in-the-loop `interrupt()` at `human_approval_gate` before ANY write. All read/diff/classify steps run autonomously; nothing irreversible happens pre-approval. Approval is per-repo (approve-all / approve-subset / edit-patch / reject). Resume payload carries only the approved set; per-repo idempotency keys stored in the checkpointer prevent duplicate branches/PRs if the thread resumes after a partial run. Hard guardrails baked into the open_prs node: never push to a default branch, never auto-merge, PRs opened as DRAFT only, never commit secrets, and a second cheap LLM/regex check rejects any patch whose diff touches files outside `.pre-commit-config.yaml`/`.yamllint.yml`. Slack digest is behind the same gate.
secrets_needed: GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY (or GITHUB_TOKEN with repo+PR scope on StratusCloudLabs)
deploy_notes: Deploy to LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments, managed Cloud SaaS). langgraph.json: {"dependencies":["."],"graphs":{"precommit_enforcer":"./src/agent/graph.py:graph"},"env":".env","python_version":"3.12","image_distro":"wolfi","pip_installer":"uv","http":{"app":"./src/agent/app.py:app"},"store":{"index":{"embed":"openai:text-embedding-3-small","dims":1536}},"checkpointer":{"ttl":{"strategy":"delete","default_ttl":43200}}}. graph.py builds StateGraph(State) with State carrying {canonical_spec, targets:list, diffs:dict, proposals:list, approved:set, prs:list}; compile with `graph = builder.compile()` and DO NOT pass checkpointer=/store= (platform injects Postgres). The interrupt-based approval requires the platform's persistence — that's the whole reason for managed Cloud over a one-shot script. app.py mounts a FastAPI route for the GitHub webhook (verify X-Hub-Signature-256 with GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET, then kick a run via the SDK). Secrets injected via the Deployments UI/API, never committed (.env is path-only on managed). Crons API registers the weekly sweep against assistant `precommit_enforcer`. Use a GitHub App (least-privilege: contents:write, pull_requests:write on StratusCloudLabs only) over a PAT. Anthropic Consumer Terms compliance: this agent only orchestrates Claude for reasoning/drafting — it does NOT train, fine-tune, eval, or distill any model, and never operates gal-model; safe to run in this workspace. v1 scope = StratusCloudLabs org only; expand to other orgs after the gate workflow is proven. Test locally with `langgraph dev` (in-memory saver) before `langgraph deploy`.
impact: Closes the stated pain point (limited org adoption, inconsistent enforcement, devs skipping hooks). Highest-value lever is the org-wide secret-scanning gap: detect-private-key currently only protects stratus, so any repo missing the config can leak a private key on commit/push — this agent finds and remediates that across the fleet. Automatable_score 7 fits a human-gated propose-PR model: the agent does 100% of the tedious detection/diff/patch-drafting and a human approves the writes. Net: drift caught within hours of a canonical bump (webhook) instead of never, weekly safety-net sweep, zero risk of unreviewed writes (draft PRs, no auto-merge). Not directly revenue-impacting (internal QA hygiene), which is why P1 not P0 — but it materially reduces secret-leak and config-rot risk across StratusCloudLabs infra repos.

trigger: Three trigger modes converge on the same compiled graph: (1) Scheduled audit — LangGraph Platform Cron (CronCreate) fires a weekly assistant run with input {"mode":"audit","scope":"all_repos"} to sweep every repo in the org allow-list and report CODEOWNERS coverage/drift; (2) PR webhook — langgraph.json `webhooks` + custom http.app route receives a GitHub `pull_request` (opened/synchronize) event, runs {"mode":"pr_check","repo":...,"pr_number":...} to verify the PR's touched paths resolve to a valid, existing owning team and that required reviewers are correctly requested; (3) On-demand remediation — a human invokes {"mode":"remediate","repo":...} from the run UI/API to generate a corrected CODEOWNERS file for a repo flagged in an audit. Trigger 1 is the primary recurring driver; 2 and 3 are event/human-initiated.
process: Code Ownership and Required Reviews (CODEOWNERS) governance — a read-and-recommend QA agent that audits GitHub CODEOWNERS coverage and correctness across the enterprise workspace, validates that referenced owner teams actually exist and have repo write access, flags drift (missing files, personal-handle bootstrap stubs like @karabil, paths with no owner, orphaned team references), and on a PR validates that changed sensitive paths map to a real owning team with reviews correctly requested. All writes (committing a generated CODEOWNERS, opening a PR, changing branch protection, posting a PR comment) are human-approval-gated via a LangGraph interrupt; the agent never merges or pushes autonomously. Confirmed scope from the live data: only 10 of 94 git checkouts carry a CODEOWNERS file; the two canonical files (Scheduler-Systems/legal, StratusCloudLabs/stratus) are well-formed and team-scoped, while agent-sandbox and pilotlight use a single personal owner @karabil as an unreplaced bootstrap stub (pilotlight even carries a TODO to swap in a platform team). The agent codifies the legal/stratus patterns as the gold standard and measures every other repo against them.
graph_steps: ingest — entry node normalizes the trigger payload into shared state (mode, repo scope, optional PR number). Reducer-backed `messages` (add_messages) plus typed fields: repos[], findings[], proposals[], approvals{}. Branches on `mode` to audit / pr_check / remediate via conditional edges from START.

fetch_repo_inventory — (audit mode) list candidate repos from the org allow-list via GitHub REST/GraphQL (gh API through a thin tool wrapper, token from env GITHUB_TOKEN). For each repo, fetch the CODEOWNERS blob from .github/, root, and docs/ in priority order. Records present/absent and raw content into state. No LLM call — deterministic tool node.

parse_and_resolve_owners — parse every CODEOWNERS rule into (glob, owners[]). For each owner handle, classify as org/team (@org/team) vs personal (@user) vs email. Resolve each @org/team against the GitHub Teams API and confirm the team has at least pull/write on that repo. Personal-handle owners on protected paths are flagged as bootstrap-stub drift. Deterministic; results appended to findings[].

llm_gap_analysis — Claude (orchestration call only, no model training) reasons over the parsed rules + repo file tree to judge coverage quality against the gold-standard patterns mined from Scheduler-Systems/legal and StratusCloudLabs/stratus: are sensitive paths (/agreements/**, /security/**, .github/workflows/**, signed agreements, scan/security workflows) owned by an appropriately segregated team? Produces structured findings with severity (critical/warn/info) and a human-readable rationale. Prompt-cached system prompt holding the gold-standard rubric.

pr_required_reviews_check — (pr_check mode) for the PR's changed files, compute which CODEOWNERS rules match, whether the resolved owning team exists, and whether GitHub has actually requested that team as a reviewer. Surfaces mismatches (e.g., a sensitive path with no owner, or an owner team that no longer exists). Read-only against the GitHub API.

synthesize_proposals — Claude drafts concrete remediation artifacts: a corrected/new CODEOWNERS file body for repos missing one or carrying personal stubs (reusing the org's existing team handles), a per-repo coverage report, and a suggested team-mapping table. Marks each proposal with the side-effect it would cause (commit, open PR, post comment, branch-protection change). Nothing is written yet.

human_approval_gate — interrupt() (LangGraph human-in-the-loop) that pauses the run and presents the proposed write actions (file diff, target repo/branch, PR body, comment text). Execution resumes only on an explicit Command(resume=...) carrying per-action approve/reject decisions. This is the hard gate in front of every irreversible/external action — required by the constraints.

execute_approved_actions — for ONLY the approved items, perform the side effect via gated tools: open a PR adding/fixing CODEOWNERS (never direct push to default branch, never auto-merge), post a PR review comment, or emit a branch-protection recommendation. Each tool re-checks the approval map before acting; rejected items are skipped and logged.

report_and_persist — write the audit summary and the action ledger (what was proposed, approved, rejected, executed) to the long-term BaseStore (semantic index on findings for cross-run trend queries) and emit the final structured report on `messages`. Edge to END. Checkpointer (platform Postgres) preserves the interrupted state across the approval pause.
tools_integrations: GitHub REST/GraphQL API via a thin custom tool wrapper (repo inventory, CODEOWNERS blob fetch, Teams API, team repo-permission check, PR files + requested reviewers, branch protection read) — token from env, read-by-default
GitHub write tools (open PR, post PR review comment) — invoked ONLY after the human_approval_gate; never push to default branch, never auto-merge
Anthropic Claude via langchain-anthropic (orchestration/reasoning only — gap analysis + proposal drafting; NO model training/fine-tuning/distillation)
LangGraph Platform managed Postgres checkpointer (auto-injected; do not pass checkpointer= to .compile())
LangGraph BaseStore with semantic index (openai:text-embedding-3-small per langgraph.json store.index) for cross-run finding history and team-mapping cache
LangGraph Platform Cron (CronCreate) for the weekly scheduled audit
langgraph.json webhooks + custom http.app (Starlette/FastAPI) route to receive GitHub pull_request webhook events
interrupt()/Command human-in-the-loop primitive for the approval gate
secrets_needed: GITHUB_TOKEN — fine-grained PAT or GitHub App installation token with read access to repo contents + members/teams across the org allow-list, and (for gated write actions) pull-request:write on target repos; injected via Platform secrets UI, never committed
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY — for Claude orchestration calls, Platform secret
OPENAI_API_KEY — for the store semantic-index embeddings (text-embedding-3-small); omit if the store index is disabled
GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET — HMAC secret to verify inbound GitHub pull_request webhook signatures on the custom http.app route
LANGSMITH_API_KEY — Platform/LangSmith control-plane auth for deploy + tracing (handled by the deploy environment)
approval_gates: Single mandatory human-in-the-loop gate (the human_approval_gate node) sits in front of EVERY external/irreversible action and uses LangGraph interrupt(); the run suspends (checkpointed) until a human resumes with Command(resume={per-action approve/reject}). Gated actions: opening a PR that adds/fixes a CODEOWNERS file, posting a PR review comment, and any branch-protection recommendation. Hard prohibitions baked into the execute node: never push directly to a default branch, never auto-merge a PR, never delete or rewrite an existing well-formed CODEOWNERS without approval. Audit/pr_check/remediate-draft phases are fully read-only and run without a gate; only the write phase is gated. Rejected items are skipped and logged, never retried silently.
deploy_notes: Deploy to LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments / managed Cloud SaaS) via `langgraph deploy` from repo root. langgraph.json: dependencies ["."], graphs {"codeowners":"./app/graph.py:graph"}, python_version "3.12", image_distro "wolfi", pip_installer "uv", store.index {embed:"openai:text-embedding-3-small",dims:1536}, checkpointer.ttl for finding retention, http.app pointing at the FastAPI app that hosts the GitHub webhook route, and a webhooks entry for the PR trigger. CRITICAL: compile the StateGraph with NO checkpointer=/store= args (the platform injects production Postgres + store; passing InMemorySaver only suits local `langgraph dev`). Export the compiled graph as a module-level `graph` (or an async factory if per-request GitHub-App token minting is needed). After deploy, register the weekly audit Cron against the deployment and set all five secrets in the Platform secrets UI. Two trigger surfaces beyond Cron: the http.app webhook (PR checks) and run-API/UI invocation (on-demand remediation). v1 scope cap: read + recommend + gated-PR only — start with the org allow-list seeded to the five named repos plus the 84 CODEOWNERS-less checkouts surfaced by the audit, expand after the gold-standard rubric is validated against Scheduler-Systems/legal and StratusCloudLabs/stratus. Compliance: this agent only orchestrates Claude for reasoning/drafting and touches GitHub metadata/config files — it does NOT develop, train, or operate any ML model, so it stays within the workspace AGENTS.md Anthropic-terms guardrail. automatable_score 6/10 reflects that detection/reporting automates cleanly but team-mapping decisions and final CODEOWNERS content genuinely need the human gate.
impact: Directly attacks all four stated pain points with measurable, evidence-backed targets. Coverage: today only 10 of 94 git checkouts (about 11%) carry a CODEOWNERS file — the weekly audit turns that invisible gap into a tracked metric and a queue of gated PRs to raise it. Correctness/drift: catches the personal-handle bootstrap stubs already live in the data (agent-sandbox and pilotlight both pin ownership to @karabil; pilotlight even carries an unactioned TODO to swap in a platform team) and any owner team that has been renamed/deleted, which silently breaks GitHub's review enforcement. Team-mapping overhead: maintains a resolved, cached map of the 7+ team handles in use across 4 orgs and flags broken references before they reach a PR. PR gate integrity: on each PR it confirms sensitive paths (signed agreements, /security/**, scan/security workflows, .github/workflows/**) actually route to a real, write-enabled owning team and that the review was requested — closing the accountability gap the process exists to enforce. Net effect: cross-functional review gates become uniformly applied and self-auditing instead of best-effort and manually maintained, while the human approval gate keeps every change reviewable and reversible.

process: Static Code Analysis & Security Scanning — triage and route the outputs of CodeQL (Python, security-extended) and Dependabot for StratusCloudLabs/stratus and Scheduler-Systems/Scheduler. The agent does NOT run scans (CodeQL/Dependabot/pre-commit stay as-is in GitHub Actions); it is the human-in-the-loop triage layer that solves the real pain points: SARIF artifacts (30-day retention, upload:never because GHAS is off) are never surfaced for review, and the 10-open-PR Dependabot cap silently bottlenecks merges. The agent pulls each CodeQL run's SARIF artifact, parses findings, dedupes against prior triage state (LangGraph store), severity-ranks them, and produces a digest. In parallel it lists open Dependabot PRs per ecosystem (npm, github-actions, terraform, pip), flags how close each repo is to the 10-PR limit, classifies each PR (security-fix vs version-bump, major/minor/patch, CI status), and recommends a merge/close/hold action. Every irreversible action (posting a digest comment/issue, applying a label, approving or merging a Dependabot PR, opening a tracking issue) is paused at a human-in-the-loop interrupt with a rendered preview; nothing is sent until a human approves. Uses Claude only to summarize/classify/draft — never to train or distill a model.
trigger: Three triggers, all landing on the same graph: (1) Scheduled cron — weekly on Monday ~09:00 PST, after Dependabot's 08:00 PST Monday run and the Sunday 03:00 UTC CodeQL scan have completed, so the digest covers the freshest scan+PR state. (2) Webhook — GitHub Actions `workflow_run` completed event for "CodeQL Analysis" (fires on push/PR to main on Python paths) hits the deployment's custom HTTP route, kicking a per-run triage. (3) Manual/API — on-demand invocation (e.g., before a release gate) via the LangGraph SDK/REST run endpoint with a repo+since-window payload.
graph_steps: ingest: normalize the trigger (cron | workflow_run webhook | manual run) into a job spec — target repos (stratus, Scheduler), time window, and whether to cover CodeQL, Dependabot, or both.

fetch_codeql_artifacts: for each in-window CodeQL run, call gh API to list workflow-run artifacts, download the codeql-sarif-python artifact (30-day retention), unzip, and load the SARIF JSON. Tolerate missing/expired artifacts (record a gap, do not fail).
parse_findings: extract rule id, severity (security-severity), message, file:line, and CWE from each SARIF result; normalize into a finding record keyed by a stable fingerprint (rule + location).
dedupe_findings: diff against prior triage state in the LangGraph store; mark each finding new / recurring / resolved so the digest only surfaces what changed.

fetch_dependabot_prs: list open PRs labeled `dependencies` per repo, bucket by ecosystem (npm, github-actions, terraform, pip), capture CI/check status, and compute open-PR count vs the 10-PR cap (flag ecosystems at or near the limit).

classify_deps: use Claude to label each Dependabot PR — security-advisory fix vs routine bump, semver bump size, mergeability — and recommend merge / hold / close, prioritizing security fixes and surfacing the bottleneck risk.

rank_and_synthesize: severity-rank CodeQL findings, merge with the Dependabot picture, and have Claude draft a single Markdown security digest plus a concrete proposed action list (comment text, labels, PRs to approve/merge, tracking issue body).

approval_gate (interrupt): render the full digest + every proposed irreversible action and pause via LangGraph `interrupt()`. Human resumes with approve / edit / reject per action. NO write happens before this returns.

execute_approved: for human-approved items only, perform the writes — open/update the digest tracking issue, post PR comments, apply labels, approve/merge selected Dependabot PRs via gh API. Each is idempotent and re-checks CI before merge.

persist_and_notify: write the new triage state (fingerprints, decisions, last-run cursor) to the store for next-run dedupe, then post the approved digest link to the notify channel (Slack/email) and finish the run.
impact: Directly attacks the three documented pain points without needing GHAS: (1) makes the otherwise-buried SARIF artifacts reviewable as a ranked weekly digest before the 30-day retention deletes them — recovering the review loop that GHAS/Security-tab would have provided; (2) turns the silent 10-open-PR Dependabot cap into an explicit bottleneck alert plus an approval-gated merge lane so security fixes don't starve behind routine bumps; (3) gives one cross-repo (stratus + Scheduler) security view for release gating. Saves the manual SARIF-download + per-PR-triage toil each Monday while keeping every merge/post human-approved. Does not increase risk: read-mostly, all writes gated. automatable_score 7 fits a triage-and-recommend agent rather than full autonomy.
tools_integrations: GitHub REST/Actions API via gh (list workflow runs, download SARIF artifacts, list/label/comment/approve/merge PRs, open issues) — fine-grained PAT or GitHub App scoped to StratusCloudLabs/stratus + Scheduler-Systems/Scheduler
Anthropic Claude (classification, severity reasoning, digest drafting) — orchestration only, no model training/distillation
LangGraph Platform managed Postgres checkpointer (HIL interrupt/resume across the approval gate)
LangGraph BaseStore (cross-run triage state: finding fingerprints, prior decisions, per-repo last-run cursor)
Custom HTTP route (langgraph.json http.app) to receive the GitHub workflow_run webhook with signature verification
LangGraph cron schedule (Monday 09:00 PST run)
Slack or email for the approved-digest notification (send is itself approval-gated)
SARIF JSON parser (sarif-tools / pydantic models) for CodeQL output
approval_gates: Single mandatory human-in-the-loop interrupt (LangGraph `interrupt()` before the execute_approved node) covers ALL irreversible actions, each independently approvable/editable/rejectable: (1) opening or updating the digest tracking issue, (2) posting any PR or commit comment, (3) applying/removing labels, (4) approving a Dependabot PR review, (5) merging any Dependabot PR (re-checks CI green at execution time), (6) sending the Slack/email digest notification. Read-only steps (artifact fetch, SARIF parse, PR listing, classification, drafting) run without a gate. Default on reject/timeout = do nothing and persist the draft for the next run. Webhook is HMAC-signature-verified before any processing.
deploy_notes: Deploy to LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments, managed Cloud SaaS). langgraph.json: graphs {"sec_scan_triage": "./agent/graph.py:graph"}, python_version "3.12", image_distro "wolfi", env injected via the platform UI/API (never commit secrets), store.index for finding-text semantic search, checkpointer left to the platform-provisioned Postgres, http.app pointing at ./agent/webhook.py:app for the GitHub workflow_run route. Compile the StateGraph WITHOUT passing checkpointer=/store= (the platform injects production Postgres + store; passing your own is local-only and ignored in the hosted runtime). Build with `langgraph build` / deploy via CLI or GitHub-connected deploy; set the Monday 09:00 PST cron on the deployed assistant; register the GitHub webhook to the deployment's HTTP route URL. v1 scope = stratus (Python CodeQL) + Scheduler + all dependabot.yml ecosystems; explicitly out of scope: running scans, any model training/eval/distillation work (prohibited under workspace AGENTS.md / Anthropic terms — including anything touching gal-model). Token must be least-privilege; if auto-merge is undesired for v1, ship with merge disabled and only label+comment+issue actions enabled behind the gate.

process: Deploy Gates & Release Validation — orchestrate, observe, and adjudicate the multi-repo tag-triggered release-gate pipelines (StratusCloudLabs/stratus deploy-stratus.yml + the deploy-{arc-config,gal-admin,gal-api,governance-serverless,agent-model}.yml family, Scheduler-Systems scheduler-api/scheduler-web release.yml, gal-run/extensions/gal-code release-test.yml). On a release tag (v*) the agent: discovers which gate workflows fire, watches their jobs (validate-release-tag action, script-syntax checks `bash -n`, the unit gates test_stratus_release_workflows + test_runner_router_callers, and the 25-min @smoke E2E on https://stratus.scheduler-systems.com), classifies each gate pass/fail/flaky/timeout, diagnoses failures with log evidence, checks concurrency/cancel-in-progress health, and produces a single go/no-go release-readiness verdict with a remediation plan. It is an orchestrator over CI — it calls Claude to reason/summarize and the GitHub/CI APIs to read state. It NEVER trains/evals/distills any model (gal-model and deploy-agent-model.yml are read-only telemetry to this agent — it only reports their gate status, never runs them). All state-changing actions (re-run a workflow, approve a deploy/environment gate, post a PR/commit status or Slack verdict, delete merged branches or stale artifacts in the post-merge maintenance job) are surfaced as human-in-the-loop interrupts and only executed after explicit approval.
trigger: Primary: GitHub webhook on release tag push matching `v*` (and `release: published` for scheduler-api), routed to the agent's `/webhooks/github` endpoint (langgraph.json `http.app`). Secondary: `workflow_run` completed events for the gate workflows to drive incremental gate-status updates. Tertiary: scheduled cron sweep (every 15 min) to catch the 25-min E2E smoke timeout window and detect stuck/queued-behind-concurrency deploys (cancel-in-progress:false means a hung run blocks the next). Manual: `workflow_dispatch`-style invocation via the LangGraph SDK/Studio for an on-demand readiness check on a given tag+repo.
graph_steps: ingest_trigger: parse the tag/repo/sha from the webhook or cron payload into typed State (release_tag, repo, sha, head_run_ids); validate it is a v* tag and a known release repo, else END.

discover_gates: query GitHub Actions API for the workflow runs triggered by this tag across the repo's release workflows (deploy-stratus / deploy-{arc-config,gal-admin,gal-api,governance-serverless} / release.yml / release-test.yml); build the expected-gate checklist (validate-release-tag, script bash -n syntax, unit gates test_stratus_release_workflows + test_runner_router_callers, @smoke E2E). Cache the gate manifest in the long-term BaseStore keyed by repo.

poll_gate_status: fan-out (Send API) one watcher branch per gate workflow run; each polls job/step conclusions and pulls logs for non-success steps. Bounded with per-gate deadline (E2E watcher deadline = 25min + 5min grace to match timeout-minutes:25).

classify_gates: reducer node merges watcher results and labels each gate PASS / FAIL / FLAKY / TIMEOUT / SKIPPED / QUEUED-BLOCKED (the last for concurrency cancel-in-progress:false head-of-line blocking).

diagnose_failures (conditional, only if any FAIL/TIMEOUT/FLAKY): Claude reads the captured log slices + the gate manifest and produces a root-cause hypothesis and concrete remediation per failing gate (e.g. distinguish a real smoke regression from a 25-min E2E timeout/Playwright flake; flag missing automated validation in repos that lack it).
concurrency_health_check: inspect the deploy-stratus / critical concurrency groups for hung or stacked runs that would block subsequent releases; surface as a finding.
compose_verdict: Claude synthesizes a single GO / NO-GO / GO-WITH-CAVEATS release-readiness verdict with the per-gate table, evidence links, and an ordered remediation/action list.

plan_actions: deterministically derive the proposed state-changing actions from the verdict (e.g. re-run flaky @smoke gate, request approval on the environment/deploy gate, post commit status + Slack message, queue post-merge branch/artifact cleanup) — produce them as proposals only, nothing executed yet.

approval_gate (interrupt): HITL pause — emit the verdict + proposed action list and `interrupt()`; wait for human resume with approved/rejected/edited action set. Resuming with no approvals ends in report-only mode.

execute_approved_actions: for each approved action call the corresponding tool (rerun_workflow, approve_deployment_gate, post_commit_status, post_slack_verdict, cleanup_merged_branches, prune_stale_artifacts), each idempotent and logged; re-interrupt if a tool reports an unexpected precondition.
persist_and_report: write the final verdict, gate manifest, action audit trail, and timing metrics to the BaseStore (keyed by tag) and to LangSmith traces; END.
tools_integrations: GitHub REST/GraphQL API (Actions: list workflow runs, jobs, steps, download logs; rerun-workflow; create commit status / check-run; delete merged branches; list/delete artifacts) via a fine-grained PAT/GitHub App token
GitHub webhooks (push tag v*, release published, workflow_run completed) -> custom Starlette/FastAPI app mounted via langgraph.json http.app, HMAC-verified
LangGraph Platform managed Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore (gate manifests, verdict history, audit trail) — provisioned by platform, do NOT pass own checkpointer/store to compile()
Anthropic Claude (claude-opus / claude-sonnet) for failure root-cause analysis and verdict synthesis — orchestration/reasoning only, no model training/eval/distillation
Slack (chat.postMessage) for the go/no-go verdict + approval prompt — approval-gated send
LangGraph SDK Cron / scheduled run for the 15-min stuck-deploy + E2E-timeout sweep
LangSmith tracing/observability for every run
Optional read-only: Playwright @smoke artifact/trace fetch from the stratus-shell/tests run to enrich E2E diagnosis
secrets_needed: GITHUB_APP_ID / GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY (or GITHUB_TOKEN fine-grained PAT) with actions:read+write, checks:write, statuses:write, contents:write (branch cleanup), and repo metadata read across the listed repos
GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET (HMAC verification for the inbound webhook app)
LANGSMITH_API_KEY (deploy + tracing; auto-injected on managed platform)
LANGGRAPH_AUTH_SECRET (custom Auth instance protecting the public webhook/API surface)
STRATUS_E2E_JWT_SECRET (read-only, only if the agent re-triggers/reads the @smoke E2E gate)
approval_gates: ["HITL interrupt() before ANY state-changing action — nothing irreversible runs without explicit human resume.", "Re-running / re-triggering any gate workflow (e.g. retry a flaky @smoke E2E) — approval required.", "Approving an environment / deployment-protection gate to allow promotion to production — approval required (the agent never auto-promotes a deploy).", "Posting a commit status / check-run, PR comment, or Slack go/no-go verdict — approval required (covered by global no-send-without-approval rule).", "Post-merge maintenance: deleting merged branches or pruning stale CI artifacts — approval required (covered by no-delete-without-approval rule), executed only on the explicitly approved set, idempotently.", "Default / fallback path is REPORT-ONLY: if approval is rejected or times out, the agent emits the verdict and audit trail and ends without mutating anything."]
