impact: Addresses the named pain points directly: (1) uneven gate complexity across repos — a single BaseStore-backed gate manifest normalizes "what gates exist per repo" and flags repos lacking automated validation (scheduler-web, gal-code, EscalateLabs/marketing, pilotlight); (2) inconsistent concurrency control — the concurrency_health_check node detects head-of-line-blocked deploys caused by cancel-in-progress:false; (3) the 25-min @smoke E2E timeout blocking releases — the agent distinguishes a true regression from a timeout/flake and proposes a scoped re-run instead of a blanket block. Net effect: one auditable GO/NO-GO verdict per release tag with evidence and a remediation plan, replacing manual log-spelunking across 6+ repos. automatable_score 8/10 — the observe/classify/diagnose loop is fully automatable; only the irreversible promote/post/delete actions stay human-gated by design. Revenue-relevant: protects production release quality for the live scheduler-api/scheduler-web paying-customer surfaces while cutting release-gate toil.

trigger: Primary: LangGraph Platform Cron (scheduled assistant run) replacing the four GitHub Actions schedules — daily-pr-recap (22:00 UTC), label-sync (04:00 UTC), close-stale-prs (06:00 UTC), and a triage pass on new PRs. The Cron fires a thread with input `{"mode": "recap" | "label_sync" | "stale_close" | "triage", "repos": [...], "dry_run": bool}`. Secondary: webhook/`workflow_dispatch`-equivalent manual run via the SDK/API (`client.runs.create`) for ad-hoc per-repo recaps or backfill, mirroring today's `workflow_dispatch` inputs. Each mode is one addressable run; the Cron schedule lives in LangGraph (CronCreate), so the existing `.github/workflows/*.yml` schedules can be retired once parity is confirmed (keep them as fallback until then).
process: Automated PR Management & Triage (QA). Consolidates four today-separate, per-repo GitHub Actions jobs into one deployed LangGraph assistant with four modes over a fixed repo set (gal-run/extensions/gal-code, StratusCloudLabs/stratus, EscalateLabs/marketing, Scheduler-Systems/*, ZenuxLabs/Zenux): (1) RECAP — daily community-PR activity digest posted to Discord; (2) LABEL_SYNC — propagate the canonical label catalog (StratusCloudLabs/stratus/.github/labels.yaml) to managed repos; (3) STALE_CLOSE — close PRs inactive >60d with a courtesy comment; (4) TRIAGE — on/over open PRs, suggest kind/* + priority/* labels (dependencies, GitHub Actions, Terraform) and flag duplicates. The agent does the analysis (Claude reasoning over `gh`-fetched JSON) deterministically and routes every write/publish/irreversible action through a human-in-the-loop interrupt before execution. NO model training/distillation — this only ORCHESTRATES calls to Claude for summarization/classification, which is explicitly permitted under the workspace AGENTS.md guardrail.
graph_steps: START -> route_mode: read state.mode, fan to the correct subgraph (recap | label_sync | stale_close | triage). State schema (TypedDict): mode, repos[], dry_run, today (UTC), team_members[] (loaded from each repo's .github/TEAM_MEMBERS + the hardcoded Anomaly list), bot_excludes[] (copilot-pull-request-reviewer, github-actions), gathered_prs[], proposed_actions[], approvals{}, results[], errors[]. Do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= to .compile() — the platform injects Postgres.

gather_pr_data (tool node): run read-only `gh pr list --search created:/updated:<today> --json number,title,author,labels,createdAt,updatedAt,reviewDecision,isDraft,additions,deletions` and `gh pr view <n> --json comments` per repo. Mirror the existing deny-by-default permission posture (only `gh pr*`/`gh search*` allowed; no webfetch/edit/write). Filter out team members and bot accounts. Populate gathered_prs.

analyze (LLM node, Claude Sonnet via langchain-anthropic, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from env): classify each PR into bug-fix / high-activity / quick-win (recap); or kind/* + priority/* label suggestions matched against labels.yaml vocabulary, with dependency/Actions/Terraform detection (triage); or build the stale set (>60d last-activity across createdAt/last commit/last comment/last review). Emit structured proposed_actions[] (each: {repo, type: post_discord|add_label|close_pr|sync_label, target, payload, reason}). Prompt-cache the static system prompt + label catalog. NO training/distillation — generation only.

render_recap (recap mode only): assemble the Discord message in existing format (New PRs Today / Active PRs Today / Quick Wins, suppressed-embed hyperlinks, per-author tags), enforce the <1800-char budget, and pre-split into <=1950-char chunks so the publish step is a dumb sender. This removes the brittle bash sed/char-count splitting from daily-pr-recap.yml.

approval_gate (interrupt — HUMAN-IN-THE-LOOP, REQUIRED before ANY write): call `interrupt(proposed_actions)` to surface the full action list (Discord text chunks, label add/remove diffs, PR close list with comment bodies) to a human via LangGraph Inbox/Agent Inbox. Human resumes with approve-all / approve-subset / reject. If dry_run=true, skip execution entirely and just record what WOULD happen (parity with close-stale-prs dryRun input). Persist decision in approvals{} via the injected checkpointer so the thread is resumable.

execute_approved (tool node, conditional on approval): for ONLY the approved actions — post_discord via Discord webhook (DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL from env, never hardcoded; honor 1s inter-message delay + rate limits), add_label/close_pr via `gh` with write scope, sync_label via the labels.yaml catalog (delete-other-labels:false to match current safe config). Wrap each call in bounded retry w/ exponential backoff on GitHub secondary-rate-limit (port the MAX_RETRIES=3 + retry-after logic from close-stale-prs.yml). Append outcomes to results[].

report -> END: write a run summary (counts: PRs scanned, actions proposed, approved, executed, skipped, errored) to results[]; on any error, append to errors[] and continue (fail-soft per-action, never abort the whole batch). Summary is returned as graph output and optionally posted to an ops Discord channel (also approval-gated if it contains anything beyond pure counts).
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS) — Agent Server runtime, auto-provisioned Postgres checkpointer + store
LangGraph Cron (CronCreate) — replaces the 4 GitHub Actions schedules
GitHub CLI `gh` (read: pr list/view/search; write: pr comment/close, label add/remove) wrapped as LangGraph tools with deny-by-default permissions
GitHub REST/GraphQL (octokit-equivalent) for the stale-PR pagination + rate-limit-aware close loop
EndBug/label-sync-equivalent logic driven by StratusCloudLabs/stratus/.github/labels.yaml catalog
langchain-anthropic ChatAnthropic (Claude Sonnet) for summarization/classification — orchestration only, NO training/distillation
Discord Incoming Webhook (post recap + ops summary)
LangGraph interrupt() + Agent Inbox / LangGraph Studio for human-in-the-loop approval
langgraph.json deployment config (graphs: {pr_triage: ./agent/graph.py:graph}, python_version 3.12, image_distro wolfi)
approval_gates: HARD REQUIREMENT — every write/publish/irreversible action is gated by a single `interrupt()` node (approval_gate) before execute_approved runs. Specifically gated: (1) posting any message to Discord (recap or ops summary); (2) adding/removing any label on a PR; (3) closing any PR (plus its courtesy comment); (4) running label-sync against managed repos (catalog diff shown, delete-other-labels stays false). Human resumes with approve-all / approve-subset(selected action IDs) / reject; rejection records intent and exits clean. `dry_run=true` bypasses execution entirely and only logs proposed actions (parity with the existing close-stale-prs `dryRun` input). Read-only gather/analyze steps are NOT gated. Decisions persist via the injected Postgres checkpointer so an interrupted thread resumes exactly where it paused. Aligns with workspace memory rules: no send/delete/close without explicit human approval.
deploy_notes: Repo layout: agent/graph.py (exports compiled `graph`; build StateGraph, .compile() with NO checkpointer/store — the platform injects Postgres), agent/tools.py (gh + Discord + label-sync tools), agent/prompts.py (cached system prompt + embedded labels.yaml catalog), langgraph.json at root, pyproject.toml with deps (langgraph, langchain-anthropic, httpx, PyGithub or gh-via-subprocess). langgraph.json: {"dependencies":["."],"graphs":{"pr_triage":"./agent/graph.py:graph"},"env":".env","python_version":"3.12","image_distro":"wolfi","pip_installer":"uv"}. Deploy via `langgraph deploy` CLI or LangSmith UI to Cloud SaaS (api.smith.langchain.com). Inject ALL secrets via the Platform UI/API, never commit .env. After deploy: create the 4 Crons (CronCreate) — recap 22:00 UTC, label_sync 04:00 UTC, stale_close 06:00 UTC, triage hourly or on-demand. KEEP the existing .github/workflows/*.yml schedules disabled-but-present as fallback until 2 weeks of parity is confirmed, then remove. Wire Agent Inbox for the interrupt approvals (mobile-reachable for Shay). Constraints honored: deploy target = LangGraph Cloud; LLM used for orchestration/summarization only (no model training/distillation — compliant with AGENTS.md gal-model guardrail); all sends/closes/label-writes approval-gated; secrets via env only. Pain points addressed: removes brittle bash Discord char-splitting (now done in render_recap), centralizes team-member/bot filtering into shared state, consolidates label-sync maintenance + non-uniform stale-PR policy into one governed agent. automatable_score 6/10 reflects that gh/Discord/label-sync are deterministic but team-filtering nuance + the per-repo secret sprawl and required human approval on every write keep it from being fully hands-off.
impact: Consolidates 4 brittle, per-repo, separately-scheduled GitHub Actions jobs (daily-pr-recap, label-sync, close-stale-prs, pr-management triage) into ONE deployed, resumable, centrally-governed LangGraph assistant with consistent team/bot filtering and one approval surface. Eliminates the fragile bash sed/char-count Discord splitting and the duplicated stale-PR policy that today is applied non-uniformly across repos. Net: less per-repo workflow maintenance, uniform stale/label policy, and human-approved-by-default writes — at the cost of moving the Discord/GH secrets into the LangGraph Platform and adding an approval step (intentional, per the no-send-without-approval rule). Low revenue impact (internal QA/governance hygiene, not customer-facing), hence P2.

process: Lint & Code Formatting Enforcement
trigger: Primarily webhook-driven: GitHub `check_run`/`workflow_run` completed=failure and `pull_request` opened/synchronize events for the in-scope repos route to the LangGraph deployment's `/runs` (or a thin GitHub-App relay → `langgraph.json` `http.app` custom route). A failing `gate` job (typecheck/lint steps in scheduler-web & scheduler-api), a failing stratus pre-commit/CI prettier/yamllint/shellcheck check, a turbo lint failure in gal-code, or a `make lint` failure in pilotlight is the canonical trigger. Secondary scheduled trigger (LangGraph Cron / `assistants.cron`) runs a weekly cross-repo "config-drift audit" with no PR attached. Manual trigger via LangGraph SDK `client.runs.create` for ad-hoc "diagnose this PR" requests. Each run is keyed by `thread_id = {repo}:{pr_number}` so re-pushes resume the same checkpointed thread rather than spawning duplicates.
graph_steps: ingest_event: normalize the inbound GitHub webhook / cron / manual payload into a typed RunInput {mode: pr_fix|drift_audit, repo, pr_number?, head_sha?, failing_checks[]}; classify mode; short-circuit to END if repo not in the allow-list or the check is unrelated to lint/format/typecheck.

fetch_signals: read-only gather. For pr_fix: pull failing check-run logs + annotations, the PR diff, and the repo's lint surface descriptor (which of eslint/tsc/prettier-json/yamllint/shellcheck/turbo/make-lint apply, from a per-repo config map). For drift_audit: fetch each repo's lint config files (.eslintrc*, .prettierrc*, .yamllint.yml, .pre-commit-config.yaml, tsconfig, Makefile lint targets, shellcheck severity). All via read-only GitHub token; no writes.

diagnose (Claude): classify each failure into {auto-fixable-formatting, rule-violation-needs-code-change, config-error, flaky/infra, severity-gap-suspected-real-bug}. For shellcheck --severity=warning failures, explicitly flag info/style findings that the warning threshold would have skipped but look like latent bugs (addresses the 'shellcheck severity may miss bugs' pain point). Emit a structured Diagnosis with confidence + cited log lines.

plan_fix (Claude): for pr_fix, draft a concrete remediation: a unified diff for formatting/auto-fixable items (mirroring what `eslint --fix`/`prettier --write` would produce) and a written change-list for rule violations that need human judgment. For drift_audit, produce a normalization proposal (single canonical prettier/eslint base config, dedup of pre-commit-vs-CI runs, recommended shellcheck severity). Compute a diff-summary, blast-radius, and reversibility note. No file is written yet.

interrupt__human_review (HARD GATE): call interrupt() with the full proposed diff/report, target action (open-PR / push-commit / post-PR-comment / open-issue), and risk summary. Execution pauses as a checkpointed thread; a human resumes via Command(resume=...) with approve | edit | reject. Nothing irreversible has happened up to this node.

route_decision: branch on the resumed human decision. reject -> record_outcome(rejected). edit -> loop back to plan_fix with the human's edits. approve -> proceed to apply_action with only the approved action(s).

apply_action (gated, idempotent): execute ONLY the human-approved irreversible action — create a fix branch + open a PR (preferred, itself reviewable/revertible), or post the diagnosis as a PR review comment, or open a fragmentation issue for drift_audit. Uses a scoped write token from env, dedups by deterministic branch/issue title keyed on head_sha, never force-pushes, never merges, never edits protected branches directly.

record_outcome: write a structured result to the LangGraph BaseStore (per-repo namespace) for the weekly drift report and metrics (fix accepted/edited/rejected, recurring rule, repo); update thread state; END.
tools_integrations: GitHub REST/GraphQL (read): check-runs, annotations, PR diff, file contents, workflow logs — read-only PAT/App token
GitHub (write, gate-only): create branch, open PR, post PR review comment, open issue — separate scoped token, used ONLY in apply_action after approval
GitHub App webhook relay → LangGraph Platform /runs (or langgraph.json http.app custom Starlette route to validate the X-Hub-Signature before enqueuing)
Anthropic Claude (langchain-anthropic ChatAnthropic) for diagnose/plan_fix reasoning — orchestration/inference only, NO training or distillation
LangGraph managed Postgres checkpointer (auto-injected; thread_id={repo}:{pr} for resumable HITL interrupts)
LangGraph BaseStore (semantic + namespaced) for cross-repo lint-config descriptors, recurring-violation memory, and drift-report accumulation
LangGraph Cron / assistants.cron for the weekly config-drift audit
Optional Slack notify (gated, approval-only) to ping #qa with the pending-approval link — never auto-acts
langgraph CLI / LangSmith Deployments for build+deploy of langgraph.json; LangSmith tracing for observability
approval_gates: interrupt__human_review is a mandatory hard gate before ANY repo-mutating or outbound action. The graph is split so every read-only node (ingest, fetch_signals, diagnose, plan_fix) runs autonomously and produces only an in-memory proposal; the LangGraph `interrupt()` primitive pauses the checkpointed thread and surfaces the full proposed diff + target action + reversibility note. A human resumes with Command(resume={decision: approve|edit|reject}). apply_action runs only on explicit approve and executes only the approved action(s). Reject ends the run with no side effects; edit re-plans. Opening a PR (rather than pushing to the branch) is the default approved action precisely because it is itself reviewable and revertible. The agent NEVER merges, NEVER force-pushes, NEVER edits protected/default branches directly, and NEVER auto-sends Slack/email — those are all behind the same gate. Per workspace policy this agent does no AI/ML model development; it only orchestrates Claude for diagnosis.
deploy_notes: Deploy to LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments, managed Cloud SaaS tier) via `langgraph deploy` from a repo whose root has langgraph.json. langgraph.json: dependencies ["."] (pyproject with langgraph, langgraph-sdk, langchain-anthropic, httpx, PyJWT/cryptography for GitHub App auth); graphs {"lint_enforcement":"./agent/graph.py:graph"}; python_version "3.12"; image_distro "wolfi"; pip_installer "uv"; env injected via the platform UI/API (the eight secrets above — none committed); http.app -> "./agent/webhook.py:app" exposing a signed GitHub-webhook intake route that verifies X-Hub-Signature-256 before creating a run; store.index enabled (openai or anthropic-compatible embeddings) so the lint-config/recurring-violation memory is searchable; store.ttl + checkpointer.ttl set so PR threads expire after merge. CRITICAL: graph.compile() must NOT pass checkpointer=/store= — the platform injects production Postgres automatically; passing InMemorySaver is local-only and is ignored/overridden in the hosted runtime. Use a factory `make_graph` only if per-request repo-config injection is needed. Per-repo lint-surface map (which linters apply where: scheduler-web=tsc+eslint, scheduler-api=tsc+eslint, stratus=prettier-json+yamllint+shellcheck@warning+terraform_fmt, gal-code=turbo, pilotlight=make lint, marketing=eslint/gate) is seeded into the BaseStore at first run and is the single source of truth for the drift audit. Register a GitHub App (not a PAT) for webhook delivery + least-privilege write; point its webhook at the deployment's signed http.app route. Add a weekly Cron (assistants.cron) for drift_audit. Wire LangSmith tracing for HITL-decision observability. Roll out repo-by-repo: start with scheduler-web (richest typecheck+lint gate) in comment-only mode, then enable open-PR mode once fix-acceptance rate is validated. v1 is diagnose-and-propose only; no auto-merge ever. Honors workspace guardrails: orchestration of Claude only, no model training/distillation, every irreversible action human-gated, secrets via env never hardcoded.
impact: Cuts the manual triage loop when a gate's typecheck/lint step or a stratus pre-commit/CI hook (prettier-json/yamllint/shellcheck) fails: the agent diagnoses the failure, proposes the exact fix as a reviewable PR, and surfaces shellcheck findings below the --severity=warning threshold that look like real bugs — directly attacking the four stated pain points (config fragmentation, pre-commit-vs-CI duplication, shellcheck severity gaps, Prettier config drift). The weekly drift audit gives one canonical view of lint-config divergence across the six repos and a normalization proposal, replacing ad-hoc per-repo cleanup. automatable_score 8 reflected as high-leverage but kept strictly proposal-only (PRs/comments/issues) so a human always approves before any repo is touched.

process: Branch Protection & Merge Requirements (GitHub branch protection / merge-gate compliance across StratusCloudLabs, Scheduler-Systems, ZenuxLabs, gal-run repos)
trigger: Three triggers into the same LangGraph deployment: (1) Scheduled cron (LangGraph Platform Crons API, e.g. weekly Mon 04:00 mirroring branch-prune.yml `20 4 * * 1`) that sweeps all in-scope repos; (2) GitHub webhook on `branch_protection_rule` created/edited/deleted and `repository` ruleset events, mounted via the custom `http.app` in langgraph.json (HMAC-verified) — this is what closes the "no audit trail for protection-rule changes" gap; (3) On-demand `/runs` POST per repo for ad-hoc audits or release-branch exception requests. Each invocation carries `{repo, mode: audit|drift|exception, actor?, event_payload?}` in the run input.
graph_steps: ingest_request: normalize trigger (cron sweep | webhook event | on-demand). For cron, fan out one subgraph run per repo in the configured scope list. Load per-repo declared baseline (the .github/branch-protection.yml expected-state file this agent introduces to make settings-only config visible-in-repo) and the resolved scope from BaseStore.

fetch_live_state: read-only GitHub API calls only — GET branch protection + rulesets for `main`/`release-*` (required status checks incl. gate-equivalent workflows, required approving review count >=1, require CODEOWNERS review, restrict direct pushes), GET .github/CODEOWNERS, GET .github/branch-protection.yml, GET recent `branch_protection_rule` audit-log entries. No mutations in this node.

reconcile_baseline: deterministic diff (pure code, no LLM) of live protection state vs declared baseline + CONTRIBUTING.md Kubernetes-model rules (no long-lived dev/staging; release-* only for supported lines; >=1 approval; CODEOWNERS on protected paths; status checks enforced; direct-push blocked). Emit a structured findings list: {repo, branch, rule, expected, actual, severity, drift_type}.

classify_findings: LLM (Claude) node — categorize each finding as compliant / benign-drift / violation / unexplained-protection-change / release-exception-candidate, attach plain-English rationale and a recommended remediation (which protection field to set, or whether an exception record is warranted). Strictly orchestration/reasoning over findings — no model training.

write_audit_record: append an immutable audit entry to BaseStore (and a per-repo audit-trail digest) capturing actor, before/after rule state, timestamp, and classification. This is a non-destructive write and is the durable audit trail the process currently lacks; it is NOT gated.

interrupt__human_approval: HITL `interrupt()` checkpoint. Pause and surface the proposed remediation set to a human reviewer (Slack/Gmail draft + LangGraph resume token). Required before ANY irreversible action: applying/repairing branch-protection rules, committing/PRing the baseline file, opening a release-branch exception PR, or posting external notifications. The graph blocks here until an explicit approve/reject/edit resume.

execute_approved_actions: only runs on approved resume. Performs the human-sanctioned writes via GitHub API: PUT branch-protection to restore baseline, open a PR adding/updating .github/branch-protection.yml, or open a tracked release-exception PR/issue. Each action is idempotent and re-records to the audit trail. Rejected items are logged and skipped.

report_and_notify: emit a run summary (compliant repos, drifts repaired, pending exceptions, unexplained changes) to the run output and, if approved in the interrupt, post a digest to Slack/GitHub issue. Persist run state via the platform Postgres checkpointer for resumability and replay.
tools_integrations: GitHub REST/GraphQL API (read: branch protection, rulesets, CODEOWNERS, audit log; gated write: PUT protection, create PR/issue) via a fine-scoped token analogous to BRANCH_PRUNE_TOKEN
GitHub webhooks (branch_protection_rule, repository ruleset events) received by a custom Starlette/FastAPI app mounted at langgraph.json http.app with HMAC signature verification
Anthropic Claude (claude-sonnet for finding classification/rationale; orchestration only, no model training/distillation)
LangGraph Platform managed Postgres checkpointer (auto-injected; do NOT pass checkpointer= to .compile()) for resumable HITL runs
LangGraph Platform BaseStore for per-repo baselines, scope config, and the append-only audit trail (store.ttl configured)
LangGraph Platform Crons API for the scheduled weekly sweep
Slack MCP / Gmail draft for the approval surface and digest (send is approval-gated)
Existing repo artifacts as ground truth: scripts/prune-branches.sh safety logic, .github/CONTRIBUTING.md, .github/CODEOWNERS, gate.yml/PR-governance workflows
secrets_needed: GITHUB_APP_TOKEN (or fine-scoped PAT) — repo admin:read + contents:write for gated PRs; injected via LangSmith Deployments env, never committed
GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET — HMAC secret for verifying inbound branch_protection_rule webhooks
AGENT_AUTH_SECRET — for the langgraph.json auth.path custom Auth instance protecting the deployment endpoints
approval_gates: Single mandatory human-in-the-loop gate implemented as a LangGraph `interrupt()` at the `interrupt__human_approval` node before the `execute_approved_actions` node. Everything upstream is read-only or non-destructive (the audit-record write is append-only and unguarded by design, since recording history is safe and is the whole point of the agent). Everything that is irreversible or externally visible is gated: (1) applying/repairing branch-protection rules via API, (2) committing or opening a PR for the .github/branch-protection.yml baseline file, (3) opening a release-branch exception PR/issue, (4) posting any Slack/GitHub external notification. The reviewer can approve all, approve a subset (per-finding), edit the remediation, or reject; rejected items are logged and skipped. Resume is via the platform `/runs/{run_id}` resume token. No action is ever auto-applied. Secrets only via env per security rules; no model training/distillation anywhere in the graph (Anthropic terms compliant — orchestration/reasoning over GitHub metadata only).
impact: Closes the three documented pain points directly: (1) makes settings-only branch protection visible-in-repo by introducing and reconciling a declared .github/branch-protection.yml baseline; (2) creates the missing audit trail for protection-rule changes via the append-only BaseStore record fed by branch_protection_rule webhooks — unexplained rule changes are caught in near-real-time instead of never; (3) turns release-branch exception handling into a tracked, gated PR/issue flow instead of ad-hoc. automatable_score is 7/10 and this design respects that ceiling: detection/diff/audit is fully automated, but every mutation stays human-gated, so it augments rather than replaces the QA/DevOps reviewer. Scope spans StratusCloudLabs/stratus, Scheduler-Systems/scheduler-web, Scheduler-Systems/scheduler-api, ZenuxLabs/Zenux, gal-run/extensions/gal-code.
deploy_notes: Deploy to LangSmith Deployments (LangGraph Platform / managed Cloud SaaS). langgraph.json: dependencies ["."], graphs {"sentinel":"./agent/graph.py:graph"}, python_version "3.12", image_distro "wolfi", pip_installer "uv", env ".env" (real secrets injected via platform UI/API not committed), store {ttl:{default_ttl:43200}} for the audit trail, auth {path:"./agent/auth.py:auth"} to protect endpoints, and http {app:"./agent/server.py:app"} to mount the HMAC-verified GitHub webhook receiver. Build the StateGraph with TypedDict state + add_messages reducer, .compile() to a module-level `graph` and do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= (platform injects managed Postgres). Wire the weekly sweep through the platform Crons API (cron `20 4 * * 1` to mirror branch-prune.yml). Reuse the proven safety predicates from scripts/prune-branches.sh (protected-branch lists, open-PR / not-merged checks) so the agent never proposes deleting or unprotecting a branch the existing tooling would protect. Start in audit-only mode (no execute node enabled) for a one-week shadow run, validate the audit trail and diffs against the live repos, then enable the gated execute path. EU vs US region per LangSmith org. Ground-truth files to package as reference context: scripts/prune-branches.sh, .github/community/CONTRIBUTING.md, .github/CODEOWNERS.

process: E2E & Smoke Testing with Playwright across gal-code (linux/windows matrix), scheduler-web (Firebase staging), and Stratus (production post-deploy smoke). A deployed LangGraph Platform agent that ingests COMPLETED Playwright E2E CI runs (JUnit XML + test-results/playwright-report artifacts), then: (1) parses results and classifies each failing spec as real-failure vs flake vs infra/timeout vs cache-miss, (2) correlates against recent history to detect flake patterns and the heartbeat-masked timeout symptom, (3) proposes concrete remediations (quarantine a flaky spec, fix browser-cache key invalidation, prune/skip windows matrix leg, re-run targeted shard), and (4) drafts the human-facing artifact (GitHub issue, Slack alert, PR comment, or workflow re-dispatch). The agent ONLY orchestrates Claude for reasoning over test telemetry — it never trains/evaluates/distills any model. Every write/irreversible action (file GitHub issue, post Slack, dispatch workflow_dispatch re-run, comment on PR, push a quarantine annotation) is paused at a human-in-the-loop interrupt and executed only after explicit operator approval inside LangGraph Studio / the Agent Inbox.
trigger: Primary: GitHub Actions webhook on workflow_run.completed for the E2E/gate/Deploy-Stratus workflows (POST to a custom Starlette route mounted via langgraph.json http.app, HMAC-verified). Secondary: scheduled cron (daily flake-trend digest over the trailing 7 days of runs). Manual: operator kicks a run from LangGraph Studio with an explicit run_id/repo to triage on demand.
graph_steps: ingest_run: webhook/cron handler normalizes the trigger into State (repo, workflow, run_id, conclusion, head_sha, matrix_leg). Verify HMAC, dedupe by run_id, short-circuit if conclusion==success AND no flaky-retry signal.

fetch_artifacts: GitHub tool node downloads JUnit XML (mikepenz report_paths) + test-results/ + playwright-report for the run; parse into per-spec records (name, status, retries, duration, error class, screenshot/trace refs). Read-only.

classify_failures: Claude node labels each failure as real_failure | flake | infra_timeout | cache_miss | env_unavailable, using error text, retry-pass deltas, the 60s-heartbeat/timeout signature, and Firebase-staging/Stratus-prod URL reachability. Emits structured verdicts.

correlate_history: query long-term BaseStore (semantic index over prior verdicts keyed by spec name + repo) to compute flake rate, first-seen, and whether this spec is already quarantined; detect browser-cache-invalidation patterns and matrix-leg-specific failures.

propose_actions: Claude node turns verdicts+history into a ranked remediation plan with exact diffs/payloads: quarantine annotation, cache-key fix (pin to PW version+arch), windows-leg skip on PR, targeted shard re-run, or 'no action / green'. No side effects yet.
route_decision: conditional edge — if plan == no-op -> persist_and_end; else -> human_gate.

human_gate: interrupt() HUMAN-IN-THE-LOOP. Surface the proposed action(s), evidence, and exact payload to the operator (Agent Inbox/Studio). Operator approves / edits / rejects each action. APPROVAL-GATED for every write.

execute_approved: on resume, run ONLY operator-approved actions via the relevant MCP/GitHub tools (create issue, PR comment, slack_send_message, workflow_dispatch re-run, commit quarantine annotation). Idempotent, audited.
persist_and_end: write verdicts + action outcomes to BaseStore for future correlation; emit run summary. Checkpointer (managed Postgres) preserves thread for resumable approvals.
tools_integrations: GitHub REST/Actions (read runs+artifacts, download JUnit/test-results/playwright-report, create issues, PR comments, dispatch workflow_dispatch re-runs, push quarantine annotations)
Slack MCP (slack_send_message / slack_send_message_draft for flake alerts + digests) — gated
Claude via langchain-anthropic (classification + remediation reasoning only; NO model training/eval/distillation)
Custom Starlette/FastAPI route mounted via langgraph.json http.app for HMAC-verified GitHub webhook intake
LangGraph managed Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore with semantic index (openai:text-embedding-3-small) over historical flake verdicts
Sentry MCP (optional, read-only) to correlate prod smoke failures on stratus.scheduler-systems.com with runtime errors
LangGraph cron (scheduled daily flake-trend digest)
approval_gates: Single hard human-in-the-loop interrupt at human_gate before ANY write/irreversible action. Every one of these is approval-gated and never auto-executed: filing/closing a GitHub issue, posting to Slack, commenting on a PR, dispatching a workflow_dispatch CI re-run, and committing a test-quarantine annotation or cache-key change. The classify/correlate/propose path is fully read-only and runs unattended; only execute_approved runs after explicit operator approve/edit/reject per action in LangGraph Studio / Agent Inbox. Approvals are resumable via the managed Postgres checkpointer (operator can approve hours later on the same thread). Secrets are injected via the platform env (langgraph.json env + Deployments UI), never hardcoded or committed.
deploy_notes: Deploy to LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments / managed Cloud SaaS tier). langgraph.json: graphs={"e2e_triage":"./agent/graph.py:graph"}, python_version="3.12", image_distro="wolfi", pip_installer="uv", http.app="./agent/app.py:app" for the GitHub webhook route + CORS, store.index.embed="openai:text-embedding-3-small" dims=1536 for flake history, checkpointer.ttl strategy=delete default_ttl~30d. CRITICAL: compile the StateGraph WITHOUT passing checkpointer=/store= — the platform injects production Postgres checkpointer+store; passing InMemorySaver is local-only and ignored/overridden in the hosted runtime. graph = builder.compile() exported as a module-level var. Deploy via `langgraph deploy` CLI or Deployments UI from repo root; inject all secrets through the Deployments UI/API, never the committed .env. Wire the GitHub App webhook (workflow_run events) to the deployment's custom http route; register the daily cron via the platform cron API. Guardrail: this agent reasons over test telemetry with Claude only and MUST NOT be used to develop/train/evaluate/distill any ML model (Anthropic Consumer Terms + workspace AGENTS.md) — it is pure QA orchestration/IaC-adjacent tooling. Concrete v1 target: scheduler-web gate.yml E2E (Firebase staging) first, then extend to gal-code linux/windows matrix and Stratus production @smoke runs.

trigger: Primary: scheduled cron (LangGraph Platform cron, e.g. daily 06:00 UTC) that sweeps recent CI runs across the target repos. Secondary: webhook ingestion via the mounted custom `http.app` (FastAPI at `./app.py:app`) bound to a GitHub Actions `workflow_run` (completed) repository webhook, so a finished test.yml / codeql-analysis.yml / gate.yml run immediately enqueues a graph run for that run_id. Tertiary: manual `workflow_dispatch`-style invocation via the Assistants API for backfills / on-demand audits of a specific repo or run.
process: Test Coverage Tracking & Artifact Management. The agent automates the manual artifact-review and trend-tracking gap: it discovers completed CI test runs across gal-run/extensions/gal-code, Scheduler-Systems/scheduler-web, StratusCloudLabs/stratus, and any repo with test.yml/gate.yml; downloads the structured artifacts (junit.xml, Playwright test-results/playwright-report, coverage reports, CodeQL SARIF) before the 7-30 day retention window expires; parses them into normalized metrics (pass/fail counts, flaky tests, line/branch coverage %, SARIF finding counts/severity); persists each run as a snapshot into the LangGraph long-term Store (BaseStore) keyed by repo+branch so it becomes the centralized coverage-history/trend database that codecov/coveralls would otherwise provide; computes deltas against the last good baseline per repo/branch; and — only when a regression threshold is crossed (coverage drop > X%, new test failures on main, new high/critical SARIF findings) — drafts a human-readable alert. All sends (Slack message, GitHub issue creation/comment, email) are gated behind a human-in-the-loop interrupt; nothing is published autonomously. The LLM (Claude) is used only to summarize/triage/explain the parsed numeric artifacts into a readable alert and to suggest likely-cause owners — it is NOT used to train, fine-tune, distill, or score any model, and it never evaluates the gal-model classifier.
graph_steps: ingest_trigger: normalize the entry payload (cron sweep window | webhook workflow_run.completed | manual {repo, run_id, branch}) into a list of (repo, run_id, head_sha, branch, workflow_name) work items; default repo set = gal-code, scheduler-web, stratus + any repo whose .github/workflows contains test.yml or gate.yml

discover_runs: GitHub REST tool — for each repo list recent completed workflow runs (Actions API), filter to test/gate/codeql workflows, dedupe against Store (skip run_ids already snapshotted) to make the graph idempotent and resumable

download_artifacts: GitHub Actions artifacts API — list + download artifacts for each run (unit junit.xml, playwright test-results/playwright-report, coverage, codeql-sarif-*) BEFORE retention expiry; stream to a tmp workspace; record artifact retention_days so near-expiry runs are prioritized

parse_normalize: deterministic parsers (NO LLM) — JUnit XML -> {tests, failures, skipped, flaky, duration}; coverage report -> {line_pct, branch_pct}; Playwright report -> {passed, failed, retries}; SARIF -> {rule_id, severity counts}; emit one normalized CoverageSnapshot per run+branch

persist_snapshot: write CoverageSnapshot into the platform Store under namespace (repo, branch) keyed by head_sha/run_id with TTL aligned to history horizon; this Store IS the centralized coverage/trend dataset (replaces codecov/coveralls)

compute_trend: load prior baseline snapshot for same repo+branch from Store; compute deltas (coverage delta %, new failures vs baseline, new SARIF findings by severity, flaky-test churn); evaluate against configured thresholds (env-driven)

triage_summarize (Claude): only if a threshold is breached — summarize the numeric deltas into a concise human-readable regression report, classify severity, and suggest likely owning area/files from the failing test paths; structured output only, no external calls
route_decision: conditional edge — if no breach -> persist run + END (silent, healthy); if breach -> proceed to approval

human_approval_interrupt: interrupt() the graph and surface the drafted alert + proposed destination (Slack channel / GitHub issue+repo / email) for human review; resume only on explicit approve/edit/reject from Studio or the resume API — reject ends without sending

execute_send (gated): on approval only — perform the irreversible action (post Slack message OR create/comment GitHub issue OR send email) via the approved channel; record the action + approver + timestamp into Store for audit
record_audit: append run outcome (healthy | alerted-approved | alerted-rejected), metrics, and any send receipt to Store; END
tools_integrations: GitHub REST API (Actions: list workflow runs, list/download artifacts; Issues: create/comment) via a fine-grained PAT or GitHub App token
Slack Web API (chat.postMessage) for approved alert delivery
Email/SMTP or transactional email API (optional alert channel)
LangGraph Platform Store (BaseStore) as the centralized coverage-history / trend datastore
LangGraph Platform Postgres checkpointer (auto-provisioned) for resumable runs + interrupt state
Anthropic Claude API (LLM) for triage summarization only
Deterministic parsers: JUnit XML, Cobertura/LCOV coverage, Playwright JSON report, SARIF 2.1.0
LangGraph Platform cron scheduler for the daily sweep
Custom FastAPI app mounted via http.app for GitHub workflow_run webhook ingestion
impact: Closes the QA observability gap with no per-repo CI rewrite. Turns ephemeral, scattered CI artifacts (7-30 day retention across gal-code @v4/7d, stratus CodeQL @v7/30d, scheduler-web, and all test.yml/gate.yml repos) into a durable centralized coverage/trend history in the Store — the codecov/coveralls capability the org explicitly never integrated — and converts manual artifact review into automated delta detection with approval-gated alerting on coverage drops, new failures, and new high/critical SARIF findings. Captures historical data before retention deletes it, enabling cross-run comparison that artifact limits currently prevent. automatable_score 5/10: parsing/trend math is fully deterministic and high-confidence; the judgment (is this drop real, who owns it, do we alert) plus all sends stay human-gated, which is why this is an assistive sentinel rather than a fully autonomous bot — hence P2, not P0. Pure read-from-CI + draft-then-approve design means low blast radius and no write access to the test pipelines themselves.
approval_gates: Single mandatory human-in-the-loop interrupt() before ANY irreversible/send action (Slack post, GitHub issue create/comment, email). The graph drafts the alert and the proposed destination, then pauses; a human approves, edits, or rejects via LangGraph Studio or the resume API. Reject = end run with no external effect. Read-only steps (discover runs, download artifacts, parse, persist snapshot to Store, compute trend) run without gating since they have no external side effects and only write to the agent's own Store. Healthy runs (no threshold breach) never reach the gate and never message anyone. Every approved send is recorded with approver identity + timestamp for audit. No auto-merge, no CI mutation, no write-back to source repos beyond the approved issue/comment.
secrets_needed: GITHUB_TOKEN (fine-grained PAT or GitHub App installation token; scopes: actions:read, contents:read, issues:write — issues:write only for the approved alert path)
GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET (HMAC verification for the workflow_run webhook into the FastAPI app)
SLACK_BOT_TOKEN (chat.postMessage to the approved alert channel)
SLACK_ALERT_CHANNEL_ID (default destination, overridable at approval time)
SMTP_URL or EMAIL_API_KEY (optional email alert channel)
OPENAI_API_KEY (only if Store semantic index uses openai embeddings per langgraph.json store.index.embed; omit if embeddings not used)
deploy_notes: Deploy to LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS). langgraph.json: graphs {"coverage_sentinel":"./agent/graph.py:graph"}, python_version "3.12", image_distro "wolfi", dependencies ["."], pip_installer "uv". Mount the webhook receiver via http: {"app":"./app.py:app"} so GitHub workflow_run events hit the deployment. Configure store.ttl (history horizon, e.g. default_ttl long enough to keep multi-month trend) and checkpointer.ttl; do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= to .compile() — the platform injects production Postgres checkpointer + Store automatically (passing InMemorySaver is local-only and ignored in hosted runtime). graph = builder.compile() exported at module scope; interrupt-before-send uses interrupt() so the platform persists state across the human pause. Register the daily sweep as a platform Cron. Inject ALL secrets via the deployment UI/API env, never in committed .env. Anthropic Consumer Terms guardrail: this agent only orchestrates Claude for summarization — it must never run/score/train/distill the gal-model classifier or any ML model, and the codeql-analysis.yml SARIF path is treated as plain security-finding data, not model input. Phase 1: gal-code (test.yml) + stratus (codeql-analysis.yml) read-only snapshotting with alerts disabled (Store-only, verify trend math). Phase 2: enable approval-gated Slack alerts. Phase 3: add scheduler-web + auto-discover remaining test.yml/gate.yml repos and enable gated GitHub-issue creation.

process: Automated Dependency Updates & Security Patches — LangGraph-orchestrated triage layer that sits ON TOP of the existing Dependabot config (StratusCloudLabs/stratus/.github/dependabot.yml: 6 ecosystems, 10-PR/ecosystem cap, weekly Mon 08:00 PST, target main). Dependabot still opens the PRs; this agent solves the three pain points it cannot: (1) PR-flood bottleneck under the 10-PR cap, (2) the weekly cadence missing critical security patches, (3) review fatigue. It does NOT replace Dependabot or the merge gate. On each run it: enumerates open dependency PRs across the configured repos (stratus, EscalateLabs/marketing, Scheduler-Systems repos, ProjectMasterLabs/pilotlight), cross-references GitHub security advisories / Dependabot alerts to classify each update by severity (critical-security / security / routine-patch / minor / major), checks each PR's required status-check state against the real gate set (PR Governance, Secret Scan, Terraform validation, repository catalog validation, Checkov, IaC scan, CodeQL), detects ecosystem queues at/near the 10-PR cap (flood signal), and groups routine same-ecosystem bumps so reviewers see one ranked digest instead of dozens of PRs. It produces a prioritized, evidence-backed triage report and a set of PROPOSED actions (label PR, request CODEOWNERS reviewer, post a grouped Slack/GitHub digest, and — only for green-gated critical-severity security PRs — propose enabling Dependabot auto-merge / approving). EVERY mutating action (label write, review request, comment/digest post, auto-merge enable, approval) is held behind a human-in-the-loop interrupt and only executed after explicit approval. The agent calls Claude purely for severity reasoning, changelog/breaking-change summarization, and digest authoring — no model training, fine-tuning, or distillation. Read-only enrichment (advisory lookup, gate-status read, PR diff read) runs unattended; the run pauses at a single approval gate before any writes.
trigger: Three triggers into the same compiled graph: (1) Scheduled cron — LangGraph Platform Cron job at Mon 06:30 PST (90 min BEFORE Dependabot's 08:00 PST window so the queue is clean) AND a daily 07:00 PST critical-only sweep to close the weekly-cadence security gap; (2) Webhook — GitHub App / repository_dispatch on Dependabot-alert 'created'/'reopened' (severity>=high) and on dependabot PR 'opened'/'synchronize', delivered to the deployment's /webhooks endpoint for near-real-time critical-patch handling; (3) Manual — on-demand invocation from the LangGraph Studio/SDK ('triage stratus npm now') for ad-hoc queue review. Webhook critical events short-circuit to the security fast-path subgraph.
graph_steps: START -> load_context: read run config (repo list, ecosystem cap=10, gate-check names, CODEOWNERS routing map, trigger type) from state.context; no secrets in graph — GITHUB_TOKEN/SLACK_BOT_TOKEN/ANTHROPIC_API_KEY injected via LangGraph Platform env

fetch_dependency_prs: GitHub API list open PRs labeled 'dependencies' across all configured repos; capture per-ecosystem counts (npm/github-actions/terraform/python) to detect flood vs the 10-PR cap; read-only
enrich_advisories: for each PR, query GitHub Security Advisories + Dependabot alerts for the bumped package; attach CVSS/severity, fixed-version, exploit-known flags; read-only

read_gate_status: for each PR fetch required status-check rollup against the real gate set (PR Governance, Secret Scan, Terraform validation, repo catalog validation, Checkov, IaC scan, CodeQL); classify green / pending / failing; read-only

classify_and_rank (Claude): LLM reasons over advisory severity + gate state + ecosystem + breaking-change risk from diff/changelog to assign tier (critical-security/security/routine/minor/major) and a merge-readiness score; summarizes breaking changes; pure orchestration, no model training
branch security_fast_path: if any critical/high security PR is green-gated -> route to high-priority proposal; else -> standard grouping (webhook critical events enter here directly)

build_grouped_digest (Claude): cluster routine same-ecosystem bumps, draft a ranked human-readable digest (top: green critical-security; then flood-relief grouping for capped ecosystems; then routine batch; then needs-attention/failing-gate), each item with evidence links — addresses review-fatigue + 10-PR-cap pain points

plan_actions: assemble concrete proposed mutations — apply labels (kind/security, security, priority), request CODEOWNERS reviewers (terraform->devops-team, security paths->security-team), post digest to Slack/GitHub, and for green-gated critical-security only: propose enabling Dependabot auto-merge or approval; nothing executed yet

HUMAN_APPROVAL_GATE (interrupt): pause via interrupt(); surface the full plan (every write, per-PR) for human review; resume accepts edits/approve-subset/reject — REQUIRED before any side effect; covers all irreversible/publish actions

execute_approved_actions: on resume, perform ONLY approved mutations through GitHub/Slack tools with idempotency guards (skip already-labeled/already-requested); auto-merge enable and approvals execute here only if explicitly approved
persist_and_report: write run summary + decisions to the Platform store/checkpointer for audit and next-run dedupe; emit final triage report; END
impact: Directly attacks all four stated pain points without touching the working Dependabot config or the merge gate. (1) 10-PR-cap bottleneck: grouping + ranked digest + auto-detect of capped ecosystems lets reviewers clear routine batches fast so the queue drains and the cap stops blocking new updates. (2) Weekly-cadence security gap: webhook-driven critical sweeps + a daily 07:00 PST critical-only run mean high/critical CVEs get surfaced (and, if green-gated, proposed for auto-merge) within hours instead of up to 7 days. (3) Review fatigue: one prioritized evidence-backed digest replaces dozens of individual PR notifications. (4) Version conflicts: not auto-resolved, but flagged early with Claude-summarized breaking-change risk so conflicts surface before merge. Net: faster MTTR on dependency CVEs across stratus + inheriting repos, lower reviewer load, full audit trail. Risk is contained because every write is approval-gated and the agent is additive (Dependabot keeps opening PRs; the gate keeps blocking unsafe merges). automatable_score=6 reflects that final merge stays human-gated — the agent prepares and proposes, humans decide.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS, Postgres checkpointer + store auto-provisioned — do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= to .compile())
LangGraph Cron (scheduled assistants: Mon 06:30 PST + daily 07:00 PST critical sweep)
LangGraph Platform webhooks/HTTP endpoint (GitHub repository_dispatch + Dependabot-alert events)

GitHub REST/GraphQL API via a GitHub App (list dependency PRs, read status-check rollup, read Dependabot/security alerts, write labels, request reviewers, enable auto-merge, approve) — least-privilege App token
GitHub Security Advisories / Dependabot Alerts API (severity + fixed-version enrichment)
Anthropic Claude (claude-opus / sonnet) via ANTHROPIC_API_KEY — severity reasoning, breaking-change summarization, digest authoring ONLY (no training/distillation)
Slack (slack_send_message_draft / slack_send_message) for the grouped digest — send is approval-gated
CODEOWNERS routing map (StratusCloudLabs/stratus/.github/CODEOWNERS: terraform->devops-team, security/scan workflows->security-team) consumed as static config
langgraph.json deployment config (graphs: dependabot_triage -> ./agent/graph.py:graph; image_distro wolfi; python_version 3.12; env injected via Platform UI, never committed)
LangGraph SDK Client + interrupt()/Command(resume=...) for the human-in-the-loop approval gate
secrets_needed: GITHUB_APP_ID / GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY (or GITHUB_TOKEN for a least-privilege GitHub App installation token — read PRs/alerts/checks, write labels/reviews/auto-merge)
GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET (verify inbound Dependabot-alert / PR webhook signatures)
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (Claude calls for classification + digest authoring)
SLACK_BOT_TOKEN (post grouped digest to the review channel)
approval_gates: Single mandatory human-in-the-loop interrupt() at the HUMAN_APPROVAL_GATE node, after planning and before ANY side effect. The pause surfaces the complete per-PR action plan: labels to apply, CODEOWNERS reviewers to request, the drafted Slack/GitHub digest text, and any proposed Dependabot auto-merge-enable or PR approval (only ever proposed for green-gated critical/high-severity security PRs whose required gate checks — PR Governance, Secret Scan, Terraform validation, repo catalog validation, Checkov, IaC scan, CodeQL — are ALL passing). Human resumes with Command(resume=...) to approve-all, approve-a-subset (with edits to the digest), or reject. Nothing is written on reject. All read-only enrichment (advisory lookup, gate-status read, diff read, classification) runs unattended before the gate. The agent NEVER merges on its own and NEVER bypasses the existing branch-protection gate — at most it proposes enabling Dependabot auto-merge, which still requires all gate checks to pass before GitHub merges. Idempotency guards on execute prevent duplicate labels/review-requests on re-runs. No model training/fine-tuning/distillation anywhere (Anthropic Consumer Terms / workspace AGENTS.md guardrail). Secrets only via Platform-injected env, never hardcoded in langgraph.json or graph code.
deploy_notes: Deploy via `langgraph deploy` (CLI) or LangSmith Deployments UI pointing at a repo whose root has langgraph.json: {"dependencies":["."],"graphs":{"dependabot_triage":"./agent/graph.py:graph"},"env":".env","python_version":"3.12","image_distro":"wolfi","http":{"app":"./app.py:app"}}. graph = StateGraph(...).compile() with NO checkpointer=/store= (Platform injects Postgres). After deploy, register two Cron schedules (Mon 06:30 PST, daily 07:00 PST critical-only) and wire the GitHub App webhook to the deployment /webhooks endpoint with GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET. Inject GITHUB_APP_* , ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, SLACK_BOT_TOKEN, LANGSMITH_API_KEY via the Platform secrets UI (never in .env committed). v1 scope: start read-only-with-approval on StratusCloudLabs/stratus npm+terraform+github-actions+python only; expand to EscalateLabs/marketing, Scheduler-Systems repos, ProjectMasterLabs/pilotlight once the digest format and severity classification are validated against a few real Dependabot cycles. Key files referenced: /Users/scheduler-systems/Documents/scheduler-systems-ltd/StratusCloudLabs/stratus/.github/dependabot.yml (source of ecosystem list + 10-PR cap + cadence), /Users/scheduler-systems/Documents/scheduler-systems-ltd/StratusCloudLabs/stratus/.github/CODEOWNERS (reviewer routing), /Users/scheduler-systems/Documents/scheduler-systems-ltd/StratusCloudLabs/stratus/.github/labels.yaml (kind/security, kind/dependencies, security labels), /Users/scheduler-systems/Documents/scheduler-systems-ltd/StratusCloudLabs/stratus/.github/workflows/gate.yml (confirms the real gate is the named-check set, not a single Gate job). Note for the human: the LangGraph Platform reference in the prompt was truncated at the deploy-flow section — confirm exact `langgraph deploy` flags/region (api.smith vs eu.api.smith) against current LangSmith Deployments docs before shipping.

process: Release Notes & Version Management Automation — a single LangGraph Platform (managed/Cloud SaaS) agent that centralizes the inconsistent per-repo release logic across gal-app, scheduler-api, scheduler-web, EscalateLabs/marketing, and pilotlight. For a given target repo + ref/channel, the agent: (1) collects the commit range since the last release (GitHub Compare API), (2) classifies commits (conventional-commit-aware, with an LLM fallback for non-conventional history) into feat/fix/breaking/chore, (3) computes a semver bump proposal (or resolves the gal-app channel scheme dev/preview/stable from release_configurations.json), (4) drafts structured, human-readable release notes grouped by category with a Slack-ready summary, then (5) HALTS at a human-in-the-loop interrupt presenting the proposed version + notes + asset plan. Only after explicit approval does it perform irreversible actions: create the git tag, create/publish the GitHub Release, and trigger the repo-appropriate downstream publish (npm publish for scheduler-api, channel-versions repository_dispatch for gal-app, Vercel/Firebase release for scheduler-web, GCP image publish for marketing). The agent does NOT build/train any ML model; it orchestrates Claude for classification/summarization only. It replaces ad-hoc git-log changelogs and the cut_release-vs-release-please drift with one consistent, audited, approval-gated path.
trigger: Three trigger surfaces, all landing on the same managed deployment: (1) LangGraph Platform Cron — scheduled runs that mirror cut_new_releases.yml (nightly 08:00 UTC for gal-app dev channel; Wednesday for weekly preview/stable) to pre-stage a draft release for approval; (2) Webhook/API thread creation from a GitHub Actions step (release dispatch or a `release:requested` label) and from a Slack slash command `/cut-release <repo> <channel|ref>`; (3) Manual run from LangGraph Studio for testing. Every path creates a thread, runs to the interrupt, and waits — nothing is published until a human resumes the thread with approve/edit/reject.
graph_steps: ingest_request: normalize {repo, channel_or_ref, trigger_source} into State; load per-repo strategy from a release_strategies map (tag-semver | gal-channel | image-publish | npm). Validate repo is one of the 5 allowlisted; reject otherwise.

collect_commits (tool node): call GitHub Compare/Commits API for the range last_release..HEAD (or branch HEAD for gal-channel). Read-only. Populate State.commits + State.last_version. For gal-app, also read release_configurations.json to resolve channel metadata (is_prerelease, changelog_slack_channel, release_base_name).

classify_and_bump (LLM node, Claude): parse conventional-commit prefixes deterministically first; for non-conventional commits (scheduler-web, marketing history) fall back to Claude classification into feat/fix/breaking/chore/docs. Compute proposed semver bump (major/minor/patch) OR map to gal channel candidate tag. Detect breaking changes -> force major + flag.

draft_release_notes (LLM node, Claude): synthesize grouped Markdown release notes (Breaking / Features / Fixes / Maintenance) + a one-line Slack summary for the channel's changelog_slack_channel. Build the asset/publish plan describing exactly which downstream action will run (npm publish | repository_dispatch to channel-versions | GitHub release assets | GCP image | Vercel deploy).

human_approval_gate (interrupt() — HARD STOP): surface proposed_version + full release_notes + asset_plan + downstream side-effects. Wait for resume payload {decision: approve|edit|reject, edited_notes?, edited_version?}. On 'edit' loop back to draft_release_notes with overrides; on 'reject' route to END with no side effects.

apply_version_and_tag (tool node, GUARDED — only reachable post-approval): create the git tag/branch per strategy (gal uses create_release_tag_and_branch semantics; others use vX.Y.Z). Idempotency check: abort if tag already exists. Writes State.created_tag.

publish_release (tool node, GUARDED): create the GitHub Release (softprops-equivalent via API) with the approved notes, prerelease flag honored. Then dispatch the repo-appropriate downstream: scheduler-api -> rely on existing release.yml npm path (release published triggers it); gal-app -> repository_dispatch update-channel-versions; scheduler-web -> tag push triggers its release.yml; marketing -> trigger GCP image publish workflow_dispatch. Each call wrapped in try/except writing partial-failure state.

post_and_record (tool node): post the Slack summary to the channel's changelog_slack_channel, write an audit record (thread id, repo, version, approver, timestamp, commit range, downstream results) to the LangGraph Store / external audit sink, and emit final State for observability. Route to END.
impact: Removes the cut_release-vs-release-please inconsistency by centralizing the version-bump decision and release-notes synthesis in one audited place while leaving each repo's existing publish mechanics intact (low-risk overlay, not a rip-and-replace). Eliminates manual git-log changelog hand-editing (scheduler-web) and error-prone manual asset/version steps. Every irreversible publish becomes explicitly human-approved with a consistent diff-style preview, reducing bad/duplicate releases. automatable_score is 6/10: classification + notes drafting fully automate, but the publish step is deliberately gated and the 5 repos use 4 different downstream mechanisms, so the agent standardizes the decision layer rather than the plumbing. Revenue-relevant for scheduler-web/scheduler-api (live paying users) — safer, faster releases of the paid web app and the published API package.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS) — Postgres checkpointer + Store auto-provisioned (do NOT pass checkpointer/store to .compile())
LangGraph Cron (scheduled triggers mirroring cut_new_releases.yml cadence)
interrupt() human-in-the-loop for the approval gate; thread resume via API/Studio
GitHub REST API (Compare/Commits read; create tag, create Release, repository_dispatch, workflow_dispatch) via a fine-scoped PAT/GitHub App token
Claude API (Anthropic) for commit classification + release-notes summarization — orchestration only, no model training/distillation
Slack API (post changelog summary to per-channel changelog_slack_channel: #dev-beta-changelogs, #release)
npm registry (indirect — scheduler-api release.yml handles publish on release-published)
gal-run/channel-versions repo (repository_dispatch event update-channel-versions)
GCP Artifact Registry / Vercel / Firebase (indirect — triggered via each repo's existing publish workflow)
LangSmith tracing/observability for run audit
release_configurations.json (gal-app channel metadata source of truth)
secrets_needed: GITHUB_TOKEN (fine-grained GitHub App installation token: contents:write + actions:write on the 5 allowlisted repos only, plus contents:read on gal-run/channel-versions)
CHANNEL_VERSIONS_REPOSITORY_DISPATCH_TOKEN (repository_dispatch to gal-run/channel-versions, matching existing workflow secret)
RELEASE_APPROVERS (allowlist of identities permitted to resume the interrupt — enforced in the resume handler/custom Auth)
approval_gates: Single mandatory hard gate at node 5 (human_approval_gate) implemented with LangGraph interrupt(): graph execution pauses with the proposed version, full release notes, and an explicit side-effects/asset plan, and CANNOT proceed to apply_version_and_tag/publish_release/post_and_record without a resume payload carrying decision=approve from an identity in RELEASE_APPROVERS. decision=edit loops back to redraft (no side effects); decision=reject ends the thread cleanly. All irreversible nodes (tag creation, GitHub Release publish, downstream dispatch, Slack post) are downstream of and unreachable before this gate, and each is additionally idempotency-guarded (abort if tag/release already exists). Conforms to: deploy target = LangGraph Cloud; LLM orchestration only (no model dev); every send/publish/irreversible action approval-gated; secrets via env only.
deploy_notes: v1 deployment: repo root contains langgraph.json -> {"dependencies":["."],"graphs":{"release_conductor":"./agent/graph.py:graph"},"env":".env","python_version":"3.12","image_distro":"wolfi","pip_installer":"uv"}. graph.py builds a StateGraph(State) with State carrying {repo, strategy, channel_meta, commits, last_version, proposed_version, release_notes, slack_summary, asset_plan, decision, created_tag, downstream_results, audit}; nodes per graph_steps; conditional edge after human_approval_gate routing approve->apply_version_and_tag, edit->draft_release_notes, reject->END; compile WITHOUT checkpointer/store (managed platform injects Postgres). Secrets injected via the Deployments UI/API, never committed (.env only references names). Add a Cron for nightly gal-app dev + Wednesday weekly. Custom Auth (auth.py) enforces approver allowlist on resume. v1 SCOPE: support strategies tag-semver (scheduler-web), npm-on-release (scheduler-api), gal-channel (gal-app) first; image-publish (marketing) and pilotlight as a follow-up since marketing has no version concept. Roll out in dry-run mode first (collect+classify+draft+interrupt, downstream calls stubbed/logged) to validate notes quality against the existing scheduler-web git-log output before enabling real publish. Tie work to a tracked GitHub issue per workspace policy; do all edits in a dedicated worktree, not a live checkout.

process: Monitoring & Alerting for CI/CD Pipeline Health — a deployable LangGraph Platform (managed "LangSmith Deployments" / Cloud SaaS) agent that runs on a cron, fans out to collect CI/CD + synthetic-probe + status-page signals across StratusCloudLabs/stratus, Scheduler-Systems repos, and EscalateLabs/marketing, classifies each anomaly into the existing severity/route model (sev2→operations-paging, sev3→operations-nonpaging/engineering-nonpaging), drafts a consolidated health digest + per-incident actions, and then HALTS at a human-in-the-loop interrupt before any irreversible action (paging, Slack post, GitHub issue/comment, status-page update). It is an orchestration agent that calls Claude purely for triage/summarization/dedup — it does NOT train, fine-tune, distill, or evaluate any ML model.
trigger: Primary: LangGraph Platform scheduled cron (every 5 min, mirroring status-alert-routing.yml's `*/5 * * * *`) creating a background run on the deployed assistant. Secondary: webhook/repository_dispatch from existing GitHub Actions (status-alert-routing.yml, indexing-health.yml, tailscale-health-check.yml, internet-connectivity-monitor.yml) POSTing a probe/workflow snapshot to the agent's custom `http` route for event-driven escalation. Manual: `langgraph` SDK/Studio run with `mode=simulate` + a `simulation_scenario` (major_outage | sustained_degraded | provider_auth | misconfiguration | operational) reusing the catalog's existing scenarios for dry-run testing.
graph_steps: collect_signals: fan-out fetch (parallel branch) of (a) GitHub Actions run conclusions/queue health via gh API for the 3 target orgs/repos, (b) synthetic probe + status-catalog snapshot from StratusCloudLabs/stratus scripts/status/status-alert-catalog.json + run-synthetic-probes output, (c) Sentry recent issues via Sentry MCP, (d) prior open health issues for dedup. Writes raw signals into state; no model call.

normalize_and_dedup: deterministic Python merge of signals into a canonical incident list keyed by component (reusing catalog component ids like growth-console.gal-dispatch); collapse duplicates against persisted prior-run state from the Postgres checkpointer/store so a flapping probe isn't re-alerted.

classify_and_route (Claude): for each candidate incident, ask Claude to assign severity + route using the EXISTING rules as ground truth — sev2/operations-paging/paging=true for sustained customer-impacting outage, sev3/operations-nonpaging for sustained degraded, sev3/engineering-nonpaging for provider-auth/misconfiguration — plus a human-readable cause summary and suggested owner. Claude is constrained to the catalog's ownershipMappings; it explains, it does not invent routes.

compute_coverage_gaps: deterministic check for which target repos lack any health workflow (the stated pain point: Scheduler-Systems repos + thin marketing coverage); emits 'coverage-gap' advisory items (non-paging) into the digest.

draft_actions: Claude composes the consolidated digest (overall status, per-incident severity/route/owner/runbook link) AND the concrete side-effect payloads — Slack message text, GitHub issue/comment bodies, status-page (status.yml) patch, and paging payload — but ONLY as drafts in state.

approval_gate (interrupt): call interrupt() to pause the run. The digest + every proposed irreversible action is surfaced to a human (Studio inbox / Slack approval shim / SDK resume). Sev2/paging actions ALWAYS require approval; sev3 issue-creation requires approval; pure read-only digest delivery to a private channel can be auto-approved via config flag but defaults OFF.

execute_approved: on Command(resume=...) only execute the human-approved subset — page via webhook, post Slack via Slack MCP, create/comment GitHub issue via gh, open PR to status catalog. Each action is idempotent (keyed by incident id) and writes an audit record (mirrors catalog auditEvidence) to the store.
persist_and_close: write this run's incident fingerprints + actions taken to the long-term store for next-run dedup/auto-resolve detection, emit run summary to LangSmith traces, END.
impact: Closes the four named pain points without rebuilding the working Stratus machinery: gives one cross-org pipeline-health view (Stratus + Scheduler-Systems + marketing), makes alert routing explicit and reviewable, flags repos with zero health coverage, and adds a human-gated escalation layer on top of the existing deterministic router. Revenue tie-in: faster detection of api.gal.run / status.scheduler-systems.com / Scheduler-Systems CI breakage protects the live products and shortens MTTR. Low blast radius because every irreversible action is approval-gated and the deterministic GitHub Actions router keeps working underneath — the agent augments, it does not replace, so a bad LLM classification cannot silently page or mutate prod. Reuses existing simulate scenarios for safe validation before go-live.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS, api.smith.langchain.com) — Postgres checkpointer + store auto-provisioned
Anthropic Claude (Opus/Sonnet via langchain-anthropic) for triage/summarization/dedup classification ONLY — no model training
GitHub API via gh CLI / REST (workflow run conclusions, queue health, issue create/comment, status catalog PR) across StratusCloudLabs/stratus, Scheduler-Systems/*, EscalateLabs/marketing

Existing Stratus status scripts as data source: scripts/status/run-synthetic-probes.mjs, refresh-status-catalog.mjs, evaluate-alert-routing.mjs, status-alert-catalog.json, docs/organization/alerts.yaml
Slack MCP (slack_send_message / slack_send_message_draft) for digest + approval-shim delivery
Sentry MCP (search_issues / search_events) reusing the repo's existing sentry-github-bridge integration
Status page surface (status.yml / status.scheduler-systems.com catalog) updated via approval-gated PR
PagerDuty/webhook paging endpoint (only for sev2 operations-paging, post-approval)
langgraph CLI for deploy + LangGraph SDK / Studio for runs, simulate mode, and interrupt resume
secrets_needed: GITHUB_TOKEN (fine-grained, read workflows/issues across the 3 orgs + write issues/PR to status catalog)
APPROVAL_WEBHOOK_SECRET (HMAC for the http resume endpoint)
approval_gates: Single hard human-in-the-loop interrupt() before the execute_approved node — nothing irreversible runs without an explicit human Command(resume). Tiered policy: (1) sev2 / paging=true → ALWAYS requires approval, no auto-path ever; (2) sev3 GitHub issue/comment creation and any status-page (status.yml) update → requires approval; (3) read-only digest delivery to a private ops Slack channel → may be auto-approved only if config flag autoApproveDigest=true (defaults OFF). simulate mode short-circuits BEFORE the gate and never sends. All approvals + executed actions are written to an audit record in the store (mirrors the catalog's auditEvidence) for traceability. No secret is ever placed in state or logs; all credentials injected via LangGraph Platform env, never hardcoded (per workspace security rule).
deploy_notes: langgraph.json at repo root: graphs {"sentinel":"./sentinel/agent.py:graph"}, python_version 3.12, image_distro wolfi, http.app mounting a FastAPI route for the GitHub webhook + HMAC-verified approval-resume endpoint, store.index enabled for semantic incident-dedup, checkpointer.ttl set so resolved-incident state ages out. CRITICAL: compile graph with builder.compile() and DO NOT pass checkpointer=/store= — the managed platform injects production Postgres automatically; an InMemorySaver would be silently overridden. Deploy via `langgraph deploy` (CLI) or GitHub-connected build; inject all secrets through the Deployments UI/API, not .env. Schedule the */5 cron as a LangGraph Platform Cron creating background runs on the 'sentinel' assistant; keep the existing status-alert-routing.yml deterministic router live in parallel (the agent reads its outputs, does not replace it) for at least one bake-in period. Validate end-to-end with mode=simulate across all 5 catalog scenarios (major_outage, sustained_degraded, provider_auth, misconfiguration, operational) and confirm the interrupt fires and NO send occurs before flipping any auto-approve flag. v1 scope deliberately excludes auto-remediation and any change to the gal-model governance path (Anthropic-terms boundary).

trigger:
Event-driven webhook ingestion (primary) plus a scheduled backlog sweep. Concretely, three triggers all land on the SAME compiled graph (one assistant, addressed via langgraph.json "graphs": {"triage": "./agent/graph.py:graph"}):
1. Jira webhook -> POST to the deployment's /runs (or /threads/{id}/runs) when an SMR issue is created or transitioned to "Needs Triage". Thread keyed by jira_issue_key for idempotency/resumability.
2. GitHub webhook -> issues.opened / labeled "defect" -> same /runs endpoint, thread keyed by repo#issue_number.
3. Scheduled backlog sweep -> LangGraph Platform Cron (assistants/{id}/crons, e.g. */30 min) that lists untriaged items across BOTH trackers and fans out one run per stale item. This is the concrete answer to the "no triage SLA / backlog dashboard" pain point: every run stamps an SLA deadline and the cron re-surfaces breaches.
Input payload schema (graph input_schema): {source: "jira"|"github", issue_ref, raw_title, raw_body, reporter, environment_hint, labels, attachments[]}. The webhook receiver is a thin custom HTTP route mounted via langgraph.json "http": {"app": "./app.py:app"} that normalizes Jira/GitHub payloads into this schema then kicks off a run — keeping tracker-specific parsing out of the graph nodes.
process:
Bug Triage & Defect Categorization (intake, severity classification SEV1-3, impact analysis, and routing between Jira SMR and GitHub issues). v1 automates the deterministic, low-risk parts of the existing bug-resolver-orchestrator's "Initial Ticket Analysis" phase (fetch, parse, classify, propose routing) and STOPS before any write — it does NOT attempt fix implementation, sub-task creation, or merging. It closes the four documented gaps: (a) defect severity matrix derived from but distinct from docs/incident-response.md SEV1-3, (b) an explicit triage-completion SLA stamped per item, (c) a single canonical routing decision reconciling the Jira-SMR/GitHub-issues split, (d) structured triage records that feed a backlog/dashboard.

LLM usage is strictly orchestration/classification (Claude reads the defect text and emits structured severity+routing JSON). NO model training/distillation. Every state-changing or externally-visible action (Jira transition, Jira comment, sub-task create, GitHub label/comment, Slack post to #incidents) is gated behind a single human-in-the-loop interrupt() node; the graph pauses with a complete proposed action set and only the approved subset is executed on resume. Secrets (JIRA_API_TOKEN, GITHUB_TOKEN, SLACK_BOT_TOKEN, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) are injected via the platform's env/secrets store and read with os.environ — never committed to langgraph.json or .env in the repo.
graph_steps: ingest_normalize: read normalized {source, issue_ref, raw_title, raw_body, environment_hint, labels} from input_schema; resolve thread by issue_ref for idempotency (re-runs update the same triage record, never duplicate). State seeded with intake timestamp.

enrich_fetch (tool node, READ-ONLY): pull full context from the source tracker — Jira REST issue.get (mirrors orchestrator's `jira issue view SMR-XXX`) or GitHub issues.get — plus linked PRs, prior duplicates via search, and reporter/environment. Read-only API scopes only; no writes here. On API failure, fall through to LLM-with-raw-text and flag low_confidence.

classify_severity (LLM node, Claude): map the defect to a DEFECT severity matrix (SEV1=data loss/security/total-feature-outage in prod; SEV2=major feature broken or >25% users, workaround poor; SEV3=minor/cosmetic/workaround exists) explicitly derived from docs/incident-response.md but adapted for defects vs live incidents. Also classify environment (prod/staging/dev — prod auto-bumps floor), affected platform (web/iOS/Android/api per native-repo split), and a dup-likelihood score. Output is a strict JSON schema (Pydantic structured output), with a confidence field.

impact_route (LLM+rules node): combine severity x environment x platform into (1) a canonical routing decision — owning team/repo, and crucially whether the canonical tracker is Jira-SMR or a GitHub issue (resolving the documented inconsistency with a deterministic rule: customer/product defects -> SMR; infra/CI/repo-internal defects -> GitHub), and (2) an SLA deadline stamped from a severity->SLA table (e.g. SEV1 triage-ack 15m, SEV2 1h, SEV3 1 business day). Assembles a proposed_actions list (transition, comment text, labels, optional Slack alert for SEV1/2).

human_gate (interrupt() — MANDATORY APPROVAL): pause the run and surface the full triage proposal: computed severity+rationale, environment, canonical tracker+team, SLA deadline, and the exact list of write actions with their payloads. Reviewer resumes with Command(resume=...) approving all / a subset / edits / reject. NOTHING external has happened before this point. This is the only gate and it is non-bypassable for any write.

execute_approved (tool node, WRITE — only runs post-approval): perform ONLY the approved actions using least-privilege write tokens — Jira transition + comment + (optional) sub-task create, or GitHub label + comment, and Slack post for SEV1/2. Each action is idempotent (guard by issue_ref + action key) and individually try/caught so one failure doesn't abort the rest; failures are recorded, not retried blindly.

persist_record (store node): write the canonical triage record (severity, env, tracker, team, SLA deadline, approver, executed actions, timestamps, breach flag) to the platform BaseStore / triage table. This is the backlog/dashboard data source and the input the scheduled cron re-reads to detect SLA breaches. END.
tools_integrations: Jira Cloud REST API (read: issue.get/search; write: transition, comment, sub-task create) — mirrors bug-resolver-orchestrator's documented jira workflows but split into read-only enrich vs gated write
GitHub REST API / Octokit (read: issues.get, search issues/PRs; write: add label, comment) for the GitHub-issues defect track
Anthropic Claude API (severity classification + impact/routing reasoning ONLY — structured JSON output, no training/distillation)
Slack Web API (post SEV1/SEV2 triage alert to #incidents — approval-gated)
LangGraph Platform managed Postgres checkpointer (auto-injected; do NOT pass a custom checkpointer to .compile())
LangGraph Platform BaseStore (triage records / backlog feed) + Cron scheduler (SLA backlog sweep)
Custom Starlette/FastAPI HTTP route via langgraph.json http.app for Jira/GitHub webhook normalization
approval_gates: Single mandatory human-in-the-loop interrupt() at the `human_gate` node, positioned AFTER all read-only classification/routing and BEFORE any write. The run pauses with the complete proposal (severity + rationale, environment, canonical tracker + owning team, SLA deadline, and every proposed write action with its exact payload).
