Reviewer resumes via Command(resume={...}) to approve all, approve a subset, edit (e.g. override severity/tracker), or reject. Only the approved subset reaches `execute_approved`. Every externally-visible/irreversible action is behind this gate: Jira transition, Jira comment, Jira sub-task create, GitHub label, GitHub comment, Slack post. Read-only enrichment (Jira/GitHub fetch, dup search) needs no gate. Idempotency by issue_ref + action key prevents duplicate writes on resume/replay. State persists across the interrupt via the managed Postgres checkpointer, so approval can come minutes or hours later (fits the SLA model).
impact: Directly addresses all four documented pain points. (1) Defect SLA: every triaged item gets a stamped severity->SLA deadline and the cron sweep re-surfaces breaches — turning "no documented triage SLA" into an enforced, measurable one. (2) Routing consistency: a single deterministic canonical-tracker rule (SMR vs GitHub) ends the ad-hoc Jira/GitHub split. (3) Automated severity: Claude-driven SEV1-3 classification replaces manual judgment, with a defect-specific matrix the company currently lacks (only incidents have one). (4) Backlog dashboard: structured triage records in BaseStore become the data source for a public-facing triage-backlog view. Risk is contained because v1 is read-heavy and every write is human-approved — it accelerates the triage decision without ceding control. Automatable_score 6/10 is honored: the agent automates intake+classification+proposal (the deterministic 60%) and leaves the judgment-call commit to a human gate. Caveat: this is application/orchestration tooling (permitted), not ML-model work; the severity classifier uses the Claude API, it does not train or distill a model.
deploy_notes: Deploy to LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments / managed Cloud SaaS) via the `langgraph` CLI or GitHub-connected build. langgraph.json: {"dependencies":["."],"graphs":{"triage":"./agent/graph.py:graph"},"http":{"app":"./app.py:app"},"python_version":"3.12","image_distro":"wolfi","store":{"index":{"embed":"openai:text-embedding-3-small","dims":1536}},"env":".env"}. CRITICAL: graph = builder.compile() with NO checkpointer=/store= args — the platform injects production Postgres automatically; passing InMemorySaver is local-only and gets overridden. Secrets are set in the Deployment UI/API (never in langgraph.json or committed .env); read via os.environ. Webhooks: register the Jira automation webhook and a GitHub repo webhook (issues, label events) at the deployment's custom http.app route, verify HMAC with WEBHOOK_SHARED_SECRET, normalize, then call the runs API. Set up the SLA backlog Cron via the platform crons API against the `triage` assistant. Local dev with `langgraph dev`; gate API/UI access with LANGSMITH_API_KEY. Use least-privilege tokens: a read-mostly Jira/GitHub token for enrich, and separate write-scoped tokens only reachable from execute_approved. Resume the interrupt from LangGraph Studio or via the SDK Command(resume=...). Reference files used to ground this spec: /Users/scheduler-systems/Documents/scheduler-systems-ltd/.claude/agents/bug-resolver-orchestrator.md (Jira workflow surface) and /Users/scheduler-systems/Documents/scheduler-systems-ltd/docs/incident-response.md (SEV1-3 definitions the defect matrix derives from). Note: the orchestrator is Flutter-era; v1 routing targets the current native repos (scheduler-ios/android/web/api).

trigger: GitHub webhook → LangGraph Platform webhook endpoint. Primary: pull_request (opened/synchronize/reopened/ready_for_review) on Scheduler-Systems/Scheduler. Secondary: workflow_run completed for "Patrol E2E Tests (Unified)" and "Code Analysis" (so the agent reacts to actual CI results, not just diffs), plus push to main (records the full-suite baseline for trending). A nightly LangGraph cron Assistant run fires the cross-environment regression matrix (dev/stg/prod) and the weekly trend digest. All triggers carry the repo, PR number, head SHA, and changed-file list in the run input; secrets (GITHUB_TOKEN, webhook signing secret, LangSmith API key, optional Slack token) are injected via the platform env, never in langgraph.json.
process: Regression Testing (QA) — see graph for the deployable design.
graph_steps: ingest_event (entry node): validate GitHub webhook signature already done at HTTP layer; normalize the trigger into State {repo, pr_number, head_sha, base_sha, event_type, changed_files[], ci_results?}. Branch on event_type via add_conditional_edges: PR/diff path, workflow_run path, or nightly-matrix path.

load_history (BaseStore read): fetch prior runs for this repo/branch from the long-term store (namespace ('regression', repo, branch)) — last full-suite baseline coverage %, per-suite pass rates, known-flaky test list. Provides the trend baseline and flaky filter for later steps.

classify_changes (Claude reasoning, read-only): given changed_files[] + repo test taxonomy (auth/calendar/billing suites, lib/** widgets, Angular src), Claude maps each changed path to the regression suite(s) that should cover it and the unit-test files expected by the 100% coverage rule. Pure mapping/summarization — emits {covered_changes[], uncovered_changes[], suites_to_run[]}.

resolve_suite_intent: deterministically mirror the repo's resolve-suite logic (smoke for PRs, full for main) and union it with suites_to_run[] from classify_changes, so a billing change forces the billing parity suite even on a smoke PR. Output: required test targets + whether existing CI already covered them.

evaluate_gate: compute coverage floor check (configurable MIN_REGRESSION_COVERAGE, default 80% line / preserve 100% unit on changed files) and the no-regression delta vs load_history baseline; combine with uncovered_changes[] and the latest ci_results (pass/fail/flaky from workflow_run). Produce a verdict: PASS, WARN (gaps but green), or BLOCK (coverage regression or required suite missing/red).

draft_artifacts (Claude, read-only): generate the human-facing deliverables — a PR comment (coverage delta table, per-suite status, mapped gaps, recommended tests to add), a proposed PR status-check conclusion, an optional SMR coverage-gap issue body, and the trend line for this SHA. Nothing is sent yet.

approval_gate (interrupt — HUMAN IN THE LOOP): call interrupt() with the full proposed payload set (comment text, label, status conclusion, issue draft, any workflow_dispatch). Reviewer returns approve / edit / reject per action. This is the single chokepoint for ALL outbound/irreversible effects; the graph pauses here using the platform's Postgres checkpointer.

execute_actions (post-approval, idempotent GitHub tool calls): for each approved action call the GitHub API tool — upsert PR comment (dedup by marker), set/update commit status check 'regression-sentinel', apply label (e.g. needs-regression-tests), create SMR issue, or trigger workflow_dispatch on flutter_web_e2e.yml for a missing required suite. Each call wrapped with retry + 'already-exists' tolerance so re-runs don't duplicate.

persist_trend (BaseStore write): append this run's record {sha, ts, coverage%, per-suite pass/fail, flaky[], gate verdict, actions taken} to the store with TTL; update the rolling baseline if event was a green push to main. Feeds load_history on the next run and the weekly digest.

nightly_matrix (separate cron-assistant subgraph): fan out the smoke suite intent across dev/stg/prod, collect divergence (env-specific failures), and route through the same draft_artifacts → approval_gate → execute_actions path to post a single cross-env matrix digest + Slack summary; END.
impact: Turns an undocumented, untracked regression process into an enforced, trended one without touching app code. Closes all four named pain points: (1) a real, enforced minimum coverage floor + no-regression-on-PR delta; (2) automatic changed-code → required-suite mapping that flags uncovered changes before merge (a billing/calendar/auth edit can no longer slip through on a smoke-only PR); (3) durable run history in the BaseStore yielding coverage/pass-rate/flaky trend lines and a weekly digest; (4) a nightly dev/stg/prod regression matrix that catches environment-specific regressions the current single-env CI misses. Reduces Patrol maintenance pain indirectly by pinpointing exactly which suites a change needs rather than re-running everything blindly, and by tracking flaky tests over time so they can be quarantined. Reviewer stays in control: every PR comment, status check, label, issue, and dispatched run is approved before it lands, so the agent augments the qa-engineer workflow rather than acting unilaterally. automatable_score 7 fits — high-value orchestration with a mandatory human gate on all consequential output.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS tier, Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore auto-provisioned)
GitHub REST/GraphQL API (PR comments, commit status checks, labels, issue creation, workflow_dispatch) via app/PAT token from env
GitHub webhooks (pull_request, workflow_run, push) into the platform webhook endpoint with signature verification

Existing CI: flutter_web_e2e.yml 'Patrol E2E Tests (Unified)' (Patrol 4.0 web+mobile, smoke/full resolve-suite) and code-analysis.yml — consumed read-only via workflow_run, optionally re-triggered via approved workflow_dispatch
Anthropic Claude via langchain-anthropic (reasoning/summarization/code-to-suite mapping ONLY — no training/distillation)
LangGraph BaseStore (semantic + key-value trend store) with TTL sweep for run-history/trending
Slack (optional, approval-gated) for the weekly trend digest and nightly cross-env matrix summary
Jira/SMR issue creation (approval-gated) to route coverage gaps to humans / qa-engineer subagent
LangGraph cron Assistants for the nightly dev/stg/prod matrix and weekly digest
secrets_needed: GITHUB_TOKEN (or GitHub App private key + app id/installation id) — repo scope for comments, status checks, labels, issues, workflow_dispatch
GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET — HMAC signature verification at the http app layer
SLACK_BOT_TOKEN — optional, only for approved digest/matrix summaries
JIRA_API_TOKEN + JIRA_BASE_URL/cloudId — optional, only for approved SMR coverage-gap issue creation
approval_gates: Single mandatory human-in-the-loop chokepoint at the approval_gate node implemented with LangGraph interrupt() (graph pauses on the platform Postgres checkpointer until a Command(resume=...) decision arrives). EVERY outbound or irreversible effect routes through it: posting/updating a PR comment, setting the 'regression-sentinel' commit status check, applying a label, creating an SMR/Jira coverage-gap issue, triggering a workflow_dispatch CI run, and sending any Slack/digest message. The interrupt surfaces the exact rendered payload for each action; reviewer can approve, edit (re-draft and re-present), or reject (log + skip) per action. Read-only steps (ingest, history load, change classification, gate evaluation, draft) run without interruption; nothing leaves the agent or mutates GitHub/Jira/Slack before explicit approval. execute_actions is idempotent (dedup-by-marker, already-exists tolerant) so an approved resume cannot double-post on retry. No secrets are ever embedded in drafts or the graph config — all injected via platform env.
deploy_notes: Managed LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments) deploy. langgraph.json at repo root: {"dependencies":["."],"graphs":{"regression_sentinel":"./agent/graph.py:graph","nightly_matrix":"./agent/matrix.py:graph"},"env":".env","python_version":"3.12","image_distro":"wolfi","http":{"app":"./agent/app.py:app"},"store":{"index":{"embed":"openai:text-embedding-3-small","dims":1536},"ttl":{"default_ttl":131400,"sweep_interval_minutes":60}},"checkpointer":{"ttl":{"strategy":"delete","default_ttl":43200}},"pip_installer":"uv"}. Build the StateGraph in agent/graph.py, .compile() to a module-level `graph`, and do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= to compile() — the platform injects production Postgres + store automatically; InMemorySaver is local-dev only. Mount a custom FastAPI app (agent/app.py:app) for the GitHub webhook receiver that verifies GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET and creates a run on the right assistant. Inject all secrets via the platform UI/API (never in langgraph.json or committed .env). The interrupt-based approval means a human client (LangGraph SDK / Agent Inbox) must resume runs — wire the approver UI before going live; until then run in WARN-only mode (no execute_actions). v1 scope: PR + workflow_run paths and the trend store; cross-env nightly matrix and Slack digest are a fast-follow behind the same approval gate. Constraint compliance: Claude used purely for orchestration/summarization/mapping (NO model training, eval-data gen, or distillation), satisfying the workspace Anthropic-terms guardrail; all writes are approval-gated; secrets via env only.

process: Manual QA Passes — human-driven testing (exploratory, user-story validation, accessibility, cross-device manual verification) for the Scheduler app, including test environment setup, data prep, and execution logging. Today this is tribal knowledge: Marionette MCP drives Android/iOS interactive testing via local `make` targets, health-check playbooks 01-06 exist, but there is no centralized test-case repository, logging is inconsistent, environment-reset procedures are undocumented, and Marionette setup (VM URI extraction, device state clearing) has knowledge gaps. The agent is the orchestration + knowledge layer that turns a feature-area request into a runnable, prereq-checked, repeatable manual test pass and produces a consistent execution log — it does NOT itself tap devices (that runs on the engineer's local Mac).
trigger: Manual/API invocation by a QA engineer at the start of a manual test pass — via the LangGraph SDK/REST `runs` endpoint, the LangGraph Studio UI, or a thin wrapper (Slack slash command / `make qa-pass FEATURE=<area> PLATFORM=<android|ios|web>`). Inputs: feature area (e.g. auth, navigation, settings, billing/paywall, accessibility), target platform(s), and optional acceptance-criteria text or SMR ticket id. v1 is on-demand only — no cron. A thread_id makes a pass resumable across interrupts so the engineer can pause, run device steps locally, and resume by posting observations back into the same thread.
graph_steps: ingest_request: validate input (feature_area, platform(s), optional acceptance_criteria / SMR id). Normalize platform — note that Marionette is NOT valid for web (VM service extensions absent) and route web passes to a Playwright/Chrome-MCP checklist branch. Initialize TypedDict state with add_messages reducer; load reusable QA knowledge (playbooks 01-06, Makefile target map, troubleshooting matrix, registered FlutterFlow widgets) from the long-term BaseStore.

build_test_plan: Claude call (orchestration only) that derives a prioritized manual test-case checklist for the feature area — combining the relevant playbook(s), the user-story acceptance criteria, an accessibility sub-checklist (a11y compliance per docs/widget/accessibility), and a cross-device matrix. Output is structured (each case = id, title, steps, expected-result, priority). This directly fills the 'no test checklists per feature area' and 'no test-case repository' gaps.

assemble_env_prereqs: deterministic node that emits the exact, ordered environment-setup + data-prep + reset sequence as copy-pasteable Makefile commands — `make marionette-health PLATFORM=

`, `make emulators-start`/`emulators-status`, `make android-avd-start`/`ios-sim-start`, `make marionette-android|ios`, plus device-state-clear (`adb shell pm clear panuilov.shay.priority` / `xcrun simctl uninstall booted panuilov.shay.priority`) and the VM-URI extraction recipe (`grep -oE 'ws://[0-9.]+:[0-9]+/[^ ]*ws'`). Encodes the previously-undocumented reset + URI-extraction knowledge so it is consistent every run. Pulls test credentials from env/secrets, never hardcoded.

human_execute_interrupt: dynamic interrupt() (human-in-the-loop). The agent presents the plan + prereq commands and PAUSES. The QA engineer runs the device steps locally (Marionette MCP / make targets on their Mac — these CANNOT run in the managed container) and resumes the thread by posting per-case observations (pass/fail/blocked, notes, optional screenshot/log refs). This is the irreversible-action gate boundary: nothing is published before the human supplies real results.

compile_execution_log: Claude call that turns the engineer's raw observations into a consistent, structured execution log (summary counts, per-case result table, failures with suspected cause + repro command, environment/flavor/platform, timestamps, evidence links) — standardizing the inconsistent manual logging. Output stays in state only; nothing is sent yet.

review_and_route: deterministic node — if any case is fail/blocked, prepare a draft GitHub blocker-issue body (title, repro steps, env, linked playbook/SMR, suggested fix) and route to the publish gate; if all pass, route to the publish gate with just the run-log artifact. Classifies whether a publish/issue-create side effect is even needed.

approval_gate_interrupt: SECOND interrupt() before ANY irreversible/external action. Surfaces the exact artifacts to be written — (a) the run log to be appended to the central test-result store, and (b) any GitHub blocker issue(s) to be opened — and waits for explicit human approve/edit/reject. Reject ends cleanly; edit loops back; approve proceeds.

publish_outputs: only after approval — appends the run log to the centralized test-result store (the missing 'centralized test-case repository / tracking'), persists the reusable/updated checklist back into the BaseStore so future passes for this feature area start from it, and opens approved GitHub blocker issue(s) via the GitHub tool. All external writes are post-gate and idempotent (dedupe issues by feature+case signature).
finalize: write a short run summary to messages, persist run metadata + updated knowledge to the store for the next pass, END.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS) — Agent Server runtime
Anthropic Claude (claude-opus / sonnet) via langchain-anthropic — orchestration/reasoning ONLY, no model training/distillation
Auto-injected Postgres checkpointer (thread persistence / resumable interrupts) — do NOT pass a custom checkpointer to .compile()
Auto-injected BaseStore with semantic index (openai:text-embedding-3-small) — reusable per-feature test-case checklists + QA knowledge (playbooks, Makefile map, troubleshooting matrix)
GitHub API (gh / REST) — gated creation of blocker issues only
Centralized test-result store for run logs (start with a GitHub repo path or Postgres table; replaces ad-hoc test-results/*.md)
Slack (optional thin trigger + notify) via gated send

Local-only, NOT in the deployed graph (documented as the human-executed half): Marionette MCP (connect/get_interactive_elements/tap/enter_text/scroll_to/take_screenshots/get_logs), Makefile targets, adb/xcrun, Playwright/Chrome-MCP for web
approval_gates: Two human-in-the-loop interrupt() pauses. GATE 1 (human_execute_interrupt): mandatory pause before results exist — the agent never fabricates test outcomes; the engineer runs the real device steps locally and posts observations back to resume. GATE 2 (approval_gate_interrupt): mandatory pause before ANY irreversible/external write — surfaces the exact run-log to be persisted and the full text of any GitHub blocker issue(s) to be opened, and requires explicit approve/edit/reject. No publish, issue-create, or Slack send happens before Gate 2 approval (honors the workspace 'no send/no delete without approval' rules). Reject ends cleanly; edit loops back to compile/review. Both gates use LangGraph dynamic interrupts so a thread_id makes the whole pass resumable.
impact: Directly attacks the named pain points: (1) creates the missing centralized, versioned test-case checklist per feature area (persisted in the BaseStore + run-log store); (2) standardizes the inconsistent manual execution log into one structured format every run; (3) codifies the undocumented environment-reset + data-prep procedures and the Marionette knowledge gaps (VM URI extraction, device-state clearing) into deterministic copy-pasteable command sequences; (4) auto-prioritizes manual test cases per feature. Net effect: faster, more repeatable, lower-tribal-knowledge manual passes with consistent evidence, while the human still does the actual device verification (automatable_score=4 — orchestration assists, human stays in the loop). Low revenue urgency (P2) but protects release quality for the live paid Scheduler app, especially the billing/paywall and auth flows.
deploy_notes: Deploy via langgraph.json at repo root (managed LangGraph Platform / Cloud SaaS). Minimal config: {"dependencies":["."],"graphs":{"qa_pass":"./qa_agent/graph.py:graph"},"env":".env","python_version":"3.12","image_distro":"wolfi","store":{"index":{"embed":"openai:text-embedding-3-small","dims":1536},"ttl":{"default_ttl":43200,"sweep_interval_minutes":60}}}. Build the StateGraph (State = TypedDict with messages: Annotated[list, add_messages] + plan/env_prereqs/observations/log/draft_issue/decision fields), .compile() to a module-level `graph` var. CRITICAL managed-platform rules: do NOT pass your own checkpointer= or store= to .compile() — the platform injects production Postgres + store; passing InMemorySaver is local-only and ignored in the hosted runtime. Inject ALL secrets via the Deployments UI/API, never commit them (.env holds only local placeholders). Deploy with `langgraph deploy` (CLI) or the UI; target api.smith.langchain.com (or eu.). The two interrupt() calls require the auto-provisioned checkpointer — confirm thread_id is passed on every run so pauses resume. Hard constraint honored: graph calls Claude/LLMs for orchestration only — no model training/fine-tuning/distillation (Anthropic Consumer Terms + workspace AGENTS.md). Document clearly that the device-execution half (Marionette/make/adb/xcrun) runs on the engineer's local Mac, NOT in the container — the graph orchestrates and logs, the human executes. v1 = on-demand trigger; add a LangGraph cron only later if scheduled nightly passes are wanted.

process: Smoke Testing — rapid critical-path validation suite (login + core features) that runs post-deployment and pre-release to confirm a build is viable before deeper testing. Currently mature/automated (Patrol 4.x, FTL, smoke-on-PR / full-on-main), but the smoke-vs-full selection is hardcoded in .github/workflows/flutter_web_e2e.yml and there is no coverage-criteria, duration, or test-value telemetry.
trigger: Three entry points into the same LangGraph Cloud deployment: (1) Webhook from the Patrol E2E GitHub Actions run completion (workflow_run: flutter_web_e2e.yml) — the primary trigger; the agent ingests the run's artifacts/JUnit and posts a smoke verdict + coverage-gap analysis back to the PR/commit. (2) Cron-scheduled weekly audit (LangGraph Platform Crons / "assistant cron") that diffs the hardcoded smoke target lists against the actual integration_test/patrol/** tree to flag uncovered critical paths (calendar sync, scheduling, payment). (3) On-demand HTTP/Studio invocation by a QA engineer with a build/PR ref. No path triggers any send/merge/file-write without passing the human-in-the-loop interrupt gate.
graph_steps: ingest_run: normalize the trigger payload into RunContext {source: webhook|cron|manual, repo, commit_sha, pr_number, suite(smoke|full), workflow_run_id}. Pull the run's artifacts (patrol-web-test-results / patrol-android-test-results, test-results/*.log, JUnit/XML) via the GitHub API tool. Read-only.

parse_results: deterministic node (no LLM) — parse JUnit/FTL output into per-test {name, status, duration_ms, flaky_retry_used} records and an overall pass/fail. Persists to the LangGraph store keyed by commit_sha for trend/duration tracking (closes the 'no duration tracking' gap).

load_critical_path_map: read the canonical critical-path -> test-file mapping from a config doc in-repo (e.g. docs/operations/SMOKE_COVERAGE.md, a new artifact the agent proposes) plus the live integration_test/patrol/** listing. Deterministic.

coverage_diff: deterministic + LLM-assist — compare the suite that actually ran (hardcoded web_target/mobile_target lists from flutter_web_e2e.yml lines 147-157) against the critical-path map. Emit gaps: e.g. patrol/calendar/**, patrol/schedule/**, premium_access exist but are absent from both smoke and full target lists.

verdict_llm: Claude reasons over parsed results + coverage diff to produce (a) a build-viability verdict (GO / NO-GO / GO-WITH-GAPS) for deeper testing, (b) a ranked list of highest-value vs maintenance-overhead smoke tests using duration + historical flakiness + critical-path weight, (c) concrete recommended smoke-set additions. Orchestration/reasoning only — no model training.

route_actions: conditional edge. If source=webhook -> propose a PR status check comment. If a coverage gap or NO-GO -> propose a GitHub issue. If cron -> propose a weekly coverage report comment/issue. If manual -> just return the verdict object. Each proposed action is assembled as a draft payload, never executed yet.

human_gate (interrupt): use interrupt() to surface every draft side-effecting action (PR comment, GitHub issue create, status check, or a proposed edit to the hardcoded selection in flutter_web_e2e.yml / new SMOKE_COVERAGE.md). Execution pauses for human approve/edit/reject. This is the mandatory approval gate for all sends and any repo write.

execute_approved: after resume with approval, perform only the approved actions via the GitHub tool (post comment / open issue / open a draft PR with the de-hardcoded selection config). Rejected/edited items are applied per the human decision. Writes the action audit + verdict to the store.
persist_and_report: append the run's metrics (durations, flaky-retry count, gaps, verdict) to the store time-series and return the structured verdict as graph output for Studio/observability.
impact: Directly closes the four stated pain points without touching the (mature, working) test runners: (1) establishes a documented critical-path coverage map and continuously diffs it against what smoke actually runs, surfacing that calendar-sync/scheduling/payment Patrol suites exist but are excluded from the hardcoded smoke/full lists; (2) makes the smoke-selection logic auditable and proposes moving it out of the CI shell into a reviewed config; (3) adds per-test duration + flaky-retry tracking persisted across runs; (4) ranks tests by value vs maintenance overhead to guide pruning. Net effect: faster, more trustworthy GO/NO-GO gating for releases (revenue-protecting — Scheduler is the live paid app) while every change to test config or any external message stays human-approved. Low blast radius: read-only by default, all mutations gated.
tools_integrations: GitHub REST/Actions API (read workflow_run artifacts, JUnit/FTL logs, PR/commit context; gated writes: PR comments, issue creation, status checks, draft PRs) via a custom LangGraph tool using a GH App/PAT from env
Anthropic Claude (claude-opus / sonnet) as the LangGraph LLM for verdict + coverage reasoning — orchestration only, NO model training/eval/distillation
LangGraph Platform managed Postgres store + checkpointer (auto-provisioned) for duration/flakiness time-series and HITL checkpoint state
LangGraph Platform Crons for the weekly coverage audit
JUnit/XML + FTL results parser (deterministic node, no external service)
Google Cloud / Firebase Test Lab results (read-only, via the artifacts the existing workflow already uploads — agent does not invoke FTL itself)
secrets_needed: GITHUB_APP_ID / GITHUB_PRIVATE_KEY (or GITHUB_TOKEN) — scoped to Scheduler-Systems/Scheduler, contents:read, actions:read, pull_requests:write, issues:write
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY — for the Claude orchestration LLM
GH_WEBHOOK_SECRET — to verify workflow_run webhook signatures on the custom http app
OPENAI_API_KEY — only if store semantic index (text-embedding-3-small) is enabled for searching past verdicts; optional
approval_gates: Single mandatory human-in-the-loop interrupt() at the human_gate node before ANY side effect. Gated actions: posting PR/commit comments or status checks, creating GitHub issues, opening draft PRs (including the proposed de-hardcoding of smoke selection in flutter_web_e2e.yml or a new docs/operations/SMOKE_COVERAGE.md), and any repo write. The human may approve / edit / reject each drafted action; on reject nothing is sent. Read-only steps (ingest, parse, coverage_diff, verdict) run autonomously. Aligns with the no-send/no-write-without-approval workspace rules. Secrets only via env, never hardcoded.
deploy_notes: langgraph.json at repo root (or a dedicated qa-agents repo): {"dependencies":["."],"graphs":{"smoke_sentinel":"./agent/graph.py:graph"},"env":".env","python_version":"3.12","image_distro":"wolfi","http":{"app":"./agent/app.py:app"},"store":{"ttl":{"default_ttl":129600,"sweep_interval_minutes":120}},"auth":{"path":"./agent/auth.py:auth"}}. Build graph = StateGraph(RunContext)...compile() exported as module-level `graph`; do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= to .compile() — the managed platform injects production Postgres for both. Use interrupt() (langgraph.types) for human_gate, resumed via Command(resume=...) from the GitHub-side approver UI or LangGraph Studio. HITL persistence is automatic via the injected checkpointer. Custom http app mounts the /webhook/github route (signature-verified with GH_WEBHOOK_SECRET) that creates a run on the smoke_sentinel assistant; wire the GitHub workflow_run webhook to it. Register the weekly cron via the Platform Crons API against the smoke_sentinel assistant. Deploy via `langgraph` CLI to LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS, api.smith.langchain.com); inject all secrets through the Deployments UI/API, never in committed .env. Hard constraint honored: agent only calls Claude for orchestration/reasoning — it performs no model training, fine-tuning, eval, or distillation, consistent with the workspace Anthropic-terms guardrail; it also never invokes Patrol/FTL itself, only reads the existing pipeline's outputs.

area: QA — Flaky Test Management (Scheduler Flutter/Patrol E2E)
trigger:
Three triggers, all landing on the same LangGraph deployment (one graph, multiple addressable entry behaviors via input `mode`):
1. CI webhook (primary) — the Patrol E2E workflow (.github/workflows/flutter_web_e2e.yml) posts the test run summary (JUnit XML / FTL result JSON + per-attempt build logs) to the deployment's `/runs` API on every PR and main-push completion. This is the ingestion + detection path. Configured via langgraph.json `webhooks` + a thin GitHub Actions step that POSTs results (no model in the loop for ingestion — pure tool node).
2. Scheduled cron (LangGraph Platform managed cron / assistant schedule) — nightly "flake sweep" that recomputes flakiness scores over the rolling N-run history in the Store, opens/updates the registry, and proposes the daily triage digest. Use the platform's built-in cron-on-assistant rather than local launchd.
3. Manual (human-initiated, via LangGraph Studio or API `mode:"diagnose"`) — a QA engineer points the agent at a single named test that is suspected flaky for deep root-cause categorization + remediation playbook drafting.
NONE of these auto-write to the repo or post to humans without passing an interrupt gate.
process:
Replaces the current state (FTL `--num-flaky-test-attempts 1` blind retry + the CI bash `should_retry` network-grep + undocumented tribal knowledge of the 3 failure patterns) with a persistent, scored, human-gated flaky-test control loop.

What it does (grounded in the real signals found in the two data sources):
- INGEST every E2E run result (Patrol JUnit/HTML/JSON + FTL `--num-flaky-test-attempts` outcomes + the per-attempt build logs that the workflow's `should_retry` already greps). A test that passed only after FTL retry, or that has mixed pass/fail across recent runs at the same SHA, is the flake signal.
- DETECT flakiness from run HISTORY (the explicit pain point: "would need test run history analysis"). Compute a per-test flakiness score over a rolling window stored in the LangGraph BaseStore (Postgres-backed, provisioned by the platform — `store` config with TTL sweep). Flip rate, retry-rescued rate, and pass-after-fail-at-same-commit are the core metrics.
- CATEGORIZE root cause into the four buckets the process names (timing / state-pollution / environment / race), using LLM (Claude) reasoning over the failure logs mapped to the THREE documented Scheduler patterns: (a) emulator-lost-internet / Firebase-emulator-down → ENVIRONMENT; (b) "passes individually, fails in sequence" → STATE-POLLUTION; (c) `pumpAndSettle` timeout / network read-timeout (the strings the bash `should_retry` already matches: "Read timed out", "Connection timed out", "Temporary failure in name resolution") → TIMING/ENVIRONMENT. Race conditions inferred from non-deterministic ordering across attempts.
- MAINTAIN the missing centralized flaky-test registry in the Store (per-test record: score, category, first-seen, last-seen, quarantine status, owning area, linked GitHub issue). This is the "no centralized flaky test registry / no flakiness metrics" gap closed.
- REMEDIATE: draft (never auto-apply) a category-specific remediation playbook + a GitHub issue, and propose quarantine (tagging a test to run-but-not-block, or skip) — ALL behind human approval interrupts.

Orchestration only — calls Claude for classification/drafting/summarization. Does NOT train, fine-tune, distill, or build any ML model (the workspace Anthropic-terms guardrail). Flakiness scoring is deterministic arithmetic, not a learned model.
graph_steps: ingest_run (tool node, no LLM): parse incoming Patrol JUnit/HTML/JSON + FTL result + per-attempt build logs; normalize to {test_id, sha, branch, outcome, attempts_used, retry_rescued, duration, raw_log_excerpt}; idempotent upsert keyed on (test_id, run_id) into the run-history Store namespace. START -> here for mode:ingest/webhook.

compute_scores (tool node, deterministic): over the rolling window in Store, compute per-test flip_rate, retry_rescued_rate, pass_after_fail_same_sha; emit candidate flakes above threshold. Pure arithmetic, NO model. Used by both ingest and the nightly cron mode.

classify_root_cause (LLM node, Claude): for each candidate flake, read the failure log excerpts and map to {timing|state_pollution|environment|race} using the 3 documented Scheduler patterns + the should_retry network-string heuristics as priors; output category + confidence + evidence quotes. Skipped for tests already categorized with high confidence and no new evidence.

update_registry (tool node): upsert the per-test registry record in Store (score, category, status, first/last seen, owner-area). Read path for the dashboard/digest. No external side effects, so no gate.

route_actions (conditional edge): branch on mode and on whether any candidate crosses an ACTION threshold. ingest with no actionable change -> END (silent). nightly cron -> draft_digest. diagnose mode -> draft_remediation for the single target. New high-confidence flake crossing quarantine threshold -> draft_remediation.

draft_remediation (LLM node, Claude): generate the category-specific remediation playbook (e.g. state-pollution -> 'clear app data between files / clearPackageData', timing -> 'replace bare pumpAndSettle with bounded waitUntilVisible', environment -> 'emulator health precheck') + a draft GitHub issue body + a proposed quarantine action (tag/skip). Produces proposals only.

draft_digest (LLM node, Claude): summarize the nightly registry delta into a triage digest (top flakes by score, new entrants, category breakdown, trend) addressed to the QA channel/issue. Proposal only.

APPROVAL_GATE_publish (interrupt node — interrupt() / human-in-the-loop): pause and surface every irreversible/outbound action (create GitHub issue, comment on PR, post Slack/Intercom digest, apply quarantine label, open a quarantine PR) for explicit human approve/edit/reject. Resume only on human command. This is the single choke point for ALL side effects.

execute_approved (tool node): on resume with approval, perform ONLY the approved actions via GitHub/Slack tools (create_issue, add_label, comment, post_message). Re-checks the approval token from interrupt state; rejected/edited items are dropped or applied as edited. -> END.

checkpoint/recovery: every node persists to the platform-injected Postgres checkpointer (langgraph.json checkpointer.ttl); a webhook burst or crash resumes mid-graph without re-posting. compile() with NO checkpointer/store args (platform injects them).
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS tier) — Agent Server runtime

langgraph.json: graphs.warden -> ./agent/graph.py:graph; python_version 3.13; image_distro wolfi; pip_installer uv; store.index (embed openai:text-embedding-3-small, dims 1536) for semantic search over historical failure logs; store.ttl sweep; checkpointer.ttl; webhooks for CI ingestion; auth.path for API-key authn on the /runs endpoint
Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore — AUTO-PROVISIONED by the platform (must NOT pass checkpointer=/store= to .compile())
Anthropic Claude API (claude-opus / sonnet) — classification, remediation drafting, digest summarization ONLY (orchestration; no model training/distillation)
GitHub REST/GraphQL (via gh app token) — read PR/run context, create+label issues, comment on PRs, open quarantine PRs (all gated)
GitHub Actions step in flutter_web_e2e.yml — thin POST of Patrol JUnit/FTL result + per-attempt build logs to the deployment /runs webhook (the only repo-side change)
Firebase Test Lab result JSON / Patrol HTML+JSON reports / JUnit XML — input artifacts
Slack (or Intercom) — gated triage digest delivery
LangGraph managed cron (assistant schedule) — nightly flake sweep (preferred over local launchd, matches 'execute on cluster not local')
secrets_needed: LANGGRAPH_INGEST_API_KEY (validates inbound CI /runs POSTs via auth.path)
OPENAI_API_KEY (only if store.index semantic search over failure logs is enabled — otherwise omit)
approval_gates: ["SINGLE consolidated HIL interrupt node (APPROVAL_GATE_publish) before ANY irreversible/outbound action. Implemented with LangGraph's interrupt()/Command(resume=...) so it survives across the Postgres checkpointer and can be answered later via Studio or API.", "Gated actions: (1) create/update a GitHub issue for a flake; (2) comment on a PR; (3) apply a quarantine label or open a quarantine/skip PR (test behavior change = irreversible-ish, must be approved); (4) post the triage digest to Slack/Intercom.", "Auto-allowed WITHOUT a gate (idempotent, internal, reversible): ingest_run, compute_scores, classify_root_cause, update_registry (Store writes only). Reading run history/PR context is never gated.", "Human can approve / edit (e.g. tweak issue body, downgrade quarantine to issue-only) / reject per-item; execute_approved honors the per-item decision and re-validates the approval token from interrupt state before calling any external tool.", "Hard guardrail: the graph has NO code path from detection to an external write that bypasses APPROVAL_GATE_publish — route_actions can only reach execute_approved through the interrupt."]
deploy_notes:
Deploy path: `langgraph deploy` (CLI) from the repo containing langgraph.json, or wire LangSmith Deployments to the GitHub repo for build-on-push. Region: default api.smith.langchain.com (use eu. only if data-residency requires).

langgraph.json essentials: graphs={"warden":"./agent/graph.py:graph"}; do NOT commit real secrets in env — inject via the Deployments UI/API (the secret list above). Set auth.path to validate the inbound CI ingestion key. Enable webhooks for the /runs ingestion endpoint. Use the managed cron (assistant schedule) for the nightly sweep, NOT local launchd — keeps with the workspace rule that heavy/scheduled execution runs on cluster, Mac is conductor only.

graph.py: build StateGraph(State), compile() with NO checkpointer=/store= (platform injects Postgres checkpointer + Store). State carries: incoming_results, candidates, registry_delta, proposed_actions, approval_decisions.

Repo-side change (one file): add a final step to .github/workflows/flutter_web_e2e.yml that POSTs the Patrol JUnit/FTL JSON + the per-attempt build logs (already produced for should_retry) to the deployment /runs webhook with LANGGRAPH_INGEST_API_KEY from Actions secrets. Tie this to a GitHub issue first (workspace rule: always a documented issue before substantive work).

v1 scope cuts to keep it shippable: start ingestion+detection+registry+digest behind the gate; defer auto-quarantine PRs to v1.1 once score thresholds are tuned on real history. Flakiness thresholds (flip_rate / retry_rescued_rate / window size) are config, not learned — seed conservatively and tune from the nightly digest. Backfill the Store with the last ~30 days of CI run artifacts on first deploy so detection isn't cold-started.

Constraint compliance: orchestration calls Claude only (no model training/fine-tune/distill — satisfies the workspace Anthropic-terms guardrail and the gal-model boundary); all sends/issue-creation/quarantine are interrupt-gated; secrets only via platform env injection, none hardcoded.
impact: Closes the named pain points directly: (1) "no automated flaky test detection / needs run-history analysis" -> the ingest+compute_scores loop over a rolling history Store; (2) "no centralized flaky test registry / no flakiness metrics" -> the registry Store namespace + nightly digest; (3) "no root-cause categorization (timing vs state vs environment)" -> classify_root_cause mapping to the 4 buckets via the 3 documented Scheduler patterns; (4) "no documented remediation playbook" -> draft_remediation generates category-specific playbooks + issues; (5) "teams lack visibility into which tests are historically unreliable" -> the gated triage digest. automatable_score 5/10 reflects that ingestion+scoring+classification automate cleanly but quarantine/remediation stay human-gated by design, so this is a force-multiplier on a human QA engineer, not full autonomy. Revenue framing: protects the Scheduler live-revenue release pipeline by cutting flaky-test noise that blocks/erodes trust in the E2E gate (per the revenue-first lens), without touching app code.

process: Production Verification After Deploy — a LangGraph Platform (managed/Cloud SaaS) agent that runs as the post-deployment validation gate for Scheduler (priority-118da). After a production release (v* tag → release.yml deploys web/android/iOS in parallel, then the flutter_web_e2e smoke gate), the agent performs a single deterministic verification pass: (1) liveness/readiness via the safe status-page endpoints, (2) the machine-readable status contract + its declared synthetic-probe catalog (all read-only, non-mutating), (3) dependency-catalog health (firestore, firebase_auth, revenuecat, brevo, gemini, intercom, fcm), (4) the CI E2E web-smoke result, and (5) recent error-rate/monitoring signals. It aggregates these into a PASS / DEGRADED / FAIL verdict with a per-signal evidence table, then either records a clean verification or — on DEGRADED/FAIL — drafts a remediation proposal (status-page incident post, on-call page, and/or rollback to the previous good version) and HALTS at a human-in-the-loop interrupt. No status post, no page, and no rollback dispatch happens without explicit human approval through the interrupt. This closes the documented gaps: undocumented rollback triggers, missing smoke-failure SLA, no health dashboard beyond the status page, and risky un-gated reactions to prod failures. Claude is used only for orchestration/summarization/classification of probe results — never for model training, distillation, or any irreversible action.
trigger: Primary: GitHub Actions webhook from release.yml after deploy-web + e2e jobs complete (success OR failure) — posts the release tag, commit SHA, per-platform deploy results, and the E2E result to the LangGraph Platform run endpoint. Secondary: scheduled cron (every 15 min via a synthetic-monitoring heartbeat) to catch post-deploy drift and give the missing continuous health signal. Manual: workflow_dispatch / Agent Server /runs call with an explicit version + commit for ad-hoc re-verification. The trigger payload always carries {version, commit_sha, deploy_results, e2e_result, triggered_by} so the graph is deterministic and replayable from the checkpointer.
graph_steps: ingest_release_event: parse trigger payload (version, commit_sha, deploy_results, e2e_result, source). Validate against expected schema; abort early to a no-op terminal state if not a production release. Seed run-scoped correlation id (reuse X-Request-Id semantics from the status contract).

fetch_status_contract: GET schedulerStatus (us-central1-priority-118da.cloudfunctions.net/schedulerStatus). Parse schemaVersion, status, readiness checks, dependency catalog, and the declared synthetic-probe catalog. This is the single source of truth for what to probe — the agent does NOT invent probes.

run_safe_probes (fan-out, read-only ONLY): execute exactly the catalog probes — GET schedulerHealthz (expect 200), GET schedulerReadyz (expect 200/503), GET schedulerStatus (expect 200/503), POST revenueCatWebhook with no Authorization + empty body (expect 401 auth-gate). Hard guardrail: refuse any probe that would create users, mutate schedules, change subscriptions, or send real email/push. Capture status code, latency, X-Request-Id per probe.

check_e2e_and_monitoring: read the CI E2E web-smoke result from the payload; pull recent error-rate / 5xx / dependency-error signals from the monitoring source (Sentry/Cloud Monitoring) for the deployed commit window. Map dependency errors to the status-contract dependency identifiers + their customer-impact strings.

classify_verdict (Claude-assisted): deterministic rules first (any required probe off-expected, E2E failure, readyz=503, or error-rate over threshold => FAIL; partial dependency degradation => DEGRADED; all green => PASS). Claude only summarizes the evidence table and writes the human-readable rationale + suggested customer-impact line. Produces {verdict, failing_signals[], suggested_action, evidence_table}.
branch_on_verdict: PASS -> record_verification (terminal). DEGRADED/FAIL -> draft_remediation.

draft_remediation: assemble the proposed irreversible actions as DRAFTS only — (a) status-page incident text scoped to the affected dependency/component (scheduler-api), (b) on-call page summary, (c) rollback plan = redeploy previous good tag (prior v* / last-known-good commit). Include SLA clock context (time since deploy, smoke-failure age).

APPROVAL_GATE (interrupt / human-in-the-loop): call interrupt() with the full evidence table + the exact draft actions and their blast radius. Execution is suspended on the Platform checkpointer until a human resumes with approve/edit/reject per action. Nothing irreversible runs before this returns.

execute_approved_actions: only for human-approved items — post status-page incident, trigger on-call page, and/or dispatch the rollback workflow (repository_dispatch / re-tag previous good version). Each call is idempotent and tagged with the correlation id. Rejected actions are logged and skipped.

record_verification (terminal): write the immutable verification record (verdict, evidence table, probe results, actions taken/skipped, approver, timestamps) to the long-term store and emit a compact summary back to the triggering GitHub run + the team channel. This record is the missing 'production health dashboard / rollback-trigger audit log'.
impact: Closes the highest-risk gaps in post-deploy QA: gives an explicit, documented rollback-trigger + smoke-failure response path with an audit trail (today there is none), an SLA clock on smoke/health failures, and a continuous synthetic-monitoring heartbeat beyond the static status page. Protects live RevenueCat-paying Scheduler users by catching a bad production release in minutes and routing to an approval-gated rollback instead of an unmonitored break. All probes are read-only against the existing safe status contract, so the agent itself never adds the 'tests run against live prod Firebase' risk. Reduces mean-time-to-detect/decide for production regressions while keeping a human firmly in control of every irreversible action.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS, Agent Server) — Postgres checkpointer + store auto-provisioned; do NOT pass own checkpointer/store to .compile()
HTTP probe client (httpx) against schedulerHealthz / schedulerReadyz / schedulerStatus / revenueCatWebhook auth-gate — read-only catalog probes only
GitHub Actions: inbound webhook from release.yml/flutter_web_e2e.yml as trigger; outbound repository_dispatch for approval-gated rollback (re-tag/redeploy previous good v*)
Anthropic Claude (via LangChain ChatAnthropic) — orchestration/summarization/classification ONLY; no model training/distillation
Status page (status.scheduler-systems.com + status.json) — read for current component state; write incident post ONLY after approval
Monitoring/error source (Sentry or Cloud Monitoring) — read recent 5xx/error-rate/dependency-error signals for the deployed commit window
Slack — post verification summary (read-only notification) and approval-request link to on-call channel
On-call paging (PagerDuty/Opsgenie) — page ONLY after human approval at the interrupt
LangGraph interrupt() / human-in-the-loop + Platform resume API for the approval gate
secrets_needed: LANGGRAPH_AUTH_SECRET (Agent Server custom auth for the webhook/resume endpoints)
approval_gates: Single mandatory human-in-the-loop interrupt() before ANY irreversible action. On a DEGRADED/FAIL verdict the graph drafts up to three actions — (1) status-page incident post, (2) on-call page, (3) rollback dispatch to the previous good version — and suspends on the Platform checkpointer. A human resumes per-action with approve / edit / reject; rejected and un-approved actions are skipped and logged. PASS verdicts and all read-only probes/notifications run without a gate. Rollback is never automatic. Secrets are injected via Platform env (never hardcoded); the rollback token is scoped to repository_dispatch on the one repo. The agent will refuse (hard guardrail) any probe that mutates production data, and performs no AI/ML model training, evaluation, or distillation — Claude is orchestration-only.
deploy_notes: Managed LangGraph Platform deploy. langgraph.json: {dependencies:["."], graphs:{"prod_verify":"./prod_verify/graph.py:graph"}, env:".env", python_version:"3.12", image_distro:"wolfi", auth:{path:"./auth.py:verify_webhook"}, store:{ttl:{default_ttl:43200}}, http:{app:"./app.py:app"} } at repo root (suggested new repo Scheduler-Systems/prod-verify-agent, or apps/prod-verify-agent). Build the StateGraph with a TypedDict state {version,commit_sha,deploy_results,e2e_result,status_contract,probe_results,monitoring,verdict,evidence_table,proposed_actions,approved_actions,correlation_id}; .compile() to module-level `graph` with NO custom checkpointer/store (Platform injects Postgres). Implement the gate with interrupt() so the run durably suspends and is resumable via the Platform resume API. Custom http app exposes POST /webhooks/github (HMAC-verified via GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET in auth.py) to start runs and POST /webhooks/resume for approval callbacks (e.g. from Slack interactive buttons). Cron heartbeat: register a Platform cron (every 15 min) hitting the same graph with source=heartbeat. Deploy via `langgraph deploy` CLI or the Deployments UI against api.smith.langchain.com; inject all secrets through the Platform env UI/API — none committed. Keep .compile() free of InMemorySaver. Verify the approval interrupt round-trips on a staged DEGRADED fixture before enabling the GitHub webhook on real releases.

process: Test Environment / Emulator Management — setup, configuration, lifecycle, parity, seeding, health checks, and resource cleanup of Firebase emulators (Firestore 8081 / Auth 9099 / Functions 5001 / Hosting 5003 / UI 4000 / Storage 9199), Android/iOS simulators, and CI Tart VMs for the Scheduler app (Firebase project priority-118da).
trigger:
Three entry points into the SAME compiled graph (LangGraph Platform addresses each as an assistant, with HITL interrupts on every irreversible step):
1. CRON (scheduled assistant run, LangGraph Platform cron) — daily 06:00 emulator + CI health/parity sweep and Tart-VM disk/zombie audit; report-only, never auto-mutates.
2. WEBHOOK / API (langgraph.json http app) — invoked by a GitHub Actions step or a developer ("seed local emulators", "validate parity", "propose Tart cleanup") via the deployment REST API; returns a thread with an interrupt the caller resolves.
3. ON-DEMAND chat thread — engineer asks in natural language ("why are my emulator tests hitting prod?", "reset+reseed emulators for SMR-242"); same graph, same interrupts.
NOTE: this agent ORCHESTRATES (reads state, diffs config, drafts commands, calls Claude to reason) and proposes; humans approve before any mutate/cleanup/seed/PR. No ML model training/distillation anywhere.
graph_steps: START -> classify_intent: LLM (Claude via langchain-anthropic) routes the incoming request to one of {health_sweep, parity_check, seed_request, emulator_lifecycle, tart_cleanup, qa_question} and writes the route + parsed args into State. Conditional edge fans out from here.

collect_state (read-only tools, runs for every route): gather ground truth in parallel — read firebase.json emulator ports, .firebaserc, docs/operations/ENVIRONMENTS.md & TESTING.md, probe each emulator health endpoint (GET :PORT plus :4000 UI), `make emulators-status`, `tart list --format json` on the bare-high-arm64 runner (via gh workflow dispatch readback / SSH-exec tool), and `gh run list` for the *-tart-vm.yml workflows. No writes. Persisted to the Postgres checkpointer.

parity_check: diff documented expectations vs observed reality. Hard-coded invariants from the repo: doc says Hosting 5000 but firebase.json says 5003 (DRIFT), TESTING.md promises a Staging project that does not exist (tests hit prod priority-118da). Emit a structured ParityReport (drifts, severity, suggested doc/config fix). Read-only; produces a finding, never edits.

health_sweep: aggregate emulator reachability + port-conflict detection (is something already bound to 8081/9099/5001/5003/9199?) + Tart disk pressure (~/.tart du -sh) + count of zombie cirrus-cli-* / stale `local` VMs from `tart list`. Classify each as OK / WARN / ACTION_NEEDED.

plan_action: LLM drafts the concrete, minimal remediation as an explicit command list bound to existing Makefile/CI surfaces ONLY (e.g. `make emulators-reset`, `make emulators-start`, `node scripts/test/seed/seed_test_fixtures.js`, `start_emulators.sh --update-data`, dispatch cleanup-tart-vm.yml with confirm_delete=DELETE). Computes a blast-radius summary: what data is lost (emulator reset wipes seed unless exported first), which VMs get deleted, whether prod priority-118da is touched (must be NEVER). Output = an ActionPlan object.

HUMAN_APPROVAL_GATE (interrupt() — the only path to any mutation): surface ActionPlan + blast-radius + diff to the human via interrupt payload. Resume value must be an explicit approve/reject/edit. Reject -> END with report. Edit -> loop back to plan_action. Approve -> proceed. Cron route is HARD-WIRED to skip this and route straight to report_and_persist (read-only by construction).

guarded_execute (only reachable post-approval): run ONLY the approved commands. Pre-flight re-asserts the prod-safety invariant (refuse if any command references priority-118da writes or `firebase deploy`). For destructive Tart cleanup, require the literal DELETE confirm token already present in the approved plan. For emulator reset, auto-export current emulator data to a timestamped backup dir FIRST (closes the 'reset loses test data' pain point) then reset+reseed. Capture stdout/exit codes into State.

verify_post: re-run the relevant read-only checks (health endpoints back up? seed row counts present? parity drift resolved? VM count reduced, disk reclaimed?). If verification fails, mark degraded and route to report.

report_and_persist: write a structured run report (ParityReport + actions taken + verify results) to the BaseStore (long-term, TTL per langgraph.json) keyed by date+route for trend tracking, post a summary back via the route's channel (cron -> Slack/issue draft requiring human send; webhook -> response body; chat -> message). For surfaced doc/config fixes (e.g. the 5000->5003 drift), draft a PR body but DO NOT push — leave as an approval-gated artifact. -> END
tools_integrations: langchain-anthropic ChatAnthropic (Claude) — intent classification, drift reasoning, plan drafting, NL Q&A (orchestration only, NO model training)
Filesystem/repo read tools — firebase.json, .firebaserc, docs/operations/{ENVIRONMENTS,TESTING}.md, scripts/emulators/*, scripts/test/seed/seed_test_fixtures.js
Shell-exec tool (sandboxed, allowlisted) — `make emulators-start|stop|status|reset`, `make android-avd-start`, `make ios-sim-start`, `make marionette-health`, emulator HTTP health probes
Firebase emulator REST/health endpoints — reachability + Firestore/Auth export-import for the pre-reset backup
GitHub via gh CLI / GitHub MCP — `gh run list/view`, `gh workflow run` for build-tart-vm.yml / provision-tart-vm.yml / cleanup-tart-vm.yml (confirm_delete=DELETE), draft PR for doc/config drift fixes
Tart CLI on bare-high-arm64 self-hosted runner (via dispatched workflow or SSH-exec) — `tart list --format json`, disk `du -sh ~/.tart`; stop/delete only through the approval-gated cleanup workflow
LangGraph Platform managed Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore (auto-injected; do NOT pass to .compile()) — thread state + long-term parity/health trend history
LangGraph Platform Cron — schedules the daily read-only sweep assistant
Slack/GitHub Issues (draft-only) — health/parity digest delivery, never auto-send
langgraph.json http app — webhook/API surface for CI + developer invocation
approval_gates: HITL is enforced via a single `interrupt()` node (HUMAN_APPROVAL_GATE) that every mutating path MUST traverse; the graph has NO edge from any read node to guarded_execute that bypasses it. Gated (irreversible) actions: emulator reset/reseed (data-loss — auto-backup first, then approve), `start_emulators.sh --update-data` (pulls from prod priority-118da), any Tart VM stop/delete (requires the literal `DELETE` confirm token inside the approved plan), dispatching build/provision/cleanup Tart workflows, opening/pushing any PR for doc/config drift fixes, and posting any Slack/issue message (draft-only, human sends). Hard invariants checked in guarded_execute pre-flight regardless of approval: REFUSE any command that writes to or `firebase deploy`s priority-118da, and REFUSE anything outside the Makefile/CI allowlist. The CRON route is structurally read-only — it cannot reach guarded_execute (compile-time conditional edge to report_and_persist), so scheduled runs never mutate. Resume contract: interrupt returns {decision: approve|reject|edit, edits?}; reject ends with report, edit loops to plan_action. Secrets (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GITHUB_TOKEN, FIREBASE_TOKEN, runner SSH key) come only from LangGraph Platform env injection — never hardcoded, never logged.
impact: Closes the named pain points without an app release: (1) automated emulator + CI health monitoring (none today) via the daily cron sweep; (2) detects and surfaces the real config/doc drift found in-repo (Hosting port docs 5000 vs firebase.json 5003) and the undocumented missing Staging project (tests hitting prod priority-118da) as the headline parity risk; (3) port-conflict detection (currently undocumented); (4) makes data seeding a first-class, repeatable, approval-gated step backed by scripts/test/seed + firebase-dev-data; (5) eliminates the 'emulator reset loses test data' gap by auto-exporting a timestamped backup before any reset; (6) brings the partially-documented Tart VM cleanup under a single auditable, confirm-gated path with disk/zombie-VM trend tracking. Net: faster, safer test envs and a documented prod-safety boundary, matching automatable_score 7. Aligns with the workspace 'orchestrate local, execute on cluster' rule — the agent is a conductor that dispatches CI workflows rather than spawning heavy local fleets.
deploy_notes: Managed LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments / Cloud SaaS tier), api.smith.langchain.com. langgraph.json: graphs={"qa-envguard":"./agent/graph.py:graph"}, dependencies=["."], python_version="3.12", image_distro="wolfi", env injected via UI (the six secrets above — never committed), http.app mounted for the webhook/API surface, store.ttl + checkpointer.ttl set for parity/health history retention, store.index for semantic recall of past drift reports. CRITICAL: compile the StateGraph WITHOUT a custom checkpointer/store — the platform injects managed Postgres; passing InMemorySaver is local-only and is ignored/overridden in the hosted runtime. interrupt() requires the durable checkpointer, so it only works correctly on the managed deployment (or local langgraph dev), not with an in-memory saver. Schedule the cron sweep via the Platform cron API against the read-only assistant config. The Tart/runner exec must target the existing bare-high-arm64 self-hosted runner — the agent dispatches the existing *-tart-vm.yml workflows rather than running tart locally. Scope check: this is application/infra orchestration calling Claude for reasoning only — it does NOT develop, train, fine-tune, or distill any model, so it is permitted under the workspace Anthropic-terms guardrail. Live revenue protection: the prod-safety invariant (no writes/deploys to priority-118da) is the single most important guard given priority-118da is the production project.

process: Content Review & Approval Workflow — generate, compliance-check, route through a typed human approval gate, and publish marketing content (social posts, emails, blogs, ads). Codifies the Growth Operating Contract rule that human review is mandatory before any social post is published or any outreach is sent, and adds the missing pieces the pain-points call out: an automated brand/tone/legal compliance check derived from /marketing/data/social-media/CONTENT-RULES.md, a documented SLA timer on the review gate, a documented per-content-type approval chain, and an explicit rejection/revision loop.
trigger: Primary: HTTP/webhook to the LangGraph Platform run API (POST a new content request with {content_type, topic/brief, target_channel, source_draft_path}). Real submissions on the managed platform come in three ways — (1) a Growth Console "Submit for review" button hitting the deployment's run endpoint, (2) a scheduled LangGraph Platform cron that picks up new drafts dropped into /EscalateLabs/marketing/data/social-media/ and enqueues one run per draft, and (3) manual kickoff from LangGraph Studio. The agent is one assistant; the content_type field selects the approval chain at runtime.
graph_steps: ingest_request (entry node): validate the incoming payload against the State schema, classify content_type into {social, email, blog, ad}, load the matching source draft (if source_draft_path given) from the marketing repo, and stamp a request_id + created_at for SLA tracking. Routes to load_context.

load_context: read brand/compliance rules from CONTENT-RULES.md, content-pillars.md, and post-templates.md via the long-term BaseStore (seeded at deploy; semantic index on). Pull the current verified product-facts block (per-user pricing, real feature list) so the compliance node has ground truth. No writes.

generate_or_normalize_draft: if no human draft was supplied, call Claude (orchestration only — generation, NOT model training) to produce a draft from the brief + the loaded templates/pillars; if a draft WAS supplied, normalize it to the channel format and produce platform variations. Output: candidate_draft + rationale. This is the only LLM-generation node.

compliance_check: deterministic + LLM-assisted gate against CONTENT-RULES.md absolute rules — no fabricated stories, no unverified metrics (flag any number/MRR/user-count/time-saving without a cited data source), no false feature claims (diff claimed features vs verified product-facts), no contradictory positioning (per-user pricing), correct founder attribution (Shay Panuilov). Emits compliance_report with status pass|fail|warn and a list of violations with line refs.

conditional_edge route_after_compliance: if compliance status == fail -> auto_revise; if warn or pass -> human_review_gate. Caps auto-revision attempts at max_revisions (default 2) to avoid loops; on cap exceeded routes to human_review_gate with the warnings attached so a human decides.
auto_revise: feed compliance violations back into a Claude call to rewrite the offending claims, increment revision_count, then loop back to compliance_check. Pure orchestration.

human_review_gate (HITL interrupt — the load-bearing approval gate): use interrupt() to pause the run and surface {candidate_draft, compliance_report, content_type, target_channel, SLA_due_at} to the designated reviewer/publisher (Shay for social/outreach per the Operating Contract). The reviewer resumes with a Command containing one of approve | reject | request_changes(+notes). Nothing publishes while interrupted; the Postgres checkpointer holds state indefinitely so the SLA timer can run.
conditional_edge route_after_human: approve -> select_publisher; request_changes -> revise_with_notes; reject -> record_outcome (terminal, logged as rejected with reason).

revise_with_notes: apply the reviewer's free-text change notes via Claude, increment revision_count, then route back to compliance_check (so human-requested edits are re-screened before re-review). Documents the rejection/revision workflow the pain-points say is missing.

select_publisher (approval-chain dispatcher): map content_type -> publish action. social -> social tool/Scheduler queue; email -> Brevo send; blog -> CMS/file publish; ad -> ads platform. This node ONLY decides; it does not act. It also re-asserts the contract rule: social and outreach require the human-approved flag to be set true or it hard-stops.

publish_action (gated irreversible side-effect): perform the actual publish/send ONLY if state.human_approved == true AND compliance status != fail. Writes go through MCP tools (Brevo for email, social tool for posts, CMS for blog, ads API for ads). Idempotency key = request_id to prevent double-publish on retry. On any auth/scope error, do not retry blindly — route to record_outcome with status=publish_failed.

feedback_capture: write the published artifact id + channel + approval metadata to the BaseStore so the (currently missing) content-performance feedback loop can later correlate 'what got approved that underperformed'. Also stamps the SLA actual-turnaround (created_at -> approved_at).

record_outcome (terminal): persist final status {published|rejected|publish_failed|expired}, compliance_report, revision_count, reviewer decision, and SLA timing into the store and emit a structured result. Posts a status line back to the Growth Console / GitHub issue per the 'report back into governance' rule. Edge to END.
impact: Directly closes 5 of the 6 named pain points: adds the automated brand/tone/legal compliance check (CONTENT-RULES.md is the spec, no human eyeballing every claim), enforces a documented per-content-type approval chain (select_publisher dispatcher), documents and times the review SLA (created_at -> SLA_due_at -> approved_at), provides the explicit rejection/revision workflow (request_changes loop re-screened by compliance), and lays the groundwork for the performance feedback loop (feedback_capture stores approval metadata keyed to published artifact ids). Preserves the Growth Operating Contract's hard rule that no social post is published and no outreach is sent without human approval — the interrupt() gate makes that structurally impossible to bypass. automatable_score is 4/5 because the publish step touches live audiences and stays human-gated by design; generation + compliance are the parts that get automated. Revenue tie-in: faster, safer content throughput on the live Brevo/social pipeline without risking false-claim or biased-collection violations that the workspace memory already flags as landmines.
approval_gates: ONE mandatory human-in-the-loop interrupt at human_review_gate (LangGraph interrupt() / resume-with-Command), which every social post and outreach email MUST pass through per the Growth Operating Contract — the designated reviewer/publisher (Shay Panuilov) is the only one who can set human_approved=true. A SECOND structural guard at publish_action: the irreversible side-effect (post/send/publish) executes only if human_approved==true AND compliance status != fail, with an idempotency key on request_id. Auto-revision is capped (default 2 loops) and a cap-exceeded or compliance-fail state can never silently publish — it always lands back at the human gate or terminates as rejected. Blog/ad publishing can optionally be configured to allow a lighter or auto-approve chain later, but social + email default to required human approval. No node sends or publishes anything before the gate.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS, api.smith.langchain.com) — Agent Server runtime
Postgres checkpointer (auto-provisioned by the platform; do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= to .compile())
BaseStore with semantic index (store.index embed=openai:text-embedding-3-small, dims 1536) — holds brand rules, product-facts, and approval/performance metadata
Anthropic Claude API (orchestration/generation only; NO training/distillation per workspace AGENTS.md)
interrupt()/Command HITL primitive for the approval gate
Source drafts + rules from /EscalateLabs/marketing/data/social-media/ (CONTENT-RULES.md, content-pillars.md, templates/post-templates.md)
Brevo MCP/API — email send (gated)
Social posting tool / Scheduler queue — social publish (gated)
Headless CMS or repo file publish — blog publish (gated)
Ads platform API (Google/Meta) — ad publish (gated)
Growth Console + GitHub issues (marketing repo) — status reporting back into governance
LangSmith tracing/observability for run + SLA visibility
secrets_needed: CMS_API_TOKEN (or repo write token for file-based blog publish)
REVIEWER_NOTIFY_WEBHOOK (Slack/email URL to ping the designated reviewer when a run hits the gate)
deploy_notes: langgraph.json at repo root: {"dependencies":["."],"graphs":{"content_review":"./agent/graph.py:graph"},"env":".env","python_version":"3.12","image_distro":"wolfi","store":{"index":{"embed":"openai:text-embedding-3-small","dims":1536},"ttl":{"default_ttl":43200,"sweep_interval_minutes":60}},"checkpointer":{"ttl":{"strategy":"delete","default_ttl":129600}}}. Build graph = StateGraph(State).compile() WITHOUT a custom checkpointer/store — the managed platform injects production Postgres; passing InMemorySaver is local-only and is ignored/overridden in the hosted runtime. Long checkpointer TTL (>= 90 days) because runs sit interrupted at the human gate while the SLA clock runs; do NOT use a short TTL that could expire a pending-approval run. State schema (TypedDict): request_id, content_type, target_channel, brief, source_draft_path, candidate_draft, compliance_report, human_approved(bool, default False), reviewer_decision, reviewer_notes, revision_count(int), max_revisions(int=2), created_at, sla_due_at, approved_at, final_status. Seed the BaseStore at first deploy with CONTENT-RULES.md / content-pillars.md / post-templates.md / verified product-facts. Real secrets injected via the LangGraph Platform UI/API, never committed to .env (workspace security rule: never hardcode secrets). Two deploy paths are equivalent: `langgraph deploy` CLI from project root, or wire the repo in the LangSmith Deployments UI. Compliance node and publish node MUST be deterministic about the gate: a fail status or human_approved=False can never reach a live send. v1 scope = social + email fully gated; blog/ad chains can ship gated and be relaxed later via config. Schedule the draft-pickup cron as a LangGraph Platform cron job (one run per new draft in /data/social-media/), not a local launchd/subprocess fleet, consistent with the orchestrate-local-execute-on-cluster rule.

process: Campaign Performance Testing & Validation — A/B variant setup, metrics collection across email/social/ads channels, statistical significance analysis, and gated promotion of winning variants (or pause/pivot of underperformers), with a feedback loop back to content generation.
trigger: Two triggers: (1) Scheduled cron (LangGraph Platform Crons API, e.g. daily 09:00) that scans active experiments for accrued metrics and re-evaluates significance; (2) On-demand run via the Assistants/Runs API or LangGraph SDK when a marketer registers a new A/B test (variants + hypothesis + success metric + min sample size). Both enter the same StateGraph entrypoint with `mode` in state distinguishing "register_test" vs "evaluate_test".
graph_steps: START -> load_experiment: read experiment definition + prior checkpoint from BaseStore (variants, hypothesis, primary metric, channel, min_sample_size, significance_threshold=0.95, guardrail/stop-loss rules)

validate_test_design: LLM-assisted + deterministic check that the test is well-formed (single primary metric, control present, sample-size/power pre-registered, no peeking-without-correction); on failure route to human_review interrupt

collect_metrics: call read-only channel connectors (Google Ads/GA4 conversions, email ESP opens/clicks/conversions, social engagement) and Stripe/RevenueCat for downstream ROI; normalize into a per-variant metrics table; never writes

analyze_significance: deterministic stats node (two-proportion z-test / Bayesian posterior for conversion; sequential-test correction to avoid false positives from repeated looks); compute p-value/credible interval, lift, and whether min_sample_size + threshold are met
classify_outcome: branch -> WINNER (significant + positive lift), LOSER/PIVOT (guardrail or stop-loss breached), INCONCLUSIVE (keep running, schedule next look)
draft_decision: LLM composes the proposed action + rationale (promote variant B, pause campaign, or continue) and a plain-English report; for INCONCLUSIVE -> persist state and END

human_approval_gate (interrupt): conditional edge — any promote/pause/pivot is an irreversible action, so the graph calls interrupt() with the proposed action, evidence, and stats; run pauses until a human resumes with approve/reject/edit via the SDK

execute_action (post-approval only): on approve, call the channel write connector to promote winner / pause loser (each external mutation wrapped so a rejection short-circuits); on reject, record decision and END
feedback_loop: write winning-variant features + losing patterns back to the content-generation knowledge store (BaseStore namespace) so future drafts inherit learnings; emit structured result
persist_and_report: checkpoint experiment status, append to centralized metrics record, optionally push a summary to Slack draft (also approval-gated if it auto-sends) -> END
tools_integrations: Google Ads API + GA4 Data API (read conversions/CTR/cost; write = pause/adjust campaign — gated)
Email ESP API (Brevo/SendGrid: read opens/clicks/conversions per variant; write = promote/send winning variant — gated)
Social APIs (LinkedIn/X read engagement metrics; write = boost/promote post — gated)
Stripe + RevenueCat (read-only downstream conversion/ROI attribution)
Anthropic Claude API (orchestration: test-design validation, decision drafting, report writing — inference only, NO model training)
LangGraph Platform Postgres checkpointer (auto-provisioned) + BaseStore (experiment registry, learnings feedback store)
LangGraph Crons API (scheduled re-evaluation trigger)
Slack MCP (optional draft-only notification of results)
Statistics library (scipy/statsmodels) for z-test/Bayesian + sequential correction — runs in-process, deterministic
approval_gates: Human-in-the-loop interrupt() before ANY irreversible action: (1) promoting a winning variant to live traffic; (2) pausing or pivoting an underperforming campaign; (3) writing changes to ad spend/budget; (4) any outbound Slack/email send. The graph surfaces proposed action + variant stats + p-value/lift + cost impact, then pauses; a human resumes via SDK with approve/reject/edit. Test-design validation also gates: a malformed/under-powered test routes to human_review before any spend is evaluated. Read/collect/analyze nodes run autonomously without gates. INCONCLUSIVE results auto-continue (no gate) since "keep running" is non-destructive.
impact: Closes the biggest documented gaps in the Marketing Automation Blueprint's analytics layer: introduces a documented A/B methodology with a pre-registered significance threshold (default 95%) and sequential-test correction to stop false-positive "winners"; centralizes per-variant metrics across email/social/ads in one store; automates significance analysis and pause/pivot decision rules; and finally wires the missing feedback loop so winning-variant patterns flow back into content generation. Statistical conclusions and the human approval gate stay authoritative — agent proposes, human disposes — so revenue-affecting ad spend and promotions are never changed autonomously. Directly protects ad budget efficiency and supports the revenue-first priority by killing underperformers faster and scaling winners deliberately.
deploy_notes: Deploy to LangGraph Platform (managed Cloud SaaS, api.smith.langchain.com). langgraph.json: dependencies ["."], graphs {"campaign_validator": "./agent/graph.py:graph"}, python_version "3.12", image_distro "wolfi", pip_installer "uv". Compile WITHOUT passing checkpointer/store (platform injects Postgres checkpointer + store automatically); configure store.index for semantic search over the learnings namespace and store.ttl for sweeping stale experiments. Use interrupt() (not deprecated NodeInterrupt) for the approval gate so runs pause durably and resume via the SDK/Runs API. Inject ALL secrets via the Deployments UI/API env injection — never commit to .env or hardcode (security rule). Set up a Cron (Crons API) for daily re-evaluation. Critical guardrails to encode in code-review: (a) every write/mutation connector must be unreachable except downstream of an approved interrupt; (b) collect/analyze nodes are strictly read-only; (c) Claude is used for orchestration/drafting/analysis only — no model training/distillation/eval-data generation, per workspace Anthropic-terms guardrail. Stats node must use a fixed sequential-correction policy to honor pre-registered looks. v1 scope: one channel at a time (start with Google Ads + GA4 since conversion tracking is already configured per google-ads-conversion-tracking.md), expand to email/social once the gate workflow is proven. Add a dry-run/test mode (mode="simulate") so campaign logic can be validated against historical metrics before any live launch — directly addresses the "no test environment" pain point.
