process: Directory & Backlink Management (SEO). Ongoing process to submit, claim, and maintain business listings (directories, review sites) and manage backlink quality: drift detection, metadata updates, listing health monitoring, broken-link remediation, review-status rechecks, and competitor listing monitoring. Today the Growth Console is the execution surface and GAL runs ad-hoc safe background jobs (crawl, recheck, email polling) with no automated drift alerting, no central health dashboard, no SLA, and weak notification integration. v1 closes the read-heavy gaps (scheduled drift+health sweep, classification, SLA-aware alerting) while keeping every mutating/irreversible action human-gated, exactly as the Growth Operating Contract requires (agent-safe vs human-required split).
trigger: Primary: LangGraph Platform cron (CronCreate) — daily 09:00 UTC full sweep over all targets in backlink-targets.json + a lighter every-6h sweep over tier-1 / recently-drifted rows. Secondary: on-demand POST to the deployment /runs (or /threads/{id}/runs) for a single directory ("recheck Capterra now") and a webhook from Growth Console when a row's status changes. Each run is one graph invocation over a batch of directory rows; thread_id = sweep batch id so state + interrupts persist across the human-approval pause.
graph_steps: load_targets — read backlink-targets.json (40 rows) + Growth Console /api/runtime/status and Firestore backlink rows; build the work queue, honoring seo_backlinks_rate_limits / seo_backlinks_locks (skip rows in cooldown, respect per-directory profiles). No mutations.

crawl_listing (agent-safe) — for each queued row, fetch the live listing URL read-only via browser-gal MCP / HTTP. On CAPTCHA or anti-bot wall, do NOT loop: emit deterministic needs_human classification with captcha blocker + correlationId (matches frozen production criteria) and continue.

check_backlink_health (agent-safe) — HEAD/GET the backlink target URLs to detect broken (4xx/5xx), redirected, or nofollow-flipped links; classify via the status-page Provider Failure Taxonomy (provider_outage/timeout/etc.).

compare_metadata (LLM, Claude) — structured diff of crawled listing metadata (name, URL, description, category, NAP) vs the canonical record; Claude classifies each delta as drift / cosmetic / false-positive and assigns severity. Orchestration-only LLM call — no model training.

assess_sla_and_competitors (agent-safe) — compute drift age vs SLA thresholds (e.g. tier-1 drift > 24h = breach), poll review-status, and read-only crawl 2-3 named competitor listings for new-listing signals.

build_remediation_plan — assemble a proposed action set: metadata-update submissions, broken-link fixes, claim/ownership tasks, Jira/GitHub backlog issues, and the alert/notification payload. Nothing executed yet.
HUMAN_APPROVAL_GATE — interrupt() surfacing the full plan (per-action diff, evidence screenshots/URLs, severity, SLA status) to the growth operator; resumes with approve/reject/edit per action.

execute_approved (approval-gated, GAL-first / human fallback) — only for approved actions: dispatch metadata-update submission via GAL background job; if CAPTCHA/anti-bot is hit, fall back to needs_human row handoff. Persist evidence (success/fail, final_url, blocker, screenshot path, credential_source, auth_path_final).

notify_and_report (approval-gated send) — post sanitized alert to Slack #growth + write status back to Growth Console / open Jira-or-GitHub backlog issues for breaches and needs_human rows. Sending is behind the same approval; status output is sanitized (Bearer/AIza redaction).
persist_state — write run summary, drift register, and SLA clock to the platform store (long-term BaseStore) + checkpointer; update last_crawl timestamps. Idempotent.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments / Agent Server) — managed Postgres checkpointer + store injected (do NOT pass checkpointer/store to .compile())

langgraph.json (graphs.directory_sentinel -> ./src/agent.py:graph, python_version 3.12, image_distro wolfi, store.index openai:text-embedding-3-small dims 1536, store.ttl + checkpointer.ttl set, auth.path -> custom Auth)
langgraph.types.interrupt() + Command(resume=...) for the human-in-the-loop approval gate
Anthropic Claude (orchestration / classification only — drift severity + summarization; NO training/distillation)
browser-gal MCP (Browserbase cloud provider) for read-only listing crawl; deterministic needs_human on CAPTCHA
Growth Console APIs: GET /api/runtime/status, Firestore backlink rows + job-store (read), status write-back
Growth CLI API (/backlink/targets, /backlink/jobs) when GROWTH_CLI_API_URL configured
Firestore rate-limit + locks (seo_backlinks_rate_limits, seo_backlinks_locks) — respect cooldown/profiles
GCP Secret Manager (directory-*-password aliases account_password/account_login) — runtime fetch only
marketing/data/backlink-targets.json (canonical metadata source of truth, 40 rows)
Slack MCP (#growth alerts — send gated)
Jira/GitHub Issues (backlog + needs_human handoff — create gated)
CronCreate (scheduled sweeps)
LangSmith tracing/observability
approval_gates: Two hard gates, both via interrupt(). GATE 1 (remediation plan): every mutating or irreversible action pauses for explicit operator approve/reject/edit before execution — metadata-update submissions to directories, listing claims/ownership steps, and Jira/GitHub issue creation. GATE 2 (notify/send): posting Slack alerts and writing status into Growth Console / opening tickets is gated on the same approval. The agent is read-only by default: load_targets, crawl_listing, check_backlink_health, compare_metadata, assess_sla_and_competitors run with zero side effects and never trip a gate. CAPTCHA/anti-bot never auto-bypasses — it returns deterministic needs_human (captcha blocker + evidence) and routes to a human-claim handoff. Ownership transfers tied to personal accounts and any social/outreach publishing remain fully human per the Growth Operating Contract (out of scope for auto-execution). Resume is per-action so an operator can approve a safe metadata fix while rejecting a risky claim in the same plan.
secrets_needed: GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS (GCP Secret Manager SA for directory-*-password aliases)
FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT (Firestore read: backlink rows, rate_limits, locks)
deploy_notes: Deploy to LangGraph Platform (Cloud SaaS tier) via `langgraph deploy` from the repo root containing langgraph.json + pyproject.toml. graphs: {"directory_sentinel": "./src/agent.py:graph"}. Build the StateGraph with a TypedDict state (work_queue, crawl_results, drift_register[], sla_breaches[], remediation_plan[], approvals{}, evidence[], correlationId) and a list reducer on the per-directory results; .compile() with NO checkpointer/store (platform injects Postgres). Inject ALL secrets through the deployment UI/API env (never in .env committed, never hardcoded — satisfies security rule). Set checkpointer.ttl (e.g. 43200) so paused approval threads survive, and store.ttl for the drift register. Configure custom auth.py for the /runs + webhook endpoints. Schedule via CronCreate after first manual run validates the read-only path. Roll out behind the existing rate-limit gates: start RATE_LIMIT_ENFORCE matching docs/backlinks-rate-limits.md (observe -> partial -> full); start in detect-only mode (gates auto-reject execution) for the first week to validate drift classification precision before enabling real submissions. Constraints honored: orchestration calling Claude only (no model dev — also satisfies the workspace Anthropic-terms guardrail), all sends/mutations interrupt-gated, secrets via env + Secret Manager aliases, CAPTCHA -> deterministic needs_human. Source grounding: /Users/scheduler-systems/Documents/scheduler-systems-ltd/EscalateLabs/marketing/GROWTH_OPERATING_CONTRACT.md, /Users/scheduler-systems/Documents/scheduler-systems-ltd/EscalateLabs/marketing/docs/backlinks-rate-limits.md, /Users/scheduler-systems/Documents/scheduler-systems-ltd/EscalateLabs/marketing/docs/status-page-integration.md, /Users/scheduler-systems/Documents/scheduler-systems-ltd/EscalateLabs/marketing/docs/backlinks-background-agents.md, /Users/scheduler-systems/Documents/scheduler-systems-ltd/EscalateLabs/marketing/data/backlink-targets.json.
impact: Closes the four named pain points: (1) automated drift alerting with a defined recheck cadence (daily full + 6h tier-1) and explicit SLA clock (was: "how often are listings rechecked?" unanswered); (2) a centralized listing-health + drift register persisted in the platform store, feeding a single dashboard view (was: none); (3) clear, structured metadata comparison via LLM diff+severity (was: "process unclear"); (4) automated competitor listing monitoring + integrated Slack/Jira notifications (was: GAL jobs not wired to notifications). Keeps the human firmly in control of every irreversible action, so it raises coverage and lowers time-to-detect drift without taking on the risk that automatable_score=6 implies. Protects SEO/backlink equity (broken-link + nofollow-flip detection) for the live marketing surface. Net: detection and triage become continuous and SLA-bound; execution stays operator-approved.

area: Marketing — Email Campaign Execution & Delivery Monitoring
process:
A LangGraph Platform (managed / LangSmith Deployments) agent that orchestrates marketing + transactional email campaigns for Scheduler and GAL, and continuously monitors deliverability — without ever sending anything without explicit human approval.

WHAT IT AUTOMATES (grounded in MARKETING_AUTOMATION_BLUEPRINT.md Phase 2 "Email Automation" layer, which is currently designed-but-unbuilt):
- Assembling a campaign send-plan (onboarding sequence step, newsletter, or announcement) from a sequence definition + HTML template + an audience segment.
- Drafting subject-line / preheader A/B variants with Claude (orchestration only — generation, NOT model training/distillation), then deterministically splitting the audience into variant buckets.
- Running pre-flight deliverability checks (SPF/DKIM/DMARC DNS records resolve & align for the sending domain; suppression-list scrub; spam-trap / role-account / hard-bounce filter; rate-budget check) BEFORE proposing a send.
- Submitting the send to the live provider (Brevo — the configured provider; SendGrid as failover) ONLY after a human approves the proposed plan at an interrupt gate.
- Ingesting provider webhook events (delivered, hard/soft bounce, spam complaint, open, click, unsubscribe) and reconciling them into per-campaign engagement + deliverability stats.
- Bounce handling: auto-classify hard vs soft, propose suppression-list additions for hard bounces & complaints (write is approval-gated).
- Unsubscribe / preference management: reconcile provider unsubscribe events into the central suppression store so future sends honor them.
- Deliverability monitoring: compute spam-complaint rate, hard-bounce rate, open/click/conversion rates per campaign; raise an alert + summary when bounce rate or complaint rate breaches thresholds (e.g. bounce >2%, complaint >0.1%).
- Provider-failover fallback: if Brevo pre-flight health check fails, propose switching the send to SendGrid (switch is approval-gated).

WHAT IT DOES NOT DO (hard constraints):
- No model training/fine-tuning/distillation — Claude is used purely as an orchestration/classification/drafting tool (Anthropic Consumer Terms guardrail per workspace AGENTS.md).
- No autonomous sends. Every send, every suppression-list write, every provider switch, every bulk preference change is held at a LangGraph `interrupt()` human-in-the-loop gate and resumed only on explicit approval (satisfies the workspace "no send without approval" + "no delete/overwrite without approval" rules).
- No hardcoded secrets — all provider keys, sender identity, and webhook signing secrets come from env injected by the LangGraph Platform, never committed.

DEPLOYMENT SHAPE (LangGraph Platform, managed):
- langgraph.json declares one graph (campaign) plus a thin custom HTTP app mount for the provider webhook receiver; python_version 3.12, image_distro wolfi, pip_installer uv.
- graphs: {"campaign": "./src/email_agent/graph.py:graph"}; graph is a module-level COMPILED StateGraph. Do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= to .compile() — the managed platform injects the production Postgres checkpointer + store automatically. Suppression list & engagement counters live in the injected BaseStore (long-term, cross-thread, namespaced by domain/campaign), so they survive across runs and webhook batches.
- env: provider keys + webhook secret declared in langgraph.json env block as references, real values set in the Platform UI/API.
- A Cron (Platform-native scheduled run) kicks the deliverability-rollup + threshold-alert pass on a schedule; webhooks are pushed to the custom HTTP route which writes events into the store and (optionally) opens/resumes a monitoring thread.

OPERATING POSTURE: local Mac is conductor only; this agent runs in LangGraph's managed cloud (consistent with the workspace "orchestrate local, execute on cluster" rule). One LangGraph thread == one campaign run; the thread persists through the approval interrupt so the human can approve hours later and the run resumes from the checkpoint.
trigger:
THREE trigger paths into the managed deployment:
1. Campaign-initiated (API/UI run): a human or upstream system (e.g. the blueprint's blog->email pipeline step, or a new-signup onboarding event) creates a thread on the `campaign` graph with input {campaign_type: onboarding_step|newsletter|announcement, sequence_id?, template_id, segment_id, ab_test?: {variants, split}}. This starts at the build-plan node.
2. Provider webhook (event ingestion): Brevo/SendGrid POST delivery/bounce/complaint/open/click/unsubscribe events to the custom HTTP route mounted via langgraph.json `http.app`. The route verifies the webhook signature (secret from env), normalizes the event, and appends it to the per-campaign engagement record in the injected store. High-severity events (spam complaint, hard-bounce spike) can resume/branch a monitoring thread.
3. Scheduled monitoring (Platform Cron): a recurring cron run triggers the deliverability-rollup pass that aggregates stored events, computes spam/bounce/open/click rates, and if a threshold is breached, produces a summary + alert (alert delivery itself, if it sends an email/Slack, is approval-gated like any other send).
graph_steps: ingest_request — validate run input (campaign_type, template_id, segment_id, optional ab_test); load sequence/template + audience segment; reject malformed input early.
resolve_audience — expand segment_id into recipient list; scrub against the suppression store (unsubscribes, prior hard-bounces, complaints, role/spam-trap addresses); dedupe; record excluded count.

draft_variants — (LLM orchestration) Claude drafts subject-line/preheader A/B variants from the template + campaign brief; deterministically assign recipients to variant buckets per the requested split. Drafting only — no model training.

preflight_deliverability — verify SPF/DKIM/DMARC DNS records resolve & align for the sending domain; check provider (Brevo) health + send-rate budget; run spam-content lint on subject/body; compute a go/no-go readiness report. On provider-unhealthy, prepare a SendGrid-failover proposal.

assemble_plan — build the full proposed send plan: provider, sender identity, final segment size (post-scrub), variant assignment, schedule window, and the deliverability readiness report. No send yet.

APPROVAL_GATE_send — interrupt(): surface the proposed plan to a human; block until explicit approve / edit / reject. (Human-in-the-loop; the thread persists on the managed checkpointer until resumed.)

execute_send — ONLY on approval, submit the batch to the approved provider via API (idempotency key per recipient/variant to make retries safe); capture provider message IDs into the campaign record. On provider error mid-send, halt and route to alert rather than blindly retrying.
await_events — thread parks; provider webhooks (via the HTTP route) accumulate delivered/bounce/complaint/open/click/unsubscribe events into the store keyed by the campaign's message IDs.
reconcile_events — classify each event (hard vs soft bounce, complaint, unsubscribe, engagement); update per-variant + per-campaign counters; identify addresses needing suppression.

APPROVAL_GATE_suppression — interrupt(): propose suppression-list additions (hard bounces + complaints) and unsubscribe reconciliations; block until a human approves the write (no autonomous data mutation).
apply_suppressions — on approval, write approved entries to the suppression store so future sends honor them.

rollup_and_evaluate — compute deliverability + engagement metrics (delivery rate, hard-bounce %, soft-bounce %, complaint %, open/click/conversion, A/B winner); compare against thresholds (bounce >2%, complaint >0.1%).

alert_or_failover — if a threshold is breached or the primary provider failed preflight, build an alert summary and, if remediation requires a provider switch or a notification send, route that through an APPROVAL_GATE before any outbound action; otherwise persist the rollup and END.
END — campaign record (plan, send result, reconciled events, metrics, A/B winner, any approved suppressions) is durably stored in the injected BaseStore for the analytics/reporting layer.
impact: Builds the entirely-missing Phase 2 Email Automation layer end-to-end: campaign execution, bounce handling, spam-complaint response, unsubscribe/preference reconciliation, SPF/DKIM/DMARC deliverability monitoring, subject-line A/B testing, and engagement rollups — every pain point listed for this process. Crucially it does so under the workspace's send-safety rules: nothing leaves without human approval, no data is mutated without approval, and no ML model is trained. Protects sender reputation (the biggest financial risk of a DIY email blast — domain blacklisting would damage Scheduler's live transactional + onboarding email and thus revenue). P2 because the underlying system is greenfield/unbuilt and email is not the current top revenue lever (RevenueCat/Scheduler ASO + paywall work rank higher), but it is a real gap with reputation-protection value once content/list volume justifies it.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed runtime; injected Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore; Platform Cron for scheduled rollups; custom HTTP app mount for webhooks)

Brevo API (PRIMARY — live, already configured: BREVO_API_KEY, BREVO_SENDER_NAME, BREVO_SENDER_EMAIL in .env.sample; existing src/brevo/ module): transactional/campaign send, webhook events, contacts/suppression lists
SendGrid API (FAILOVER provider — proposed switch is approval-gated)
Anthropic Claude API (orchestration only: subject/preheader A/B drafting, bounce/complaint classification, deliverability summary; NO training/distillation)
DNS resolver / dmarcian-style check for SPF/DKIM/DMARC record verification on the sending domain
Provider webhook receiver (signature-verified HTTP route mounted via langgraph.json http.app)
Existing email HTML templates in EscalateLabs/marketing/email-templates/ and sequence definitions per blueprint automations/marketing/email/
Optional: Slack or Gmail for threshold-breach alert delivery (alert send itself is approval-gated)
approval_gates: THREE hard human-in-the-loop interrupt() gates, all resumable on the managed checkpointer: (1) APPROVAL_GATE_send — no batch is submitted to Brevo/SendGrid until a human reviews the full send plan (provider, sender, post-scrub segment size, A/B assignment, schedule, SPF/DKIM/DMARC readiness report) and explicitly approves/edits/rejects. (2) APPROVAL_GATE_suppression — no write to the suppression list (hard-bounce/complaint additions, unsubscribe reconciliations) happens without approval, honoring the workspace 'no delete/overwrite without approval' rule. (3) APPROVAL_GATE in alert_or_failover — any provider switch (Brevo->SendGrid) or any outbound notification/alert send is itself approval-gated. Reject at any gate ends the run cleanly with state preserved; edit re-enters the assemble/reconcile node. This directly enforces the workspace 'no send without approval' and 'no ML model dev in Claude Code' rules.
deploy_notes:
REPO LAYOUT (new, under a worktree of EscalateLabs/marketing per worktree-hygiene rules — do NOT edit the live checkout in place): src/email_agent/graph.py exporting a module-level `graph = builder.compile()` (NO checkpointer=/store= args — the managed platform injects them); src/email_agent/webhook_app.py exporting `app` (signature-verifying Starlette/FastAPI route).

langgraph.json (root): { "$schema":"https://langgra.ph/schema.json", "dependencies":["."], "graphs":{"campaign":"./src/email_agent/graph.py:graph"}, "http":{"app":"./src/email_agent/webhook_app.py:app"}, "env":".env", "python_version":"3.12", "image_distro":"wolfi", "pip_installer":"uv", "store":{"index":{"embed":"openai:text-embedding-3-small","dims":1536},"ttl":{"default_ttl":525600,"sweep_interval_minutes":1440}}, "checkpointer":{"ttl":{"strategy":"delete","default_ttl":43200}} }.

STATE: TypedDict with campaign_type, template_id, segment_id, ab_test, resolved recipients, variant_assignment, deliverability_report, provider, plan, message_ids, events, metrics, approvals[]; messages list uses add_messages reducer for the LLM-orchestration nodes.

PERSISTENCE: suppression list + cumulative per-campaign engagement counters go in the injected BaseStore (cross-thread, namespaced e.g. (domain, 'suppression') and ('campaign', campaign_id, 'events')) so they outlive a single thread and survive webhook batches; per-run plan/approval state lives on the injected checkpointer.

IDEMPOTENCY: execute_send uses a per-recipient+variant idempotency key so a resumed/retried thread never double-sends — critical because the thread parks across the approval interrupt and could be resumed more than once.

CRON: register a Platform Cron run on the campaign graph (or a dedicated rollup graph) for the scheduled deliverability-rollup + threshold-alert pass.

VERIFY BEFORE DEPLOY: confirm SPF/DKIM/DMARC are actually published for SENDING_DOMAIN (currently undocumented per pain points) and run a tiny approval-gated smoke campaign to a seed/test list before any real audience send. Tie the build to a documented GitHub issue first (workspace 'always documented GitHub issue' rule). Deploy via `langgraph deploy` (CLI) or the Platform UI; set all secrets in the Platform UI/API, never in committed files."]

process: Prospecting & Lead Finding — multi-channel discovery of high-intent prospects (partnerships, reviews, guest posts, and Scheduler buyers) across Reddit, Twitter/X, Google, Facebook groups, forums, and LinkedIn-by-title. The agent automates the read-heavy half of the existing `/connect-prospects` workflow: it runs the proven per-channel search queries, extracts candidate posts/profiles, enriches contact + company context, scores intent, dedupes against the existing book, and writes qualified leads into the `qualified.json` contract. Every action that touches the outside world in a non-read way — actually posting/DMing/commenting, sending enrichment requests that cost credits, or writing new records to the shared prospect store — is held at a human-in-the-loop interrupt for review and approval. The LLM is used only for query generation, intent classification, enrichment summarization, and draft engagement copy; no model training/eval/distillation occurs.
trigger: Two entry points on LangGraph Platform: (1) a Cron schedule (platform-native cron, e.g. weekday 08:00 for high-intent subreddits/Twitter, weekly for medium-intent + Facebook/forums) that kicks off a fresh discovery sweep per channel; (2) on-demand via the API/SDK `runs` endpoint or Studio, passing `{channel, queries_override?, max_candidates}` to mirror the `/connect-prospects [channel]` argument. Runs are resumable threads so a sweep interrupted at the approval gate can be picked up later.
graph_steps: START -> plan_sweep: load run config (channel, target list, max_candidates); LLM expands the canonical per-channel query set from connect-prospects.md (Reddit subreddit+site:reddit.com queries, Twitter intent/pain/competitor-frustration operators, Google wide-net, FB/forums) tuned to today's date window; emits a query plan.

discover (fan-out, one branch per channel via Send): execute read-only searches — WebSearch for Google/cross-platform + Reddit `site:reddit.com`; platform/social APIs (Twitter, Reddit) where keys exist; LinkedIn restricted to title/decision-maker lookup, NEVER post search. Collect raw candidate posts/profiles with url, author, snippet, timestamp.
dedupe_against_book: read existing `qualified.json` (prospects[] + summary) from the long-term Store/source; drop candidates already tracked or previously rejected; keep only net-new.

score_intent: LLM classifies each candidate against the documented high-intent signals ('what do you use', 'looking for recommendations', 'currently using X but', team-size, budget, recency<7d, low-reply-count) -> hot/warm/cool + signal rationale + confidence; cool/low-confidence dropped or flagged.

INTERRUPT #1 enrich_gate (human-in-the-loop): present the scored hot/warm shortlist; pause for approval BEFORE spending paid email-enrichment/contact-finder credits. Operator approves/edits/declines per-row.

enrich (only on approved rows): call enrichment services for email/company/role; LLM summarizes fit + suggests outreach angle (partnership / review ask / guest post / product). All raw enrichment kept for audit.
draft_engagement: LLM drafts channel-appropriate, rules-compliant copy (helpful-first, multi-option, disclosed affiliation, NEVER promo-first) — produced as DRAFTS ONLY, never auto-sent.

INTERRUPT #2 publish_and_persist_gate (human-in-the-loop): single approval surface showing (a) new/updated records to be written to qualified.json with hot/warm/cool tally, and (b) any engagement drafts. Nothing is written or sent until the human approves; the human can edit rows, downgrade scores, or reject. This gate covers both the irreversible store-write and any outbound action.

commit_results: on approval, upsert approved prospects into the store (update prospects[] + recompute summary counts + last_updated) and route approved drafts to their delivery sink (or leave as approved-draft artifacts if no send integration is wired). -> END
On no-approval / timeout: persist the shortlist + drafts to the thread state and exit cleanly so the run is resumable; nothing irreversible happens.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform managed Postgres checkpointer (resumable threads, injected — do NOT pass to .compile())
LangGraph Platform managed Store (BaseStore) as the persisted prospect book + reject-list, with semantic index (embed: openai:text-embedding-3-small) for fuzzy dedupe
WebSearch (Google / cross-platform site:reddit.com sweeps)
Reddit API / search (high-priority channel)
Twitter/X API (intent + pain-point + competitor-frustration queries)
LinkedIn — title/decision-maker lookup ONLY (post search explicitly excluded per the runbook)
Email/contact enrichment service (paid; gated behind enrich interrupt)
Anthropic Claude (query expansion, intent scoring, enrichment summary, draft copy — orchestration only, no model training)
qualified.json data contract at EscalateLabs/marketing/data/prospects/ (prospects[] + summary{total,hot,warm,cool,engaged,converted})
LangGraph native Cron for scheduled sweeps
LangSmith tracing/observability (built into the platform)
impact: Directly attacks the named pain points: replaces manual contact research, automates lead scoring (the hot/warm/cool taxonomy already scaffolded in qualified.json), routes around LinkedIn-post unreliability by pinning the high-yield channels (Reddit/Google/Twitter) and restricting LinkedIn to title lookup, and centralizes intent capture into the existing store. Feeds the revenue-priority Scheduler funnel (live RevenueCat subscribers) by surfacing high-intent buyers faster, while the approval gates keep it compliant with the workspace 'no send/no irreversible action without approval' rule. automatable_score is 6/10 — discovery + scoring + dedupe + enrichment-prep are genuinely automatable; the judgment calls (who to actually contact, what to say, what to spend credits on) stay human, which is exactly where the two interrupts sit. Realistic v1: cut per-sweep analyst time from hours to a single review pass, with a clean audit trail in LangSmith.
approval_gates: Two mandatory human-in-the-loop interrupts implemented via LangGraph `interrupt()` + `Command(resume=...)`: (1) enrich_gate — pauses before spending paid enrichment credits on the scored shortlist; operator approves/edits/declines per row. (2) publish_and_persist_gate — single approval surface that covers BOTH the irreversible store write (upsert into qualified.json + summary recompute) AND any outbound engagement. No external post/DM/comment is ever auto-sent: the LLM produces drafts only, and the operator must approve before anything leaves the system or is persisted. On reject/timeout the run persists state and exits resumable, performing nothing irreversible. Aligns with workspace rules: no send without approval, no delete/overwrite without approval, always tie to a documented issue.
deploy_notes: Deploy to LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments / managed Cloud SaaS). langgraph.json: graphs {"prospect_scout":"./prospect_scout/agent.py:graph"}, python_version 3.12, image_distro wolfi, pip_installer uv. Compile the StateGraph WITHOUT a custom checkpointer/store — the platform injects managed Postgres checkpointer + Store automatically; passing InMemorySaver is local-only and will be ignored. Configure langgraph.json `store.index` = {embed:"openai:text-embedding-3-small", dims:1536} for dedupe, and `store.ttl`/`checkpointer.ttl` for housekeeping. Inject ALL secrets via the platform UI/API env (never in committed .env). Use the fan-out at discover via `Send` for per-channel parallelism. Persist the prospect book in the managed Store (source of truth); optionally mirror back to qualified.json via the commit sink if the marketing repo path is reachable from the deployment, otherwise emit the approved upsert as a run artifact for a separate committer. Schedule sweeps with platform Cron (weekday 08:00 high-intent; weekly medium-intent). Set channel concurrency limits to respect API rate limits. Tie work to a documented GitHub issue in EscalateLabs/marketing before first deploy. IMPORTANT: this agent does orchestration/classification only — it must not be used to train, fine-tune, or distill any model (Anthropic Consumer Terms / workspace AGENTS.md guardrail).

process: Cold Email Outreach Campaigns (integration partnerships, review requests, guest post pitches) for EscalateLabs/marketing — prospect research, enrichment, template selection + personalization, A/B subject assignment, follow-up sequencing, and engagement tracking. v1 keeps the agent firmly inside the GROWTH_OPERATING_CONTRACT boundary: agent does "draft generation, research, prep only"; every send, blocklist write, and CRM mutation is human-approved via a LangGraph interrupt() before it executes against Brevo. The deployed graph is orchestration-only (calls Claude/LLMs for copy + sentiment, no model training/distillation).
trigger: Two entry modes on LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments): (1) On-demand run via the SDK/REST `POST /threads/{id}/runs` with input `{campaign_type: partnerships|reviews|guestposts, batch_size, dry_run}` — this is the migration of the existing `/cold-outreach <type>` command into a hosted assistant. (2) Scheduled daily cadence run via a LangGraph cron (or external scheduler hitting the runs API) at 09:00 recipient-TZ Tue–Thu to: process due follow-ups, poll Brevo webhook-fed engagement events, and re-score replies. Both modes pause at the send/blocklist/CRM interrupts and surface the batch for human approval; no email leaves the graph without a resume carrying approved=true.
graph_steps: load_state: read data/prospects/qualified.json + data/outreach/outreach-log.json (create if absent) from the long-term BaseStore (store-backed, not committed) into typed State; resolve campaign_type, batch_size, dry_run, daily cap from RemoteConfig-style limits node

compliance_preflight (HARD GATE, deterministic): verify sender-domain DKIM/SPF/DMARC status flag; confirm a List-Unsubscribe header + visible unsubscribe link + postal address are present on the chosen template; abort the whole run with a structured error if any are missing (encodes the verified #389 deliverability landmines so the agent cannot route around them)

research_prospects: for partnerships/reviews/guestposts, fan out WebSearch + WebFetch on target sites to discover/qualify contacts (DA>30, active, accepts pitches, non-competing); dedupe against existing log; emit candidate prospect records
enrich_and_validate: per prospect gather company_size/recent_news/personalization_hooks; run email-address syntax+MX validation; reject role-account-only or unverifiable addresses

suppression_check (deterministic): cross-check every recipient against the Brevo marketing blocklist + local unsubscribe/blocklist set + sentiment-neutrality rule (reject any cohort selected on positivity signals — G2/Capterra ToS guard); drop suppressed/biased entries before any drafting

select_template_and_ab: pick template A/B/C/D per campaign type, assign A/B subject-line variant (round-robin to keep variants balanced, min-50/variant target recorded), bind real per-recipient merge fields (fixes the fake {{FIRSTNAME}} static-html defect — each recipient gets its own rendered body)
draft_personalize (LLM/Claude): generate the personalized opening line + P.S. + 3 guest-post angles from the hooks; keep body <150 words; produce a per-recipient draft object (no send)

INTERRUPT #1 approve_send_batch (human-in-the-loop): surface the full batch (recipient, subject variant, rendered body, hooks, suppression status) for human review; human can edit/approve/reject per-row; resume payload must carry approved=true + final edits or the send node refuses

send_via_brevo: ONLY on approved rows and only if dry_run=false — call Brevo transactional API with per-recipient rendered htmlContent + List-Unsubscribe header; honor daily cap (10–100) + 2–5min spacing; write each send result back to outreach-log.json in the store; on dry_run, write would-send records only

ingest_engagement: consume Brevo open/click/reply/bounce/unsubscribe webhook events (via the deployment's custom http app route) and reconcile into the log; auto-add unsubscribes + hard-bounces to the local suppression set
score_replies (LLM/Claude): classify each reply as positive/neutral/negative/question/auto-reply + a lead score (sentiment + ICP fit + engagement + speed); attach to prospect
INTERRUPT #2 approve_hot_lead_actions (human-in-the-loop): for positive/hot leads, propose CRM upsert + meeting-booking reply draft; pause for human approval before any CRM write or reply send

schedule_followups: compute due follow-ups (Day 5/12/20 sequence), enqueue them as next-cadence-run work items, and emit a campaign metrics report (sent/delivered/opened/clicked/replied/positive/meetings) to data/reports/; END
impact: Migrates the manual, markdown-template `/cold-outreach` command into a resumable, governed hosted agent without violating the contract. Directly attacks the listed pain points: closes the no-ESP-integration gap (wires the already-present Brevo SDK behind a compliance gate), replaces manual JSON tracking with a store-backed log + webhook-driven engagement ingestion, adds the missing unsubscribe/blocklist sync and email-address validation as hard gates, automates A/B variant assignment with real per-recipient personalization (fixes the verified fake-merge defect), and adds reply-sentiment lead scoring + a gated CRM path. Net effect: agent does the slow prep/research/drafting/tracking work at machine speed while a human still owns every irreversible/external action — exactly the automatable_score:5 ceiling for this process. Revenue-relevant because the first review/partnership wave escapes 0-review invisibility for the live-revenue Scheduler product, but it is the slow/low-ceiling lever (do not let it crowd out pricing fixes).
approval_gates: Two mandatory LangGraph interrupt() pauses make this safe to deploy: (1) approve_send_batch — NO email is sent to Brevo until a human reviews the rendered per-recipient batch and resumes with approved=true (encodes 'sending or approving outreach = Human-required' from GROWTH_OPERATING_CONTRACT and the global no-send-without-approval rule); (2) approve_hot_lead_actions — NO CRM write or meeting-reply send happens without explicit human approval. Plus a non-skippable deterministic compliance_preflight gate (DKIM/SPF/DMARC + unsubscribe + postal address) and a suppression_check gate (Brevo blocklist + local opt-outs + sentiment-neutral cohort enforcement) that the LLM cannot route around. dry_run=true short-circuits the send node entirely for safe testing. Because the managed runtime injects the production Postgres checkpointer, an interrupted run durably parks awaiting human resume — no message ever leaves on a timeout.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS) — Agent Server, auto-injected Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore (do NOT pass custom checkpointer/store to .compile())
Anthropic Claude API (orchestration only: personalization copy, guest-post angle generation, reply-sentiment classification — NO model training/distillation)
Brevo (@getbrevo/brevo, already a dependency) — TransactionalEmailsApi send, marketing blocklist/contacts lookup, template management; reuse existing automation/src/brevo/index.js wrapper
Brevo webhooks -> custom Starlette/FastAPI http app mounted via langgraph.json `http.app` to ingest open/click/reply/bounce/unsubscribe events
WebSearch + WebFetch (deferred tools) for prospect research and contact/DA qualification
Email-address validation (syntax + MX) at enrich_and_validate
Long-term store-backed persistence of data/prospects/qualified.json, data/outreach/outreach-log.json, data/reports/ (read at load_state, written after sends) — NOT git-committed mutable state
LangGraph cron / scheduled runs API for the daily Tue–Thu cadence (follow-ups + engagement poll)
Optional CRM connector (HubSpot/sheet) behind interrupt #2 — currently a stubbed upsert tool since the contract notes CRM is 'mentioned without infrastructure'
secrets_needed: STORE_INDEX_OPENAI_API_KEY (only if enabling semantic store index; optional)
CRM_API_KEY (optional, only when CRM connector enabled)
deploy_notes: Repo root for the deployment lives at EscalateLabs/marketing/automation/ (Brevo SDK + data already there); add a langgraph.json there. Concrete v1 langgraph.json: { "$schema":"https://langgra.ph/schema.json", "dependencies":["."], "graphs":{"cold_outreach":"./outreach_agent/graph.py:graph"}, "env":".env", "python_version":"3.12", "image_distro":"wolfi", "http":{"app":"./outreach_agent/webhook_app.py:app"}, "store":{"ttl":{"default_ttl":43200,"sweep_interval_minutes":60}}, "checkpointer":{"ttl":{"strategy":"delete","default_ttl":43200}}, "pip_installer":"uv" }. State is a TypedDict (campaign_type, dry_run, batch[], approved flag, metrics) with add-style reducers on list fields; build with StateGraph(...).compile() and export module-level `graph` — do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= (managed runtime injects production Postgres). Use interrupt() (not deprecated NodeInterrupt) at the two approval nodes and resume with Command(resume=...). Deploy via `langgraph deploy` CLI or the LangSmith UI pointed at the marketing repo; inject all secrets through the LangSmith deployment env UI/API, never in committed .env. Hard prerequisites before first non-dry_run send (carried over from the verified #389 landmines): (a) DKIM/SPF/DMARC on the sender domain confirmed green — compliance_preflight will abort otherwise; (b) outreach-log.json mutated only in the store, not committed; (c) cohort must be sentiment-neutral to satisfy G2/Capterra ToS. Anthropic-terms guardrail: this graph only calls Claude for copy/classification — it must never be extended to train/distill/evaluate any model. First production runs should be dry_run=true to validate the batch/interrupt flow before any live email.

trigger:
Three entry modes, all into one compiled graph deployed on LangGraph Platform (managed Cloud SaaS):
1. CRON (primary, daily 06:00 UTC via the platform Crons API) — reconcile lifecycle: pull Scheduler user/subscription state, recompute segment membership (trial_users, trial_expiring, inactive_users, churned_users, team_creators) against brevo-event-sync-config.json conditions, diff vs Brevo lists, and stage list add/remove + send batches for approval. This directly fixes the "contact list population is manual" pain point without needing app-side webhooks first.
2. WEBHOOK (event-driven) — POST to the deployment's /runs endpoint from the Scheduler app on user_signup / trial_expiring / user_inactive (the inbound webhook paths already declared in brevo-automation-config.json). Each event creates a thread that enriches the contact, computes the right workflow step, and stages the send.
3. ADHOC (human/CLI) — replaces the four slash commands (/brevo-sync-all, /brevo-upload-template, /brevo-test-email, /brevo-setup-automation) as graph invocations with a `mode` input. Template sync and test sends route through the same approval gate.
Idempotency: every run keys on (contact_email, workflow, step_order, trial_end_date|inactivity_bucket) stored in the LangGraph Store so a re-run or duplicate webhook never double-sends.
process:
Brevo Email Marketing & Automation. The agent is an ORCHESTRATION layer over the Brevo v3 REST API and the existing config/template artifacts in EscalateLabs/marketing — it calls the LLM only to draft/QA copy and to explain proposed diffs to the human; it never trains, fine-tunes, distills, or evaluates any model (Anthropic Consumer Terms compliant — pure app orchestration).

What v1 owns:
- Template lifecycle: read /email-templates/*.html, compute contentHash, diff against brevo-templates.json, and create/update Brevo SMTP templates (port of automation/src/brevo/index.js uploadTemplate/syncAllTemplates into typed tools). Writes back template IDs + hashes + uploadedAt.
- Segment reconciliation: evaluate the documented conditions from brevo-event-sync-config.json (e.g. TRIAL_END_DATE <= now+7d AND TRIAL_STATUS=='active') to compute desired list membership, diff vs live Brevo lists, and produce an add/remove plan.
- Lifecycle send orchestration: for each eligible contact pick the correct workflow step (welcome / trial_expiring / re_engagement) from brevo-automation-config.json, resolve templateKey -> Brevo templateId, and build a transactional send batch with FIRSTNAME/EMAIL params.
- Guardrails the human asked for, enforced IN-GRAPH before any send: frequency cap (max 2 emails/week, min 24h between sends — tracked per-contact in Store), suppression/unsubscribe check (pull Brevo blocklist + unsubscribes and filter the batch — closes the "no suppression/blocklist sync" gap), and bounce/complaint exclusion (read Brevo transactional events, suppress hard-bounced/spam-complaint addresses — closes "no bounce/complaint handling").
- Every list mutation and every send is staged into a typed "proposed_actions" object and HALTED at an interrupt for human approval (per crm-brevo-automation-playbook.md "Human Approval Scope" + workspace rule "no send without approval"). Test sends (test-email mode) also gate, but to a single allowlisted internal address.

What v1 explicitly defers (named so scope is honest): creating new Brevo automation *workflows* programmatically (v1 reads the config and sends step-by-step itself rather than provisioning Brevo's native automation engine), A/B testing setup, and segment rules beyond the documented lists. These map to the deferred items in the process pain_points.
graph_steps: ingest_and_route — entry node. Reads `mode` from graph input (cron_reconcile | webhook_event | sync_templates | upload_template | test_email). Loads brevo-automation-config.json, brevo-templates.json, brevo-event-sync-config.json into state. Conditional edge dispatches to the matching branch. State: TypedDict with messages (add_messages reducer), mode, config blobs, run_context (source, dry_run), proposed_actions=[], decision=None.

fetch_state — (cron/webhook branches) Tool node. Pulls current truth: Scheduler user/subscription records (read-only GET against scheduler-api / Firestore export, NEVER writes app data) for cron; or the single event payload for webhook. Also GETs live Brevo lists, contact attributes, blocklist/unsubscribes, and recent transactional events (bounces/complaints). No mutation.

compute_desired_state — Pure-Python node (no LLM, deterministic). Evaluates the documented segment conditions and workflow-step selection. Emits desired list membership + candidate send batch keyed by idempotency tuple. Checks the Store for already-sent keys and drops duplicates.

apply_guardrails — Deterministic filter node. Removes from the candidate batch: suppressed/unsubscribed addresses, hard-bounce/complaint addresses, and any contact over the frequency cap (max 2/week, 24h min gap) using per-contact send history in the Store. Produces the final `proposed_actions` (list adds/removes + filtered send batch +, for sync modes, template create/update diffs).

draft_and_explain — The ONLY LLM node. For template sync it can QA/normalize subject lines and flag broken merge tags (e.g. the known fake-FIRSTNAME risk); for sends it writes a concise human-readable summary of exactly who gets what and why each was included/excluded. Output is advisory text attached to proposed_actions — it does not decide to send.

human_approval_gate — interrupt() node (LangGraph human-in-the-loop). Surfaces proposed_actions + the LLM explanation and BLOCKS. Resume value: {approve|reject|edit}. On reject -> END with audit log. On edit -> loop back to apply_guardrails with the human's trimmed batch. Nothing irreversible runs before this returns approve.

execute_brevo_mutations — Tool node, runs ONLY after approval. Performs the Brevo writes: createSmtpTemplate/updateSmtpTemplate (template modes), addToList/removeFromList (reconcile), sendTransacEmail (sends, batched with retry+rate-limit backoff). Each successful send writes its idempotency key + timestamp to the Store for the frequency cap.

persist_and_report — Writes back template IDs/hashes to brevo-templates.json and listIds to brevo-automation-config.json (via a PR or artifact, not a silent commit), appends a daily anomalies/approvals log entry per the playbook, and emits a run summary (counts sent/suppressed/capped/failed). Edge to END.
tools_integrations: Brevo v3 REST API — transactional sends (sendTransacEmail), SMTP templates (create/update/getSmtpTemplates), contacts & lists (createContact, updateContact, lists/{id}/contacts/add|remove), blocklist & unsubscribes (GET contacts with blacklisted filter), transactional event log (bounces/complaints). Wraps @getbrevo/brevo semantics as typed LangGraph tools.

Scheduler app data source (READ-ONLY) — scheduler-api Go endpoints or a Firestore export of users/subscriptions/teams to compute segments; the agent must never write app/user data (Firestore-write safety per workspace memory).
LangGraph Platform Store (BaseStore) — per-contact send history for frequency caps + idempotency keys; survives across runs. No semantic index needed for v1 (store.index can be omitted).
LangGraph Platform Postgres checkpointer — auto-provisioned; do NOT pass a custom checkpointer/store to .compile().
LangGraph Crons API — schedules the daily cron_reconcile run.
Repo artifacts (read + PR-write) — /email-templates/*.html, data/brevo-templates.json, data/brevo-automation-config.json, data/brevo-event-sync-config.json in EscalateLabs/marketing.
Anthropic Claude API (langchain-anthropic) — single LLM node for copy QA + approval-summary generation only.
Optional notify channel (Slack/Gmail MCP) — push the approval request to a human; the interrupt is the real gate, this is just the surfacing.
approval_gates: HARD human-in-the-loop interrupt() at human_approval_gate is mandatory before ANY irreversible Brevo action — every list add/remove, every template create/update, and every transactional/test send. The graph cannot reach execute_brevo_mutations without a resume value of approve. Test sends are additionally restricted to a single allowlisted internal address (no external recipients in test mode). Reject ends the run with an audit entry; Edit loops back so the human can trim the batch. A `dry_run` run_context flag lets a run stop at draft_and_explain and emit the plan with zero side effects (useful for the daily reconcile preview). This satisfies the workspace rules: no send/delete/overwrite without explicit approval, and the playbook's Human Approval Scope (campaign approval + suppression-list validation).
secrets_needed: SCHEDULER_API_TOKEN (read-only, for segment data; or GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS for a Firestore read export)
BREVO_SENDER_EMAIL / BREVO_SENDER_NAME (hello@scheduler-systems.com / John from Scheduler — env, not hardcoded)
TEST_EMAIL_ALLOWLIST (comma-separated internal addresses for test_email mode)
deploy_notes:
Target: LangGraph Platform managed Cloud SaaS (api.smith.langchain.com).

langgraph.json:
- dependencies: ["."] with a pyproject.toml pinning langgraph, langchain-anthropic, httpx (Brevo REST via httpx — avoids the heavy SDK), pydantic.
- graphs: {"brevo_lifecycle": "./agent/graph.py:graph"} where `graph = builder.compile()` with NO checkpointer/store args (platform injects Postgres + Store).
- python_version: "3.12", image_distro: "wolfi", pip_installer: "uv".
- env: ".env" locally; on the managed platform inject all secrets via the Deployments UI/API, never commit them (security rule: never hardcode secrets).
- http: mount a small FastAPI app only if a custom signature-verifying webhook route is wanted; otherwise use the built-in /runs endpoint guarded by the platform API key + BREVO_WEBHOOK_SECRET check inside ingest_and_route.

State export: build a StateGraph(State) with add_messages reducer on messages, compile to module-level `graph`. Use interrupt()/Command(resume=...) for the gate (current HIL pattern), not the deprecated interrupt_before.

Deploy flow: `langgraph build` locally to validate, then deploy via CLI or the LangSmith Deployments UI pointing at the EscalateLabs/marketing repo (or a dedicated agent subfolder/worktree per workspace worktree-discipline — do NOT edit the live marketing checkout in place). Provision the daily cron after first successful deploy via the Crons API.

Rollout: ship with dry_run defaulted TRUE for the first week so the daily reconcile only produces approval previews (zero sends) until segment math is verified against live Brevo numbers — mirrors the Scheduler IDOR Phase-0 'measure in prod before mutating' lesson. Wire app-side inbound webhooks (signup/trial/inactivity) AFTER the cron reconcile path is trusted, since cron reconciliation already closes the manual-list-population gap on its own.

Compliance: orchestration only — no ML model dev/training/eval anywhere in the graph (Anthropic Consumer Terms / AGENTS.md guardrail). The single LLM node is copy-QA + summarization, which is permitted application use.

Tie to a documented GitHub issue in EscalateLabs/marketing before implementation (workspace rule: always a documented issue first).
impact: Closes four of the named pain points without waiting on app-side work: (1) automated contact-list population via daily segment reconciliation instead of manual; (2) suppression/blocklist sync now enforced in-graph before every send; (3) bounce/complaint suppression now automated; (4) frequency caps (2/week, 24h gap) enforced programmatically with persistent per-contact history. Consolidates the four ad-hoc slash commands and the mock-prone index.js into one auditable, approval-gated, idempotent deployed service. Revenue-relevant: the trial_expiring and re_engagement sequences target conversion and win-back for the live Scheduler paying-user funnel (per revenue-first priority), while the hard approval gate + test-allowlist protect sender reputation and avoid the prior fake-FIRSTNAME / biased-collection landmines flagged for the marketing pipeline.

process: Review Request Campaigns (G2/Capterra/GetApp/TrustRadius/SaaSHub) — milestone-triggered, honest-feedback review asks for Scheduler and GAL, gated behind human approval per the Growth Operating Contract.
trigger: Webhook ingest on LangGraph Platform (POST to the deployment's custom HTTP route mounted via langgraph.json `http.app`). Two real producers: (1) Scheduler/GAL product apps fire a milestone event (3+ schedules, 5+ logins, 10+ actions/0 errors, 1st GAL dispatch, 7+ dispatches/week) — currently UN-built, so v1 also accepts (2) a batch/cron-driven kickoff that scans Growth Console `reviewProfiles[]` + a milestone queue. Each run is keyed by (userId, milestone) and runs the graph once per candidate. A scheduled LangGraph cron (every 6h) drains the 14-day-delayed email queue. NO send happens without passing the approval interrupt.
graph_steps: ingest_event: validate webhook/cron payload against an EventSchema (userId, email, productName in {Scheduler,GAL}, milestone, milestoneSummary, occurredAt, source). Reject malformed events to END with status=rejected. State uses a TypedDict with add_messages reducer; NO checkpointer/store passed to .compile() (platform injects Postgres).

load_profile: read-only fetch of the user's Growth Console reviewProfiles[] entry + contact record (tools: gc_get_review_profile, brevo_get_contact). Resolves lastAskAt per milestone, existing-review flags per tracked platform, marketing opt-out, and open-support-ticket flag. Pure reads — no side effects.

eligibility_gate: deterministic rule engine (NOT an LLM) encoding the campaign's hard send rules — one ask per milestone per 60 days (reviewProfiles[].lastAskAt), skip if already reviewed on ANY tracked platform, skip if open support ticket, skip if recent negative survey, skip if opted out of marketing, and cross-app 60-day dedupe (Scheduler+GAL share the email key). Branch: ineligible -> record_skip -> END; eligible -> select_channel.

select_channel: deterministic channel + platform picker. Channel = in-app vs email per the campaign tables (email path is the only one this agent SENDS; in-app prompts are owned by product teams and only emitted as a recommendation event). Platform priority G2 > Capterra > GetApp > TrustRadius > SaaSHub, choosing the first tracked platform the user has NOT reviewed, with reviewUrl sourced live from reviewProfiles[].reviewUrl (never hardcoded).

draft_request: LLM node (Claude) renders the milestone-triggered copy from email-templates/review_request_milestone_triggered.md — honest-feedback framing enforced via a system prompt + a post-generation lint check that rejects positive-bias phrasing ('5 stars','positive review','great rating'). Fills merge params (FIRSTNAME fallback 'there', PRODUCT_NAME, MILESTONE_SUMMARY, REVIEW_URL, PLATFORM_NAME) and picks A/B subject variant A by default. Output is a structured draft object only.

HUMAN_APPROVAL [interrupt]: interrupt() pauses the graph and surfaces the rendered draft (subject, body, recipient, platform, milestone, dedupe evidence) to a human via the Growth Console email-draft workflow. Resume payload = {decision: approved|edited|rejected, editedBody?}. This is the mandatory gate from GROWTH_OPERATING_CONTRACT.md; no path to send_email bypasses it.
route_decision: deterministic branch on the resumed decision. rejected -> record_skip(reason=human_rejected) -> END. approved/edited -> send_email.

send_email: the ONLY irreversible action node. Creates+sends the Brevo transactional email (tool: brevo_send_review_request -> automation/src/brevo runBrevo/send path) from hello@scheduler-systems.com. Idempotency key = (userId, milestone, 60d-window) to survive retries. On Brevo error, route to error_handler (pause that milestone, do not retry-blast).

writeback: write status back to Growth Console reviewProfiles[] — set lastAskAt=now for the milestone, askChannel, platformAsked, brevoMessageId, status=sent. Also schedule the review-completion poll. Tool: gc_update_review_profile (scoped write, idempotent upsert).

record_skip / error_handler / END: skip writes a non-sending audit row (reason) to reviewProfiles[] history; error_handler records the failure, flags the milestone as paused, and emits an alert for human inspection. All terminal paths converge on END with a typed RunResult (status, reason, messageId?).
impact: Removes the two manual bottlenecks called out as pain points — manual reviewProfiles[].lastAskAt dedupe checks and manual draft assembly — while preserving the non-negotiable human-approval gate. Turns a draft-stage, fully-manual playbook into a deterministic, auditable, approval-gated pipeline. Revenue impact is INDIRECT (review collection -> social proof -> conversion), so it ranks below Scheduler revenue/paywall work per the revenue-first priority; honest value is operator-time saved and consistency/compliance (no double-asks, no positive-bias copy, no opted-out sends). Blocked-by-others on the real per-app milestone webhooks (Scheduler + GAL product teams) — v1 ships the orchestration + email + writeback against the cron/batch trigger and a stub webhook, ready for the apps to wire in. Tie all work to marketing#389 (absorbs #153, #164) per the always-documented-issue rule.
tools_integrations: Growth Console reviewProfiles[] (read: gc_get_review_profile; write: gc_update_review_profile) — Firestore-backed; the source of truth for dedupe (lastAskAt), review URLs, and outcome writeback
Brevo transactional email via automation/src/brevo (brevo_get_contact, brevo_send_review_request) — sender hello@scheduler-systems.com; the only send surface
Claude (Anthropic) as the LLM for draft_request only — orchestration/copy-rendering ONLY, no model training/eval/distillation (respects the Anthropic-terms guardrail in AGENTS.md)
LangGraph Platform custom HTTP route (langgraph.json http.app) for the milestone webhook + a LangGraph cron for the 14-day delayed-email drain and review-completion polling
Support/ticketing + survey signal source for the skip-if-open-ticket / skip-if-negative-survey rules (read-only flag check; wired to whatever Growth Console already exposes)

Reference assets (read-only at design time, baked into prompts/config): data/campaigns/review-collection-g2-2026.md, email-templates/review_request_milestone_triggered.md, data/outreach/gal/g2-design-partner-review-pack.md
approval_gates: Single mandatory human-in-the-loop gate via LangGraph interrupt() at HUMAN_APPROVAL — every email is rendered, then paused for a human to approve/edit/reject through the Growth Console email-draft workflow (draft -> approved -> sent), exactly as mandated by GROWTH_OPERATING_CONTRACT.md ('sending or approving outreach' and 'review asks to named partners or customers' are Human-required). send_email is unreachable without an approved/edited resume; rejected and ineligible paths terminate without sending. GAL design-partner asks (g2-design-partner-review-pack) stay OUT of the automated send path — the agent may draft them but the contract requires they go from a real human mailbox, so for those the agent stops at the draft and hands off. No other node performs an irreversible/external-visible action; all reads are side-effect-free and the single writeback is an idempotent upsert.
deploy_notes: Target: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (Cloud SaaS managed). langgraph.json: graphs {"review_campaign":"./agent/graph.py:graph"}, dependencies ["."], python_version "3.12", image_distro "wolfi", pip_installer "uv", http {"app":"./agent/http.py:app"} mounting the signed milestone webhook + the GAL-handoff draft endpoint, plus checkpointer.ttl for run cleanup. CRITICAL: compile with builder.compile() and do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= — the platform injects production Postgres; an InMemorySaver would be ignored in the hosted runtime and would break the interrupt/resume that the approval gate depends on. Export the compiled graph as module-level `graph`. Secrets injected via the deployment UI/API, never committed (honor security.md 'never hardcode secrets' + the .env stays a placeholder). Deploy via `langgraph deploy` (CLI) or Git-connected build; verify the interrupt round-trips (interrupt -> Growth Console -> resume) in a staging assistant before pointing real producers at it. Webhook auth via WEBHOOK_SIGNING_SECRET HMAC check in http.py before the graph runs. v1 ships against the cron/batch trigger because the per-app milestone webhooks are not yet built (pain point) — leave the signed webhook route live but stubbed for Scheduler/GAL to integrate against later. Add a kill-switch env (CAMPAIGN_PAUSED) checked in ingest_event, and per-milestone pause state in error_handler so a single bad milestone pauses itself, not the whole campaign (per the template's deliverability guidance). NOT for AI/ML model work — orchestration calling Claude only; this stays within the Anthropic-terms guardrail.

process: Directory Submission & Backlink Acquisition — a managed LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments) agent that orchestrates the EXISTING growth-cli/automation submission stack. It does NOT replace Playwright/GAL execution; it is the durable conductor that selects backlink targets, builds the submission prompt from the canonical SubmissionProfile, dispatches the actual browser run to the existing GAL/agent-browser/Browserbase path, interprets the structured DispatchResult, persists job state to Firestore, and HARD-STOPS at a human-in-the-loop interrupt whenever the run reports needsHuman (CAPTCHA, Cloudflare, ownership transfer, auth wall, login failure). The Mac stays a conductor; the heavy Playwright/CAPTCHA-audio work runs on GAL/Browserbase, matching the "orchestrate local, execute on cluster" rule. The agent only calls Claude/LLMs for orchestration + prompt assembly — no model training/distillation (Anthropic Consumer Terms guardrail).
trigger:
Three entrypoints, all converging on one compiled graph:
1) Operator-initiated from Growth Console (growth.scheduler-systems.com/backlinks) — operator picks a target (or "batch all not_started in tier N") and the Console POSTs a run to the LangGraph deployment with {targets[], mode: submit|update, dryRun}. This is the canonical, contract-blessed surface.
2) Resume — operator answers a pending human-in-the-loop interrupt (CAPTCHA cleared / ownership confirmed / abort) via Command(resume=...), and the same thread continues from the interrupted node.
3) Scheduled recheck (separate assistant on the same deployment, invoked by the existing daily GitHub Actions cron) — status rechecks + email-verification polling + drift crawl only. Recheck is agent-safe per the contract and takes NO submission action; if it finds a row that needs (re)submission it creates a pending Firestore job and notifies the operator, never auto-submitting.
graph_steps: load_run_context: read /data/backlink-targets.json (opportunities[]) + fetch canonical SubmissionProfile (Firestore seo_backlink_metadata via growth-cli submission-profile) + ActiveCredential for the target's owner account; resolve which of the 5 named directories (BetaList, BetaPage, LaunchingNext, StartupRanking, AlternativeTo) or universal-fallback handler applies. State seeded into typed StateGraph (TargetState).

guard_and_select: filter targets — skip status in {submitted,approved,pending_approval}; skip automation_difficulty=hard or login_method=google_oauth and route them straight to needs_human queue (contract: OAuth/ownership transfers are human-only); de-dupe against open seo_backlink_jobs. Emits an ordered worklist; conditional edge to END if worklist empty.

create_job: for the current target, write a Firestore seo_backlink_jobs doc (status=in_progress, startedAt) via the existing createBacklinkJob/updateJobStatus contract — single source of truth, idempotent on (target,profile) so resumes don't double-create.

build_submission_prompt: LLM (Claude) assembles the directory-specific submission payload strictly from SubmissionProfile fields (brandName, descriptions, pricingPlans, categories, socialLinks) honoring submissionRules.forbiddenCompetitors. Orchestration-only LLM use. Secrets/credentials are referenced by secret_ref, never inlined into the prompt.

dispatch_submission: call the EXISTING execution path (runGalAgentDispatch / submit-dispatcher) — GAL API background session over browser-gal MCP, or Browserbase provider — to run the real Playwright submission on the cluster. Returns the established structured DispatchResult {success, needsHuman, blocker, final_url, screenshot_paths, signalType}. This node performs the only irreversible external action and is the approval boundary's downstream guard.

interpret_result: branch on DispatchResult. success && !needsHuman -> persist_success. needsHuman || blocker in {captcha_blocked,cloudflare_blocked,login_failed,auth_wall,url_not_found,unknown_blocker} -> human_gate. signalType=transient_error -> retry_or_fail (bounded exponential backoff, max 3, respecting the existing rate-limiter; then mark failed).

human_gate (interrupt): call LangGraph interrupt() with a typed payload {directory, blocker, screenshotUrls, lastUrl, suggestedAction}. Execution durably pauses (Postgres checkpointer). Operator is notified (Slack/Console) and resumes with Command(resume={decision: cleared|owner_confirmed|skip|abort}). cleared/owner_confirmed -> re-enter dispatch_submission from current page; skip -> mark job needs_human and advance; abort -> mark failed. NOTHING is submitted past this gate without explicit human resume.

persist_success: updateJobStatus(completed, result={liveUrl:final_url, submittedAt}); write seo_backlink_metadata_snapshot; flip the opportunities[] row status to submitted/pending_approval; attach screenshot evidence refs.
retry_or_fail: bounded retry on transient_error; on exhaustion updateJobStatus(failed, lastError) and route to human_gate as a soft escalation rather than silently dropping.
advance_or_finish: pop next target from worklist -> loop back to create_job; when worklist exhausted, emit run summary (counts: completed/failed/needs_human) into Firestore + Console and END.
approval_gates:
HARD human-in-the-loop interrupt() gates, all enforced server-side in the graph (cannot be bypassed by the Console caller):
1) PRE-SUBMIT CAPTCHA/anti-bot gate — any blocker in {captcha_blocked, cloudflare_blocked} pauses before any token submission; operator clears the challenge in a visible browser then resumes (matches GROWTH_OPERATING_CONTRACT 'GAL attempts audio challenge first; falls back to human').
2) OWNERSHIP/AUTH gate — login_method=google_oauth, magic_link claims, auth_wall, or login_failed pause for the named owner-account holder (contract: 'ownership transfers tied to personal accounts' are human-required). No personal-account action proceeds without resume.
3) FINAL-SUBMIT confirmation for first-ever submission to a directory OR when dryRun=false on a tier-1 target — interrupt to confirm the assembled listing copy before the one irreversible POST. (Re-submissions/updates to already-onboarded directories can be auto if no blocker.)
4) RECHECK is read-only by design — the recheck assistant has no dispatch_submission node, so it cannot submit; any required submission becomes a pending job + operator notification.
Resume contract: Command(resume={decision}) where decision in {cleared, owner_confirmed, skip, abort}. Every gate is logged to seo_backlink_jobs with who/when. Aligns with the workspace 'no send/no irreversible action without explicit human approval' rule.
tools_integrations: Firestore (firebase-admin) — collections seo_backlinks, seo_backlink_jobs, seo_backlink_metadata_snapshots; project scheduler-systems-web; reuse existing firestore-backlinks.js + backlink-jobs.ts contracts
growth-cli library surface (submission-profile, backlink-jobs, backlink-targets, credential-resolver) — wrapped as LangGraph tool nodes; do not reimplement
GAL API dispatch (GAL_API_URL/GAL_API_KEY) over browser-gal / browser-gal-local MCP — the actual Playwright execution path (cluster, not local)
Browserbase provider (AGENT_BROWSER_PROVIDER=browserbase) as cloud browser alternative
/data/backlink-targets.json — opportunities[] target metadata + drift baseline
GCP Secret Manager — directory passwords/login identities by secret_ref (fetched by execution layer, never by the LLM)
Gmail/email polling (existing automation/src/utils/gmail.js) — verification-email polling in the recheck assistant only
Slack (notify operator that a thread is interrupted / awaiting resume) and the Growth Console UI (run trigger + resume)
GitHub Actions (existing daily cron) — invokes the recheck assistant
Anthropic Claude (orchestration + submission-copy assembly ONLY — no training/distillation)
LangGraph Platform managed Postgres checkpointer + store (provisioned by platform; do NOT pass a custom checkpointer to .compile())
secrets_needed: GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS (or GOOGLE_SHEETS_SERVICE_ACCOUNT JSON) for firebase-admin / Firestore
GCP_SECRET_MANAGER access (for directory login secret_refs, resolved by the execution layer)
TWOCAPTCHA_API_KEY (optional — audio/CAPTCHA-solve attempt before human fallback)
GROWTH_CONSOLE_SHARED_SECRET (verify run requests originate from the Console; enforced in langgraph.json custom auth.path)
deploy_notes: Target = LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS, api.smith.langchain.com). langgraph.json: python_version 3.11, image_distro wolfi, pip_installer uv, dependencies ["."], graphs {"submit": "./agent/graph.py:graph", "recheck": "./agent/recheck.py:graph"} (two addressable assistants on one deployment), auth {path: "./auth.py:console_auth"} to validate the Console shared secret, env injected via UI/API (NEVER committed). CRITICAL: .compile() with NO checkpointer/store args — the platform injects production Postgres; interrupt()/Command(resume=) durability depends on it. State is a typed StateGraph(TargetState) with add_messages reducer for the LLM sub-steps and an explicit worklist/cursor for the per-target loop; use a factory graph if per-run config (provider, dryRun) is needed. The real Playwright/CAPTCHA/GAL execution stays OUT of the LangGraph container (it calls the GAL API/Browserbase) so the deployment stays light and the Mac is never the executor — consistent with the orchestrate-local/execute-on-cluster memory. checkpointer.ttl set generously (interrupts may sit hours awaiting an operator). Wire the existing GitHub Actions daily cron to invoke the 'recheck' assistant; the Console invokes 'submit'. Tie all work to a tracked GitHub issue in EscalateLabs/marketing per workspace policy. v1 scope-limit: only the 5 named directories + universal fallback; expansion waves are Project Master work per the Growth Operating Contract. Build in a worktree (StratusCloudLabs/.../worktrees or EscalateLabs/marketing/worktrees), not the live checkout. Code grounded in: /Users/scheduler-systems/Documents/scheduler-systems-ltd/EscalateLabs/marketing/growth-cli/src/lib/{types.ts,backlink-jobs.ts}, /automation/src/directories/submit-dispatcher.js (runGalAgentDispatch + needsHuman/blocker enum), /automation/src/utils/{firestore-backlinks.js,captcha.js}, /data/backlink-targets.json, and GROWTH_OPERATING_CONTRACT.md.
impact: Low-to-moderate revenue impact (SEO backlinks are slow-compounding, not direct paid conversion — flagged per revenue-first rule: this is NOT the fastest cash lever; the existing stack already works, so the agent's value is durability/resumability, not new capability). Concrete wins: (1) durable resumable runs — a CAPTCHA at directory #4 of a batch no longer loses progress on the first three; the thread parks at an interrupt and resumes hours later. (2) Single Firestore source-of-truth enforced by the graph, removing the 'merge != deploy / scattered state' class of bug seen elsewhere in the workspace. (3) Contract-faithful human gates make the CAPTCHA/ownership-transfer fallback auditable (who cleared what, when) instead of ad-hoc. Risk surface is well-bounded: the only irreversible action (the submission POST) sits behind a server-side interrupt the Console caller cannot skip, and recheck is structurally read-only.

process: Cohort Export for Marketing Campaigns — export user cohorts from the product database (Firestore priority-118da, optionally GAL Postgres) into Brevo contact lists for lifecycle email campaigns. Five cohorts: trial_users, trial_expiring (<=7d), inactive_users (7d+), churned_users (30d+), team_creators (created team, zero invites). The agent computes each cohort, governs the contact attribute set (FIRSTNAME, LASTNAME, EMAIL, TRIAL_START_DATE, TRIAL_END_DATE, LAST_LOGIN, PLAN_TYPE, TEAM_SIZE, FEATURES_USED), reconciles against the live Brevo list (additions + removals of converted/churned members), and applies a GDPR/suppression filter before any write. Replaces the missing export pipeline; the existing /automation/src/brevo/index.js only syncs email templates, not cohorts. This is pure orchestration over Firestore/Brevo APIs and Claude (LLM) for summarization/diff-explanation — NO model training/distillation. Every Brevo write (create list, add contacts, remove contacts) is irreversible and gated behind a human-in-the-loop interrupt that surfaces a full diff for approval before execution. Secrets (BREVO_API_KEY, Firestore service-account JSON, GAL DB DSN, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) come from LangGraph Platform env injection, never hardcoded.
trigger: Two triggers. (1) Scheduled refresh via LangGraph Platform Cron (e.g. daily 06:00 UTC) per cohort, producing an approval-pending run. (2) On-demand / webhook invocation (the config's inbound user-signup, trial-expiring, user-inactive webhooks mounted on the custom http app) to refresh a single cohort. Each run STOPS at the approval interrupt; a human resumes with approve/reject/edit to commit the Brevo writes.
graph_steps: load_config: read /data/brevo-automation-config.json — cohort definitions, attribute schema, frequencyCaps, webhooks; resolve which cohort(s) this run targets and current Brevo listId (may be null = needs creation).
ensure_list (conditional): if listId is null, propose creating the Brevo contact list. Routes into the approval gate as a CREATE_LIST action; does not create until approved.

extract_cohort: query the product DB (Firestore priority-118da via service account; GAL Postgres read-replica optional) for raw member rows matching the cohort predicate (trial window, last_login age, team-without-invites join). Read-only.

normalize_attributes: map raw rows to the governed attribute set (FIRSTNAME/LASTNAME/EMAIL/TRIAL_START_DATE/TRIAL_END_DATE/LAST_LOGIN/PLAN_TYPE/TEAM_SIZE/FEATURES_USED); drop rows with missing/invalid email; enforce no fake/blank FIRSTNAME merge fallback (the known G2-merge landmine).

governance_filter: apply GDPR + suppression rules — exclude unsubscribed/blocklisted contacts (respectUnsubscribes), exclude beyond-retention records, exclude opted-out; emit a compliance report. Hard-fail the run if suppression source is unavailable rather than sending unfiltered.

diff_reconcile: pull current Brevo list membership, compute additions and removals (converted/churned to remove). LLM (Claude) summarizes the diff into a human-readable approval brief (counts, sample rows, risk notes) — orchestration/summarization only.

approval_gate (interrupt): NodeInterrupt/interrupt() surfaces {action, list, +N adds, -M removes, compliance summary, sample contacts}. Run halts. Human resumes via Platform API with approve / reject / edit-scope. Nothing has been written to Brevo yet.

apply_writes (post-approval): execute approved Brevo mutations only — createContactList (if approved), batch addContactToList / removeContactFromList, update contact attributes. Idempotent, chunked, rate-limit-aware; writes resolved listId back so config null is filled.

finalize: persist run record to the LangGraph store (cohort, counts, approver, timestamp, Brevo response ids), update listId mapping, emit metrics/audit log; on partial failure, record what committed for safe re-run.
impact: Operationalizes the currently-null cohort pipeline: turns 5 config-defined cohorts into live, refreshed Brevo lists that feed the welcome / trial-expiring / re-engagement campaigns. Removes manual list creation and manual cleanup of converted/churned members, adds daily/weekly auto-refresh, and bakes in the GDPR/suppression + no-fake-merge guardrails that are currently absent (and that have already bitten the team on G2/Brevo sends). Revenue-relevant: trial_expiring + team_creators cohorts directly drive trial-to-paid conversion on the live RevenueCat path, while the approval gate keeps it safe against accidental mass-sends to suppressed or non-consented users.
approval_gates: Single mandatory human-in-the-loop interrupt (approval_gate node) before ANY irreversible Brevo write. It batches all mutations for the run — list creation, contact additions, and contact removals — into one approval brief containing exact counts, a compliance/suppression summary, and sample contacts. Resume options: approve (commit all), reject (abort, nothing written), or edit (e.g. approve adds but defer removals, or cap batch size). No auto-approve path. Cron-triggered runs also stop here — they create a pending-approval run, they do not auto-send. Secrets are never echoed into the approval payload.
deploy_notes: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS). langgraph.json: python_version 3.12, image_distro wolfi, dependencies ['.'], graphs {'cohort_export':'./agent/graph.py:graph'}, env injected via UI/API (NOT committed), http.app mounts a FastAPI app exposing the 3 inbound webhook routes (/api/webhooks/brevo/user-signup, trial-expiring, user-inactive), store enabled for run history/listId mapping. DO NOT pass checkpointer=/store= to .compile() — the platform injects Postgres persistence; the interrupt requires the platform checkpointer to pause/resume. Use LangGraph Platform Cron for the scheduled per-cohort refresh. Provision read-only DB access (Firestore SA scoped to read; GAL Postgres read-replica DSN). Brevo SDK (@getbrevo/brevo equivalent / REST) writes only after approval. Auth on the http app (auth.path) so webhooks aren't open. Persist resolved Brevo listId back to the config/store so the null IDs get filled exactly once.
secrets_needed: WEBHOOK_SHARED_SECRET (auth for inbound http app routes)
tools_integrations: Firestore (priority-118da) read-only via Firebase Admin SDK
GAL Postgres read-replica (optional)
Brevo API (contact lists: create list, add/remove contact, update attributes, read membership, read suppression/unsubscribe)
Anthropic Claude API (diff + compliance summarization, orchestration only — no model training)
LangGraph Platform Cron (scheduled refresh)
LangGraph custom FastAPI http app (inbound webhooks)
LangGraph Store (run history + cohort->listId mapping)
config file /data/brevo-automation-config.json
