process: Unsubscribe & Blocklist Management — reconcile Brevo suppression state (unsubscribes, hard/soft bounces, spam complaints) back into the Firestore contacts CRM, verify webhook endpoint health, enforce the 180-day sunset policy for unengaged contacts, and maintain a GDPR-grade audit trail. Every action that mutates a contact's deliverability state (suppress in CRM, sunset-remove, GDPR erase) or that re-enables sending is approval-gated via a LangGraph human-in-the-loop interrupt before it touches Brevo or Firestore. Read/diff is fully automated; write/erase/unsuppress is human-approved.
trigger: Primary: LangGraph Cloud cron (scheduled assistant run) once daily at 06:00 Europe/Jerusalem for the full reconcile + sunset sweep. Secondary: webhook-driven near-real-time path — Brevo outbound event webhook (unsubscribed / hard_bounce / spam) POSTs to the deployment's custom http.app route, which creates a run on the same graph with a single-contact payload so unsubscribe requests are respected immediately. Manual trigger: on-demand run from LangGraph Studio / API for backfill, a specific contact GDPR-erasure, or webhook-health spot check.
graph_steps: ingest_event: normalize input — either a cron 'full_sweep' payload or a single Brevo webhook event {type, email, reason, ts}. Validate signature/shape; route to the right lane via conditional edge. State carries run_mode, candidates[], diffs[], audit_batch[].

check_webhook_health (cron lane only): call Brevo GET /webhooks + a synthetic round-trip probe against the deployment's own /api/webhooks/brevo/event route; record last-received-event age. If endpoint is unhealthy, set state.webhook_degraded=true and flag in the summary (read-only; no mutation).

fetch_brevo_suppression: page Brevo's transactional blocklist / contacts API for unsubscribed, hardBounced, softBounced, complained, plus unengaged (zero opens) signals. Read-only. Accumulate into candidates[] with reason + source timestamp.
fetch_crm_state: read Firestore (priority-118da) contacts where status in {active, ...} for the candidate emails (and, in sweep mode, scan for lastEngagementAt older than 180d). Read-only.

compute_diff: deterministic reconcile (LLM used only to summarize/explain, never to decide eligibility). Produce three buckets: (a) suppress_in_crm — Brevo-suppressed but CRM still active; (b) sunset_candidates — 180d no engagement, not already suppressed; (c) gdpr_erase — explicit right-to-be-forgotten requests. Attach evidence to each row.

approval_gate (interrupt): emit an interrupt with the proposed mutation set (counts + per-row evidence + dry-run preview). Human reviews in Studio/Inbox and resumes with approve / reject / edit-subset (Command resume). Unsubscribe-respect writes (status->unsubscribed) can be policy-marked auto-approve in config since they are legally required and non-destructive; sunset-removal and GDPR-erase always require explicit human approval. No tool call to Brevo/Firestore happens before resume.

apply_mutations (post-approval, idempotent): for approved rows only — write contact.status='unsubscribed'/'suppressed' to Firestore, push CRM-originated unsubscribes up to Brevo blocklist (two-way sync), execute sunset removal, and perform GDPR erasure (delete PII / Brevo contact). Each write keyed by contact id + reason hash for idempotency; partial-failure rows are collected, not retried blindly.

write_audit_trail: append an immutable audit record per mutation (who-approved, what, reason, source event, before/after, run id, timestamp) to a Firestore suppression_audit collection. Always runs, even on partial failure, so the trail is complete.

report_and_notify: post a run summary (diff counts, applied vs skipped, webhook health, deliverability-target deltas like bounce/complaint rate) to the marketing Slack channel and as the graph's output. Read-only/idempotent; Slack post itself is informational, not approval-gated.
tools_integrations: Brevo API (@getbrevo/brevo / REST): read suppression+blocklist+contacts, read webhook config, push CRM-originated unsubscribes to blocklist, delete contact for GDPR erase — extend existing automation/src/brevo/index.js which today only does transactional templates

Firestore (priority-118da) contacts collection: read contact status/lastEngagementAt, write status transitions, GDPR PII erase; reuse existing repo Firestore utils (automation/src/utils/firestore-*.js, credential-resolver.js)
Firestore suppression_audit collection (new): append-only audit trail
LangGraph Platform built-in Postgres checkpointer + store (auto-provisioned; do NOT pass custom checkpointer to .compile())
Custom http.app (Starlette/FastAPI mount in langgraph.json) exposing /api/webhooks/brevo/event for the real-time lane, with Brevo signature verification
Claude (Anthropic) via langgraph for summarization/explanation of diffs only — orchestration/reasoning, NOT model training/distillation
Slack (existing marketing channel) for run-summary notifications
LangGraph Cloud cron / scheduled assistant for the daily sweep
Human-in-the-loop interrupt surfaced via LangGraph Studio / Agent Inbox for the approval gate
secrets_needed: GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS or FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON (priority-118da, scoped to contacts + suppression_audit)
approval_gates: Single mandatory HITL interrupt at approval_gate before any write to Brevo or Firestore. Policy tiers: (1) respect-unsubscribe writes (CRM status->unsubscribed) may be config-flagged auto-approve because they are legally required, reversible-safe, and non-destructive; (2) sunset removal and (3) GDPR right-to-be-forgotten erasure ALWAYS require explicit human approve/reject/edit-subset and can never auto-approve. Resume via LangGraph Command(resume=...) carrying the approved row subset. apply_mutations and gdpr_erase are unreachable until resume. Re-enabling sending / un-suppressing a contact is treated as an irreversible-risk action and is always human-gated. Slack notification is informational only (not gated).
impact: Closes the highest-risk gap in the marketing email program: unsubscribes are currently NOT reflected back into the CRM (compliance + reputation risk), there is no hard/soft-bounce or complaint hygiene, no sunset policy, no webhook verification, and no audit trail — exactly the pain points listed and corroborated by workspace memory (no unsubscribe/blocklist in send path, no audit trail, GDPR RTBF not automated). Automating the read+diff and gating only the writes protects deliverability targets (bounce <2%, complaint <0.1%, unsub <0.5%), makes unsubscribe-respect immediate and provable, and gives a defensible GDPR audit trail — all without granting the agent unsupervised destructive power over contact data. automatable_score 4: the reconcile/diff/health-check is fully automatable; the destructive writes are intentionally kept human-in-the-loop.
deploy_notes: Target: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS, EU region recommended given GDPR/EU contact data -> eu.api.smith.langchain.com). New repo (or subdir) under EscalateLabs/marketing, e.g. automation/agents/suppression-sentinel, deployed via the langgraph CLI or GitHub-integration UI. langgraph.json: graphs.suppression_sync='./agent/graph.py:graph'; python_version 3.12; image_distro wolfi; pip_installer uv; http.app='./app.py:app' mounting the Brevo webhook route + CORS locked to Brevo origins; checkpointer.ttl strategy delete (keep operational state short — the durable record is the Firestore audit trail, not checkpoints). Do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= into .compile() — the platform injects production Postgres. Secrets injected via the deployment UI/API, never committed (.env only for local). Schedule the daily sweep as a cron on a default 'sweep' assistant; the webhook route creates ad-hoc single-contact runs. Build out from existing automation/src/brevo/index.js (extend with suppression/blocklist/contact-delete calls) and reuse the repo's Firestore credential-resolver. Pre-launch: backfill-run in dry-run/reject mode to validate the diff against a known sample before enabling any auto-approve tier. Note org guardrail — this is ordinary orchestration/IaC + Claude-as-reasoner; no ML model is trained, evaluated, or distilled, so it is within Anthropic terms.

process: Drip & Lifecycle Email Sequences — welcome (Day 0/3/7), trial_expiring (Day -7/-3/-1), and re_engagement (Day 7/30/60 inactive) drip campaigns. Currently configured in Brevo (3 workflows, 5 contact lists, 6 templates, conditional sends) but not operational: no webhook trigger ingestion from Scheduler/GAL, no automated list population, manual user-attribute sync (TEAM_SIZE/FEATURES_USED/LAST_LOGIN), hardcoded delays, no behavior-linked next-step timing. The LangGraph agent replaces Brevo's broken native automation engine: it ingests product lifecycle events, owns durable per-user sequence state (one thread per user x sequence), enriches/syncs attributes, evaluates step conditions and frequency caps against live behavior, drafts each send, and (after human approval for v1) fires the email via Brevo's transactional API and updates lists. Brevo stays as the template store + ESP/delivery layer; LangGraph owns the orchestration and timing brain.
trigger: Three trigger paths, all landing on the deployed Agent Server (LangGraph Platform). (1) Product webhooks from Scheduler (scheduler-api Go) and GAL emit lifecycle events (user_signup, team_created, invites_sent, feature_used, subscription_converted, login, trial_start/trial_end_date_set) to a custom HTTP route mounted via langgraph.json `http.app` (POST /webhooks/product-event, HMAC-verified). Each event creates or advances a thread keyed thread_id=`{user_id}:{sequence}`. (2) A LangGraph cron (scheduled run) wakes due threads — replaces Brevo's day-offset timers and enables timezone/trial-length-aware delays. (3) Brevo outbound webhooks (email_opened, email_clicked, unsubscribed, bounced) POST to /webhooks/brevo-event to update per-user behavior state used for next-step timing and suppression. No event is trusted blindly: signature verification + idempotency key on every inbound call.
graph_steps: ingest_event: validate HMAC signature + idempotency key, normalize the inbound product/Brevo/cron payload into a typed LifecycleEvent, resolve user_id and target sequence (welcome | trial_expiring | re_engagement), and load-or-init the per-user sequence thread state (current step, last_sent_at, sent_history, suppression flags). State persists in the platform-injected Postgres checkpointer — no checkpointer passed to .compile().

enrich_attributes: call read-only product tools (get_user_profile, get_user_attributes -> TEAM_SIZE/FEATURES_USED/LAST_LOGIN/trial_end_date/timezone/locale, get_subscription_status) to refresh attributes the conditions depend on. Cache in the long-term BaseStore (semantic index off; keyed by user_id) so re-engagement runs months later still have context. This is the automated replacement for manual attribute sync.

select_step: deterministic router (no LLM) — given sequence + elapsed time + current step, pick the candidate step from a config mirror of brevo-automation-config.json (welcome 0/3/7d, trial_expiring -7/-3/-1d relative to live trial_end_date, re_engagement 7/30/60d inactive). Computes the dynamic due-time using the user's actual trial length, timezone, and last activity instead of hardcoded delaySeconds.

evaluate_conditions: deterministic guard evaluating the step's Brevo conditions against enriched attributes (no_team_members -> TEAM_SIZE==0, not_converted -> subscription!=active, inactive_7/30/60_days -> now-LAST_LOGIN>=threshold) PLUS global guards: frequencyCaps (maxEmailsPerWeek=2, minDelayBetweenEmails=24h), respectUnsubscribes, bounce/complaint suppression, and not-already-sent-this-step idempotency. If any guard fails -> branch to schedule_next (skip the send) or END. This is where churn-signal / behavior-driven skips live.

ab_select_and_personalize: choose an A/B content variant for the step (deterministic hash of user_id -> variant bucket, logged for later open/click attribution) and use Claude ONLY to assemble personalization params + optionally tune subject-line tone within strict guardrails (FIRSTNAME, EMAIL, TEAM_SIZE, FEATURES_USED, trial days remaining). Output is structured params bound to the existing Brevo templateId — Claude does not author the email body or invent offers; it fills a fixed schema. Resolve templateKey -> Brevo templateId via the brevo-templates mapping.

draft_send: build the exact SendSmtpEmail payload (templateId, to, params, sender hello@scheduler-systems.com, tags=[sequence, step, variant], List actions) and produce a human-readable preview. This node performs NO network send.

HUMAN_APPROVAL_GATE: interrupt() before any send / list mutation. Surfaces recipient, sequence/step, variant, rendered subject + personalization params, and which conditions passed. Human resumes with approve / edit-params / skip / unsubscribe-user via Command(resume=...). v1 hard rule: every outbound email and every Brevo list/contact write is gated. (Roadmap: auto-approve low-risk welcome steps once trust is established, keep trial_expiring + re_engagement gated.)

execute_send: on approval, call Brevo sendTransacEmail (idempotency-keyed) + sync contact list membership (add to trial_users/trial_expiring/inactive_users etc., remove on conversion). Wrapped in try/retry with bounded backoff; on hard failure record error and route to a dead-letter/notify path rather than silently dropping.

record_and_schedule_next: append to sent_history, update last_sent_at + current step, persist behavior expectations (awaiting open/click for next-step timing), then either schedule the next step's due-time (cron will re-wake this thread) or END the sequence (e.g. converted, unsubscribed, sequence complete). Emit OpenTelemetry span + structured log for observability.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Agent Server) — Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore injected by platform; do NOT pass checkpointer/store to .compile()
Custom Starlette/FastAPI app mounted via langgraph.json http.app for /webhooks/product-event, /webhooks/brevo-event (HMAC + idempotency)
LangGraph cron / scheduled runs to wake due threads (replaces hardcoded day-offset delays; enables timezone + trial-length aware timing)
Brevo Transactional Emails API (@getbrevo/brevo): sendTransacEmail, contact list add/remove, template mapping via brevo-templates.json — delivery + template store only
Brevo outbound event webhooks (email_opened, email_clicked, unsubscribed, bounced) for behavior-linked timing + suppression
Scheduler product API (scheduler-api Go) read-only tools: get_user_profile, get_user_attributes (TEAM_SIZE/FEATURES_USED/LAST_LOGIN/trial_end_date/timezone), get_subscription_status
GAL product webhook source for lifecycle events
Anthropic Claude via langgraph orchestration — personalization-param assembly + bounded subject tuning ONLY (no body authoring, no model training/distillation)
Config mirror of /data/brevo-automation-config.json (workflows, conditions, frequencyCaps) as the deterministic step/condition source of truth
OpenTelemetry instrumentation (workspace standard) for per-send spans + eval hooks; LangSmith tracing
human-in-the-loop interrupt()/Command(resume) for the approval gate (Slack/email/LangGraph Studio inbox as the approver surface)
approval_gates: HARD GATE before every irreversible action. A single HUMAN_APPROVAL_GATE node calls interrupt() prior to (a) any outbound email send and (b) any Brevo contact-list / contact-attribute write. The interrupt payload shows: recipient email + user_id, sequence + step + A/B variant, rendered subject, full personalization params, the conditions that passed/failed, and frequency-cap status. The reviewer resumes via Command(resume=...) with one of: approve | edit-params (re-renders, re-gates) | skip-this-step | unsubscribe-user (writes suppression, ends sequence). Nothing sends on graph resume without an explicit approve. Secondary safety gates that auto-skip (no human needed, fail-closed): respectUnsubscribes, bounce/complaint suppression, maxEmailsPerWeek=2, minDelayBetweenEmails=24h, and per-step idempotency so a replayed webhook never double-sends. v1 ships fully gated; auto-approving only the lowest-risk welcome Day-0 step is a deliberate post-trust roadmap item, never trial_expiring or re_engagement. Per workspace policy: no message is sent without drafted-and-approved human sign-off.
deploy_notes: Deploy target: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS, api.smith.langchain.com). langgraph.json: dependencies ["."], graphs {"lifecycle":"./agent/graph.py:graph"}, python_version "3.12", image_distro "wolfi", pip_installer "uv", http.app -> "./agent/app.py:app" (custom webhook routes), store.ttl + checkpointer.ttl set for long-lived re_engagement threads (60+ day waits — set generous default_ttl so threads survive). CRITICAL: graph = builder.compile() with NO checkpointer=/store= args — the platform injects production Postgres; passing InMemorySaver is local-only and ignored/overridden in the hosted runtime. Real secrets injected via the Deployments UI/API, never committed (.env is for local dev only; AGENTS.md/security rule = never hardcode secrets). thread_id convention `{user_id}:{sequence}` keeps the three sequences independent per user. Cron schedule a 'wake due threads' run (e.g. hourly) to fire time-based steps. Compliance: orchestration calls Claude for personalization-param assembly only — NO model training/distillation/eval-data generation (Anthropic Consumer Terms guardrail in AGENTS.md). Migration: keep brevo-automation-config.json as the canonical step/condition config (load it into the graph), retire Brevo's native automation timers, but keep Brevo as ESP + template store. v1 acceptance: dry-run with internal test users (FIRSTNAME=Test) end-to-end through the approval gate before pointing real product webhooks at it. Tie work to a tracked GitHub issue per workspace policy. Source of truth files: config /Users/scheduler-systems/Documents/scheduler-systems-ltd/EscalateLabs/marketing/data/brevo-automation-config.json, Brevo client /Users/scheduler-systems/Documents/scheduler-systems-ltd/EscalateLabs/marketing/automation/src/brevo/index.js, templates /Users/scheduler-systems/Documents/scheduler-systems-ltd/Scheduler-Systems/brevo-email-templates/ (and EscalateLabs/marketing/email-templates/).
impact: Activates a revenue-driving lifecycle engine that is currently 100% built-but-dark in Brevo (templates + workflows defined, lists empty, webhooks unwired — zero emails flowing). Directly serves Scheduler's live RevenueCat-paid funnel: trial_expiring drips defend trial->paid conversion (the premium $2.99/$4.99 path) and re_engagement wins back churned users — the fastest no-app-release revenue lever. Replaces the broken pieces the workspace already flagged: incomplete webhook ingestion, manual attribute sync, hardcoded delays, no behavior-linked timing, no A/B. Risk is contained because v1 is fully human-approval-gated (no rogue sends to real users) and reuses existing Brevo templates/ESP rather than rebuilding delivery. automatable_score 5/10 reflects real integration surface (product webhooks + attribute APIs must exist/be reachable) — the agent design isolates that risk behind read-only enrichment tools + idempotent, gated sends.

trigger: Three triggers, all landing on the same LangGraph deployment via the Assistants API: (1) Cron — LangGraph Platform `crons` schedule, weekday 14:00 UTC only (respects the command's "no outreach 10pm-7am local / business hours" rule), runs the `auto` assistant over due prospects. (2) Event/webhook — `/data/prospects/qualified.json` change (new prospects added by the upstream `/connect-prospects` job) POSTs a run to the `reply` assistant via the custom `http.app` route. (3) Manual — operator invokes a specific assistant (`reply` | `dm` | `followup` | `report`) from LangGraph Studio or the SDK with an explicit prospect-id list. Each run is one thread keyed by `campaign_date` so checkpointer state (rate-limit counters, daily metrics) resumes correctly across interrupts.
process: Prospect Outreach and Engagement (Social/Multi-Channel) — porting EscalateLabs/marketing `/prospect-outreach` (reply, dm, followup, report, auto) to a deployable LangGraph Platform agent. The agent is the brain (load -> categorize sentiment -> select sequence/template -> draft personalized copy -> rate-limit gate -> HUMAN APPROVAL -> dispatch action -> log -> advance status); a SEPARATE Playwright worker is the hands. This split is mandatory: LangGraph Platform's serverless Agent Server runtime is stateless and cannot host a long-lived authenticated Playwright browser session, and the company rule "Orchestrate local, execute on cluster" forbids heavy local subprocess fleets. So all browser posting/DM/connect actions run in an out-of-band Playwright executor (existing helper functions in the command file: verifyLogin, clickWithFallback, fillWithFallback, screenshotOnError; reuse the per-platform replyToTweet/sendTwitterDM/commentOnLinkedIn/sendLinkedInConnection/sendLinkedInMessage/replyToReddit/commentOnFacebook). The agent only emits an approved job spec and polls for the result. v1 closes the named pain points: automated reply sentiment categorization (LLM node, replaces manual), engagement-velocity scoring (fast reply -> bump priority), and CRM sync to Google Sheets (the command documents the sheet schema and helpers but it was never wired). Scope guard: orchestration calls Claude for classification/drafting only — NO model training/fine-tuning/distillation, consistent with the workspace Anthropic-terms guardrail.
graph_steps: load_prospects: read /data/prospects/qualified.json + outreach-log.json (or the action's explicit prospect-id list) into State; resolve each prospect's current status in the new->replied->engaged->dm_sent->following_up->converted/closed flow and compute days-since-last-contact from the log

categorize_sentiment: NEW node closing a pain point — for prospects with an inbound response captured by the Playwright worker, call Claude to classify sentiment (positive/neutral/negative/objection/spam) and intent; for fresh prospects classify the original post into pain-point + buying-stage. No model training, pure inference
score_velocity: NEW node — derive engagement-velocity signal (fast reply, like, profile-visit timestamps from the log) and re-rank/bump priority so hot+fast prospects are actioned first

route_action: conditional edge — map (status, sentiment, days-since) to next step: status=new->compose_reply; replied+positive->compose_dm; replied+no-response & day>=3->compose_followup; engaged+interested->compose_conversion (Calendly/trial/email-handoff); negative/spam->mark_closed; report-only run->build_report

rate_limit_gate: load per-platform daily counters from checkpointer/Store (Twitter 30 reply/10 dm, LinkedIn 25/15, Reddit 15/10; max 10 actions/hr; 2h break after 20; business-hours-only); if quota exhausted for a platform, defer those prospects to the next thread instead of dropping them

compose_message: Claude drafts the personalized message by filling the platform/sequence-specific template (Twitter reply A/B/C, DM value-first/soft-pitch/demo, LinkedIn connect-note/msg-1/msg-2, Reddit helpful no-promo, email seq 1-3) with the prospect's real pain point, name, team size; output is a DRAFT only

interrupt__human_approval: HARD STOP via interrupt() — surfaces {prospect, platform, action, drafted_message, target_url, rate_limit_status} for human review; operator edits/approves/rejects per item; on resume, only APPROVED items proceed. This gate covers every irreversible action (public reply, comment, DM, connection request, email). Nothing posts without it

dispatch_playwright_job: for each approved item, write a job to the out-of-band Playwright executor (HTTP queue / k3s job) carrying {platform, action, url/username, approved_text, dry_run flag}; DO NOT run a browser inside the LangGraph runtime

await_result: poll the executor (or receive callback) for {success, screenshot_path_on_error, posted_url}; bounded retries with backoff; on selector-break failure, capture screenshot + leave prospect status unchanged for manual retry (acknowledges fragile-selector pain point)

log_and_advance: append to /data/prospects/outreach-log.json (out-001 schema), advance prospect status, set next_action + next_action_date, update conversions.json on demo_booked/trial, and bump daily counters in Store

crm_sync: NEW node closing a pain point — upsert prospect row + daily metrics to the documented Google Sheet (id 1rviR0FzLswdvQx9tYZel76mOmkXt_XbuwTg8VaG_pYk) via Sheets API using a service-account credential from env

build_report: aggregate outreach-metrics.json (replies/dms/followups sent, responses, positive, demos, trials, conversion_rate) and emit a run summary; for report-only invocations this is the terminal node
END
impact: Revenue-relevant but slow/indirect lead-gen, and gated by a hard truth: qualified.json is currently EMPTY (0 prospects) and the upstream /connect-prospects discovery has not produced inventory, so this agent has nothing to act on until that feeder runs — hence P2, not P1. When fed, it removes the three biggest documented frictions (manual sentiment categorization, no engagement-velocity tracking, no CRM sync) and lets one operator review/approve a batch in minutes instead of driving Playwright by hand. Caps downside via the approval gate (no rogue posts -> no platform bans, which is the dominant risk for this account given G2-ToS biased-collection concerns already flagged in memory). Net: modest top-of-funnel acceleration for Scheduler B2B shift-scheduling leads, low marginal cost, but should not jump ahead of live-revenue work (Scheduler RevenueCat path, web deploy).
tools_integrations: Anthropic Claude API (sentiment classification + message drafting; inference only, no training)
LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Agent Server, auto Postgres checkpointer + Store, crons, custom http.app webhook route)
Out-of-band Playwright executor worker (Twitter/LinkedIn/Reddit/Facebook posting+DM+connect; reuses existing helper fns; runs on k3s/ARC per 'execute on cluster', NOT inside LangGraph)
Google Sheets API (CRM sync — documented sheet, currently unimplemented)
Filesystem/object store for /data/prospects/*.json (qualified, outreach-log, conversions, outreach-metrics) — mounted via executor, not the stateless runtime
Calendly link injection for meeting booking (link templated into approved conversion messages; no auto-booking in v1)
Optional: Slack for approval notifications + run reports (mcp__slack or webhook)
Email enrichment provider for social->email capture (deferred to v2; manual entry in v1 per pain point)
approval_gates: SINGLE mandatory human-in-the-loop interrupt() at interrupt__human_approval, executed AFTER drafting and AFTER the rate-limit check but BEFORE any dispatch_playwright_job. It batches every pending irreversible action (public reply, comment, DM, connection request, conversion/email send) into one review payload containing prospect context, platform, action type, full drafted text, target URL, and current rate-limit headroom. Operator approves/edits/rejects per item; the graph resumes via the Command/resume API and dispatches ONLY approved items. Hard rules enforced around the gate: nothing posts on dry_run; nothing posts outside business hours; nothing posts once a platform's daily quota is hit (deferred instead). report and CRM-sync read paths are NOT gated (no external side effects). A second lighter confirmation is recommended before crm_sync writes only if the run also created NEW sheet rows. compile() the graph WITHOUT a custom checkpointer/store so the platform injects production persistence that survives the interrupt.
deploy_notes: langgraph.json: dependencies ["."], graphs map 5 assistants to factory functions in one module — {"reply":"./agent/graph.py:make_reply","dm":"./agent/graph.py:make_dm","followup":"./agent/graph.py:make_followup","report":"./agent/graph.py:make_report","auto":"./agent/graph.py:make_auto"} (each returns the compiled StateGraph specialized by entry-route). python_version "3.11", image_distro "wolfi", pip_installer "uv". Set env via the LangSmith Deployments UI/API (never commit secrets) — satisfies the workspace 'never hardcode secrets' rule. checkpointer.ttl strategy delete default_ttl ~43200; store.ttl for daily rate-limit counters (sweep daily). Mount custom http.app for the qualified.json-change webhook + a /report read endpoint; CORS locked to internal origin. Crons: one weekday 14:00 UTC schedule -> auto. CRITICAL build constraints: (1) do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= to .compile() — the platform injects Postgres; (2) the Playwright browser MUST NOT run in this container (serverless, no persistent authed session) — it lives in the separate executor reached via PLAYWRIGHT_EXECUTOR_URL on the cluster; (3) keep all model calls inference-only to stay within Anthropic terms. v1 cut-lines: auto email enrichment, auto meeting-booking, and LinkedIn post-search are explicitly deferred (link templating + profile-based only). Pre-launch blocker to flag to human: populate qualified.json via /connect-prospects before scheduling the cron, or the agent runs against an empty set.

process: ASO (App Store Optimization) Tracking
trigger: Primary: scheduled cron on LangGraph Platform (Crons API) every 14 days at 09:00 to fire the snapshot cycle (mirrors aso-config.json cycle.frequency_days=14, next_snapshot_due). Secondary: on-demand manual run via the SDK/Studio for an ad-hoc snapshot or a metadata-change-impact check (e.g. right after a human edits store metadata). The graph branches on an input `mode` field: "snapshot_cycle" (full bi-weekly run) | "track_change" (log a just-made metadata change + set a measurement reminder) | "competitor_review" (monthly competitor positioning pass).
graph_steps: load_state (node): read aso-config.json, aso-keywords.json, aso-ranking-history.json, aso-change-log.json from the configured repo/data store into graph State; compute days-since-last-snapshot and resolve `mode`. Uses langgraph BaseStore (long-term) for the rolling rank history so it survives across cron runs.
route (conditional edge): branch on mode -> snapshot_cycle | track_change | competitor_review.

fetch_rankings (node, snapshot_cycle): for each of the 25 keywords, query iOS rank via the free public iTunes Search API (entity=software, country=US, limit=200, match by bundleId/trackId) and Android rank via Playwright Play Store scrape (match package panuilov.shay.priority). Auto-selects AppTweak API path instead IF APPTWEAK_API_KEY is present (currently lapsed -> free-scraping). Rate-limited with randomized delays; returns partial results on failure. This is read-only public data, no irreversible action.
compute_deltas (node): port of rankings.js compareRankings -> improved/declined/unchanged/new/dropped buckets with per-keyword delta (prev_rank - cur_rank, positive=better).

correlate (node, LLM-assisted): join the delta buckets against aso-change-log.json entries whose status=pending_measurement and whose measurement window has elapsed; mark expected_impact vs measured_impact, and flag movements NOT explained by any logged change as candidate competitor-driven shifts. Claude summarizes likely cause per significant mover (>=5 positions).

competitor_scan (node, competitor_review branch or as enrichment): pull the top-3 competitor positions per keyword from the same public store searches and detect competitor metadata/positioning shifts vs last cycle; produce a market-gap note (e.g. underserved healthcare/retail scheduling verticals from aso-keywords.json keyword_opportunities).

generate_report (node, LLM): port of report.js generateReport into a richer markdown ASO report — summary table, top gainers/losers, dropped keywords, correlation findings, quick wins (ranks 11-25), and prioritized recommendations (subtitle/keyword-field tweaks). Output is a PROPOSAL only.

human_review (interrupt, APPROVAL GATE): call interrupt() to surface (a) the new ranking snapshot to be committed to aso-ranking-history.json, (b) the report file to be written, (c) any proposed edits to aso-keywords.json (e.g. promote a keyword_opportunity to tracked) or recommended store-metadata changes. Human approves/edits/rejects. NOTHING is persisted or sent before approval.

persist_outputs (node, post-approval): write the approved snapshot to aso-ranking-history.json (append), save aso-report-<date>.md, bump aso-config.json cycle (current_cycle, last_snapshot, next_snapshot_due), and apply any approved keyword-list edits — committed via a gated git branch/PR in EscalateLabs/marketing, never a direct push to main.

notify (node, post-approval, APPROVAL-GATED send): deliver the approved report to the human (Slack/email draft). The send itself is behind the same human_review approval; the draft is created but only dispatched once approved.

track_change_handler (node, track_change branch): append a structured entry to aso-change-log.json (field, platform, old/new value, keywords_targeted, expected_impact, rankings_at_change, status=pending_measurement) and schedule the +7-14d measurement reminder — also gated through human_review before the file write/commit.
impact: Removes the manual bi-weekly snapshot toil and makes the cycle actually run on schedule (today it is manual with no automation — a stated pain point). Automates the two analyses humans skip: (1) correlating rank movements with logged metadata changes (expected-vs-actual impact, currently filled in by hand), and (2) flagging unexplained movements as competitor-driven. Surfaces keyword_opportunities (healthcare/retail/roster) and quick wins each cycle. Net effect: tighter ASO feedback loop -> better keyword targeting -> more organic App Store installs for Scheduler, the live revenue product. Low blast radius because the agent is read-only on stores and write-gated everywhere else. Does NOT fix the fragile-Playwright or static-keyword-strategy root causes by itself, but makes them visible and cheap to act on.
tools_integrations: iTunes Search API (free, public, no auth — iOS ranks)
Playwright headless Chromium + stealth (Play Store ranks; the fragile path — pin a browser image in langgraph.json dockerfile_lines)
AppTweak API (optional, auto-used if APPTWEAK_API_KEY set; currently lapsed/archived since 2026-03-11)
Anthropic Claude (correlation, market-gap analysis, report prose — orchestration/inference only, NO model training)
Git/GitHub (EscalateLabs/marketing repo — gated PR to update aso-*.json data files and commit aso-report-<date>.md)
Slack or email (approved-report delivery; send is approval-gated)
LangGraph Platform Crons API (14-day schedule)
LangGraph BaseStore + Postgres checkpointer (managed-injected; persists rank history + interrupt state across cron runs)
secrets_needed: APPTWEAK_API_KEY (optional — enables AppTweak path; absent today)
GITHUB_TOKEN (scoped to EscalateLabs/marketing, for the gated data-file/report PR)
approval_gates: ["human_review interrupt() BEFORE any persistence: gates (a) committing the new snapshot to aso-ranking-history.json, (b) writing aso-report-<date>.md, (c) editing aso-keywords.json (keyword-strategy changes), and (d) any recommended store-metadata change copy.", "Send gate: the report notification (Slack/email) is dispatched ONLY after the same approval — draft is prepared, send withheld until approved.", "Git write gate: all data-file/report writes land on a feature branch + PR in EscalateLabs/marketing; never a direct push to main (mirrors workspace git-workflow + worktree-discipline rules).", "track_change writes to aso-change-log.json are gated through human_review before the file write/commit.", "No store mutation by design: the agent NEVER touches App Store Connect / Google Play Console — live metadata edits remain a manual human action, logged back via track_change. This is the hard irreversible-action boundary."]
deploy_notes: Managed LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments, formerly LangGraph Cloud). langgraph.json: graphs={"aso":"./aso_agent/graph.py:graph"}, python_version 3.13, image_distro wolfi, pip_installer uv, dependencies ["."]. Compile the StateGraph WITHOUT passing checkpointer/store (platform injects managed Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore — required for the human_review interrupt to durably pause/resume between the cron fire and human approval, which may be hours later). Playwright needs system browser deps -> add dockerfile_lines to apt-install chromium deps and run `playwright install --with-deps chromium` (or pin a Playwright base_image); this is the main build risk and the source of the existing fragility. Configure the Crons API for a 14-day cadence after first deploy (cron resumes the graph by injecting mode="snapshot_cycle"). Secrets injected via the Deployments UI/API, never committed (env in langgraph.json points at non-secret config only). State schema (TypedDict) should carry: mode, cycle_name, keywords[], current_rankings[], delta buckets, change_log_matches[], competitor_positions[], report_md, proposed_data_patches{}, approval_decision. Use a factory graph fn if per-run config (e.g. iosOnly/androidOnly) is needed. Reuse the existing JS logic as the spec for compareRankings/generateReport but reimplement in Python for the managed runtime; data-file schemas (aso-config/keywords/ranking-history/change-log) are the contract and must be preserved exactly. ANTHROPIC TERMS GUARDRAIL: this agent does orchestration + Claude inference only; it must not train/fine-tune/eval any ML model (e.g. nothing touching gal-model).

process: SEO & Backlink Research — keyword/competitor-gap monitoring, Moz-graded backlink opportunity identification, GSC indexing-health checks, and human-approved outreach. This LangGraph deployment is a THIN ORCHESTRATOR over the already-built Growth Console (seo-tools, deployed at seo.scheduler-systems.com). It does NOT re-implement Moz/GSC/Firestore/Brevo logic; it calls the existing Next.js API routes (which already own credentials, the Moz daily-search cap via getMozMaxSearchesPerDay() in lib/moz.ts, the seomoz.com/v2/usage quota guard, and the Brevo send approval gate that only releases drafts whose status==="approved"). LangGraph adds: continuous scheduled cadence (the #1 pain point — research is currently one-shot slash-command snapshots), cross-source synthesis (GSC rank deltas + Moz DA + competitor gaps -> a single ranked opportunity list), state/memory of what was already pursued, and a hard human-in-the-loop interrupt before any irreversible action (outreach email, indexing request, campaign creation). NOTE: legacy /data/seo/outreach/*.json trackers are DEPRECATED (migrated to Growth Console Firestore: seo_backlinks, seo_keyword_gap_cache, keyword snapshots, seo_moz_rate_limits, outreach campaigns); the agent treats Firestore-via-API as the system of record and never writes the legacy files.
trigger: Primary: scheduled cron (LangGraph Platform Cron / assistant cron) — weekly full sweep (mirrors the existing gsc-weekly-pull cadence) + a lighter daily indexing-health + money-keyword rank-drop check. Secondary: on-demand HTTP run via the Agent Server /runs endpoint (e.g. invoked from a Growth Console button or a marketing slash command) with input {mode: "keyword_gap" | "backlink_prospect" | "indexing_health" | "full_sweep", competitors?: string[], seed_keywords?: string[]}. Webhook-resume: human approval/rejection of an interrupted run is delivered back via the platform's resume API (Command(resume=...)).
graph_steps: ingest_signals: read-only fan-out to Growth Console — GET /api/gsc/snapshot?property=sc-domain:scheduler-systems.com (rank/CTR/position), GET /api/keyword-gap (cached Ubersuggest gap rows), GET /api/outreach (existing campaigns + backlink state from Firestore seo_backlinks). Pure reads; no cost, no side effects.

check_moz_budget: GET /api/moz/usage -> {remaining, percentRemaining}. Gate node: if remaining below a configured floor (e.g. <15% or below reserved daily cap from getMozMaxSearchesPerDay), set state.moz_throttled=true and SKIP all paid Moz calls this run, annotating the report. Protects the $250/mo budget — the explicit pain point.

indexing_health: POST /api/index-check {urls:[...money/landing pages], action:'check'} and GET /api/indexing-issues for coverage problems. Read-only diagnostic; collects not-indexed / soft-404 / crawl issues into state.

find_prospects: if NOT moz_throttled — POST /api/moz/prospects (link-intersect: sites linking to competitors getsling/wheniwork/deputy/homebase/7shifts but not to us), enriched with DA. Classifies each into directory|resource_page|editorial|other using the existing host/path hints. Dedupes against Firestore seo_backlinks + existing outreach campaigns already in flight.

rank_and_synthesize: LLM (Claude via ANTHROPIC_API_KEY — orchestration/summarization ONLY, no model training) merges GSC rank deltas + low-CTR surfaces + keyword-gap rows + Moz-graded prospects into a single scored, deduped opportunity list with a recommended action + draft outreach (mapped to one of the 5 templates in /data/seo/outreach/email-templates.md) per item. Writes the synthesis to thread state + long-term store.

checkpoint_report: persist the run report (read-only artifact) and emit it as the run output; optionally file a GitHub issue mirroring the existing seo-alert pattern for rank drops on money keywords. No irreversible external action yet.

HUMAN_APPROVAL_GATE (interrupt): for any item whose recommended action is irreversible — send outreach email, request indexing, create/advance an outreach campaign — call interrupt() with the full proposed payload (recipient, template, body, target URL) and PAUSE. Nothing leaves the system without explicit human resume.

execute_approved: on resume, ONLY for human-approved items: outreach -> POST /api/email/send {draftId} (server-side gate additionally rejects any draft not status==='approved' — defense in depth); indexing -> POST /api/index-check {action:'request-indexing'}; campaign -> POST/PATCH /api/outreach. Rejected items are recorded as 'skipped_by_human' and never retried automatically.

record_outcomes: write final state to Firestore via Growth Console (campaign status, prospect status via /api/moz/prospects update_status, backlink metadata snapshot via PATCH /api/backlinks/snapshot) and update long-term store so the next scheduled run dedupes correctly and can later attribute ROI (close the 'no ROI tracking' gap by stamping which backlink/keyword each action targeted).
tools_integrations: Growth Console API (seo-tools @ seo.scheduler-systems.com) — system of record; machine-actor auth via SEO_TOOLS_AUTH_TOKEN/GROWTH_API_KEY: /api/gsc/snapshot, /api/keyword-gap, /api/moz/usage, /api/moz/prospects, /api/index-check, /api/indexing-issues, /api/outreach, /api/email/templates, /api/email/send, /api/backlinks/snapshot, /api/backlinks/reconcile
Google Search Console (read-only, indirect) — accessed only through Growth Console (lib/gsc, webmasters.readonly), never directly from the agent
Moz API v2 (lsapi.seomoz.com) — accessed only through Growth Console, behind the usage-quota gate; protects the $250/mo cap
Brevo (transactional email) — outreach send, only via /api/email/send approved-draft gate
Anthropic Claude API (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) — orchestration/synthesis/draft generation only; NO model training/distillation
LangGraph Platform: managed Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore (long-term dedupe/ROI memory) injected by the platform; Cron for scheduling; interrupt()/Command(resume) for human-in-the-loop
GitHub (gh) — optional, mirror rank-drop alerts to Scheduler-Systems/marketing with label seo-alert (matches existing gsc alerts.js)
Firestore (indirect, via Growth Console): seo_backlinks, seo_keyword_gap_cache, keyword snapshots, seo_moz_rate_limits, outreach campaigns
secrets_needed: GAL_API_TOKEN (optional — only if triggering credentialed Ubersuggest/agent-browser scrapes via GAL dispatch; not required for read-only cached gap data)
approval_gates: HARD human-in-the-loop interrupt() before EVERY irreversible/external action — (1) sending any outreach email (POST /api/email/send), (2) requesting indexing (POST /api/index-check action=request-indexing), (3) creating or advancing an outreach campaign (POST/PATCH /api/outreach). The interrupt surfaces the full payload (recipient, chosen template, rendered body, target URL/DA) for review; the run stays paused until a human resumes with approve/reject per item. Defense in depth: the Growth Console /api/email/send route independently enforces status==='approved' and returns 403 otherwise, so even a buggy agent cannot send an unapproved draft. Read-only steps (GSC/Moz-usage/keyword-gap/index-check, prospect discovery, synthesis, report) run autonomously without a gate. This satisfies the workspace 'No send without approval' and 'No delete without approval' rules. Cost gate (non-human, automatic): check_moz_budget node short-circuits paid Moz calls when quota is low.
impact: Converts the #1 pain point — one-shot, manual, snapshot-only research — into continuous, deduped, cross-source monitoring with persistent memory, while spending zero new infra effort because the Growth Console already implements Moz/GSC/Firestore/Brevo with auth, quota guards, and the send approval gate. Adds the missing automated ranking-position tracking (parity with ASO), automated rank-drop/low-CTR alerting on money keywords, continuous backlink-prospect surfacing graded by DA, and a foundation for ROI attribution (stamping which keyword/backlink each action targets) — directly closing the 'no continuous monitoring / static target list / no ROI tracking / GSC not integrated into the loop' gaps. automatable_score is 5/10 because the value-producing terminal step (outreach) is intentionally human-gated and externally dependent, so this automates the entire research+synthesis pipeline (high leverage) but not the human relationship/sending decision. Revenue framing per workspace priority: SEO/backlinks are a slow, indirect lever for the live Scheduler product — useful compounding traffic, not fast cash — so P2; the build is cheap precisely because it reuses existing infra.
deploy_notes: Deploy to LangGraph Platform (managed Cloud SaaS) via langgraph.json at repo root: dependencies ['.'], graphs {'seo_research':'./agent/graph.py:graph'}, python_version '3.12', image_distro 'wolfi', store with semantic index (embed openai:text-embedding-3-small) for dedupe/ROI memory + ttl sweep, checkpointer ttl tuning. CRITICAL: compile the StateGraph WITHOUT passing checkpointer=/store= — the platform injects production Postgres persistence; passing InMemorySaver is local-only and is ignored/overridden in the hosted runtime. Inject ALL secrets via the platform UI/API (never in env file committed to repo, never hardcoded — satisfies workspace security rules). State schema carries: signals (gsc/gap/prospects), moz_budget, ranked_opportunities, pending_approvals, executed, skipped_by_human. Use interrupt() (not raw input) so the run is durably suspended in the checkpointer and resumable via Command(resume=...) days later when the human reviews. Schedule via platform Cron: weekly full_sweep (align to existing gsc-weekly cadence) + daily indexing_health+rank-drop. Keep the agent stateless about credentials — it only holds the machine-actor token to the Growth Console; Moz/GSC/Brevo creds stay server-side in the Console (GCP Secret Manager), never entering LLM context. Per workspace rule: do NOT build/run any ML model here — this is pure orchestration calling Claude for text synthesis only. Local Mac is conductor only; run the deployment on the platform, not as a local subprocess fleet.

process: Blog and Content Production - end-to-end blog post pipeline (ideation, AI drafting, human review, multi-channel distribution) for two products with distinct brand voices: Scheduler (honest, no-fabrication, 5 content pillars) and Stratus (direct/technical/confident, banned-words list). A single LangGraph StateGraph routes by product, drafts with Claude against the correct voice rules, pauses at every human gate via interrupt(), and only after explicit approval performs the irreversible publish/distribute actions (git-PR blog publish, social posts, email blast). State (topic, product, outline, draft, review edits, distribution targets, approval decisions) persists in the platform-injected Postgres checkpointer so a thread can sleep for the 20-30 min human-review window and resume exactly where it paused.
trigger: Primary: scheduled cron (LangGraph Platform native cron) firing per the content calendar - Scheduler targets ~5 posts/week (Mon blog, etc.), Stratus on its own calendar drawing from the 5-post launch series queue in stratus/blog/_meta.yaml (status=draft). Each cron tick creates one run with {product, topic_hint} and the thread then blocks at human gates. Secondary/on-demand: POST to the deployment /threads + /runs API (or LangGraph Studio) with an explicit topic to start an ad-hoc post. Resumption of a paused run is driven by humans submitting the interrupt response (approve / edit / reject) via the Agent Inbox / API; no time-based auto-advance past any gate.
graph_steps: intake (START): normalize input into State {product: 'scheduler'|'stratus', topic, topic_hint, source_ref}; load the matching voice profile + banned-words list; for Scheduler fetch canonical pricing/claims from seo.scheduler-systems.com/api/submission-profile so the draft never overclaims; pick next queued topic from _meta.yaml if topic_hint only

ideate_outline: Claude generates a structured outline (H2/H3, target keyword, meta description, CTA, internal-link map for Stratus) constrained to the product voice rules and the allowed content pillars; conditional edge -> outline_gate

outline_gate (HITL interrupt #1): interrupt() surfaces the outline for human approve / edit / reject; on edit, merge human edits into State and re-enter outline (loop); on reject, route to END with status=killed; on approve, continue. NOTHING irreversible has happened yet

draft: Claude writes the full draft from the approved outline, enforcing voice rules (Stratus: ban 'leverage/seamless/revolutionary/...', precise tech terms, <=8-word headlines; Scheduler: no fabricated metrics, cite named sources, only real shipped features) -> automated_qa

automated_qa: deterministic checks (banned-word regex, headline length, broken-link/internal-link validation, claim/pricing cross-check vs submission-profile, plagiarism/duplicate-slug check vs existing posts). Failures annotate State; routes to review_gate carrying a QA report (does not auto-publish on pass)

review_gate (HITL interrupt #2): interrupt() presents draft + QA report for the 20-30 min human review; human submits final edits / approve / reject; edits loop back through automated_qa; approve sets approved_draft and records reviewer identity + timestamp

publish_gate (HITL interrupt #3 - irreversible): interrupt() shows the exact publish payload (target repo path stratus/blog/<slug>.md or Scheduler blog target, _meta.yaml status flip draft->published, commit/PR title). ONLY on explicit confirm does publish_blog run

publish_blog (tool/effect): commit the post file + flip _meta.yaml status to published via a git/PR tool using a scoped token from env (GITHUB_TOKEN); capture published URL; idempotency key = slug+content-hash to prevent double-publish on resume/replay

distribute_fanout: Claude generates the per-channel derivatives - social variants (LinkedIn/X/FB) per content-pillars voice, and the email blast copy per STRATUS-EMAIL-TEMPLATES / Scheduler templates. Produces a distribution package; routes to distribute_gate

distribute_gate (HITL interrupt #4 - irreversible batch): interrupt() shows the full social + email package and the recipient/audience scope for one batch approval; human can drop individual channels; on confirm proceed

distribute_social + distribute_email (tools/effects): post/schedule social (Buffer/LinkedIn/X API from env keys) and send email (Brevo/SendGrid from env) - each guarded by its own idempotency key; Google Search Console submit for the published URL; per-channel failures are recorded but do not roll back the blog publish
finalize (END): write run summary to State (published URL, channels sent, reviewer, timestamps, any QA flags); emit completion event/webhook for the analytics layer; thread closes
tools_integrations: Anthropic Claude API (langchain-anthropic ChatAnthropic) - the ONLY model use: orchestration/generation, no training/fine-tuning/distillation
LangGraph Platform managed Postgres checkpointer + store (auto-injected; do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= to .compile())
LangGraph native cron scheduler (content-calendar triggers)
LangGraph human-in-the-loop interrupt() + Agent Inbox / Studio for the 4 approval gates
GitHub API / git PR tool (publish blog file + flip _meta.yaml status in StratusCloudLabs/stratus and Scheduler blog target)
Google Search Console API (submit published URL - per Stratus publishing checklist)
Social APIs: LinkedIn, X/Twitter, optionally Buffer/Hootsuite for scheduling
Email: Brevo (primary) or SendGrid
Scheduler Growth Console: GET seo.scheduler-systems.com/api/submission-profile for canonical pricing/claims (anti-overclaim guard)
Source-of-truth doc reads at runtime: STRATUS-BRANDING.md / stratus blog _meta.yaml (Stratus voice + queue), content-pillars.md (Scheduler voice), MARKETING_AUTOMATION_BLUEPRINT.md (pipeline)
langsmith tracing/observability (built into the platform) for per-node eval of voice compliance
secrets_needed: GITHUB_TOKEN (scoped to blog publish repos: contents+PR)
SUBMISSION_PROFILE_URL (default seo.scheduler-systems.com/api/submission-profile) + any auth token it needs
approval_gates: Four explicit human-in-the-loop interrupt() gates, each resumable from the Postgres checkpointer so a thread can pause for minutes/hours: (1) outline_gate - approve/edit/reject the AI outline (no irreversible effect yet); (2) review_gate - the core 20-30 min human review/edit of the full draft, captures reviewer identity + timestamp, edits loop back through automated_qa; (3) publish_gate - IRREVERSIBLE: shows the exact commit/PR + _meta.yaml status flip payload, blog file is written only on explicit confirm (idempotency key = slug+content-hash); (4) distribute_gate - IRREVERSIBLE batch: shows the full social + email package and audience scope, human can drop individual channels before the single confirm, each channel send guarded by its own idempotency key. Rule enforced in graph topology: every node that writes to GitHub, posts social, sends email, or submits to Search Console sits strictly downstream of a confirmed interrupt; no edge bypasses a gate. Reject at any gate routes to END(status=killed) with state preserved for audit. No time-based auto-advance past any gate.
deploy_notes: langgraph.json: {"dependencies":["."],"graphs":{"content_forge":"./content_forge/graph.py:graph"},"env":".env","python_version":"3.12","image_distro":"wolfi","pip_installer":"uv"}. graph.py builds StateGraph(State) with a TypedDict State (product, topic, topic_hint, source_ref, voice_profile, outline, draft, qa_report, approved_draft, reviewer, published_url, distribution_package, channel_results, status) and compiles WITHOUT a checkpointer/store (platform injects production Postgres - passing InMemorySaver is local-only and ignored in hosted runtime). The 4 gates use langgraph interrupt(); resumption via Command(resume=...) through the /runs API or Agent Inbox. Enable store.index in langgraph.json (openai:text-embedding-3-small) if you want semantic dedup of past topics/posts; add checkpointer.ttl to expire old threads. Deploy: `langgraph deploy` (CLI) or connect the repo in LangSmith Deployments (Cloud SaaS, api.smith.langchain.com) - never commit real secrets to .env; inject all of secrets_needed via the deployment UI/API env settings. Configure native cron schedules per the content calendar (separate cron per product). Wire the finalize webhook to the analytics layer. CONSTRAINT-COMPLIANT: Claude is used only for orchestration/generation - no model training, fine-tuning, evaluation-for-training, or distillation occurs anywhere in this graph; this is ordinary application/infra automation. Automatable_score 75 maps cleanly: AI does ideation/draft/QA/derivatives (the volume), humans keep the 4 creative/irreversible decisions (the bottleneck the blueprint flags). Suggested first deploy = Stratus only (its blog is already file-based with _meta.yaml status states, the cleanest publish target), then add the Scheduler blog target once its publish surface is confirmed.
impact: Directly attacks the four named pain points. (1) 20-30 min manual editing time per post: AI handles ideation, full drafting, deterministic QA, and all per-channel derivatives, so the human's job shrinks to review/approve rather than authoring - same human time-box but covering far more output toward the 5 posts/week Scheduler target. (2) Human review bottleneck: it stays a deliberate gate (correctly - quality/legal), but it becomes the ONLY hands-on step, and threads pausing in Postgres mean reviewers act async via the Agent Inbox without blocking the pipeline. (3) Cross-platform distribution coordination: collapsed into one approved distribution_package fanned out to blog + social + email + Search Console from a single gate, with per-channel idempotency so resumes never double-post. (4) Brand-voice consistency across two products: enforced structurally - product-specific voice profiles, banned-word/headline QA, and the Scheduler anti-overclaim check against the canonical submission-profile - so voice rules are applied every run instead of relying on human memory. Net: the blueprint's '10x output, same team' goal becomes operational while keeping every irreversible publish/send under explicit human control.

process: Social Media Content Planning & Publishing — multi-platform (LinkedIn, X, Facebook) content strategy spanning the content calendar, 5 content pillars (Building in Public, Industry Insights, Product Updates, Founder Journey, AI Governance), posting schedules, and CONTENT-RULES.md-governed pre-publish verification. v1 turns the weekly cadence (Tue product/technical, Thu industry insight, optional Sat founder reflection) into drafted, fact-checked, founder-approved, scheduled posts. The bottleneck this removes is manual verification + scheduling coordination; the bottleneck it explicitly preserves is Shay's approval (human-in-the-loop interrupt before any publish/schedule).
trigger: Two triggers. (1) Scheduled cron (LangGraph Platform `crons` / Assistant cron) firing each plan cycle — recommended Sunday ~17:00 EST to draft the upcoming Tue/Thu (+optional Sat) batch ahead of the 8-10am EST posting windows. (2) On-demand HTTP run (`POST /threads/{id}/runs`) for ad-hoc one-off posts (e.g., real event recap, milestone). Each run is keyed to a thread so the Postgres checkpointer persists the interrupt/resume state across the approval gap.
graph_steps: load_context — fetch CONTENT-RULES.md, calendar-framework.md, content-pillars.md from the EscalateLabs/marketing repo (read-only, via a pinned ref or BaseStore-cached copy) and the live canonical submission profile (GET seo.scheduler-systems.com/api/submission-profile) for current pricing/product facts. Determine which slots this cycle needs (Tue=product/technical, Thu=industry-insight, optional Sat=founder-reflection) and the monthly theme rotation.

select_pillar_and_topic — map each calendar slot to a content pillar and pick a topic only from that pillar's 'Safe topics' list, enforcing the 80% value / 15% engagement / 5% promotion ratio across the batch. No topic without a real, verifiable hook (no invented stories/metrics).

draft_posts — Claude generates per-platform variants (LinkedIn long-form, X short/threadable, Facebook) tagged with pillar, platform, target post time (LinkedIn 8-10am EST, X 9am/12pm EST), and an explicit claims-list extracting every factual assertion (numbers, customer quotes, feature claims, pricing, competitor comparisons).

verify_claims — run the CONTENT-RULES.md verification gate as a deterministic + LLM-assisted check: cross-check each extracted claim against the submission profile (pricing is per-user/tiered NOT flat, NOT offline-first) and named-source requirement for stats; flag fabricated stories/quotes, unverified metrics, false feature claims, contradictory positioning, attribution to John Rush instead of Shay. Any unresolved claim -> route back to draft_posts (bounded retries) or strip the claim.

pre_publish_checklist — assemble the calendar-framework pre-publish checklist (every claim verifiable, no fabricated stories, features match product, pricing matches, ratio respected) into a single reviewable approval payload per post.

HUMAN_APPROVAL_interrupt — interrupt() the graph and surface the full batch (drafts + per-claim verification evidence + checklist) to Shay via a notification channel; block until Shay returns approve / edit / reject per post. This is the mandatory gate — nothing downstream runs without it.
apply_decisions — resume with Shay's Command(resume=...): drop rejected posts, apply edited copy verbatim (re-running verify_claims on any edited text), keep approved-as-is.

schedule_publish — for approved posts ONLY, call the scheduling/publish tool (Buffer/Hootsuite API or direct LinkedIn/X/Facebook API) to schedule each at its platform-optimal EST window. This is the sole irreversible/external-side-effect node and runs strictly post-approval. Writes scheduled-post IDs back to BaseStore for idempotency (no double-posting on retry).
record_and_report — persist the cycle outcome (what was approved, scheduled IDs, posting times, pillar/ratio tally) to the store and emit a summary back on the thread for the weekly performance loop.
impact: Removes the two automatable pain points (manual verification + multi-platform scheduling coordination) while keeping the founder-approval gate that the rules mandate, turning a weekly manual chore into a Sunday-night review-and-approve session. Aligns with the existing MARKETING_AUTOMATION_BLUEPRINT Workflow 2 (Social Media Pipeline) but adds the enforced CONTENT-RULES verification layer that the blueprint's '100% automated' posting lacked — which matters because the repo already shows real overclaim risk (directory profiles overclaiming time-tracking/AI; pricing drift between content-pillars.md and the canonical profile). Revenue tie-in: protects Scheduler/GAL brand integrity and feeds the top-of-funnel that drives signups, without risking false claims that erode trust. automatable_score 60 is honest: drafting + verification + scheduling automate well; topic taste and final approval stay human.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform managed deployment (LangSmith Deployments) — Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore auto-provisioned; do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= to .compile()
Claude API (Anthropic) via langchain-anthropic — orchestration/drafting only, NO model training/distillation
GitHub / repo read access to EscalateLabs/marketing for CONTENT-RULES.md, calendar-framework.md, content-pillars.md (read-only, pinned ref)
Canonical submission-profile API (GET seo.scheduler-systems.com/api/submission-profile) as pricing/product source of truth for the verify_claims node
Social scheduling: Buffer or Hootsuite API (recommended for v1) OR direct LinkedIn API + X/Twitter API v2 + Facebook Graph API
Notification channel for the approval interrupt (Slack DM to Shay or email) carrying the resume link/run id
LangGraph crons for the weekly Sunday plan trigger
LangSmith tracing for observability of drafts vs. verification verdicts
secrets_needed: SUBMISSION_PROFILE_URL (or API token if the endpoint is auth-gated)
BUFFER_ACCESS_TOKEN or HOOTSUITE_ACCESS_TOKEN (if using an aggregator)
SLACK_BOT_TOKEN or SMTP/email creds for the approval-notification channel
approval_gates: Single mandatory human-in-the-loop gate at HUMAN_APPROVAL_interrupt, implemented with LangGraph's interrupt() + resume via Command(resume=...). It sits AFTER drafting/verification and BEFORE the only irreversible node (schedule_publish). Shay reviews each post with its per-claim verification evidence and the pre-publish checklist, then returns approve / edit / reject per post; edits are re-verified before they can proceed. schedule_publish executes for approved posts only and writes scheduled-post IDs to the store for idempotency so a resume/retry never double-posts. No publish, schedule, or any external send can occur on any path that has not passed this gate — matching CONTENT-RULES.md ('Submit for Shay's approval; only then publish') and the workspace 'no send without approval' rule. Secrets are injected via the platform env at deploy time, never hardcoded.
deploy_notes: langgraph.json: dependencies ["."], python_version "3.13", image_distro "wolfi", pip_installer "uv"; graphs {"social_content_planner": "./agent/graph.py:graph"}; configure store.index (openai:text-embedding-3-small / 1536) so prior-cycle topics are semantically dedup-searchable, and a checkpointer.ttl so abandoned (never-approved) threads sweep. The compiled graph must NOT set its own checkpointer/store — the managed platform injects production Postgres versions; an InMemorySaver would be silently overridden and is local-only. Define State with an add_messages-reduced messages field plus typed fields for slots, drafts[], claims[], verification_verdicts[], approval_decisions[], scheduled_ids[]. Deploy via the langgraph CLI or the LangSmith Deployments UI against api.smith.langchain.com (use eu. region if data residency requires). Set up a cron on the deployed assistant for the Sunday 17:00 EST plan run. Two compliance guardrails to enforce in review: (1) this is pure LLM orchestration — no AI/ML model training/eval/distillation (Anthropic Consumer Terms / workspace AGENTS.md) — fully permitted; (2) the verify_claims node is the load-bearing safety control — keep its source-of-truth reads (submission profile + pinned CONTENT-RULES) authoritative and fail-closed (if the profile fetch fails, block the affected post rather than publishing on stale facts).

process: SEO & Backlink Acquisition — autonomous execution of the safe slice of the Growth Console backlink/directory loop (research enrichment, directory submissions, backlink rechecks, metadata-drift detection, email-verification polling, GSC indexing health), with every irreversible or account-personal action routed through a human-in-the-loop interrupt. The agent is the "GAL background agent" the existing code already anticipates: run-directory-updates.js header literally describes the migration "GAL button in Growth Console -> GAL background agent -> dispatchDirectorySubmit() -> Playwright", and the stable contract is dispatchDirectorySubmit(). This LangGraph deployment becomes that background agent. Firestore collection seo_backlinks (project scheduler-systems-web, doc IDs ${sheet}--${rowNumber}) stays the source of truth; the agent reads scriptable rows, acts, and writes results back via the existing writeRunResult() contract — it does NOT introduce a competing store. Honors GROWTH_OPERATING_CONTRACT.md exactly: agent-safe work (crawl/drift, rechecks, email polling, structured metadata compare, draft/issue prep) runs unattended; CAPTCHA/anti-bot is GAL-first (attempt audio challenge) then human fallback; publishing, outreach sends, and ownership transfers tied to personal accounts are human-required and gated. Orchestration only — calls Claude for classification/drafting/decision and Playwright/MCP tools for execution; performs NO model training/distillation (complies with workspace Anthropic-terms guardrail since this orchestrates an LLM, it does not build one).
trigger: Three triggers. (1) Scheduled LangGraph Platform cron (the platform's native Crons API) — e.g. backlink-recheck + drift sweep every 6h, email-verification poll every 30m, GSC indexing-health daily. These run the "safe background jobs" the contract authorizes. (2) Human-initiated from Growth Console (https://growth.scheduler-systems.com/backlinks) — an operator clicks a row/action which POSTs a Run to the deployment thread (mirrors the planned "GAL button"). (3) Webhook on inbound directory verification email (optional, to short-circuit polling). Each run targets a sheet filter (default scheduler-systems.com) + optional --directory scope, mirroring run-directory-updates.js CLI flags so behavior is identical to the current runner.
graph_steps: ingest_and_scope (entry): normalize the Run input into State — job_type (recheck | drift | submit | email_poll | gsc_health | research_enrich), sheet filter, directory scope, dry_run flag. Load scriptable rows via getScriptableRows() from utils/firestore-backlinks.js (seo_backlinks). NO model call — pure data fetch.

classify_rows (Claude orchestration): for each candidate row, ask Claude to bucket the action into AGENT_SAFE (crawl/recheck/poll/metadata-compare/draft) vs HUMAN_REQUIRED (ownership transfer, personal-account claim, publish/send) vs CAPTCHA_LIKELY, per GROWTH_OPERATING_CONTRACT.md. Output a typed plan; this is the policy gate that keeps unsafe work off the autonomous path.

branch_by_job_type (conditional edge): route to recheck_node | drift_node | submit_node | email_poll_node | gsc_health_node | research_node based on job_type and the safety bucket. HUMAN_REQUIRED rows are diverted straight to the approval interrupt and never auto-executed.

execute_safe_node (one of recheck/drift/email_poll/gsc_health/research): perform the agent-safe action. recheck = live listing crawl + review-status fetch; drift = structured metadata compare (live vs stored) and flag deltas; email_poll = Gmail search for verification link; gsc_health = call Search Console URL Inspection (indexing-health.js logic) for the critical pages; research = enrich DA/traffic/gap via the SEO MCP tools. These write results but take NO irreversible external action — safe to run unattended.

submit_prepare_node (for job_type=submit): resolve credentials via credential-resolver.js (growth-cli API -> Firestore config/active-credential — never hardcoded), build the submission payload, and dry-run dispatchDirectorySubmit() to detect CAPTCHA / anti-bot. If clean and AGENT_SAFE -> proceed to interrupt. If CAPTCHA_LIKELY -> captcha_node.

captcha_node (GAL-first per contract): attempt the audio-challenge fallback once. On success continue to the approval interrupt; on failure set needs_human=true with the screenshot and reason, and route to the interrupt as a human-required item rather than retrying blindly.

approval_gate (interrupt() — HUMAN-IN-THE-LOOP, BLOCKING): for ANY submit/publish/send/ownership action, pause the graph with a structured proposal (directory, target URL, fields, credential identity used, CAPTCHA status, dry-run diff). The thread stays parked on LangGraph Platform's Postgres checkpointer until a human resumes with approve / edit / reject via Command(resume=...). Nothing irreversible happens before this returns approved.

execute_approved_node: only reachable after approval. Call dispatchDirectorySubmit() (the stable contract) for real (dry_run=false). Capture listing URL, status, screenshots. This is the single place external irreversible writes occur, and it is unconditionally downstream of the interrupt.

persist_and_report_node: write outcomes back via writeRunResult() to seo_backlinks (sheet--row doc), update review-status/drift fields, and post a status summary as the contract requires (Growth Console / issue comment, not scattered notes). Emit drift/CAPTCHA-failure findings as a marketing GitHub issue draft for the human owner. END.
impact: Directly attacks the named pain points: removes the manual coordination of the recurring loop (rechecks, drift, email polling — explicitly the contract's "recurring operations") by running them on platform crons; turns CAPTCHA from a hard manual stop into a GAL-first audio attempt with a clean human fallback; and keeps the $250/mo Moz spend bounded by gating paid-tier gap-analysis calls behind the same orchestrator (free tools first, escalate only when needed). automatable_score is 70 because the high-value-but-unsafe slice (ownership transfers tied to personal accounts, outreach sends, publishing) is deliberately kept human-gated — the agent automates the safe ~70% and makes the unsafe 30% a fast one-click approval instead of a full manual workflow. Revenue framing per workspace priorities: SEO/backlinks is a slow top-of-funnel bet (not protected live RevenueCat revenue), so P2 — ship it as cost/time savings on existing growth ops, do not divert from revenue-critical Scheduler work. Reuses existing, tested contracts (dispatchDirectorySubmit, writeRunResult, getScriptableRows, credential-resolver) so build risk is low.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS tier; api.smith.langchain.com) — Agent Server runtime, auto-provisioned Postgres checkpointer + store, native Crons API for scheduled triggers, interrupt()/Command for HITL
Anthropic Claude API (orchestration/classification/drafting only — model is consumed, never trained/distilled, per workspace Anthropic-terms guardrail)
Playwright (directory submission/crawl) via the existing automation/src/directories/* adapters and submit-dispatcher.js dispatchDirectorySubmit() stable contract
Firestore (collection seo_backlinks, project scheduler-systems-web) via firebase-admin — source of truth, accessed through getScriptableRows()/writeRunResult()
Growth CLI API (https://growth.scheduler-systems.com + local api server) for credential resolution (config/active-credential) and Growth Console status writeback
Gmail MCP (verification-email polling / link extraction)
Google Search Console URL Inspection API (indexing-health.js logic, webmasters.readonly) for GSC indexing health

Directory targets: BetaList, BetaPage, LaunchingNext, StartupRanking, AlternativeTo (plus existing adapters: Capterra/G2/GetApp/Crozdesk/SaaSHub/SoftwareSuggest/TrustRadius/SourceForge/StackShare/F6S/Crunchbase/FinancesOnline)
SEO data tools: Ahrefs + Semrush (free tiers) and Moz Pro API (paid, $250/mo — gated/escalation-only) for DA/traffic and competitor gap analysis
GitHub (marketing repo) for issue/draft prep and closure notes per the operating contract
approval_gates: HARD interrupt() gate before any irreversible or personal-account action — implemented as a LangGraph dynamic interrupt that parks the thread on the platform's Postgres checkpointer until a human resumes via Command(resume={approve|edit|reject}). Gated actions (from GROWTH_OPERATING_CONTRACT.md 'Human-required'): real directory submission/registration (execute_approved_node), any outreach send, any social/listing publish, and ownership transfers tied to personal accounts (these are diverted at classify_rows and NEVER auto-executed — they can only be hand-completed by the named account holder, the agent just prepares the row handoff). CAPTCHA is GAL-first (one audio attempt) then falls to the same human gate on failure. Agent-safe actions (crawl/drift/recheck/email-poll/GSC-inspect/research/draft) run WITHOUT a gate, exactly as the contract authorizes. dry_run defaults true on submit paths so the human approves a concrete diff. Secondary guardrail: classify_rows is the policy filter — any row Claude buckets HUMAN_REQUIRED or CAPTCHA_LIKELY cannot reach execute_approved_node without passing the interrupt.
secrets_needed: GOOGLE_SHEETS_SERVICE_ACCOUNT (or GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS / FIREBASE_ADMIN service-account JSON for seo_backlinks Firestore in scheduler-systems-web)
GROWTH_API_BASE_URL + GROWTH_API_TOKEN (growth-cli API for credential resolution and Growth Console writeback)
GMAIL_OAUTH credentials / Gmail MCP token (verification-email polling)
DIRECTORY_SUBMISSION_CREDENTIALS are NOT stored as static env — resolved at runtime from Firestore config/active-credential via credential-resolver.js, never hardcoded
deploy_notes: Repo: new langgraph-seo-agent package (or a worktree under EscalateLabs/marketing/worktrees/) wrapping the existing automation/src JS contracts. langgraph.json at root: {"dependencies":["."],"graphs":{"seo_backlink_ops":"./src/agent.py:graph"},"env":".env","python_version":"3.12","image_distro":"wolfi","checkpointer":{"ttl":{"strategy":"delete","default_ttl":43200}}}. Build graph as a StateGraph, .compile() to module-level `graph` — do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= (platform injects production Postgres). State (TypedDict): job_type, sheet, directory_scope, dry_run, rows, plan (safe/human/captcha buckets), pending_action, approval_result, results, findings. The Python graph shells out to / calls the proven Node contracts (dispatchDirectorySubmit, getScriptableRows, writeRunResult) so logic isn't reimplemented — keep dispatchDirectorySubmit() as the stable seam exactly as run-directory-updates.js documents. Deploy via `langgraph deploy` CLI (or LangSmith UI) to the managed Cloud SaaS tier; inject all secrets through the platform UI/API (never commit). Wire native platform Crons: recheck+drift q6h, email_poll q30m, gsc_health daily — each a separate cron POSTing a Run with the matching job_type. Human approvals: surface interrupt payloads to the operator via Growth Console (subscribe to thread interrupts) and resume with Command(resume=...). Tie work to a documented GitHub issue in marketing first (workspace rule). Anthropic-terms note: this is orchestration that CALLS Claude — it does not train/distill/serve any model, so it is permitted; keep gal-model and any ML-eval code out of this deployment entirely. v1 scope cut: ship recheck + drift + email_poll + gsc_health (pure agent-safe, zero irreversible writes) FIRST to validate the platform + crons with no approval risk, then enable the gated submit path once the interrupt UX is confirmed in Growth Console.

process: App Store Optimization (ASO) for the Scheduler app across iOS and Android — keyword research, ranking tracking, competitor analysis, metadata-change recommendations, change logging, and bi-weekly cycle reporting. This LangGraph (managed) agent ports the existing five slash-command workflow (/aso-start, /aso-track-change, /aso-track-keywords, /aso-research, /aso-competitor-analysis) into one resumable StateGraph deployed on LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments). It reads/writes the same five data artifacts (aso-config.json, aso-keywords.json, aso-ranking-history.json, aso-change-log.json, aso-competitor-analysis.json) but treats every write/publish step as approval-gated. The agent ORCHESTRATES (calls Claude for analysis/recommendations + calls ranking-data sources) — it does NOT train, fine-tune, distill, or evaluate any ML model.
trigger: Two triggers. (1) Scheduled: a LangGraph Platform cron (assistant cron) on the 1st and 15th of each month at 00:00 UTC for the bi-weekly cycle, plus a weekly Monday cron for keyword-only ranking refresh — mirrors the existing biweekly-aso-export.yml cron '0 0 1,15 * *'. (2) On-demand: a thread created via the platform API/SDK with input {mode: "cycle" | "track_keywords" | "research" | "competitor_analysis" | "track_change", cycle_name?, keyword?, competitor?, change_description?} — one entrypoint per former slash command. Both paths run the same graph; mode routes to the right subgraph.
