graph_steps: START -> route_mode: read input.mode and conditionally branch to the matching subgraph (cycle / track_keywords / research / competitor_analysis / track_change). Validate args; if mode missing or ambiguous, interrupt() asking the human which run to perform.

load_state: read the 5 ASO artifacts from the long-term BaseStore (namespace ('aso','scheduler')) seeded from repo data/aso-*.json — aso-config.json (metadata, cycle dates, archived AppTweak status), aso-keywords.json (25 tracked keywords + categories), aso-ranking-history.json, aso-change-log.json, aso-competitor-analysis.json. Compute days_since_last_snapshot.

fetch_rankings (cycle + track_keywords): select data source via the same auto-detect as automation/src/aso/index.js — if APPTWEAK_API_KEY present use AppTweak API tool; else free-scrape (iTunes Search API for iOS, Play Store scrape for Android). Per keyword record rank_ios, rank_android, top-3 competitor positions. READ-ONLY external calls (no gate). Tag every datapoint with source+confidence (scrape ranks are approximations).

research_keywords (research mode): call Claude to expand/score keyword opportunities and competitor keyword strategies across the 6 categories (Primary/Secondary/Industry/Feature/Long-tail/Brand); produce a proposed delta to aso-keywords.json. No external publish.

analyze_competitors (competitor_analysis mode): for When I Work / Deputy / Sling (+ secondary tier) fetch live store metadata (WebFetch read-only), call Claude to score ASO (title/subtitle/desc/visuals/reviews), build keyword-gap + white-space analysis; produce proposed aso-competitor-analysis.json.

compare_and_analyze (cycle): diff current vs previous rankings (improved/declined/new/lost/stable), cross-reference aso-change-log.json to attribute movement to logged changes, compute ASO health score and summary metrics.

generate_recommendations: Claude produces Quick Wins / Strategic / Long-term recommendations and concrete metadata-change proposals (title<=30, subtitle<=30, iOS keyword field<=100 chars) for iOS + Android, each with rationale, targeted keywords, expected impact, effort. NOTHING is written or published yet.

human_review_gate (interrupt #1 — REQUIRED before any write): surface the proposed JSON deltas + the cycle report + any recommended live store metadata changes to the human via interrupt(). Human resumes with approve / edit / reject per item. Live App-Store-Connect / Play-Console edits remain MANUAL by the human — the agent only recommends, it never touches the stores.

persist_artifacts (post-approval): write the approved deltas back to the BaseStore and to a working branch's data/aso-*.json (cycle snapshot appended to aso-ranking-history.json, change recorded to aso-change-log.json for track_change mode, updated config/keywords/competitor files). Idempotent, keyed by cycle_name/date.

publish_gate (interrupt #2 — REQUIRED, separate from review): the ONLY step that opens a PR. Replaces the auto-merge: create branch chore/aso-cycle-<date>, commit approved data files, open PR via gh API tool with the generated report as the body. Explicitly DO NOT run `gh pr merge --admin` — merge stays a human action (this fixes the existing no-auto-merge concern). Optional notify step posts the report link to Slack/email for the human.

END: emit final state {report_md, pr_url?, approved_changes[], next_snapshot_due (date+14d), source_used, unresolved_items[]}. Checkpointer persists the thread so an interrupted cycle resumes exactly where the human left off.
impact: Removes the manual slash-command babysitting of the bi-weekly ASO cycle and, critically, replaces the current dangerous auto-merge (`gh pr merge --squash --admin` in biweekly-aso-export.yml) with two explicit human-in-the-loop gates — directly addressing the existing fix/aso-export-no-auto-merge branch. Keeps a single resumable, observable run (LangSmith traces) for the whole cycle instead of 5 disconnected commands. Revenue tie-in is indirect and slow: better organic ASO -> more installs of the per-user $2.99/$4.99 paid app, but ASO is a long-horizon lever (30/90/180-day goals in the guide), so this is genuinely P2 vs revenue-critical work. Automatable score 65 is honest: ranking capture + analysis + report drafting automate well; live store metadata edits and final PR merge stay human.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS) — Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore auto-provisioned; do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= to .compile()
Claude (Anthropic API via langchain-anthropic) — analysis, recommendations, competitor scoring, report drafting (orchestration only, no model training/distillation)
iTunes Search API (free, public, no auth) — iOS keyword ranking capture (ported from automation/src/aso/scraper.js searchIos)
Google Play Store scrape via Playwright/playwright-extra + stealth — Android ranking capture (ported from scraper.js); run as an external tool/service, not inside the managed graph if heavy
AppTweak API tool — used ONLY when APPTWEAK_API_KEY is configured; currently archived (subscription lapsed 2026-03-11), so default path is free-scraping
WebFetch/WebSearch tool — read-only competitor store-page metadata and keyword research
GitHub via gh CLI / REST tool — open PR with data deltas + report (PR creation gated; merge stays manual)
Slack or email notifier (optional) — post cycle report + PR link to human for review
Existing repo data contract: EscalateLabs/marketing/data/aso-config.json, aso-keywords.json, aso-ranking-history.json, aso-change-log.json, aso-competitor-analysis.json
secrets_needed: GITHUB_TOKEN (PR creation only; scope without auto-merge/admin)
APPTWEAK_API_KEY (optional — only when AppTweak subscription restored; absent today -> free-scrape path)
APPTWEAK_EMAIL (optional — legacy Playwright export, only if scraping AppTweak UI)
approval_gates: Two mandatory human-in-the-loop interrupt() gates, never bypassed. Gate 1 (human_review_gate): before ANY write to the BaseStore/repo, the human reviews the proposed JSON deltas, the cycle report, and any recommended live store metadata changes; resumes with approve/edit/reject per item. Gate 2 (publish_gate): a separate explicit approval before opening the GitHub PR; the agent NEVER merges (removes the existing `gh pr merge --admin`) — merge is a human action in GitHub. Live App Store Connect / Google Play Console metadata edits are out of scope for the agent entirely (recommend-only, human applies them). A third implicit gate: route_mode interrupts if the requested run mode is ambiguous. All secrets via env injection on the platform; nothing hardcoded. No ML model is trained, fine-tuned, distilled, or evaluated — orchestration calling Claude/LLMs only.
deploy_notes: langgraph.json at repo root: {"dependencies":["."],"graphs":{"aso":"./aso_agent/graph.py:graph"},"env":".env","python_version":"3.12","image_distro":"wolfi","store":{"index":{"embed":"openai:text-embedding-3-small","dims":1536}},"checkpointer":{"ttl":{"strategy":"delete","default_ttl":43200}}}. graph.py builds a StateGraph(State, input_schema=ASOInput, output_schema=ASOOutput) with conditional edges from route_mode, uses interrupt() at both gates, and exports a module-level compiled `graph` WITHOUT a custom checkpointer/store (platform injects Postgres). Heavy Playwright scraping should run as an out-of-process tool/microservice (or the existing automation/src/aso scraper called over HTTP) rather than inside the agent container to keep the image lean and avoid browser deps in wolfi; iOS uses the dependency-free iTunes Search API directly. Deploy via `langgraph deploy` CLI or the LangSmith UI; inject all secrets through the platform UI/API (never in .env committed to git). Schedule the bi-weekly and weekly runs with platform assistant cron jobs to retire biweekly-aso-export.yml. First rollout: dry-run mode (gates surface proposals but persist nothing) until a human has validated 1-2 full cycles, then enable persist + PR. Seed the BaseStore once from the current data/aso-*.json files. Constraint compliance: deploy target is LangGraph Cloud; orchestration-only (no gal-model / no AI-ML model dev); both irreversible actions (repo write, PR open) gated; secrets via env.

process: Landing Page Copywriting & A/B Testing — automates the design-analyze loop for hero-section conversion optimization across the main landing page and the five use-case pages (/restaurant-scheduling, /retail-scheduling, /healthcare-scheduling, /small-business, /enterprise). The agent ingests the EscalateLabs/marketing/data/landing-page guideline corpus (copywriting-guidelines.md, current-audit.md, ab-testing.md, use-case-pages.md, hero-optimization.md), audits a target page, generates guideline-compliant copy variants (headline / subheadline / CTA / hero-visual brief / above-fold social proof), verifies every claim against the product claims-truth ledger, designs a single-variable A/B test with a pre-registered hypothesis and metric, and — once a test is running — pulls experiment data and runs a fixed-rule significance analysis to recommend ship/keep-control/keep-running. It writes nothing live and ships nothing on its own: copy commits, experiment creation, and winner promotion are all human-approval interrupts.
trigger: Three entry points into the same deployed graph (each becomes an addressable run via the LangGraph Platform Runs/Threads API): (1) on-demand HTTP invoke from a marketing operator — payload {page_slug, mode: "design"|"analyze", optional brief} — the everyday path; (2) scheduled cron (LangGraph Platform Cron / external scheduler) running the "analyze" mode daily at 09:00 for every test whose status is RUNNING, to apply the 14-day-floor + 95%-significance + 30-day-cutoff rules and surface tests that are ready to call; (3) webhook from the experimentation tool (PostHog/Google Optimize/VWO) when a test reaches its sample-size target, kicking off an analyze run. No trigger ever auto-ships; triggers only advance the graph to the next human-approval interrupt.
graph_steps: START -> load_context: read the 5 landing-page guideline files + the product claims-truth ledger (per-user $2.99/$4.99, one-click deterministic roster builder, chat, CSV export — NOT time-tracking/clock, NOT offline, NOT AI/auto-scheduling, NOT flat-rate) into state via the injected BaseStore; route on state.mode.
route(mode) -> conditional edge: 'design' branch -> audit_page; 'analyze' branch -> fetch_experiment_data.

audit_page (design): score the target page's current hero against copywriting-guidelines.md (John Rush Rule, headline formula, 3-line/4-line limits, friction-answering, mobile-first 320px order, specificity) and current-audit.md issues; emit a structured findings list.

generate_variants (design): produce 3-5 single-variable variants for the prioritized hero element (Headline > CTA > Hero Image > Social Proof), each as control-vs-variant with a one-line rationale, drawing from hero-optimization.md candidates and use-case-pages.md positioning. LLM (Claude) generation only — no model training.

verify_claims (design): adversarial check of every generated/selected claim against the claims-truth ledger. Any variant asserting time-tracking, offline-first, AI/auto-scheduling, or flat-rate pricing for an app that bills per-user is FLAGGED and rewritten or dropped. Hard gate — design cannot proceed with an unverifiable claim.

design_test (design): assemble one A/B test object — variable, control, variant, primary metric, pre-registered hypothesis, 50/50 split, sample-size/duration plan (>=14 days AND >=1,000 visitors), 95% significance threshold, 30-day cutoff.

INTERRUPT #1 approve_test_plan (human-in-the-loop): surface the full plan (variants + claim-verification report + test design) to the operator. interrupt() pauses the thread; resume payload = approve | edit | reject. Nothing has been written yet.

commit_copy_and_create_experiment (design, post-approval): ONLY after approval — write the chosen copy to a marketing-repo branch/PR (never to main, never a deploy) and create the experiment draft in PostHog/Optimize. Both are emitted as proposed diffs/payloads; actual git push + experiment activation are themselves gated behind INTERRUPT #1's approval scope. -> END (test now RUNNING, owned by the experimentation tool).
fetch_experiment_data (analyze): pull live experiment stats (impressions, conversions per arm, elapsed days) from the experimentation tool's read API.

guard_duration (analyze): conditional edge — if elapsed < 14 days OR visitors < 1,000 -> emit 'keep running, too early' and END (no significance test run, prevents peeking/early-stopping). Else -> analyze_significance.

analyze_significance (analyze): compute conversion lift + two-proportion test; classify as WINNER (>=95% sig), LOSER, or INCONCLUSIVE. If day >= 30 and still inconclusive -> recommend keep-control per the framework's 30-day rule.

INTERRUPT #2 approve_decision (human-in-the-loop): present the stats + recommended action (promote variant / keep control / keep running). interrupt() pauses; resume payload authorizes the irreversible promotion.

promote_or_archive (analyze, post-approval): ONLY after approval — open the promotion PR (winning copy to the page) or archive the test; record outcome + the 30-day success metric back into the BaseStore for future audits. Never auto-merges, never deploys. -> END.
impact: Compresses the manual design-and-analysis halves of a 14-day-minimum test cycle: auto-audits pages against documented guidelines, drafts guideline-compliant + claim-verified variants in minutes instead of copywriting review cycles, enforces the 14-day / 1,000-visitor / 95%-significance / 30-day rules consistently (removes human peeking/early-stopping bias), and keeps the 6 pages' positioning coherent. Automatable score is 50: variant generation, statistical analysis, and rule-enforcement are highly automatable, but creative judgment, the claims-truth gate, and any live change stay human-owned. Directly protects revenue by preventing the agent from shipping the known overclaim landmine (time-tracking / offline / AI-scheduling / flat-rate copy on a per-user-billed product) into live marketing.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform (managed / LangSmith Deployments) — injected Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore (do NOT pass checkpointer/store to .compile())
Anthropic Claude (claude-opus / claude-sonnet) via langchain-anthropic — orchestration/generation only, NO model training/distillation
Repo file reader for EscalateLabs/marketing/data/landing-page/*.md (guideline corpus) — read-only tool
Product claims-truth ledger (config/JSON seeded into BaseStore) — the anti-overclaim source of truth
GitHub API (PR-only branch writes for copy; never push to main, never deploy) — write path lives entirely behind approval interrupts
PostHog (or Google Optimize / VWO) experimentation API — read stats + create experiment draft; webhook source for sample-size-reached trigger
scipy / statsmodels two-proportion significance test (in-graph compute)
LangGraph Platform Cron + Runs/Threads API for the scheduled daily analyze sweep
langgraph.json: dependencies ['.'], graphs {'lp_optimizer':'./agent/graph.py:graph'}, python_version 3.12, image_distro wolfi, store.index embed openai:text-embedding-3-small for guideline retrieval
approval_gates: Two mandatory human-in-the-loop interrupt() gates, both fail-closed (no resume = no action): (1) approve_test_plan — blocks BEFORE any copy is written to the repo or any experiment is created; operator sees generated variants, the claims-verification report, and the full test design (variable/control/variant/metric/duration/significance plan) and must approve|edit|reject; the post-approval node performs PR-only writes (no main commit, no deploy). (2) approve_decision — blocks BEFORE any winner promotion or test archive; operator sees the significance stats and recommended action and must authorize the irreversible promotion PR. Additional hard guards (non-interrupt, deterministic): claims-truth verification gate (design cannot pass with an unverifiable/overclaiming statement), and the duration guard (no significance call before 14 days AND 1,000 visitors; 30-day cutoff defaults to keep-control). Every send/publish/irreversible action is gated; all secrets (Anthropic key, GitHub token, PostHog key) injected via LangGraph Platform env, never hardcoded.
deploy_notes: Build a minimal repo: agent/graph.py exporting a module-level `graph = builder.compile()` (StateGraph over a TypedDict state holding mode, page_slug, guideline_corpus, claims_ledger, audit, variants, claim_report, test_design, experiment_stats, decision); langgraph.json at root pointing graphs.lp_optimizer -> ./agent/graph.py:graph, dependencies ['.'], python_version 3.12, image_distro wolfi, store.index {embed:'openai:text-embedding-3-small', dims:1536} for semantic retrieval over the guideline corpus, checkpointer.ttl for thread cleanup; pyproject.toml with langgraph, langchain-anthropic, scipy/statsmodels, httpx (PostHog), PyGithub. Critical: do NOT pass checkpointer= or store= to .compile() — the managed platform injects production Postgres versions; InMemorySaver is local-only. Implement the two human gates with langgraph.types.interrupt() inside their nodes and resume via Command(resume=...). Use the factory form (path:make_graph) only if per-request config is needed. Deploy via `langgraph deploy` (CLI) or the LangSmith Deployments UI; inject all six secrets through the platform env (never .env in git). Seed the claims-truth ledger into the BaseStore on first run (load_context idempotently upserts it). Cron for the daily analyze sweep created via LangGraph Platform Cron against the Runs API with input {mode:'analyze', scope:'all-running'}. Worktree discipline: do real implementation in StratusCloudLabs/stratus/worktrees/ or EscalateLabs/marketing/worktrees/, not in the live marketing checkout; tie the work to a documented GitHub issue first. This is application/orchestration code only — no ML model is developed, trained, or served, so it is within Anthropic-terms guardrails.

trigger: Three entry points, all converging on one graph: (1) HTTP webhook POST to the Agent Server custom route `/webhooks/brevo` mounted via `http.app` in langgraph.json — receives both Scheduler-app lifecycle events (user_signup, user_login, trial_expiring, team_created, team_invite_sent, subscription_changed, user_inactive, user_churned) and Brevo outbound events (email_opened, email_clicked, unsubscribed, hard_bounce, spam_complaint). HMAC-signature-verified in the auth.py handler before any graph run. (2) Daily cron (LangGraph scheduled run / external Cron at 02:00 UTC mirroring brevo-event-sync-config.json syncSchedule) that drives the scheduled-check events (trial_expiring, user_inactive, user_churned) and a template-drift scan. (3) Manual operator invocation via the LangGraph SDK/Studio for one-off template sync, test sends, or campaign setup (mirrors the four /brevo-* slash commands). Each invocation creates one thread keyed by contact email + workflow so the Postgres checkpointer resumes cleanly across the human-approval interrupt.
process: Email Marketing Automation (Brevo) for Scheduler Systems — orchestration-only LangGraph agent that wraps the existing brevo-*.json config + email-templates/ as the source of truth. It does NOT replace Brevo's own automation engine wholesale; it acts as the governed control plane that (a) keeps templates in sync with content-hash drift detection, (b) decides which lifecycle email to queue for a given contact event against frequency caps + suppression state, (c) drafts/personalizes the send, and (d) pauses at a human-in-the-loop interrupt before ANY irreversible action (real send, template create/update in Brevo, list mutation, automation enable). Claude is used only for reasoning/copy-personalization/decision and summary — never trained, fine-tuned, or distilled (complies with workspace Anthropic-terms guardrail). All Brevo writes go through tool nodes that are interrupt-gated. Reads (template hashes, contact attributes, list membership, deliverability metrics) are unguarded. The agent encodes the real workflows from brevo-automation-config.json (welcome Day 0/3/7, trial_expiring Day -7/-3/-1, re_engagement Day 7/30/60), the conditions (no_team_members, not_converted, inactive_N_days), the frequencyCaps (max 2/week, min 24h between, respectUnsubscribes), and the 300/day free-tier rate budget. Addresses the named pain points: (1) template maintenance across clients -> hash-drift detect + optional Claude-assisted spam-score/render lint before gated upload; (2) rate limits -> a token-bucket budget node that defers sends and returns retry-after rather than blowing the 300/day cap; (3) webhook complexity -> single normalized ingest node mapping both inbound app events and Brevo outbound events to a canonical schema; (4) manual template testing -> gated test-send node with sample params; (5) contact-attribute management -> attribute-upsert node mapping the brevo-event-sync-config contactAttributes from app payloads. Critical landmines this spec must respect (from workspace memory): suppression/unsubscribe must be honored BEFORE send (hard gate, not just a cap), no fake-FIRSTNAME merge sends, and biased-collection rules apply to review-request templates — so review_request_* templates are flagged require_extra_approval.
graph_steps: ingest_and_verify (entry node): normalize the incoming payload into a canonical Event{type, contact_email, attributes, source} schema; reject if HMAC sig already failed in auth.py. Branches by event class: lifecycle-app-event | brevo-outbound-event | operator-command (sync/test/setup) | cron-tick.

load_context (read-only tools): fetch contact from Brevo (GET /contacts/{email}), current list memberships, last-N send history + per-week count, global suppression/unsubscribe + hard-bounce/complaint state, and load brevo-templates.json + brevo-automation-config.json from the configured store/repo. Populates State with everything the policy node needs. No writes.

route_by_event (conditional edge, no LLM needed for routing): map event type -> candidate workflow+step using brevo-automation-config.json (user_signup->welcome step1; trial_expiring->trial_expiring steps; user_inactive/churned->re_engagement; brevo unsubscribed/complaint->suppression_update; operator sync->template_sync subgraph; operator test->test_send; cron-tick->scheduled_scan).

suppression_and_eligibility_gate (deterministic guard, fail-closed): hard-stop if contact is unsubscribed, hard-bounced, or spam-complained -> route straight to END with reason='suppressed' (NEVER send). Then enforce frequencyCaps (>2/week or <24h since last -> defer with retry-after). Then evaluate step conditions (no_team_members, not_converted, inactive_7/30/60_days) against loaded attributes. Ineligible -> END with logged reason.

rate_budget_check (deterministic): consult the daily token-bucket (300/day free-tier, 30 req/min). If budget exhausted, set status=deferred and emit a scheduled re-run for the next window instead of sending. Protects the cap called out as a pain point.

compose_or_decide (Claude reasoning node, READ/REASON ONLY): for a send, select template by templateKey, validate required merge attributes are PRESENT and real (block synthetic FIRSTNAME), build the Brevo `params` map from contact attributes, and have Claude produce a short human-readable rationale + a deliverability lint note (spam-trigger words, missing unsub/physical-address, image ratio) drawn from brevo-deliverability-checklist.json. For a template-sync, Claude diffs old vs new HTML and summarizes what changed. Output is a proposed action object only — nothing is sent here.

human_approval (interrupt() — THE gate): call LangGraph `interrupt()` with the full proposed action (recipient, template id+name, resolved params, rationale, lint findings, current rate-budget remaining, and an extra require_extra_approval=true flag for review_request_* templates per biased-collection rules). Run pauses; Postgres checkpointer persists state. Operator resumes with Command(resume={decision: approve|edit|reject}). On edit, params/subject are patched and re-linted. On reject, route to END with audit log. This node guards ALL irreversible actions: real send, template create/update, list add/remove, automation enable.

execute_brevo_write (tool node, runs ONLY post-approval): performs exactly the approved Brevo API call(s) — POST /smtp/email (templateId+params) for sends, POST/PUT /smtp/templates for sync, POST /contacts/lists/{id}/contacts/add|remove for list moves, PUT /contacts/{email} for attribute upsert. Idempotency key = thread_id+step to survive retries. Honors 2s inter-call spacing + 60s backoff on 429.

persist_and_record (write node): update brevo-templates.json (id, contentHash, timestamps) on sync, append to a send/audit log (timestamp, template, recipient, messageId, decision, approver), decrement the rate bucket, and write the run summary to the LangGraph store for cross-thread frequency-cap accounting.

scheduled_scan (cron-only branch): runs the daily checks from brevo-event-sync-config (trial_expiring <=7d, inactive >=7d, churned >=30d), fans out one eligibility-gated candidate action per matching contact, AND does a template content-hash drift scan; every resulting action still funnels through suppression_gate -> human_approval before any write.

notify_and_end: emit a concise result (sent / deferred / suppressed / rejected / synced) back to the webhook caller or operator and to an ops channel; END. Every terminal path carries a machine-readable reason for observability.
tools_integrations: Brevo REST API v3 (api.brevo.com/v3): /smtp/email (send), /smtp/templates (create/update), /contacts, /contacts/{email}, /contacts/lists/{listId}/contacts/add|remove, /contacts/attributes/normal/{name}, /contacts/events — wrapped as LangGraph tool nodes; reads unguarded, writes interrupt-gated
Brevo inbound webhook receiver (custom Starlette route mounted via langgraph.json http.app: ./app.py:app, path /webhooks/brevo) for email_opened/clicked/unsubscribed/hard_bounce/spam_complaint

Scheduler app lifecycle event source (Firestore Cloud Functions / Go scheduler-api posting user_signup, user_login, trial_expiring, team_created, team_invite_sent, subscription_changed, user_inactive, user_churned) — per brevo-event-sync-config.json
Anthropic Claude via langchain (reasoning/personalization/diff-summary/deliverability-lint ONLY — no training/fine-tuning/distillation)
LangGraph Platform managed Postgres checkpointer (auto-injected; do NOT pass a custom saver to .compile()) for thread persistence across the approval interrupt
LangGraph managed BaseStore (long-term, for cross-thread frequency-cap counters and the daily 300/send rate-budget token bucket; store config in langgraph.json)

Repo config as source of truth: brevo-templates.json, brevo-automation-config.json, brevo-event-sync-config.json, brevo-deliverability-checklist.json, email-templates/*.html (mounted via dependencies or fetched from git)
LangGraph custom Auth (langgraph.json auth.path -> ./auth.py:my_auth) for HMAC webhook-signature verification + operator API-key authz
Ops notification channel (Slack/Gmail MCP or webhook) for approval prompts and run summaries
LangSmith tracing/observability (native on LangGraph Platform) for per-run audit of every gated decision
approval_gates: Single mandatory `interrupt()` node (human_approval) that EVERY irreversible action must pass through before execute_brevo_write runs. Gated actions: (1) any real email send (transactional /smtp/email), (2) template create/update in Brevo (/smtp/templates POST/PUT), (3) contact list add/remove, (4) attribute upsert that changes consent-relevant fields, (5) enabling/modifying a Brevo automation workflow. The interrupt payload shows recipient, resolved template+params, Claude rationale, deliverability lint, and remaining rate budget; operator resumes with Command(resume={approve|edit|reject}). Hard fail-closed pre-gates that can SHORT-CIRCUIT to END with no human needed (because the safe action is to NOT send): suppression/unsubscribe/hard-bounce/spam-complaint state, frequency-cap breach (defer), and exhausted 300/day budget (defer). Extra elevated approval flag (require_extra_approval) on review_request_* templates to honor G2/Capterra biased-collection ToS. Synthetic/missing merge attributes (e.g. fake FIRSTNAME) block the proposal from ever reaching send. Read-only nodes (load_context, drift scan, eligibility eval) are NOT gated. Aligns with workspace rules: no send/publish without explicit human approval, and no delete/list-removal without approval.
deploy_notes: Deploy as a managed LangGraph Platform deployment (LangSmith Deployments / Agent Server). langgraph.json at repo root: {"dependencies":["."],"graphs":{"brevo_agent":"./agent/graph.py:graph"},"env":".env","python_version":"3.12","image_distro":"wolfi","auth":{"path":"./agent/auth.py:my_auth"},"http":{"app":"./agent/app.py:app","cors":{"allow_origins":["https://app.brevo.com"]}},"store":{"ttl":{"default_ttl":43200,"sweep_interval_minutes":60}},"checkpointer":{"ttl":{"strategy":"delete","default_ttl":43200}},"pip_installer":"uv"}. CRITICAL: graph.py must `graph = builder.compile()` with NO checkpointer=/store= args — the platform injects production Postgres + store; passing InMemorySaver is local-only and is ignored/overridden in the hosted runtime. Build State as a TypedDict (event, contact, candidate_action, lint, decision, status, reason) with add_messages reducer only where needed; the approval pause uses interrupt() + Command(resume=...). Secrets injected via the Platform UI/API, never in committed env (.env is path-only; honors 'never hardcode secrets' + read-from-env rules). Two deploy paths: `langgraph deploy` via CLI from the project dir, or Studio/Git-connected build — both hit api.smith.langchain.com. Webhook + cron: register the daily 02:00 UTC scheduled run for scheduled_scan; point Brevo's webhook + the Scheduler Cloud Function at the deployment's /webhooks/brevo route. v1 SCOPE: keep this as the governance/decision control plane on top of the existing brevo-*.json config — do NOT have it author or run gal-model or any ML (out of scope per Anthropic terms). Rollout safety: ship with all sends in approval-required mode (no auto-approve allowlist) for the first cohort; only consider a narrow auto-approve for already-approved template+segment combos AFTER deliverability metrics (bounce<2%, complaint<0.1%, unsub<0.5% from brevo-deliverability-checklist.json) are verified live, and confirm suppression-list sync is real before any volume. Live-state caveat from workspace memory: template IDs in brevo-templates.json are currently null and lists' listId are null — the first real run is necessarily a gated /brevo-sync-all + list-create pass to populate them. Worktree discipline: implement in StratusCloudLabs or EscalateLabs worktrees/, not in the live marketing checkout; tie work to a GitHub issue first per workspace rules.
impact: Automatable score 80. Removes the manual, slash-command-driven loop (/brevo-sync-all, /brevo-test-email, /brevo-setup-automation) and replaces it with an event-driven, always-on lifecycle engine while keeping a human in the loop on every send — directly protecting deliverability and Brevo ToS compliance (the suppression/unsubscribe and biased-collection landmines that previously made this risky to automate). Concretely: (1) template drift is caught automatically instead of by memory; (2) the 300/day free-tier cap stops being a foot-gun via the budget node (defers instead of failing or over-sending); (3) trial-expiring and re-engagement sends fire on real app events rather than ad-hoc operator runs, which is the highest-leverage revenue path (trial->paid conversion and win-back for the live RevenueCat-monetized Scheduler app); (4) every action is traced in LangSmith for audit. Revenue-relevant per workspace priorities (lifecycle email drives the premium/subscription path). Lift is modest because the config + templates already exist — this is wrapping, not building from scratch. Risk is contained by fail-closed suppression gating and approval-required defaults; the main residual risks are the null template/list IDs (first-run bootstrap) and the unverified domain auth/suppression-sync called out in the deliverability checklist, both of which must be resolved before any unattended volume.

process: Testimonial & User Feedback Collection — watch support/DM conversations for "aha!" moments, draft a frictionless testimonial quote, get human approval, log to testimonials.json, generate a social graphic, and distribute across website, social, sales deck, and newsletter. Also categorizes/routes all inbound feedback (bug/feature/UX/pricing/praise/competitor/churn) per the existing taxonomy.
trigger: Three entry points, all hitting the same graph: (1) LangGraph cron (e.g. daily 09:00) that pulls the day's cohort + recent inbound from Intercom/Brevo and scans for aha-moment signals; (2) webhook/HTTP POST from Intercom conversation events for near-real-time capture; (3) manual invoke from a marketer with a pasted quote + user metadata. All converge on the StateGraph entrypoint with a normalized FeedbackItem.
graph_steps: ingest: normalize the trigger payload into State.feedback_items[] (source channel, user/company, role, raw_text, ts). Cron path calls intercom.search_conversations + brevo segment pull; webhook path maps one event; manual path takes the pasted item verbatim.

classify: LLM (Claude) tags each item against categorization.md taxonomy (Bug/Feature/UX/Pricing/Praise/Competitor/Churn), sentiment 1-5, and severity (Critical/Important/Nice-to-have). Conditional edge fans out: Praise/Competitor-positive with strong sentiment -> testimonial branch; Critical/Important -> routing branch; everything -> log branch.
detect_aha: heuristic + LLM scorer flags 'saved me X hours', speed praise, competitor-favorable comparisons. Emits aha_score and a candidate boolean. Only candidates proceed to drafting.

draft_testimonial: Claude writes a 1-2 sentence quote in the frictionless-template voice ('[quote]' - Name, Role at Company) plus the exact approval-request message to send the user. Writes to State.draft (never sent yet).

INTERRUPT #1 (consent-request approval): interrupt() surfaces the draft + outreach message to a human in LangGraph Studio/Agent Inbox. Human approves/edits/rejects sending the permission ask to the user. Resume only on approve. This is the 'ask the user for permission' gate.

send_consent_request: on approval, post the approval-ask message to the user via Intercom (the ONLY outbound user-facing send; approval-gated above). Records pending-consent state and parks the thread.
await_user_consent: separate resumable entry (webhook on user reply) re-enters the graph; LLM checks whether the user said yes/tweak/no. Tweaks loop back to draft_testimonial.

INTERRUPT #2 (publish approval): once the user consents, interrupt() shows the FINAL testimonial + generated social-graphic spec + the full distribution checklist (website block, LinkedIn/X post copy, Wall-of-Love deck entry, newsletter blurb). Human must approve before ANY asset is written or published.
generate_assets: build social-graphic spec (brand colors, high-contrast) via a Figma-template tool call OR emit a ready-to-render spec; write nothing public yet.

persist_testimonial: append approved entry to testimonials.json (Name, Role, Company, Quote, Date, source) — file did not exist before, agent creates it; commit via a PR tool, not direct push to a live site.

distribute: execute the approved distribution fan-out — open PR to website testimonials block, draft (not auto-post) LinkedIn/X via social-media pipeline, append Wall-of-Love entry, queue Brevo newsletter block. Each external publish that is irreversible stays draft/PR for a final human merge.

route_feedback: for non-testimonial items, Critical -> Slack #eng-alerts (approval-gated send), Important -> create Linear/Jira issue, and the 'same feature request 3x in a week' + competitor-switch rules raise flags. All items -> log + sentiment metrics.
digest: roll up tracking metrics (response rate, top category, sentiment moving average) into State for the monthly product digest; no send without the standard approval gate.
tools_integrations: Intercom MCP (search_conversations, get_conversation, get_contact) for reads; an outbound reply tool for the consent-ask — gated
Brevo API (segment pull for daily cohorts; newsletter block queue) — read free, send gated
Anthropic Claude API (classify, aha-detect, draft quote, consent interpretation) — orchestration/LLM-call only, NO model training/distillation
Slack MCP (slack_send_message to #eng-alerts) — gated
Linear/Jira issue creation for Important routing
GitHub (gh CLI / PR API) to open PRs against EscalateLabs/marketing for testimonials.json, website testimonials block, social-media drafts, sales deck Wall-of-Love
Figma template / social-graphic generator (or emit a render spec) for the visual asset
LangGraph Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore (managed, auto-injected) for thread persistence, dedupe of already-processed conversations, and 3x-feature-request counting
LangGraph Studio / Agent Inbox as the human-in-the-loop approval surface for both interrupts
impact: Removes the four documented pain points: manual feedback monitoring/extraction (cron + classifier auto-scans cohorts and inbound), graphic-creation overhead (templated asset spec), approval delays (structured interrupt inbox instead of ad-hoc DMs), and asset versioning (testimonials.json as single source of truth, all publishes via PR so history is tracked). Marketing keeps the 15-20 min/day human loop but only for judgement calls (the two approval gates), not for scanning, drafting, tagging, or asset prep. Automatable score is 55 precisely because the two consent/publish gates and the brand-voice drafting must stay human-reviewed; the agent automates the surrounding 100% of monitoring/extraction/routing/asset-prep. Revenue-relevant: faster social proof onto the live landing page + sales deck supports the paid-conversion funnel.
approval_gates: TWO hard human-in-the-loop interrupt() gates: (Gate 1) before sending the consent-request message to the user via Intercom — no outbound user message is sent without approve/edit; (Gate 2) before any asset is persisted or distributed (testimonials.json append, website/social/deck/newsletter publish) — human approves the final quote + graphic + channel list. Additionally, every irreversible external action is downgraded to a draft/PR requiring a separate human merge: website + deck changes open PRs (no direct push to live), social posts are created as drafts (no auto-post), newsletter is queued (not blasted), and Slack #eng-alerts escalations are themselves approval-gated. Cron/webhook reads and LLM classification run unattended; nothing that sends, publishes, commits to a live surface, or messages a user is autonomous.
deploy_notes: LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments / managed Cloud SaaS). langgraph.json at repo root: dependencies ["."], graphs {"testimonial": "./agent/graph.py:graph"}, python_version "3.12", image_distro "wolfi", store.index {embed openai:text-embedding-3-small, dims 1536} for dedupe/similarity of feedback, checkpointer.ttl strategy delete default_ttl ~43200, http.app for the Intercom/Brevo webhook routes, auth.path for a custom Auth guard on webhooks. CRITICAL: compile the StateGraph WITHOUT passing checkpointer=/store= — the managed platform injects production Postgres checkpointer + store; passing InMemorySaver is local-only and is ignored/overridden in the hosted runtime. Both interrupts use interrupt() (NodeInterrupt/Command resume), surfaced via Agent Inbox; threads are durable so a consent reply days later resumes the same thread. Secrets injected via the LangSmith UI/API (env in langgraph.json is only a path; never commit real values — satisfies the no-hardcoded-secrets rule). Schedule the daily cohort scan with a LangGraph cron job. Constraint compliance: graph only calls Claude/LLMs for orchestration, classification, and drafting — it does NOT train, fine-tune, distill, or evaluate any model, so it is permitted under the workspace Anthropic-terms guardrail. Deploy via `langgraph deploy` (CLI) or the GitHub-connected build path; target region api.smith.langchain.com (or eu.). v1 scope cut: ship with Intercom + testimonials.json PR + Slack escalation + the two gates first; add Figma render, full social auto-draft, and newsletter queue as fast-follows.

process: Competitor Monitoring & Review Response — weekly automated collection of competitor negative reviews/complaints across G2, TrustRadius, GetApp, Capterra, app stores, and social (X, Reddit, LinkedIn) for the 6 tracked competitors (When I Work, Deputy, Homebase, 7shifts, Sling, Connecteam); LLM-driven categorization against the existing pain-points taxonomy; generation of the weekly competitor-complaint digest; and drafting of playbook-aligned review/social responses that are HELD for human approval before any send. Replaces the manual report-generation + multi-platform monitoring + response-coordination burden currently spread across the weekly-competitor-intel.yml GitHub Action and manual analyst work.
trigger: Primary: time-based cron via LangGraph Platform scheduled run (weekly, Monday 00:00 UTC — mirrors the existing `cron: '0 0 * * 1'` in weekly-competitor-intel.yml). Secondary: manual `workflow_dispatch`-equivalent invocation via the LangGraph SDK/Assistants API (POST /threads/{id}/runs) for ad-hoc reruns. The agent resumes from a human-in-the-loop interrupt when an approver responds via the Agent Inbox / SDK resume call.
graph_steps: load_config — read competitors, source-map targets, monitoring keywords, response playbook, and current pain-points.json taxonomy from repo data (or injected config); no network

collect_signals (fan-out) — orchestration-only tool calls per source: Tavily/WebSearch + Playwright-MCP scrape for G2/TrustRadius/GetApp/Capterra 1-3 star reviews, app-store recent low ratings, and X/Reddit/LinkedIn complaint queries; normalize each hit into a raw-signal record {competitor, platform, url, rating, text, date}; per-source try/except so one platform failure does not abort the run
dedupe_and_filter — drop signals already seen (checkpointer/store keyed by url-hash), drop off-topic and non-negative items via keyword + sentiment pre-filter

classify_signals — Claude call maps each signal to a pain-points.json category (pricing/features/usability/support/reliability/bugs) and the matching Scheduler advantage + best-fit playbook scenario (1-4); flags 'high-value' alternative-seeking posts

draft_responses — Claude drafts playbook-tone replies ONLY for platforms where Scheduler can legitimately respond (X, Reddit, LinkedIn) per channel-specific rules; review-site items are routed to pain-point/landing-page updates instead of replies; nothing is sent yet

human_approval_gate (interrupt) — single batched interrupt() surfacing the proposed weekly digest, the pain-points.json additions, and every drafted social/forum response for approve / edit / reject; run pauses here until a human resumes

apply_approved (post-resume) — for approved drafts: emit/queue sends ONLY through approved, auditable channels (e.g. open a GitHub issue/PR comment with the approved reply text, or hand off to the existing marketing send pipeline); update pain-points.json additions; never auto-post to a competitor review site

publish_report — render the weekly-report-template.md digest, write to data/seo/reports/, and post the tracking-issue comment (mirrors the existing Action's gh issue comment step); persist run summary to the long-term store for week-over-week trend tracking
impact: Eliminates the manual weekly digest generation and multi-platform monitoring toil (the stated top pain points; automatable_score 70) and makes competitor-complaint intelligence consistent and trend-trackable week over week. Feeds the pain-points database and 'Scheduler vs [Competitor]' landing pages with fresh, categorized ammunition, and gives marketing pre-drafted, on-tone responses to alternative-seeking prospects — accelerating competitive-displacement outreach. Revenue-adjacent (top-of-funnel competitive intel), not a direct paid-user lever, hence P2; protects the live Scheduler franchise rather than shipping new paid features. Caps human effort to a single weekly approve/edit pass instead of hours of collection + writing.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS runtime, auto-provisioned Postgres checkpointer + store)
Anthropic Claude API (classification + response drafting — orchestration/inference only, NO model training/distillation)
Tavily / web search API (review + social discovery)
Playwright (review-site + app-store scraping for 1-3 star reviews; weekly cadence to avoid IP bans per monitoring-spec)
Reddit API (PRAW/Snoowrap) for subreddit complaint monitoring
X/Twitter search and LinkedIn search (social complaint signals)
GitHub API / gh CLI (write digest to data/seo/reports/, comment on tracking issue #13, open response-handoff issues)
LangGraph Agent Inbox / SDK resume endpoint (human-in-the-loop approval surface)
Existing repo data contract: monitoring-spec.md, source-map.md, response-playbook.md, weekly-report-template.md, pain-points.json
approval_gates: Exactly one mandatory human-in-the-loop gate via LangGraph `interrupt()` (the `human_approval_gate` node) BEFORE any irreversible action. It batches three approvals into one weekly review: (1) the weekly digest content, (2) pain-points.json additions, and (3) every drafted social/forum reply. The run stays paused (persisted by the managed Postgres checkpointer) until a human resumes with approve / edit (return edited text) / reject per item. Hard rules: NO reply, comment, or post is emitted before resume; rejected items are dropped; edited items use the human-supplied text verbatim; the agent never auto-posts to a competitor-owned review site (those become pain-point/landing-page action items only). All sends route through auditable channels (GitHub issue/comment or the approved marketing pipeline), never hardcoded credentials — every secret comes from injected env. Read-only collection/classification/report-drafting run autonomously; only the send/publish boundary is gated.
deploy_notes: Deploy as a managed LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments / Cloud SaaS) Python deployment. langgraph.json: {"dependencies":["."],"graphs":{"competitor_intel":"./agent/graph.py:graph"},"env":".env","python_version":"3.12","image_distro":"wolfi","pip_installer":"uv","store":{"index":{"embed":"openai:text-embedding-3-small","dims":1536}},"checkpointer":{"ttl":{"strategy":"delete","default_ttl":43200}}}. CRITICAL: do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= to .compile() — the managed platform injects production Postgres automatically; export a module-level compiled `graph`. Use interrupt()/Command(resume=...) for the approval gate (durable across the managed checkpointer). Inject all secrets via the Deployments UI/API env, never committed in .env. State schema: TypedDict with messages (add_messages reducer), raw_signals, classified_signals, drafted_responses, digest_markdown, pain_point_additions, approval_decision. Configure the weekly run via a LangGraph Platform Cron (Monday 00:00 UTC) to retire the cron half of weekly-competitor-intel.yml; keep the Action only as a thin trigger/Playwright-runner if browser scraping must stay in CI, or move scraping into the deployment image. Migration: reuse the existing data contract files and report output path (data/seo/reports/) and the tracking-issue comment so downstream Growth Console at /competitors keeps working unchanged. Respect Anthropic terms — this is orchestration calling Claude for classification/drafting only; absolutely no model training/eval/distillation in this deployment.

process: Product Launch Coordination (Product Hunt / DevHunt / G2 multi-platform launches) for Scheduler Systems, run out of EscalateLabs/marketing/data/launches/. Today it is a documented-but-human-executed playbook (pre-launch-checklist.md, launch-day-plan.md, supporter-outreach.md, engagement-strategy.md, devhunt-plan.md, post-launch.md) with assets tracked as GitHub issues in the marketing repo. The v1 agent is a deploy-on-LangGraph-Platform (managed, api.smith.langchain.com) orchestrator that automates the prep/coordination/tracking spine — checklist verification, timeline derivation, draft generation, supporter-list assembly, cross-platform timing plans, comment-reply drafting, and post-launch stat compilation — while leaving every send/publish/irreversible action as a human-in-the-loop interrupt, exactly matching GROWTH_OPERATING_CONTRACT.md (Product Hunt launch execution, social publishing, and outreach sending are HUMAN-REQUIRED; agent role = "asset prep, checklist support, draft generation, research").
trigger:
Three entry points, all routed to the same compiled StateGraph (graph id "launch" in langgraph.json):
1) On-demand thread create via LangGraph SDK / Assistants API — operator opens a launch run by passing {launch_slug, platform, launch_date_pst} (e.g. POST a new thread + run). Primary path for "start coordinating the GAL/Scheduler PH launch."
2) Scheduled cron (LangGraph Platform native cron on the deployment) — a daily 07:00 PST tick that, for any active launch thread within its T-minus window, re-checks asset readiness, advances the timeline, and surfaces the day's due drafts as an interrupt for human review. This is what replaces "founder remembers what day it is on the checklist."
3) Webhook resume — operator replies to an approval interrupt (approve / edit / reject) from Slack/email, which resumes the interrupted run. No publish ever happens without this resume carrying an explicit human decision.
graph_steps: ingest_launch_context: load launch_slug + platform + launch_date_pst from run input; read the six playbook .md files (pre-launch-checklist, launch-day-plan, supporter-outreach, engagement-strategy, devhunt-plan, post-launch) and any platform-specific pack (gal/product-hunt-launch-pack.md) from EscalateLabs/marketing/data/launches/ via the github_read tool; load GROWTH_OPERATING_CONTRACT.md to bind the human/agent control matrix into state.policy.

derive_phase: compute current phase from launch_date_pst vs now (pre-launch T-30..T-1, launch-day hour-by-hour, post-launch T+1/T+7). Conditional edge routes to the matching subflow. Idempotent so the daily cron tick re-enters cleanly.

verify_assets (pre-launch): parse the markdown checklists into structured items (logo 240x240, gallery 1270x760 x up to 8, demo video 30-60s, taglines <=60 chars, description <=260 chars, maker comment present). Reconcile against open launch GitHub issues in EscalateLabs/marketing via github_list_issues. Emit a readiness report of done / missing / blocked items. Read-only, no gate.

draft_assets (LLM, Claude via langchain-anthropic): generate/refresh the human-reviewable drafts the playbooks call for — tagline options, PH/DevHunt descriptions, Maker's Comment, X/LinkedIn launch thread, supporter email + DM bodies (using engagement-strategy.md voice and the supporter-outreach 'never ask for upvotes' rule). Output is draft-only.
human_gate__assets (interrupt): present drafts + readiness report; wait for approve/edit/reject. NOTHING leaves the agent before this resumes.

stage_outputs (post-approval, write-but-reversible): on approval, write approved drafts back into the repo as a PR/branch (github_write_pr) and open/update launch GitHub issues with checklist status — reversible artifacts only; still no external publish.

build_supporter_plan: assemble the coordinated-timing plan across platforms (PH 00:01 PST go-live, 06:00 first push, 09:00 email blast, 12:00 midday, 15:00 DM wave, 18:00 final) and the supporter outreach batches (existing users / network DMs / community channels / X thread), each tagged with its required human owner from the contract. Read-only planning.

human_gate__publish_actions (interrupt, PER irreversible action): for each send/publish/DM/email-blast/social-post and for posting the Maker's Comment, raise a separate interrupt carrying the exact payload + target. Operator approves/edits/rejects each. This is the hard constraint surface — the agent NEVER calls a send/publish tool without a resumed human decision for that specific action.

execute_or_handoff (post-approval): for approved actions, either (a) emit a ready-to-paste payload + deep link for the human to execute (default, since PH/social are human-required per contract), or (b) where a safe API exists and was explicitly approved, perform only the reversible part. Record every decision and outcome into thread state + a github issue comment.

launch_day_engagement (LLM, gated): on launch day, poll public PH/social signals (read-only crawl), and for each new comment draft a reply using the engagement-strategy.md scenario templates; every reply is queued behind human_gate__publish_actions before any posting handoff.

post_launch_rollup: after T+1, compile the transparent stats pack (upvotes, unique visitors, trial signups, paid conversions) from operator-supplied + read-only sources, draft the 'here are our exact numbers' blog/thread, and draft the top-20-signup thank-you emails — all draft-only behind the publish gate.

report_and_persist: write a structured run summary to the BaseStore (semantic-indexed, per langgraph.json store config) and a closure comment on the launch GitHub issue; checkpointer (managed Postgres) preserves thread state so the next cron tick or operator resume continues the same launch.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS, api.smith.langchain.com) — runtime/Agent Server
langgraph CLI + langgraph.json (graphs: {"launch": "./launch_agent/graph.py:graph"}, python_version 3.13, image_distro wolfi, pip_installer uv)
langgraph SDK (thread/run create, interrupt + resume) — operator entry + webhook resume
LangGraph Platform native cron (daily T-minus tick)
langchain-anthropic (Claude Opus/Sonnet for drafting — orchestration calling the model only; NO training/distillation)
GitHub via gh/Octokit MCP — read launch .md assets + GROWTH_OPERATING_CONTRACT.md, list/update launch issues, open PRs with approved drafts (repo: EscalateLabs/marketing)
langgraph.interrupt() / Agent Inbox (human-in-the-loop approval surface)
Slack (notify operator of pending interrupts; approvals resume the run) — draft/notify only
Email/ESP (e.g. Brevo) for the supporter blast — DRAFT ONLY, send is human-executed
Product Hunt + DevHunt + G2 — read-only public signal crawl + ready-to-paste payloads/deep links; submission/publish is human-executed per contract
Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore (auto-provisioned by the managed platform; do NOT pass checkpointer/store to .compile())
LangSmith tracing/observability (built into the deployment)
impact: Directly attacks the four named pain points: (1) cross-platform timing — the timeline subflow + native cron turn the hour-by-hour PST plan into an actively-advanced state machine instead of a static markdown the founder must watch; (2) supporter outreach at scale — batched, owner-tagged, draft-then-gate generation of the email/DM/community/X copy; (3) asset + messaging consistency — checklist parsing reconciled against GitHub issues plus single-source draft generation from the playbook voice; (4) post-launch maintenance — automated 48h/T+7 rollup and thank-you/stat drafts. Net effect: collapses the founder's 16-hour launch-day coordination load and the multi-week prep tracking into review-and-approve, while keeping the documented automatable_score=45 reality honest — every actually-irreversible step (publish, send, submit, post Maker's Comment) stays human-owned, so the agent raises throughput without taking on the launch-execution risk the Growth Operating Contract explicitly reserves for humans. Revenue tie-in: launches are top-of-funnel for Scheduler's paid plans; faster, more consistent launches with maintained post-launch follow-up improve trial->paid capture.
approval_gates:
Two distinct interrupt classes, both enforced via langgraph.interrupt() and resumed only by an explicit human decision (approve / edit / reject):
1) human_gate__assets — single gate over all generated drafts + the readiness report before anything is even written back to the repo. On approve, writes are limited to REVERSIBLE artifacts (PR/branch, GitHub issue updates) — never external publish.
2) human_gate__publish_actions — a SEPARATE, per-action gate for every irreversible/external step: supporter email blast, each DM/community post, each X/LinkedIn launch tweet, posting the PH Maker's Comment, submitting/going-live on PH/DevHunt/G2, and every launch-day comment reply. The agent's default on approval is to emit a ready-to-paste payload + deep link for the named human owner (per GROWTH_OPERATING_CONTRACT.md: PH launch execution, social publishing, outreach sending = HUMAN-REQUIRED); it performs an action via API only when a safe reversible API exists AND that specific action was explicitly approved. No tool call that sends, publishes, posts, or submits is reachable on any code path that has not been resumed by a human decision carrying that exact payload. All decisions + outcomes are logged to thread state and a GitHub issue comment for audit.
secrets_needed: GITHUB_TOKEN / GH_PAT (read launch assets + GROWTH_OPERATING_CONTRACT.md, manage EscalateLabs/marketing launch issues, open PRs)
BREVO_API_KEY (or ESP key) — held for DRAFT context only; send remains human-executed
deploy_notes: Deploy target = LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments managed Cloud SaaS (api.smith.langchain.com; switch base to eu. if EU residency is required). langgraph.json at repo root of a dedicated launch_agent project (recommend a worktree under StratusCloudLabs/stratus or a new EscalateLabs repo, NOT an edit-in-place on the live marketing checkout): graphs={"launch":"./launch_agent/graph.py:graph"}, dependencies=["."], env=".env", python_version="3.13", image_distro="wolfi", pip_installer="uv", and a store.index block (openai:text-embedding-3-small / dims 1536) for semantic run-history recall plus a checkpointer.ttl. CRITICAL: graph = builder.compile() with NO checkpointer=/store= args — the managed platform injects production Postgres persistence and store automatically; passing InMemorySaver is local-only and is ignored/overridden in the hosted runtime. Build via `langgraph deploy` (CLI) or the LangSmith UI from the connected GitHub repo; inject all secrets through the platform UI/API, never committed to .env in git (Security Rules: never hardcode secrets — read from env). Configure native cron on the deployment for the daily 07:00 PST T-minus tick and register the approval-resume webhook (verified with LANGGRAPH_WEBHOOK_SECRET). Anthropic Terms compliance: this is pure orchestration that CALLS Claude for drafting/coordination — it does NOT train, fine-tune, distill, or evaluate any model, so it is permitted to be built in Claude Code. Test plan: dry-run a past launch thread end-to-end with both interrupt classes firing and assert no send/publish/submit tool is invoked without a resumed human decision; verify the cron tick is idempotent across re-entry; verify drafts land as a PR (reversible) and never auto-publish. First real use: a low-stakes DevHunt or G2 review-pack run before fronting a Product Hunt Product-of-the-Day attempt.

trigger:
Three deployable triggers, all landing on the same compiled graph (one assistant, mode selected via `config.configurable.campaign_type`):
1) Cron (LangGraph Platform scheduled run / Crons API) — bi-weekly Mon 09:00 for the John Rush research+cadence refresh and the weekly follow-up sweep (re-scan in-flight sequences for Day-4 / Day-10 / Day-30 due steps). This is the primary autonomous driver.
2) API/SDK invocation (`/runs` or `/threads/{id}/runs`) — operator kicks a specific campaign: `campaign_type` in {cold_email, linkedin_dm, partner_outreach, review_request, directory_submission, john_rush_cadence}, plus a target list or single prospect.
3) Webhook/manual resume — a paused thread (interrupted at an approval gate) is resumed via `/threads/{id}/runs` with the human's decision payload. No new trigger needed; the same thread carries state.
Every run is keyed by a stable `thread_id` (e.g. `campaign:{type}:{target_id}`) so checkpointed state + follow-up scheduling survive across the multi-day cadence.
process:
v1 agent scope and behavior:

WHAT IT AUTOMATES (the pain points): prospect research + list-building, per-prospect email/DM personalization at scale, follow-up coordination (Day-4 / Day-10 breakup, 30-day review plans), response tracking, and campaign-performance roll-up. It generates drafts and schedules; it NEVER sends or writes irreversibly on its own.

GROUNDING IN THE ACTUAL REPO (load-bearing paths):
- Templates: /EscalateLabs/marketing/data/seo/outreach/email-templates.md (Template 1 resource-page inclusion, Template 2 integration partnership, Template 3 editorial citation) and the partner DM sequence in /EscalateLabs/marketing/data/partnerships/outreach-playbook.md (Initial ping → Day-4 follow-up → Day-10 breakup).
- Review campaigns: /EscalateLabs/marketing/data/campaigns/review-collection-g2-2026.md, the PMP "Get Reviewed" draft (data/seo/outreach/submissions/pmp-get-reviewed-submission.md), and the Zapier blog pitch (.../submissions/zapier-blog-pitch.md).
- John Rush cadence: .claude/commands/marketing/john-rush-research.md + data/john-rush/ (tactics-master.json, queue-summary.md, posted/log.json), bi-weekly.
- Live prospect DB: data/prospects/qualified.json (currently empty; populated by /connect-prospects). The agent appends qualified prospects here as a DRAFT change behind approval.

CRITICAL DATA-STATE FACT the spec must respect: data/seo/outreach/outreach-tracker.json and data/seo/outreach/directory-submissions.json are DEPRECATED (migrated 2026-04-10 to the Growth Console Firestore at seo.scheduler-systems.com, collection seo_backlink_metadata_snapshots; marketing#309/#369/#370). The agent must NOT write to those JSON files. Tracking writes target the Growth Console API behind an approval gate; the deprecated files are read-only context only.

ORCHESTRATION-ONLY POSTURE: the graph calls Claude/LLMs for research synthesis and copy generation; it does not train/fine-tune/distill any model (workspace Anthropic-terms guardrail). LangGraph platform injects the Postgres checkpointer + store automatically — graph is compiled WITHOUT a custom checkpointer/store.

LangGraph state (TypedDict): campaign_type, channel, targets[] (prospect records), per-target stage (researched/drafted/awaiting_approval/scheduled/sent/follow_up_due/closed), drafts[] (subject+body or DM text + which template), schedule[] (next_action_date per target), approvals[] (decision log), metrics (counts by stage/outcome), and `errors`. Long-term BaseStore holds per-prospect history + dedupe/suppression list (never re-contact someone who replied/opted out) keyed across threads.

HUMAN-IN-THE-LOOP: every send/publish/irreversible action is an `interrupt()` (native LangGraph HIL). The interrupt payload surfaces the rendered draft, the recipient, the channel, and the proposed send time; the human resumes with approve / edit / reject. Nothing leaves the system without an approve. This satisfies the workspace "No send without approval" rule exactly.
graph_steps: START → load_context: read templates (email-templates.md, outreach-playbook.md, review-collection-g2-2026.md), the active prospect DB (data/prospects/qualified.json), and the long-term suppression/history store; branch on config.configurable.campaign_type

research_and_qualify: (for cold_email/linkedin_dm/partner_outreach) LLM + web research to find/enrich prospects, apply qualification criteria, dedupe against the store + qualified.json; produce candidate prospect records. For review_request/directory_submission, load the target site + 30-day plan instead
GATE A — interrupt('approve_prospect_list'): human reviews the qualified/enriched list and any proposed append to qualified.json before any drafting; approve/edit/reject

personalize_draft: per approved target, select the matching template and generate personalized subject+body (email) or ping/follow-up text (DM) or review-request/submission copy; fill differentiators from verified Scheduler facts only (no overclaiming time-tracking/AI)

GATE B — interrupt('approve_send'): the core HIL gate — human sees each rendered draft + recipient + channel + proposed send time; approve / edit-and-approve / reject. THIS IS THE IRREVERSIBLE-ACTION GATE. No tool sends before this resolves

dispatch: only for approved items — create the send via the channel tool. Default to draft-creation tools (Gmail create_draft, Slack draft) so a human still clicks send; direct-send tools are allowed ONLY post-approval and are themselves logged

GATE C — interrupt('approve_tracking_write'): before persisting outcomes to the LIVE Growth Console (seo.scheduler-systems.com) or appending to qualified.json, confirm the write (these are the real records of record; deprecated *.json files are never written)
schedule_followups: compute next_action_date per target (Day-4, Day-10 breakup, or 30-day review milestones), write schedule entries + checkpoint; the thread parks until the cron sweep re-enters
track_and_measure: on re-entry (cron) ingest responses (Gmail thread reads / Growth Console status), update per-target stage + metrics, move replied/opted-out prospects to the suppression store

report: emit a campaign roll-up (drafts generated, approved, sent, replies, conversions, follow-ups due) to the operator via the run result and an optional Slack draft → END (or loop back to research for the next wave)
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments) — managed Cloud SaaS deploy via langgraph.json + langgraph CLI; auto-provisioned Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore
langgraph.interrupt / HIL approval gates (the load-bearing control-flow primitive)
Anthropic Claude (Opus/Sonnet via API) — research synthesis + copy generation ONLY (no training/distillation)
Gmail MCP (create_draft, search_threads, get_thread, label) — draft cold/review emails + read replies for response tracking; default to drafts, send only post-approval
Slack MCP (slack_send_message_draft, slack_send_message) — operator notifications + approval prompts as drafts
WebSearch / WebFetch — prospect research, contact enrichment, John Rush tactic scraping

Growth Console API (seo.scheduler-systems.com, Firestore seo_backlink_metadata_snapshots) — the live record of record for outreach/backlink tracking (replaces deprecated tracker JSONs); writes are approval-gated
Repo file I/O — read templates/playbooks under /EscalateLabs/marketing/data/...; append to data/prospects/qualified.json behind a gate
LinkedIn/X outreach — DRAFT-ONLY in v1 (no compliant send API; agent prepares ping/follow-up text for the human to paste). Note: linkedin-service-reply skill is PAUSED, so no auto-DM)
LangGraph Crons API — bi-weekly John Rush refresh + weekly follow-up sweep
approval_gates: Three explicit interrupt() gates, all mandatory: GATE A approve_prospect_list (before drafting + before any append to qualified.json); GATE B approve_send (per-draft, the core irreversible-action gate — every email/DM/review/submission requires approve/edit/reject before any dispatch); GATE C approve_tracking_write (before persisting to the live Growth Console or qualified.json). Defaults are deny: an unresolved interrupt parks the thread indefinitely, nothing is sent. Dispatch defaults to draft-creation tools (human still clicks send) even after approval. Hard rule enforced in graph: the deprecated outreach-tracker.json / directory-submissions.json are never written. Matches workspace policies: No send without approval, No delete/overwrite without approval, secrets via env only.
secrets_needed: OPENAI_API_KEY (only if store semantic index uses text-embedding-3-small; optional)
deploy_notes: langgraph.json at repo root: dependencies ["."], graphs {"outreach":"./agent/graph.py:graph"}, python_version "3.12", image_distro "wolfi", env injected via the LangSmith Deployments UI (NEVER committed). store.index uses an embedding model for the prospect/suppression memory; checkpointer TTL left to platform defaults. CRITICAL: graph.compile() takes NO checkpointer= / store= — the managed platform injects production Postgres for both; passing InMemorySaver would be ignored/override-broken in prod. Deploy with the `langgraph` CLI (Cloud SaaS tier, api.smith.langchain.com) or the Deployments UI. Use the Crons API for the bi-weekly + weekly schedules. Thread IDs keyed `campaign:{type}:{target_id}` so multi-day cadence state persists. Guardrails to encode at build time: (1) no ML training/distillation — orchestration calls to Claude only; (2) all sends/writes pass interrupt(); (3) deprecated *.json trackers are read-only, writes go to Growth Console; (4) honor a suppression list in the store so replied/opted-out prospects are never re-contacted; (5) copy generation must use only verified Scheduler claims (per-user $2.99/$4.99, deterministic roster builder, chat, CSV) — do not assert time-tracking/AI scheduling the product does not ship. v1 explicitly DRAFT-ONLY for LinkedIn/X (no compliant send path; linkedin-service-reply is paused). Tie work to a GitHub issue in Scheduler-Systems/marketing before shipping (workspace rule). Owning paths all under /EscalateLabs/marketing/.
impact: Cuts the highest-friction parts of the bi-weekly outreach motion: prospect research/list-building, per-prospect personalization, and the Day-4/Day-10/30-day follow-up coordination that currently slip manually. automatable_score is 55 because the irreversible half (actually sending email/DMs, submitting reviews, writing the live record of record) stays human-gated by policy — so realistic win is roughly 50-60% of the operator's drafting + tracking + scheduling time, not the sending itself. Highest-leverage first campaigns to wire up: the prepared-but-unsent CRITICAL backlink targets (PMP "Get Reviewed", Zapier blog pitch — both status submission_prepared) and the G2 30-day review plan, because the drafts already exist and only need the personalize→approve→track loop. Revenue tie-in: directory/review backlinks + partner cross-promo feed top-of-funnel for the live Scheduler product. Risk to manage: G2/TrustRadius ToS on biased/incentivized review collection and email suppression/unsubscribe hygiene — the approval gates + suppression store are the mitigations.

process: Creative Assets & Ad Copy Management — production and lifecycle management of marketing creative (ad copy variations, social posts, video scripts, landing-page visual briefs) for Scheduler and GAL across Google Ads, social ads, and brand-asset organization. The agent ingests a creative brief (campaign, platform, asset type, target ad group/keywords), pulls the live brand-canonical facts and existing assets from the EscalateLabs/marketing repo, drafts platform-correct copy/script variants for A/B testing, runs a deterministic brand-and-claims compliance gate against CONTENT-RULES.md (no fabricated stories, no unverified metrics, no false feature claims, char-limit enforcement), then HARD-STOPS at a human-in-the-loop interrupt. Nothing is committed, published, or pushed to any ad platform without explicit human approval. On approval it writes drafts into the repo data tree and opens a PR; it never auto-launches ads or auto-posts. A second optional path summarizes creative performance metrics (CTR/CPC/conversions/QS) pulled read-only from Google Ads + GA4 and recommends winners/losers, again gated before any budget or status change.
trigger: Three triggers, all converging on the same StateGraph entrypoint: (1) On-demand HTTP run via LangGraph Platform Assistants API / SDK (a marketer or the growth-cli posts a creative brief) — primary v1 trigger. (2) Scheduled cron (LangGraph Platform native cron, e.g. weekly Mon 08:00) for the read-only performance-review path that surfaces underperforming creatives for human action. (3) GitHub webhook (issue labeled `creative-request` in EscalateLabs/marketing) mounted via the custom `http.app` in langgraph.json, which normalizes the issue into a brief and starts a thread. All write/publish actions remain interrupt-gated regardless of trigger.
graph_steps: ingest_brief: normalize the incoming brief (campaign=scheduler|gal, platform=google_ads_rsa|linkedin|x|video_script|landing_visual_brief, asset_type, target ad_group/keywords, variant_count) into typed State; validate required fields, else route to clarify/END.

load_context: read-only tools fetch brand-canonical facts (submission-profile API / CONTENT-RULES.md product facts), the relevant existing artifacts (ads/*.md, post-templates.md, content-pillars.md), and prior variants so the agent reuses real structure and avoids contradicting current pricing/positioning.

generate_variants: Claude drafts N platform-correct creative variants for A/B testing (RSA headlines<=30c / descriptions<=90c; LinkedIn/X per template; video script; or a landing-visual brief). Orchestration-only LLM call — no model training/finetuning.

compliance_gate: deterministic + LLM-assisted check against CONTENT-RULES.md — flags fabricated stories, unverified metrics (MRR/user counts/savings), false/absent features, contradictory positioning, and enforces char limits + negative-keyword/brand-voice constraints. Annotates each claim verifiable|needs-source|reject.
route_compliance: conditional edge — if any hard violation, loop back to generate_variants with the violations as feedback (bounded retries, max 2), else proceed.

human_review (INTERRUPT): interrupt() surfaces the drafted variants + per-claim compliance annotations + a unified diff preview to a human; resumes only on Command(resume={decision: approve|edit|reject, edits, approved_variant_ids}). This is the mandatory approval gate before ANY persistence/publish.

apply_approved: ONLY after approve — write approved variants to data/social-media/** or ads/** , open a PR on EscalateLabs/marketing (git tool), and emit a launch-ready package; STILL does not auto-launch the Google Ads campaign or auto-post — launch remains a human action documented in the PR.

performance_review (alt path, read-only): pull Google Ads + GA4 metrics (CTR, CPC, conversions, Quality Score) for live creatives, rank A/B winners/losers vs scheduler-search-campaign success thresholds, and produce a recommendation report; any budget/status/pause change is itself interrupt-gated.
finalize: persist run summary + thread state via the platform-injected Postgres checkpointer/store, post a status comment back to the triggering issue/channel, END.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments / managed Cloud SaaS) — deploy target; platform-injected Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore (do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= to .compile())
Anthropic Claude (orchestration LLM only — drafting/critique; explicitly NO model training, finetuning, or distillation)
Repo filesystem tools (read ads/*.md, data/social-media/**, assets/**, post-templates.md, content-pillars.md, CONTENT-RULES.md; write only post-approval)
Git/GitHub (gh CLI or PyGithub) — read issues labeled creative-request, open PR with approved drafts, post status comments (write actions gated)
Growth Console submission-profile API (seo.scheduler-systems.com /api/submission-profile) — read-only brand-canonical pricing/feature facts
Google Ads API (read-only metrics: CTR/CPC/conversions/Quality Score; any campaign/budget/status mutation interrupt-gated)
Google Analytics 4 Data API (read-only key-events/conversions for cross-checking)
Slack (optional) — surface review requests / status notifications to the approver
LangGraph custom http.app (Starlette/FastAPI) for the GitHub webhook ingress + CORS
LangSmith tracing/observability for run inspection and eval of compliance-gate accuracy
approval_gates: Mandatory human-in-the-loop interrupt() at human_review before ANY irreversible/outbound action — no copy is committed, no PR is opened, no asset is published, and no ad is launched/edited until a human resumes with Command(resume={decision: approve|edit|reject}). Reject ends the run with no writes; edit re-enters compliance_gate; approve is the only path to apply_approved. The compliance_gate is a second, automated guard that must pass (or be human-overridden in the interrupt) before review. In the performance_review path, every budget/status/pause mutation is independently interrupt-gated. The agent never calls a Google Ads write endpoint, never auto-posts to social, and never pushes to a protected branch — it opens a PR and documents the manual launch step. All gates honor bounded retries (max 2) to prevent runaway loops.
deploy_notes: Deploy via langgraph.json at repo root of a dedicated agent package (not committed inside the live marketing checkout — build in a worktree per workspace worktree discipline). Config skeleton: {"dependencies":["."],"graphs":{"creative_copilot":"./agent/graph.py:graph"},"env":".env","python_version":"3.12","image_distro":"wolfi","http":{"app":"./agent/app.py:app"},"store":{"index":{"embed":"openai:text-embedding-3-small","dims":1536}},"checkpointer":{"ttl":{"strategy":"delete","default_ttl":43200}},"pip_installer":"uv"}. CRITICAL platform rules followed: graph variable is the compiled StateGraph; do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= to .compile() (platform injects production Postgres). State uses a reducer (add_messages) and typed TypedDict; human_review uses interrupt()/Command(resume=...) so threads survive restarts via the injected checkpointer. Secrets injected via LangGraph Platform UI/API, NEVER committed in env (only non-secret defaults in .env). Anthropic Consumer Terms compliance: orchestration/LLM-calling only — no AI/ML model development, training, eval-of-models, or distillation; this is permitted application/automation code. Cron for the weekly performance-review path configured via LangGraph Platform native cron (assistant scheduled run), not local launchd. Add a LangSmith eval dataset for the compliance_gate (labeled good/violating copy) to measure false-negative rate before trusting auto-pass. Note current naming: hosted product is LangSmith Deployments / LangGraph Platform (api.smith.langchain.com, eu. for EU). Repo write target paths: data/social-media/**, ads/**, assets/social-images/** — writes only via PR after approval.
impact: Cuts the hands-on time for the most repetitive 65%-automatable parts of creative ops — drafting platform-correct A/B copy variants, enforcing char limits, and the per-claim brand/claims compliance review (the exact CONTENT-RULES.md checklist done manually today) — while keeping Shay's mandatory pre-publish approval intact (memory: no send/publish without approval; revenue-protective for the live Scheduler listing where overclaiming time-tracking/AI is a known risk).
