Directly addresses the four stated pain points: multi-platform requirement handling (per-platform generate step), variation-testing coordination (structured N-variant output with IDs), performance-metric tracking (read-only Ads/GA4 review path with winner/loser ranking against existing success thresholds), and brand consistency (canonical submission-profile facts + deterministic compliance gate prevent contradictory positioning/false feature claims across variants).

process: Growth Console Operations (Backlink Management UI) — a LangGraph Cloud (LangSmith Deployments, managed) agent that becomes the orchestration brain behind the Growth Console's existing `seo_backlink_jobs` control plane. Today Growth Console (Next.js, Firestore-backed, at growth.scheduler-systems.com/backlinks) dispatches five "safe" background job types to a separate GAL deployment via POST https://api.gal.run/api/sessions and tracks them in the seo_backlink_jobs collection. This agent replaces the opaque fire-and-forget GAL dispatch for the read/decision-heavy job types with a transparent, checkpointable LangGraph state machine that: (1) pulls a backlink row + its metadata snapshot + directory automation-support flag, (2) decides the next safe step, (3) executes read-only/idempotent work itself (recheck review status, poll verification email, crawl live listing for drift), and (4) HARD-STOPS at a human-in-the-loop interrupt before any irreversible/external-write action (submit_or_update_directory) or any CAPTCHA/MFA/auth-screen/anti-bot checkpoint, exactly mirroring the existing needs_human semantics in backlink-jobs.ts. The agent calls Claude only for classification/summarization/next-step reasoning (orchestration), never for model training/distillation. v1 scope = the three read/diagnostic job types end-to-end + the gated submit path stubbed behind an interrupt; it does NOT replace the GAL directory submit-dispatcher automation, it governs when that automation is allowed to fire. Grounding files: /Users/scheduler-systems/Documents/scheduler-systems-ltd/EscalateLabs/marketing/seo-tools/src/lib/backlink-jobs.ts (job types, SAFE_JOB_TYPES, needs_human, prompts), src/lib/gal-api.ts (dispatch shape), src/lib/submission-drift.ts (DirectoryMetadataSnapshot + drift fields), src/lib/directory-automation.ts (AUTOMATED_DIRECTORY_KEYS), src/app/api/backlinks/jobs/route.ts (isSafeBacklinkJobType gate), and .github/workflows/seo-tools-backup.yml (daily 02:15 UTC Firestore backup, 90-day retention).
trigger: Primary: HTTP POST from Growth Console's existing POST /api/backlinks/jobs handler — instead of (or alongside) dispatchGalBackgroundSession, it creates a LangGraph run via the Agent Server runs API (thread-per-backlink-job, thread_id = the seo_backlink_jobs doc id) with input {sheetName, rowNumber, backlinkId, directory, jobType}. Secondary: a scheduled cron (LangGraph cron or the existing daily-guest-post-recheck.yml / indexing-health.yml GitHub Actions calling the deployment) for batch recheck_review_status + crawl_live_listing_for_drift sweeps. Resumption: Growth Console UI "Approve / Reject" button on a job in waiting_external/needs_human state POSTs a resume command (Command(resume=...)) to the interrupted thread.
graph_steps: START -> load_context: server-side read of the backlink row (seo_backlinks), latest DirectoryMetadataSnapshot (seo_backlink_metadata_snapshots), automation-support flag (hasAutomationHandler), and prior job history for the row. Validates jobType is in SAFE_JOB_TYPES; non-safe -> route straight to human_review interrupt. No LLM call. Writes correlationId into state.

classify_and_plan (Claude, orchestration-only): given row state + job history, pick the next safe step and emit a structured decision {action: recheck|poll_email|crawl_drift|propose_submit|needs_human, rationale, confidence}. Uses bounded output schema; never proposes a write here, only a plan.

conditional edge route_by_jobtype: branch on the planned action to the matching executor node (recheck_review_status -> recheck_node, poll_verification_email -> poll_email_node, crawl_live_listing_for_drift -> drift_node, submit_or_update_directory -> approval_gate, resume_after_human_checkpoint -> resume_node).

recheck_node (read-only tool): fetch current directory listing/review status for the row, ask Claude to classify approved|pending|rejected|needs_human, write a job evidence note. Idempotent — safe to retry. -> persist_outcome.

poll_email_node (read-only tool): query the verification/approval mailbox (Gmail/Brevo read scope) for matching verification or claim emails for the directory, summarize whether the queue can advance. Read-only. If a click-to-verify link is required -> approval_gate. Else -> persist_outcome.

drift_node (read-only crawl): crawl the live listing, diff observed metadata vs DirectoryMetadataSnapshot comparedFields, produce MetadataDriftSummary {status: in_sync|drifted|unknown, driftFields}. Read-only. If drifted AND would require an external edit to fix -> approval_gate; else -> persist_outcome.

approval_gate (HUMAN-IN-THE-LOOP interrupt): for any external write (submit_or_update_directory, fixing drift, clicking a verification link) OR any CAPTCHA/MFA/auth-screen/anti-bot signal, call interrupt() with the full proposed action + evidence + secret-ref (never the secret value). Sets job status=waiting_external/needs_human in Firestore so the Growth Console UI surfaces an Approve/Reject control. Graph pauses; checkpointer persists state.

resume_node: on Command(resume={decision, operator_email, notes}) — if approved, perform the single gated action via the existing GAL submit-dispatcher / directory automation handler (or mark for it), recording operator identity; if rejected, mark needs_human with notes. Never re-runs already-completed human steps (mirrors resume_after_human_checkpoint prompt).

persist_outcome: write structured result back to seo_backlink_jobs (status, resultSummary, evidenceNote, driftSummary, galSessionId if delegated) and append a row-level history entry; update backlink runState. No LLM. -> END.

error/guard edges: provider-failure classification (classifyProviderFailure) routes retriable failures to a bounded retry, non-retriable to needs_human; unknown loginMethod or missing automation handler routes to approval_gate; any auth/CAPTCHA/MFA detection anywhere routes to approval_gate.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Cloud / LangSmith Deployments (managed Agent Server) — managed Postgres checkpointer + store auto-injected; do NOT pass checkpointer/store to .compile()
Anthropic Claude (via langchain-anthropic) — orchestration/classification/summarization only; NO training/distillation
Firestore (firebase-admin, scheduler-systems-web project): collections seo_backlinks, seo_backlink_jobs, seo_backlink_metadata_snapshots, plus per-row history subcollections
Growth Console HTTP API: POST /api/backlinks/jobs (trigger), job status/history routes (write-back surface)
GAL deployment (api.gal.run /api/sessions) — delegate only the actual gated directory submit to the existing GAL submit-dispatcher after human approval
GCP Secret Manager — resolve projects/<project>/secrets/<NAME> refs at execution time; only secret REFS travel through graph state
Read-only listing crawler (HTTP fetch) for drift detection
Verification mailbox read (Gmail or Brevo API, read scope) for poll_verification_email
GitHub Actions schedulers already present (daily-guest-post-recheck.yml, indexing-health.yml) as cron callers
LangGraph custom Auth (auth.py) validating the existing SEO_TOOLS_AUTH_TOKEN machine token so only Growth Console can create runs
secrets_needed: SEO_TOOLS_AUTH_TOKEN (inbound machine-token auth from Growth Console + reused as the agent's own bearer to call back protected Growth Console routes)
VERIFICATION_MAILBOX_CREDENTIALS (Gmail or Brevo read-scope key for poll_verification_email)
GCP_SECRET_MANAGER_SA (resolve directory password secret refs at run time)
approval_gates: ["HARD interrupt() before submit_or_update_directory (creates/updates an external listing) — the single most irreversible action; never auto-fires in v1.", "HARD interrupt() before any external write to fix detected drift (editing live listing metadata).", "HARD interrupt() before following any email verification/claim link (poll_verification_email may read freely but acting on a link is gated).", "HARD interrupt() + status=needs_human on any CAPTCHA, anti-bot block, MFA prompt, or unexpected auth screen (exactly mirrors backlink-jobs.ts rule: stop and mark a human checkpoint).", "HARD interrupt() for unknown loginMethod / missing automation handler (cannot be scripted -> human).", "Job-type guard: any jobType NOT in SAFE_JOB_TYPES (e.g. raw attempt_captcha_submission) is rejected/gated, matching the existing isSafeBacklinkJobType server gate.", "Resume requires an explicit operator decision + operator_email recorded in history before the gated action runs; rejection -> needs_human, never silent.", "No secret values in graph state, logs, or LLM prompts — only Secret Manager refs; resolution happens inside the executor node at the moment of an already-approved action."]
impact: Removes the opaque fire-and-forget GAL dispatch for the three read/diagnostic job types and makes them transparent, resumable, and auditable, directly attacking the stated pain points: GAL dispatch latency (no full session spin-up for read work), drift-detection accuracy (structured field-level diff vs DirectoryMetadataSnapshot with confidence + evidence), and dashboard complexity for non-technical users (clear Approve/Reject checkpoints instead of raw job state). Keeps Growth Console as canonical data store — the agent only reads/writes through existing Firestore collections and the jobs API, so the daily 02:15 UTC backup + 90-day retention still fully covers it. Crucially it tightens the safety boundary: every irreversible/external action is now an explicit human-gated interrupt rather than an inferred needs_human inside a remote GAL session. Automatable_score 80 is realistic precisely because the high-risk 20% (live submits, CAPTCHA/MFA, drift fixes) stays human-gated by design.", "deploy_notes": "ignored"
deploy_notes: Deploy as a managed LangGraph Cloud deployment (LangSmith Deployments / Agent Server). langgraph.json: dependencies ["."], graphs {"backlink_ops":"./agent/graph.py:graph"}, python_version "3.12", image_distro "wolfi", auth {"path":"./auth.py:auth"} validating SEO_TOOLS_AUTH_TOKEN, env injected via the platform UI (never committed). Build the StateGraph with a typed State (job context, plan decision, drift summary, outcome, operator decision) and a reducer on history; compile WITHOUT a custom checkpointer/store (platform injects Postgres). Use interrupt() + Command(resume=...) for HITL; thread_id = seo_backlink_jobs doc id so a job maps 1:1 to a resumable thread. Cutover plan: (1) ship behind a GAL_DISPATCH_BACKEND-style feature flag so POST /api/backlinks/jobs can route recheck/poll/drift to the LangGraph deployment while submit_or_update_directory still flows to GAL until the gated path is validated; (2) shadow-run the three read job types against a handful of rows and compare drift/recheck outputs to current GAL sessions; (3) enable the approval_gate-fronted submit path only after the UI Approve/Reject control is wired. v1 explicitly out of scope: replacing the GAL submit-dispatcher handlers, any autonomous submit, any model training. Verify the daily backup still captures all three collections post-cutover before relying on it for recovery.

process: App Store Listing Management (ASO) — bi-weekly optimization cycle for Scheduler Systems' iOS (app id 6446867570) and Android (panuilov.shay.priority) listings. The agent runs the established loop: snapshot current keyword rankings, diff against the previous snapshot in aso-ranking-history.json, run competitor metadata analysis, surface optimization opportunities, draft a Markdown cycle report and proposed metadata changes, then STOP for human approval before anything is written back to the repo or any store. It replaces the existing GitHub Action (.github/workflows/biweekly-aso-export.yml, cron 0 0 1,15 * *) whose dangerous behavior is auto-merging the data PR with `gh pr merge --admin` and the manual /aso-* slash-command flow (/aso-start, /aso-track-keywords, /aso-research, /aso-competitor-analysis, /aso-track-change). All file commits, PR creation/merge, and any App Store Connect / Google Play metadata pushes become human-in-the-loop interrupts. The agent only orchestrates LLM reasoning + read/search tools; it does NOT train, fine-tune, distill, or evaluate any ML model (Anthropic Consumer Terms guardrail in AGENTS.md).
trigger: Two entry points into the same compiled graph (assistant id "aso"): (1) Scheduled — LangGraph Platform Cron on `0 6 1,15 * *` UTC kicks a new thread with input {cycle_name: null, mode: "biweekly"} on the 1st and 15th, mirroring the current GH Action cadence but invoking the deployed agent's /runs endpoint instead of merging a PR. (2) On-demand — POST to the deployment /threads/{id}/runs with mode in {"snapshot","keywords","research","competitors","track_change"} so a human can run any single phase ad hoc (replaces the individual /aso-* slash commands). A webhook from the marketing repo (config drift / new competitor added) can also start an ad-hoc competitors run.
graph_steps: load_state: read aso-config.json, aso-keywords.json, aso-ranking-history.json, aso-change-log.json from the marketing repo (GitHub contents API, read-only token) into State; resolve cycle metadata (current_cycle, last_snapshot, next_snapshot_due) and the 25 tracked keywords + 5 competitors. Branch on `mode`.

fetch_rankings: for each tracked keyword + platform, get current rank. Primary path = web-search tool (AppTweak is archived: aso-config.apptweak.archived==true, no API key, automation_status 'free-scraping'); optional Playwright-scrape subgraph as fallback. Tool is bounded + retried; never writes. Produces an in-memory snapshot object matching the aso-ranking-history snapshots[] schema (keyword, rank, difficulty, category, platforms.ios/android.rank).

diff_and_score: LLM(Claude) compares the new snapshot to the most recent snapshot in history; computes improved/declined/unchanged/new/dropped, top gainers, top losers, dropped keywords (same shape as aso-report-2026-04-15.md). Correlates movements with recent aso-change-log entries.

competitor_analysis: (runs in biweekly + competitors modes) web-search competitor metadata (titles/subtitles/keyword signals) for the 5 configured competitors; Claude produces the aso-competitor-analysis.json structure (currently missing on disk — agent creates it) with ASO scores, head-to-head gaps, and opportunities.

draft_recommendations: Claude synthesizes the diff + competitor gaps into (a) a dated Markdown cycle report (data/aso-report-YYYY-MM-DD.md) and (b) a concrete proposed-metadata-change set (title/subtitle/keyword-field deltas for iOS/Android, each within char limits), plus a proposed aso-change-log.json append entry.

APPROVAL GATE 1 — review_report: interrupt() surfacing the draft report + proposed JSON file diffs (config/keywords/ranking-history/change-log) and proposed metadata changes. Human resumes with approve / edit / reject. Nothing has been written yet.

commit_data: on approval only — open a branch and PR in the marketing repo with the snapshot appended to aso-ranking-history.json, updated aso-config.json (cycle++ , last_snapshot, last_synced), the report .md, and the change-log append. Creates the PR; does NOT merge.
APPROVAL GATE 2 — confirm_merge: interrupt() before merging the data PR (the old workflow's `--admin` auto-merge becomes an explicit human confirm). Optional.

APPROVAL GATE 3 — push_store_metadata: only if human approved metadata changes in Gate 1 — interrupt() again immediately before any App Store Connect / Google Play write (Fastlane deliver/supply or store API). This is the highest-risk irreversible action and is gated separately and OFF by default in v1 (recommend leaving store push as a documented manual step for the first few cycles).
finalize: post a summary (PR link, report link, what was/ wasn't pushed) back to the run output / optional Slack notify; update next_snapshot_due. END.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS tier) — Postgres checkpointer + store auto-provisioned; do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= to .compile()
LangGraph Platform Cron (0 6 1,15 * * UTC) for the biweekly trigger
Anthropic Claude (claude via langchain-anthropic) — the only model use; orchestration/reasoning only, NO model training/eval/distillation
Web search tool (Tavily or equivalent) — primary ranking + competitor metadata signal while AppTweak is archived
Playwright scrape subgraph (fallback ranking path; mirrors existing automation/src/aso/scraper.js + apptweak.js)
GitHub contents/PR API (read token for load_state; write token gated behind Gate 1/2) against EscalateLabs/marketing data/ files
App Store Connect API / Google Play Developer API or Fastlane deliver/supply — Gate 3 only, OFF by default in v1
Slack MCP (optional) for the finalize notification
human-in-the-loop interrupt() (LangGraph native) for all three approval gates
LangGraph SDK /runs + /threads endpoints for on-demand single-phase invocation
secrets_needed: GITHUB_TOKEN_RW (PR create/merge, injected via Platform secrets, only used after approval gates)
APPTWEAK_EMAIL / APPTWEAK_PASSWORD (Playwright fallback only; AppTweak currently archived/lapsed — optional)
APPTWEAK_API_KEY (future direct-API path; not configured today)
APP_STORE_CONNECT_API_KEY / GOOGLE_PLAY_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON (Gate 3 store push only; OFF by default in v1)
approval_gates: Three interrupt()-based human-in-the-loop gates, all required because the actions are repo/store writes (irreversible/external): Gate 1 review_report — human approves/edits/rejects the drafted cycle report, the proposed JSON file diffs, AND the proposed store-metadata deltas before ANY write occurs (this is the core gate). Gate 2 confirm_merge — explicit human confirm before merging the marketing-repo data PR, directly replacing the old workflow's `gh pr merge --admin` auto-merge. Gate 3 push_store_metadata — separate, stricter confirm immediately before any App Store Connect / Google Play metadata write; this gate is OFF by default in v1 (store push stays a documented manual step for the first cycles). On reject/timeout at any gate the run ends having written nothing. Secrets only via Platform env injection — never hardcoded.
impact: automatable_score 75/100. Removes the two riskiest/most-manual parts of today's flow: (1) the GH Action's blind `--admin` auto-merge of ranking data PRs (replaced by gated, reviewable commits), and (2) manual ranking verification + manual competitor-metadata tracking (the pain points), by orchestrating bounded web-search/scrape + Claude diffing on the established bi-weekly cadence. Adds the historical-trend analysis and the missing aso-competitor-analysis.json artifact that the process calls for but that does not exist on disk today. Net: ~2 marketing hours per bi-weekly cycle saved, faster reaction to rank drops (e.g. the -16 on 'employee scheduling' seen 2026-04-15), with a human still owning every store-facing and repo-merge decision. Low blast radius: a read-only failure just produces no PR; nothing reaches a store without explicit approval.
deploy_notes: langgraph.json: graphs {"aso":"./aso_agent/graph.py:graph"}, python_version 3.11, image_distro wolfi, pip_installer uv, env injected via Platform UI (NOT committed). State = TypedDict(messages[add_messages reducer], mode, cycle_name, config, keywords, history, new_snapshot, diff, competitor_analysis, report_md, proposed_metadata, proposed_file_diffs, approvals{report,merge,store}). Compile with builder.compile() ONLY — the managed runtime injects Postgres checkpointer+store; passing InMemorySaver/custom saver is local-only and is ignored/overridden in hosted. Use interrupt() (not the deprecated interrupt_before for these) for the three gates so resume can carry edited payloads. Deploy via `langgraph deploy` (CLI) or GitHub-connected build against api.smith.langchain.com. Create the Cron job post-deploy: schedule 0 6 1,15 * *, assistant "aso", input {mode:"biweekly"}. Disable/retire .github/workflows/biweekly-aso-export.yml once the agent is live to avoid double-running. Anthropic guardrail: orchestration calls Claude only — no training/eval/distillation; this is permitted application/infra work. v1 keeps Gate 3 (store push) OFF; ship the read+diff+report+gated-PR loop first, add store push after a few trusted cycles. Repo target for reads/writes: EscalateLabs/marketing data/ (aso-config.json, aso-keywords.json, aso-ranking-history.json, aso-change-log.json, aso-report-*.md, new aso-competitor-analysis.json).

trigger: Two triggers into the same LangGraph Platform deployment (one graph, assistant id "funnel"): (1) Scheduled cron via LangGraph Platform native Crons API — POST a cron on the deployed assistant, expression "0 7 * * 1" (Mon 07:00 UTC), matching the existing weekly-kpi-export.yml schedule, so the agent supersedes/augments the script path. (2) On-demand HTTP run (POST /runs/wait or /runs/stream against the deployment) for "real-time visibility" — fired ad hoc by Shay or by a lightweight GitHub Actions step / webhook when a Brevo deal-stage change is suspected. Both enter at START with the same input schema {review_date?: str, mode: "scheduled"|"adhoc", require_live: bool}. Thread id is keyed per ISO-week (e.g. "funnel-2026-W23") so the checkpointer carries the cross-week stall state needed for the 2-consecutive-week guardrails.
process: Funnel & Conversion Analytics (QA / sales-funnel measurement). Replaces the brittle weekly cron-script-to-PR loop with a stateful LangGraph agent that (a) pulls live funnel counts from Brevo, (b) computes conversion rates, (c) evaluates the conversion guardrails INCLUDING the 2-consecutive-week stall rules using durable checkpointer state instead of re-reading prior markdown, (d) drafts the machine-generated KPI block + a human-readable stall analysis, and (e) HALTS at a human-in-the-loop interrupt before any irreversible action (opening/committing a PR, posting a Slack/Brevo alert). Orchestration only — it calls Claude (Opus/Sonnet via Anthropic API) to summarize/triage stalls and draft alert copy; it does NOT train, fine-tune, distill, or score any ML model. All funnel math stays deterministic Python (ported from generate-weekly-kpi-review.mjs: pct(), classifyDealStage(), the qualifying/proposal/won/lost buckets) so the LLM never invents numbers — the LLM only narrates and recommends. Directly attacks the stated pain points: stalls between lead->qualified and qualified->proposal get a dedicated stall-detector node with persisted streak counters, and the adhoc trigger removes the "lacks real-time visibility" gap. The required 2-week observation window is satisfied by reading last week's persisted streak from the per-week thread lineage rather than waiting for two manual reviews.
graph_steps: START -> ingest_funnel: node calls Brevo v3 (GET /contacts for newContacts30d via count/x-total-count header; paginate GET /crm/deals; resolve stage names via GET /crm/pipeline/details/{id}) using BREVO_API_KEY from env. Pure data fetch, retries with backoff, writes raw counts to state. On hard failure, falls back to last good snapshot from the long-term Store and flags degraded=true.

compute_metrics: deterministic Python (no LLM) port of generate-weekly-kpi-review.mjs — classifyDealStage() buckets into qualifying/proposal/won/lost, then leadToQualifiedRate/qualifiedToProposalRate/proposalToWonRate via pct(). Emits the structured funnel object into state.

load_prior_state: read previous ISO-week funnel + stall streak counters from the injected Postgres BaseStore (namespace ('funnel','streaks')). This is what powers the '2 consecutive weeks' guardrails without re-parsing markdown.

evaluate_guardrails: deterministic rules engine. Lead->Qualified stall (new leads>0 but qualifying==0), Qualified->Proposal stall (qualifying>0 but proposal==0), Proposal->Won stall (proposal>0 but won==0). Increment/reset per-rule streak counters; a rule FIRES only when its streak reaches 2. Produces fired_alerts[] with severity.

narrate_and_draft (LLM, Claude Sonnet by default; Opus if any alert fired): given ONLY the computed numbers + fired_alerts, draft (i) the machine-generated KPI markdown block in the exact BEGIN/END sentinel format the repo already uses, and (ii) a concise stall root-cause hypothesis + recommended owner action (Kruti for lead/qualify, Shay for proposal/won). Temperature low; numbers are passed in, never generated.

route_actions: conditional edge. If no alerts fired AND mode==scheduled -> still proposes the routine weekly PR. If alerts fired -> assembles BOTH the PR draft and the alert-send payload (Slack/Brevo/email). If mode==adhoc with no change -> route to END with a read-only summary (no side effects).

human_review (interrupt): interrupt() pauses the run and surfaces the full proposed action set — PR title/branch/body + file diffs for reviews/weekly/<date>-kpi-review.md and kpis/support-slas.md, plus any alert message text — for explicit approve/edit/reject. NOTHING is sent or committed before resume with a Command(resume=...).

execute_approved (post-approval, idempotent): on approve, performs ONLY the approved actions — open/commit the GitHub PR (branch chore/weekly-kpi-export, label operating-review, mirroring create-pull-request@v6 today) and/or post the approved alert. Each external call wrapped so a partial failure is retryable; writes an action-receipt to the Store.

persist_state -> END: write this week's funnel snapshot + updated streak counters back to the BaseStore so next week's load_prior_state sees them; emit final structured result (metrics, alerts, actions_taken, pr_url).
tools_integrations: Brevo API v3 (GET /contacts, GET /crm/deals, GET /crm/pipeline/details/{id}) — read-only ingest
Anthropic Claude API (Sonnet default, Opus on alert) — narration/triage/draft only, NO model training/distillation
GitHub API (gh / REST) — open PR on Scheduler-Systems/business-ops-admin, branch chore/weekly-kpi-export, label operating-review (approval-gated write)
Slack (chat.postMessage to ops channel) — stall alert (approval-gated write)
Brevo transactional email OR ops email — optional alert channel (approval-gated write)
LangGraph Platform native Crons API — schedule the Mon 07:00 UTC run
LangGraph injected Postgres checkpointer (thread state) + BaseStore (cross-week streak counters & last-good snapshot)
LangSmith tracing/observability (auto on the platform)
approval_gates: Single mandatory human-in-the-loop interrupt() at the human_review node BEFORE any irreversible/external action. The graph compiles WITHOUT a custom checkpointer/store (platform injects production Postgres) and relies on interrupt()/Command(resume=) so the run durably pauses. Gated actions: (1) opening or committing the GitHub PR that mutates reviews/weekly/*.md and kpis/support-slas.md; (2) posting any Slack/Brevo/email stall alert. Read-only steps (Brevo ingest, metric compute, guardrail eval, draft generation) run without approval. adhoc no-change runs short-circuit to END with zero side effects. execute_approved performs ONLY the explicitly approved subset and is idempotent (action-receipt in Store guards against double-send on resume/retry). Reject path discards drafts and persists state without acting. No auto-send, ever — matches workspace rule "never send any message without explicit human approval".
secrets_needed: INTERCOM_ACCESS_TOKEN (optional, only if extending to the support-SLA severity rollup the existing script also produces)
deploy_notes: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS, us region api.smith.langchain.com). langgraph.json at repo root: {"$schema":"https://langgra.ph/schema.json","dependencies":["."],"graphs":{"funnel":"./funnel_agent/agent.py:graph"},"env":".env","python_version":"3.12","image_distro":"wolfi","pip_installer":"uv","store":{"index":{"embed":"openai:text-embedding-3-small","dims":1536}},"checkpointer":{"ttl":{"strategy":"delete","default_ttl":43200}}}. agent.py builds StateGraph(State, context_schema=...) and exports graph = builder.compile() — CRITICAL: do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= to compile(); the platform injects production Postgres (passing InMemorySaver would be silently overridden). State uses TypedDict with reducers; mode/require_live/review_date in input_schema. Deploy via `langgraph deploy` (CLI) or the LangSmith UI from this repo; inject all secrets through the deployment UI/API env, NEVER commit them (.env holds only non-secret defaults). After deploy, create the cron with the Crons API on assistant "funnel" (cron "0 7 * * 1"). Migration path: run the agent in parallel with weekly-kpi-export.yml for 2 weeks (so the 2-week streak state warms up and outputs can be diffed against the script's PR), then disable the GitHub Actions schedule and let the agent own the weekly PR. Keep generate-weekly-kpi-review.mjs as the reference oracle for the deterministic compute port. Anthropic Terms guardrail: orchestration/narration only — assert in code review that no node trains/fine-tunes/distills/evals an ML model.
impact: Closes the three stated pain points: (1) "conversion stalls between lead and qualification" and "qualified deals not moving to proposal" get a dedicated, persisted stall-detector with per-rule streak counters and an LLM root-cause hypothesis routed to the right owner (Kruti/Shay). (2) "requires 2-week observation for alerts" is preserved as a guardrail but automated — the durable BaseStore streak counters mean the window advances every week with zero manual review, so an alert fires the moment a stall hits its 2nd consecutive week. (3) "lacks real-time visibility" is solved by the adhoc HTTP trigger that runs the same read-only ingest+compute on demand and returns a summary with no side effects. automatable_score 85 is well-supported: ingest/compute/guardrail/draft are fully automated and deterministic; only the send/commit step is human-gated by policy. Revenue relevance: the funnel measured here (Brevo deals -> Won) is the B2B shift-scheduling sales pipeline, so faster stall detection directly protects new-revenue conversion — consistent with the revenue-first priority.

process: Review Collection & Reputation Management — milestone-triggered, multi-platform (G2, Capterra, GetApp, TrustRadius, SaaSHub) review request orchestration for Scheduler Web and GAL-Run. A deployable LangGraph Platform (managed/Cloud SaaS) graph that ingests product milestone events, applies hard send-rule gates (negative-experience suppression, cross-platform dedup, 60-day-per-milestone-per-user cooldown), selects the best platform per user by priority, drafts a Brevo milestone email using the existing copy template, and creates a Brevo email draft in the EXISTING `draft -> approved -> sent` state machine. The actual send is NEVER performed by the agent autonomously: the graph emits a human-in-the-loop interrupt and only resumes once a human marks the draft `approved` via the Growth Console contract. v1 is orchestration-only (calls Claude for copy personalization + judgment, calls platform/Brevo/Firestore tools for IO); it trains/distills NO model and hardcodes NO secrets.
trigger: Webhook ingestion of a product milestone event written by Scheduler Web / GAL-Run (Firestore doc create or HTTP webhook into the custom Starlette app mounted via `langgraph.json` `http.app`). Each event = {userId, email, product, milestoneType, milestoneSummary, occurredAt}. Two entry modes: (1) real-time per-event run on milestone fire, queued with the 14-day delay built into the campaign rule; (2) a scheduled daily sweep (LangGraph cron / external scheduler hitting the Agent Server `/runs`) that re-evaluates aged milestones whose 14-day window has elapsed and no review yet exists. Thread/checkpoint keyed by `{userId}:{milestoneType}` so concurrent duplicate fires collapse onto one interruptible thread.
graph_steps: START -> ingest_event: validate webhook payload against milestone schema, normalize {userId,email,product,milestoneType,milestoneSummary,occurredAt}, reject malformed/unknown milestone types (END with no-op).

load_profile: read Growth Console reviewProfiles[] + per-user review state from Firestore (BaseStore namespaced by userId); fetch reviewProfiles[].url/platform/lastScrapedAt and per-user lastAskAt/reviewedPlatforms/optOut/openTicket/negativeSurvey.

gate_send_rules (HARD, deterministic, no LLM): suppress if user opted out of marketing email; OR has an open support ticket; OR has a recent negative survey response; OR already has a review on ANY tracked platform; OR was asked for this milestone within 60 days; OR the 14-day post-milestone window has not yet elapsed (route back to a delayed/scheduled re-check, not a send). Any failed gate -> record_outcome(skipped, reason) -> END.

dedup_check: cross-app + cross-platform dedup — confirm no other product (Scheduler vs GAL) asked this same human within the 60-day window (dedupe key = email + 60d), and pick the highest-priority platform the user has NOT yet reviewed (G2 > Capterra > GetApp > TrustRadius > SaaSHub) using reviewProfiles[].url at runtime (never hardcoded).

select_platform_and_personalize (Claude call, generation-only): choose the platform + subject variant (A/B/C) and fill MILESTONE_SUMMARY/PRODUCT_NAME/PLATFORM_NAME/REVIEW_URL into the review_request_milestone_triggered template; enforce honest-feedback framing (never solicit positive reviews). Output is copy + chosen REVIEW_URL params only.

create_brevo_draft (idempotent tool call): call existing createEmailDraft() to persist a draft with status='draft' (recipients=[user email], subject, body, templateId, params). Idempotency key = {userId}:{milestoneType}:{60d-window} to prevent duplicate drafts on retry. This is a draft only — NOT a send.

APPROVAL GATE -> interrupt(): emit interrupt with the rendered draft + dedup/gate evidence for human review in Growth Console. Graph pauses on the managed Postgres checkpointer. Human transitions draft 'draft'->'approved' (sets approvedBy) out-of-band per GROWTH_OPERATING_CONTRACT; Command(resume=approval_decision) wakes the thread.
branch_on_decision: if rejected/edited -> record_outcome(skipped/edited) -> END (no send). If approved -> send_node.

send_node (only reachable post-approval): call existing sendApprovedDraft(draftId) which atomically claims 'approved'->'sending' (double-send-safe) and sends via Brevo transactional; on failure markEmailDraftFailed (no stuck 'sending').

record_outcome -> write_back: update Growth Console reviewProfiles[]/per-user state (lastAskAt, askedPlatform, milestone, draftId, send result) to Firestore + BaseStore; emit metrics (asked/skipped/sent/failed + skip reason). -> END.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS Agent Server) — Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore auto-provisioned; do NOT pass own checkpointer/store to .compile()
Custom Starlette/FastAPI app mounted via langgraph.json http.app for the milestone webhook ingress + Growth Console approve/reject callback
Anthropic Claude API (claude-opus/sonnet) for copy personalization + platform/variant judgment ONLY — orchestration calls, no training/distillation/eval-data generation
Growth Console reviewProfiles[] (existing growth-cli types.ts ReviewProfile{platform,url,averageRating,reviewCount,lastScrapedAt})

Brevo transactional email via EXISTING seo-tools email-drafts.ts state machine (createEmailDraft / updateEmailDraft / claimDraftForSending / sendApprovedDraft / markEmailDraftSent / markEmailDraftFailed) — reuse, do not reimplement the gate
Firestore (priority-118da or growth project) for milestone events + per-user review state + write-back
Review platform reference data (G2/Capterra/GetApp/TrustRadius/SaaSHub) sourced from reviewProfiles[] at runtime — URLs never hardcoded in copy
LangSmith tracing/observability + cron/scheduler for the daily aged-milestone sweep

Existing assets: data/campaigns/review-collection-g2-2026.md (send rules), email-templates/review_request_milestone_triggered.md (copy), GROWTH_OPERATING_CONTRACT.md (human-required: sending/approving)
approval_gates: MANDATORY human-in-the-loop interrupt() before ANY email send — this is the single irreversible action. The graph creates a Brevo draft at status='draft' and then interrupts; it can ONLY resume into send_node via Command(resume=...) after a human has transitioned the draft to 'approved' (with approvedBy set) through the Growth Console workflow. This mirrors the existing enforced contract: email-drafts.ts rejects marking 'sent' unless prior status was 'approved', and sendApprovedDraft() atomically claims 'approved'->'sending' so a second concurrent caller fails (double-send-safe). GROWTH_OPERATING_CONTRACT lists 'sending or approving outreach' as Human-required. No autonomous send, no bulk blast, no auto-approve path. Rejected/edited at the gate => skip + record, never send. Hard pre-gate suppressions (open ticket / negative survey / existing review / opt-out / cooldown / window-not-elapsed) are deterministic and run BEFORE the draft is created, so negative-experience users never reach a draft. Secrets (Brevo API key, Anthropic key, Firestore creds, webhook signing secret) injected via LangGraph Platform env/secret store at deploy time and read from env — never committed to langgraph.json/.env-in-repo and never hardcoded.
deploy_notes: Managed LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments / Cloud SaaS). langgraph.json: graphs={"review_collection":"./agent/graph.py:graph"}, http.app="./agent/app.py:app" for the webhook + approval-callback ingress, python_version="3.12", image_distro="wolfi", env injected via platform UI (NOT committed), store.index for reviewProfiles semantic lookup optional, checkpointer.ttl for thread retention. graph.py builds StateGraph(...).compile() WITHOUT a custom checkpointer/store (platform injects Postgres). interrupt()/Command(resume=) for the approval HIL. Deploy via `langgraph` CLI or Git-connected build to api.smith.langchain.com. NOT to be confused with the deployed GAL agent stack. AI/ML guardrail: this is orchestration calling Claude only — it develops/trains/distills NO model (compliant with workspace Anthropic-terms rule). v1 scope = single graph, real-time + daily-sweep entry; product-side milestone emission (Scheduler/GAL repo changes) and Firestore dedup-schema design are the stated pain-point prerequisites and are tracked separately under marketing#389 — the agent consumes those events but does not implement them. Pre-deploy blockers to flag: (1) reviewProfiles[] currently has no per-user lastAskAt/reviewedPlatforms fields (ReviewProfile is product-level, not user-level) — needs the Firestore per-user review-state schema before dedup/cooldown gates can run; (2) milestone webhook emission from product repos not yet implemented. Until both land, deploy in dry-run mode (create drafts, always interrupt, never auto-resume).
impact: Converts the currently-manual, draft-phase review campaign into a governed, milestone-triggered pipeline that produces fewer/higher-quality asks at positive-engagement moments, with deterministic negative-experience suppression and cross-app/cross-platform dedup — directly targeting the campaign's success metrics (10 G2 reviews/30d, 5 Capterra/60d, >30% open, >8% click, <0.5% unsub). automatable_score 65 is consistent: the orchestration/gating/drafting is fully automatable, but the send stays human-gated by contract and two upstream integrations (product milestone emission + per-user Firestore review-state schema) are unbuilt, capping near-term autonomy. Reputational/compliance protection: honest-feedback-only framing and the hard pre-draft suppression gates keep it inside G2/platform ToS and avoid asking unhappy or already-reviewed users.

process: Email Lifecycle & Retention Messaging — automated, approval-gated lifecycle email sequencing for Scheduler/GAL users. The agent ingests a normalized user-activity event (signup, trial-start, inactivity, team-created, churn, trial-expiring, holiday-cohort), decides which of the 7 lifecycle templates (if any) should fire for that user given their stage and suppression rules, resolves and validates the Brevo template-variable mapping against the contact record, renders a send-decision, then HARD-STOPS at a human-in-the-loop interrupt for explicit approval before any Brevo send. Only on approval does it call Brevo's transactional/automation send. Every send, skip, and suppression is logged with an idempotency key so the same user+stage+window never double-fires. This is orchestration over Brevo + an LLM copy/eligibility reasoner ONLY — it does NOT train, fine-tune, or distill any model.
trigger: Primary: webhook POST to the deployment's custom HTTP route (langgraph.json `http.app`) from the planned app endpoints — /api/webhooks/brevo/user-signup, /trial-expiring, /user-inactive, /event — once Scheduler/GAL apps emit lifecycle events (this emission is the current blocker). Each webhook creates/resumes a thread keyed by user_id+stage. Secondary: a daily cron (LangGraph Platform scheduled run / CronCreate) that sweeps the Brevo contact DB for time-based stages the apps don't push as events — day 3-5 education, 7+ day inactivity, trial -7/-3/-1 expiry, 30+ day churn win-back, and the Dec 22-25 holiday year_in_review cohort. Both paths funnel into the same StateGraph entrypoint.
graph_steps: ingest_event — normalize incoming webhook/cron payload into State {user_id, email, stage, contact_attrs, params, source, idempotency_key}; validate required identity fields; route invalid/unknown stages to END with a logged skip

resolve_contact — fetch the Brevo contact (FIRSTNAME, TRIAL_START_DATE, TRIAL_END_DATE, LAST_LOGIN, PLAN_TYPE, TEAM_SIZE, FEATURES_USED) and merge with event params; flag missing attributes needed by the candidate template

classify_stage — deterministic eligibility engine maps stage -> candidate template (get_started_today / exclusive_features / unlock_premium_benefits / we_miss_you / your_team_is_waiting / re_engage_users / year_in_review) using the documented trigger windows; emits 'no eligible template' -> END when none match

check_suppression — enforce guardrails BEFORE any LLM/send work: unsubscribe/blocklist check, frequency cap (max 1-2 marketing emails/week per contact), idempotency-key dedupe (same user+stage+window already sent?), and quiet-stage rules; suppressed -> log + END

validate_template_mapping — load template ID from brevo-templates.json (FAIL CLOSED if id is null/unsynced), verify every {{contact.*}} and {{params.*}} variable the template references resolves to a non-empty value (esp. year_in_review's SCHEDULES_CREATED/HOURS_SAVED/TEAM_MEMBERS/SHIFTS_SCHEDULED/DAYS_ACTIVE); unresolved variables -> route to needs_review

compose_send_decision — LLM reasoner (Claude via API) produces a human-readable send proposal: chosen template, subject, resolved merge variables, why-this-stage rationale, and a deliverability/lint pass on the rendered preview; NO send happens here

approval_gate (interrupt) — REQUIRED human-in-the-loop: graph interrupts and surfaces the full send proposal (recipient, template, rendered variables, rationale) and waits; resumes only on an explicit approve/reject/edit command. Reject -> log + END; edit -> back to validate_template_mapping; approve -> proceed
send_via_brevo — ONLY reachable post-approval: call Brevo transactional/automation API with the approved templateId + params, write the idempotency key + Brevo messageId to the store, log the send

record_outcome — persist send/skip/suppression result, update last-sent timestamp for frequency capping, emit metrics (eligible/suppressed/sent/rejected) for open/click reconciliation via the /event webhook -> END
tools_integrations: Brevo API (contacts read, transactional send v3, automation/list triggers, blocklist + unsubscribe status)
Anthropic Claude API (eligibility rationale + copy/deliverability lint — orchestration only, no model training/distillation)
LangGraph Platform managed Postgres checkpointer (thread persistence for the interrupt) + BaseStore (idempotency keys, last-sent timestamps, frequency-cap ledger)
Custom HTTP app mounted via langgraph.json http.app (Scheduler/GAL webhook receivers: user-signup, trial-expiring, user-inactive, event)
LangGraph scheduled run / cron for time-window sweeps (education, inactivity, expiry, churn, holiday cohort)
Scheduler/GAL activity-event source (the not-yet-built app emitters feeding the webhooks)
email-templates/ HTML + data/brevo-templates.json (template key -> Brevo templateId + variable manifest)
secrets_needed: WEBHOOK_SHARED_SECRET (HMAC verification for inbound app/Brevo webhooks)
approval_gates: Single mandatory human-in-the-loop interrupt at the approval_gate node, immediately before send_via_brevo — the ONLY node that performs the irreversible Brevo send. The graph uses LangGraph's interrupt() to pause and persist state in the managed Postgres checkpointer, surfacing the full send proposal (recipient email, chosen template, fully-rendered merge variables, stage rationale, deliverability lint). It resumes only via an explicit Command(resume=...) carrying approve / reject / edit. Reject and 'no eligible template' / suppressed paths terminate without sending. v1 ships with per-message approval (every send reviewed). A follow-up can add a bounded auto-approve allowlist (e.g. transactional day-0 onboarding) gated by a separately-approved policy, but win-back/upsell/holiday-blast and any bulk cohort send ALWAYS require manual approval. Secrets are read from env only; nothing hardcoded.
impact: Unblocks the stalled lifecycle program (templates done, sends never wired) by giving a single governed path from event -> approved send, while structurally preventing the documented landmines: fail-closed on null/unsynced template IDs and unresolved merge variables (no broken {{FIRSTNAME}} blasts), enforced unsubscribe/blocklist honoring and 1-2/week frequency caps, idempotency to stop double-fires, and a mandatory approval gate so no email leaves without a human OK. Targets the automatable_score of 70: eligibility, mapping validation, dedupe, and rendering are fully automated; the human only approves the final send.
deploy_notes: Deploy to LangGraph Platform (managed Cloud SaaS, api.smith.langchain.com). langgraph.json: graphs {\"lifecycle\": \"./agent/graph.py:graph\"}, dependencies [\".\"], python_version 3.12, image_distro wolfi, http.app -> ./agent/webhooks.py:app (mounts the 4 webhook receivers with HMAC verification + CORS locked to the app origins), store.index for contact/template semantic lookup optional, checkpointer.ttl for thread cleanup. CRITICAL: graph.compile() must NOT pass a custom checkpointer/store — the platform injects production Postgres automatically; passing InMemorySaver is local-only and is ignored/overridden in the hosted runtime. Inject all secrets via the Deployments UI/API (never in committed .env). v1 rollout: (1) sync templates to Brevo so brevo-templates.json IDs are non-null (the validate_template_mapping node fails closed until then), (2) deploy with cron sweep DISABLED and only the webhook path live, run test sends through the approval gate to a seed list to validate deliverability + variable mapping, (3) enable the cron sweep for time-window stages once the apps emit events. Owning repo/worktree: EscalateLabs/marketing (create a worktree under EscalateLabs/marketing/worktrees/ — do not edit the live checkout). Tie work to a GitHub issue before starting per workspace policy.

trigger: Primary: LangGraph Platform scheduled cron assistant, weekly Mon 06:00 UTC (after the existing GitHub Actions Moz/ads collector finishes its 00:00 UTC run, so fresh Firestore + report data exists). Secondary: on-demand HTTP invoke (`POST /threads/{id}/runs`) for an ad-hoc single-competitor deep dive, passing `{competitor, focus}` in input. Tertiary: webhook from the existing `weekly-competitor-intel.yml` workflow's final step (instead of cron) so the agent runs immediately after data collection succeeds — preferred once wired, eliminates the timing race.
process: Competitive Analysis & Positioning (Marketing). The agent is an LLM analysis + synthesis layer that sits ON TOP of the existing deterministic collectors (Moz competitor metrics, ads transparency scrape, Firestore competitor_intel* collections, ASO config roster). It does NOT replace the GitHub Actions data pipeline — it consumes its output and adds the four things the current pipeline cannot do: (1) ASO/app-store metadata CHANGE detection across the 5 tracked competitors (title/subtitle/keywords/description diffs vs last snapshot), (2) feature-positioning diff and market-gap synthesis vs Scheduler, (3) a lightweight predictive read ("competitor X is likely pushing into vertical Y based on metadata + ads + backlink trend"), (4) a single consolidated weekly intelligence brief with prioritized action items. EVERYTHING the agent would publish or persist is approval-gated via a human-in-the-loop interrupt: the GitHub issue #13 comment, the committed markdown report, any Firestore write of LLM-authored analysis (kept in a SEPARATE collection from the deterministic data so it never corrupts the source-of-truth), and any Slack/email digest. The agent only calls Claude for reasoning/summarization — no model training/distillation. Hard guardrail enforced in code AND in the system prompt: getsling.com / Sling is a FORBIDDEN acquisition-target competitor — the agent must never fetch, analyze, or publish backlink/ASO analysis about Sling (mirrors the existing `forbidden` flag in moz-competitors.js and the Sling-skip in alerts.js).
graph_steps: load_context (node): read latest competitor_intel run + snapshots + alerts from Firestore via a read-only tool, load aso-config.json competitor roster, load prior LLM-authored brief from the separate competitor_intel_analysis collection for diffing. Filter out forbidden=Sling at the data layer. Populate State.competitors, State.prev_snapshot, State.prev_brief.

fetch_aso_metadata (node): for each non-forbidden competitor, fetch current app-store page metadata (title/subtitle/keywords/short+full description, rating, last-updated) via a bounded WebFetch tool with retries; this is the read-only collection the current pipeline lacks. Errors are non-fatal per-competitor (record error field, continue).

detect_changes (node, deterministic + LLM): diff fetched ASO metadata against prev_snapshot to find concrete metadata changes; merge with the deterministic Moz/ads alerts already in Firestore. LLM classifies each change as MAJOR/MINOR and tags type (keyword-shift, repositioning, pricing-signal, feature-push). Emits State.changes[].

synthesize_brief (node, LLM/Claude): conditional edge — only runs if there are changes OR it's the scheduled weekly run. Produces (a) positioning diff vs Scheduler, (b) formally documented market-gap list, (c) predictive read with confidence, (d) prioritized action items. Output is structured JSON validated against a schema. Self-critique sub-step re-checks the brief for unsupported claims and the Sling exclusion before proceeding.

human_review (interrupt): interrupt() surfaces the rendered brief + the exact side effects it will cause (issue comment text, report file diff, Firestore analysis doc, optional Slack/email digest). Human resumes with approve / edit / reject. No irreversible action happens before this returns approve.

publish (node, gated): only on approve. Writes the LLM brief to the SEPARATE competitor_intel_analysis Firestore collection (never the deterministic competitor_intel collection), opens a PR (not a direct push) adding the markdown brief under data/seo/reports/, and posts the GitHub issue #13 comment. Each external write wrapped so a partial failure is logged, not silently swallowed.

notify (node, gated): on approve, send the approved digest to the marketing Slack channel and/or an email draft (draft-only for email, per workspace no-send-without-approval rule — the interrupt already counts as approval for the Slack post; email stays a draft). END.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS tier) — Postgres checkpointer + store auto-injected; do NOT pass checkpointer/store to .compile()
Anthropic Claude (Opus/Sonnet via langchain-anthropic) — reasoning/summarization ONLY, no training/distillation

Firestore (firebase-admin) — read competitor_intel / competitor_intel_runs / competitor_intel_alerts (source of truth, read-only); write ONLY to a new competitor_intel_analysis collection for LLM output
WebFetch (bounded, retried) — competitor app-store pages for ASO metadata-change detection
Moz API — consumed indirectly via the data already collected by the existing GitHub Actions pipeline (agent does not re-call Moz)
GitHub API (gh / REST) — open a PR for the markdown brief + comment on tracking issue #13 (no direct push to main)
Slack (incoming webhook or Slack MCP) — approved weekly digest to marketing channel
Gmail MCP create_draft — email digest as DRAFT only (human sends)

Existing repo modules reused as reference: weekly-competitor-intel.js, automation/src/competitors/{moz-competitors,alerts,firestore-push}.js, seo-tools/src/lib/competitor-intel.ts (data model), data/aso-config.json (competitor roster)
LangGraph interrupt() for human-in-the-loop approval gate
approval_gates: Single mandatory human-in-the-loop interrupt() in the human_review node BEFORE any side effect. It blocks ALL irreversible/published actions: (1) GitHub issue #13 comment, (2) PR creation adding the markdown brief (chosen over direct push specifically so it is reviewable/revertible), (3) Firestore write of the LLM brief to competitor_intel_analysis, (4) Slack digest post, (5) email digest (additionally constrained to draft-only — never auto-sent, per the no-send-without-approval rule). Resume options: approve / edit-then-approve / reject. Reads (Firestore reads, Moz-derived data, WebFetch of public app-store pages) are NOT gated. Hard policy guardrail independent of the gate: Sling/getsling.com is excluded from all fetch+analysis+publish at the code level and reasserted in the system prompt. State is checkpointed (managed Postgres) so an unapproved run can sit interrupted across the week and resume on the next human touch without re-running collection.
secrets_needed: GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS_JSON (or service-account JSON for firebase-admin / Firestore project scheduler-systems-web)
GITHUB_TOKEN (PR create + issue comment, scoped to EscalateLabs/marketing)
SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL (or SLACK_BOT_TOKEN if using Slack MCP)
deploy_notes: All secrets injected via the LangGraph Platform UI/API as env vars — none committed (langgraph.json `env` only points to local .env for dev; never commit real keys; complies with workspace no-hardcoded-secrets rule). langgraph.json: dependencies ["."], python_version "3.12", image_distro "wolfi", pip_installer "uv", graphs {"competitive_intel": "./agent/graph.py:graph"}, store.index for semantic recall of past briefs (embed openai:text-embedding-3-small, dims 1536), checkpointer.ttl strategy delete default_ttl ~43200 (30d so a long-interrupted weekly run survives). graph = builder.compile() with NO checkpointer=/store= args (managed platform injects production Postgres). Build State as TypedDict with add_messages reducer + typed fields (competitors, changes, brief, prev_brief). Deploy via `langgraph deploy` CLI from EscalateLabs/marketing (or a dedicated sub-repo) targeting Cloud SaaS control plane api.smith.langchain.com. TIMING: prefer wiring the existing weekly-competitor-intel.yml to webhook-trigger this assistant on success rather than a blind 06:00 cron, to avoid racing the data collector. SCOPE/COMPLIANCE: orchestration-only, calls Claude for analysis — no ML model dev, satisfies the AGENTS.md Anthropic-terms guardrail. Keep LLM output in competitor_intel_analysis (separate from the deterministic competitor_intel source of truth) so a bad brief can never corrupt collected data. Recommended: file/find a GitHub issue in EscalateLabs/marketing to track this build (per always-documented-issue rule) and build in an isolated worktree, not the live marketing checkout. automatable_score 70 — the data-collection half is already automated; the agent automates the analysis/synthesis/alerting half but deliberately keeps a human gate on every publish, so this is assist-not-autopilot by design.
impact: Closes the four named pain points: (1) competitor metadata changes now auto-diffed instead of tracked manually; (2) adds the missing predictive read; (3) market-positioning gaps become a formally documented, versioned artifact (PR'd markdown + Firestore analysis doc); (4) MAJOR-move detection generates an alert/digest the current pipeline can't produce. Consolidates scattered weekly outputs (Firestore + committed report + issue comment) into one reviewed brief. Revenue-relevant only indirectly (marketing for Scheduler, the live-revenue product) — hence P2, not P0/P1; it is intelligence/positioning, not a direct paid-user-facing release. Low risk: read-only against the source-of-truth data, every write gated by a human, forbidden-competitor (Sling) policy enforced in code.

process: Pricing & Positioning Copy — definition and management of the pricing model, value messaging, positioning copy, promotional messaging, discount policy, upgrade paths, and entitlement matrices for Scheduler Core ($750/mo), GAL Ops ($1,500/mo), and Unified Platform ($2,750/mo). Today this is fully manual: copy is hand-written, discount exceptions require manual ADR edits, there is no variant/A-B framework, and pricing/packaging changes are not integrated with product or repo infrastructure (automatable_score 40, so the agent assists and gates — it does not autonomously change live pricing).
trigger: Three entry points, all landing on the same compiled graph (each graph in langgraph.json becomes an addressable assistant): (1) On-demand human request via LangGraph SDK / Agent Server REST run ("draft 3 promo variants for GAL Ops", "validate this 22% discount", "propose entitlement-matrix change") — the primary v1 trigger. (2) GitHub webhook (langgraph.json `webhooks`) when a PR touches model/pricing.md, model/packaging.md, model/icp.md, or decisions/ADR-*.md in Scheduler-Systems/business-ops-admin — agent runs a guardrail-consistency review and posts a draft comment (never an approving review). (3) Scheduled cron run (LangGraph Platform cron) for a weekly positioning/copy drift check against the three plans. No auto-publish on any trigger.
graph_steps: ingest_and_classify: load the canonical model from the read-only repo snapshot (model/pricing.md price architecture + discount policy + guardrails, model/packaging.md entitlement matrix + upgrade path, model/icp.md segments/exclusions, decisions/ADR-0001) into typed state; classify the request intent into {copy_variant_generation, discount_validation, entitlement_change, positioning_drift_check}; pin a content snapshot hash for auditability.

retrieve_context: pull plan-relevant facts via the BaseStore semantic index (configured in langgraph.json store.embed=openai:text-embedding-3-small) — prior approved copy, prior ADR exceptions, ICP segment language, exclusions list — so generated copy never contradicts documented positioning (e.g. never claims on-prem, full-autonomy, or zero-human-checkpoint, which are explicit ICP exclusions).

guardrail_rule_engine: deterministic (non-LLM) validation node. Encodes pricing.md as code: min contract value $750/mo; discount 0-15% = auto-allowed, 16-25% = REQUIRES founder approval, >25% = REQUIRES written exception + ADR update; annual = 10% equivalent. Computes effective price, flags any plan/discount/floor violation, and sets approval_tier. This node decides which interrupt gate fires.

generate_drafts: LLM (Claude via orchestration only — NO model training/distillation) produces the requested artifact as STRUCTURED variants — e.g. 2-3 labelled A/B positioning/promo copy variants per plan, a redlined entitlement-matrix diff, or an upgrade-path message — each annotated with the ICP segment it targets and a self-check that it cites only documented capabilities. Pure generation; writes nothing.

consistency_critic: a second LLM pass plus deterministic checks that cross-validate drafts against the entitlement matrix and exclusions (no over-claiming a capability not in the plan's Included column; promo discounts within guardrails; messaging matches outcome-linked pricing principle). Emits a pass/fail report and required-fix list; loops back to generate_drafts at most N times.

human_review_interrupt (HITL GATE): dynamic interrupt() that surfaces the drafts + guardrail report + diff for human review. ALWAYS fires before any artifact leaves the agent. Human can edit/approve/reject inline (Agent Inbox / SDK resume).

discount_approval_interrupt (HITL GATE, conditional): fires only when approval_tier from the rule engine is founder (16-25%) or exception (>25%). Routes to the correct approver, captures the written justification, and for >25% blocks until an ADR-update intent is confirmed — directly automating the documented manual ADR gate.

draft_adr_and_pr (gated action, NO direct push): only reached after the required interrupts resolve APPROVED. Generates an ADR-000X successor doc (template from decisions/ADR-0000-template.md, referencing ADR-0001) and/or a redlined pricing.md/packaging.md change as a git branch + PR body via the GitHub tool. v1 STOPS at opening a draft PR against business-ops-admin — humans merge. Posting copy to any live surface (site, Brevo, Stripe price object) is OUT of v1 scope and would each be its own future interrupt-gated node.

finalize_and_log: persist the approved variant set, guardrail verdict, approval tier, approver identity, justification, content snapshot hash, and PR URL to the Postgres checkpointer/store for audit; emit run summary. This is the durable record that replaces today's manual ADR-update trail.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS tier, Agent Server runtime) — platform-injected Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore; do NOT pass own checkpointer/store to .compile()

langgraph.json: graphs={pricing_copilot: ./agent/graph.py:graph}, python_version 3.13, image_distro wolfi, store.index.embed=openai:text-embedding-3-small dims 1536, checkpointer.ttl, webhooks for the GitHub trigger, auth.path for per-caller authz
Anthropic Claude API via langchain-anthropic (Opus for copy generation + critic, Sonnet for the cheap classify/consistency pass) — orchestration/inference only, no fine-tune/eval/distillation

GitHub via PyGithub or gh REST as a LangGraph tool — read model/*.md + decisions/ADR-*.md from Scheduler-Systems/business-ops-admin; write = open DRAFT PR + non-approving review comment ONLY (HITL-gated)
GitHub webhook source -> langgraph.json webhooks (PR-touches-model-files trigger)
LangGraph human-in-the-loop interrupt() + Agent Inbox / SDK resume for the two approval gates
LangGraph cron (weekly positioning-drift run)
OpenAI embeddings (store semantic index) — embeddings only
Read-only canonical sources: model/pricing.md, model/packaging.md, model/icp.md, decisions/ADR-0001 (+ ADR-0000 template)
approval_gates: Two mandatory human-in-the-loop interrupts, both implemented as LangGraph interrupt()/resume so nothing irreversible happens without a human. GATE 1 human_review_interrupt: ALWAYS fires before any draft (copy, entitlement diff, ADR text, PR body) leaves the agent — human edits/approves/rejects. GATE 2 discount_approval_interrupt: conditional, driven by the deterministic rule engine encoding pricing.md — 0-15% needs no special gate; 16-25% blocks for FOUNDER approval; >25% blocks for written exception AND confirmed ADR-update intent. The draft_adr_and_pr node is unreachable until the required gate(s) resolve APPROVED, and even then it only opens a DRAFT PR (a human merges). Posting to any live commercial surface (website, Brevo, Stripe, app entitlements) is explicitly out of v1 scope; each such send would be its own future interrupt-gated node. The webhook trigger may only post a non-approving draft comment, never an approving PR review. No autonomous merge, deploy, send, or price change anywhere in v1.
deploy_notes: All secrets injected via the LangGraph Platform deployment UI/API (env), never committed — satisfies the no-hardcoded-secrets rule. Compiled graph must NOT receive a custom checkpointer/store in .compile(); the managed platform provisions Postgres persistence automatically (an InMemorySaver would be silently overridden). Anthropic Terms compliance: this agent only orchestrates and calls Claude/OpenAI for inference + embeddings — it performs NO model training, fine-tuning, evaluation, or distillation, so it is permitted application/orchestration work. Deploy from the project root containing langgraph.json + pyproject.toml via `langgraph deploy` (CLI) or the LangSmith Deployments UI; pick region (api. vs eu.). v1 GitHub scope is intentionally write-limited to draft PRs and draft comments against Scheduler-Systems/business-ops-admin (main is the default branch) — give the GITHUB_TOKEN repo scope but rely on branch protection + the HITL gates, not token power, for safety. The agent's deterministic guardrail node is the single source of discount-policy truth and must be unit-tested against pricing.md before first run. Out of scope for v1 (explicitly): dynamic pricing, live A/B serving infrastructure, and any write to Stripe/Brevo/website — v1 only generates governed variants + scaffolds the ADR/PR that humans approve and merge. business-ops-admin is a clean checkout on main; do all real PR work from an isolated worktree per workspace worktree-discipline rules, not the live checkout.
impact: Attacks all four stated pain points without touching live revenue. (1) No A/B framework -> structured, ICP-tagged copy/promo VARIANTS become the agent's default output, giving the team a variant library to test even before serving infra exists. (2) Discount approval requires manual ADR updates -> the deterministic rule engine + discount_approval_interrupt enforce the 0-15/16-25/>25 tiers automatically and auto-scaffold the ADR-000X successor + PR, converting a manual, error-prone, often-skipped step into a gated, audited one. (3) No variant testing for positioning -> consistency_critic guarantees variants never contradict the entitlement matrix or ICP exclusions (a real risk today, e.g. the directory listing already over-claims time-tracking/AI per workspace memory). (4) Pricing/packaging changes not integrated with infra -> webhook trigger makes any PR touching model/*.md get an automatic guardrail-consistency review, closing the loop between copy/policy and the repo. Net: faster, consistent, auditable pricing/positioning artifacts with founder control preserved at every irreversible step; protects the documented commercial guardrails that underpin Scheduler/GAL revenue.

trigger: Primary: LangGraph Platform Cron (scheduled assistant run) at 07:00 ET Mon-Fri for the daily attribution pull, plus a weekly Monday 08:00 ET run that produces the full multi-touch report. Secondary: on-demand HTTP invoke (POST /threads/{id}/runs against the deployment) from a Slack slash-command or the growth-CLI for ad-hoc "pull attribution for date range X" requests. The cron config is stored in langgraph.json-adjacent platform Cron, not in app code. Each run starts a fresh thread keyed by run-date so the Postgres checkpointer gives idempotent resume on transient API failures.
process: Attribution & Campaign Tracking — a recurring multi-channel measurement loop that (1) ingests conversion/engagement data from Google Ads, GA4, Brevo, and the growth-CLI campaign tracking JSON; (2) reconciles them against a defined multi-touch attribution model; (3) audits whether tracking is actually live across gal.run, gal-run.io, and app.scheduler-systems.com; and (4) drafts a human-reviewable attribution report + a punch-list of setup gaps. v1 deliberately scopes to READ + DRAFT. It does NOT auto-install gtag.js/GTM containers, does NOT create/edit Google Ads conversion actions, does NOT push events into Brevo, and does NOT send any report — all of those are approval-gated or explicitly out of scope for v1. The agent is an orchestration graph that calls Claude only for reasoning/normalization/report-drafting (never for any model training/eval/distillation), and reaches external systems via read-scoped API tools. This addresses the stated pain points by: detecting tags-not-installed per domain (tag-firing audit node), flagging missing cross-domain Conversion Linker, reporting which Brevo events are/aren't wired, and computing attribution under a first concrete model (position-based 40/20/40 over a 30-day lookback) so the team has a defined model instead of none. Tag installation and GTM container changes are surfaced as a structured remediation checklist for a human to execute, since editing infra is a high-risk irreversible action and several domains (gal.run / gal-run.io) sit outside this repo's deploy control.
graph_steps: START -> load_config: read run window (date range, lookback), the channel registry, and the multi-touch model definition from env + a Firestore/JSON config doc (no secrets in code). Validate that required read-only credentials are present; if a credential is missing, mark that channel degraded and continue (fail-soft per channel, never abort the whole run).

ingest_fan_out (parallel branches, each fail-soft and independently checkpointed): (a) google_ads_pull -> read conversions + conversion-action status via Google Ads API read scope; (b) ga4_pull -> read key events + traffic source / session-source-medium dimensions via GA4 Data API; (c) brevo_pull -> read campaign open/click/conversion metrics + which event tracking is configured via Brevo API; (d) growth_cli_pull -> read the campaign tracking JSON / call growth-CLI read tools (list campaigns, statuses). Each branch normalizes to a common touchpoint schema {channel, campaign, utm, timestamp, identity_hint, conversion_flag, value}.

tag_health_audit: for each domain (gal.run, gal-run.io, app.scheduler-systems.com) verify tracking is live read-only — fetch the page and inspect for gtag.js / GTM container id / Conversion Linker; cross-check GA4 'is data arriving from this hostname in the last 24h' and Google Ads conversion-action 'recording conversions' status. Output a per-domain {tag_present, gtm_present, conversion_linker_present, data_flowing} matrix. This is the concrete check for the 'tags not installed / cross-domain not set up' pain point.

reconcile_identities: Claude-assisted normalization node — collapse UTM variants, map campaign aliases across Ads/GA4/Brevo/growth-CLI to canonical campaign ids, and stitch touchpoints into per-conversion journeys using available identity hints (gclid, GA4 client_id surrogate, UTM). Deterministic rules first; Claude only resolves ambiguous campaign-name/UTM matches. No PII leaves the run; identity hints are hashed.

apply_attribution_model: compute credit per channel/campaign under the v1 model (position-based 40/20/40, 30-day lookback) AND a last-click baseline for comparison. Emit per-channel spend (Ads), attributed conversions, attributed value, CAC, and model-vs-last-click delta. Flag low-confidence journeys (single-touch-only, missing-source).

synthesize_report (Claude): draft (i) the attribution summary report (channel ROI table, week-over-week deltas, model comparison), and (ii) a prioritized remediation punch-list derived from tag_health_audit + degraded channels + missing-event findings (e.g. 'install Conversion Linker in GTM on gal-run.io', 'wire Brevo purchase event'). Output is structured (report.md draft + checklist.json), written to the run's artifact store — NOT sent anywhere.

human_approval_gate (interrupt): pause the graph with interrupt() and present the drafted report + checklist + the exact list of any side-effecting actions the human could authorize (send report to Slack/email, open growth-CLI/GitHub issues for the remediation items, write metrics row back to the tracking JSON). The run resumes only on an explicit human resume command carrying an approval decision per action. Default on no-approval = persist drafts only.

gated_actions (only the actions the human explicitly approved, each individually re-checked): post/send report, create remediation issues, append the computed attribution snapshot to the campaign-tracking store. Every action is idempotent (keyed by run-date) and logged. END.

on_error / degraded path: any node may route here to record a partial result + structured failure note in the report ('Google Ads pull failed: <reason>, figures exclude paid'), so the run always produces a reviewable artifact rather than crashing.
tools_integrations: Google Ads API (read-only: conversions, conversion-action status, gclid)
Google Analytics 4 Data API (read-only: key events, session source/medium, hostname, remarketing-audience status)
Google Tag Manager API (read-only in v1: list containers/tags/Conversion Linker to verify config)
Brevo API (read-only: campaign metrics + configured event-tracking inventory)
growth-CLI (existing @scheduler-systems/growth-cli MCP/HTTP read tools + campaign tracking JSON in EscalateLabs/marketing)
HTTP fetch tool (per-domain gtag.js/GTM tag-firing audit on gal.run, gal-run.io, app.scheduler-systems.com)
Anthropic Claude (orchestration reasoning only: UTM/campaign normalization + report drafting — NO model training/eval/distillation)
LangGraph Platform managed Postgres checkpointer + store (auto-injected; do NOT pass a custom checkpointer to .compile())
Slack or email (DRAFT/send only behind approval gate)
GitHub Issues / growth-CLI job creation (remediation punch-list items — behind approval gate)
secrets_needed: GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS_JSON (GA4 Data API + GTM API read service account)
ATTRIBUTION_CONFIG_DOC (Firestore path / JSON path for model + channel registry — value, not a secret)
approval_gates: ["HARD interrupt() before ANY send: the drafted attribution report is never delivered to Slack/email without explicit per-run human approval; default-on-no-response is persist-draft-only.", "HARD interrupt() before creating remediation GitHub issues / growth-CLI jobs from the punch-list — human selects which items to file.", "HARD interrupt() before writing the computed attribution snapshot back to the campaign-tracking JSON / Firestore (idempotent by run-date, but still a write -> gated).", "SCOPE GATE (v1 = read-only for infra): the agent NEVER auto-installs gtag.js, NEVER creates/edits GTM tags or containers, NEVER creates/edits Google Ads conversion actions, NEVER pushes Brevo events. These remain human-executed; the agent only produces the checklist. (GTM API write scope is intentionally excluded from v1 credentials.)", "SECRETS GATE: all credentials injected via LangGraph Platform env/secret manager; none committed to langgraph.json or repo. Read scopes only for the data-pull credentials.", "TERMS GATE: Claude is used strictly for orchestration/normalization/drafting — no model training, evaluation, fine-tuning, or distillation occurs anywhere in the graph."]
deploy_notes: Deploy to LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments, managed Cloud SaaS, region us unless EU residency is required). langgraph.json at the deployment repo root: {"$schema":"https://langgra.ph/schema.json","dependencies":["."],"graphs":{"attribution":"./agent/graph.py:graph"},"env":".env","python_version":"3.12","image_distro":"wolfi","pip_installer":"uv","checkpointer":{"ttl":{"strategy":"delete","default_ttl":43200}},"store":{"ttl":{"default_ttl":10080,"sweep_interval_minutes":60}}}. Build the StateGraph with input_schema (date range + on-demand overrides), a shared touchpoint-list reducer state, and context_schema for the active attribution model; compile WITHOUT passing checkpointer=/store= (the platform injects managed Postgres). Export module-level `graph = builder.compile()`. The human_approval_gate uses langgraph.interrupt() so it resumes cleanly via the platform's resume API after a human approval payload. Schedule the two recurring runs with Platform Cron (daily 07:00 ET pull, weekly Mon 08:00 ET full report); on-demand triggers hit POST /threads/runs. Secrets set via the Deployments UI/API, never in .env-committed. Put the deployment repo under StratusCloudLabs or a marketing-owned repo with read-scoped service accounts; gal.run / gal-run.io tag installation (the actual remediation) is executed by a human via GTM since those domains are outside this repo's deploy control. Suggested rollout: ship read-only ingest + tag_health_audit + draft-only first (no gated_actions wired), validate the attribution numbers against a manual week, then enable the approval-gated send/issue/write actions one at a time. Define the multi-touch model in the config doc (start position-based 40/20/40, 30-day lookback) so it's tunable without redeploying.
impact: Closes the loop on the stated current_state (tracking documented but not deployed, no defined attribution model). Gives the team a defined, tunable multi-touch model (vs none today), a daily per-domain proof of whether tags are actually firing on gal.run / gal-run.io / app.scheduler-systems.com, a reconciled cross-channel ROI/CAC view across Ads+GA4+Brevo+growth-CLI, and an actionable remediation punch-list for the cross-domain GTM + Brevo-event gaps — all human-reviewed before anything is sent or written. automatable_score 55 reflects the hard boundary: measurement/reporting is highly automatable, but the actual tag/GTM/conversion-action installation stays human-executed and approval-gated.

process: Directory & Backlink Acquisition — automated submission to startup/software directories (BetaList, BetaPage, LaunchingNext, StartupRanking, AlternativeTo, + 100 tracked) with CAPTCHA handling, email-verification polling, and backlink upkeep, fronted by the Growth Console and backed by the existing Playwright + 2Captcha automation in EscalateLabs/marketing/automation.
trigger: Three trigger surfaces, all hitting the same compiled graph (one assistant): (1) LangGraph Cron — a scheduled "upkeep + safe background" sweep (crawl/recheck/poll/drift-scan) every 6h, equivalent to today's cron/workflow_dispatch; (2) Growth Console button — the canonical execution surface POSTs a Runs API request ({mode:"submit"|"recheck"|"resume", directory|batch, sheetName}) per the documented migration path (Growth Console -> GAL background agent -> dispatchDirectorySubmit); (3) Resume webhook — Gmail push / poll resolution and human-approval decisions resume an interrupted thread via Runs API with Command(resume=...). The graph is the stable contract; only the trigger changes.
graph_steps: load_work (node): read scriptable rows from Firestore seo_backlinks via getScriptableRows({sheetName, directory}); also reconcile against /data/backlink-targets.json tier/difficulty. Emit per-target work items into State. Read-only, no approval.

classify_route (conditional edge): for each target, branch on intent. Read-only/idempotent (status recheck, crawl, email poll, metadata-drift scan, 2Captcha balance check) -> safe_background lane.
