Irreversible (new submission, account creation, paid listing, verification-link click that activates, ownership/account migration) -> guarded lane.

safe_background (node): run GAL-safe jobs — recheck listing live-status, crawl backlink presence, scan metadata drift (compare live listing vs canonical COMPANY_INFO), check verification status. Writes only evidenceNote + metadataDrift flags via writeRunResult (never runState=completed). No approval.

captcha_solve (node): on submission lane, detectCaptcha -> tier escalation: stealth (no-op) -> OAuth login -> 2Captcha solve (recaptcha_v2/v3, hcaptcha, turnstile). On audio-challenge fallback or unsolved/no-API-key -> set needsHuman and route to human_gate.

draft_submission (node): assemble the submission payload (form fields from COMPANY_INFO + per-directory submission_details) WITHOUT submitting. Produce a diff/preview artifact. LLM (Claude) only fills/normalizes copy + maps categories — never trains/evaluates a model.

human_gate (interrupt): interrupt() the graph for any irreversible/risky action — final submit/publish, paid listing (BetaList $29/$299/$999), ownership transfer / account migration (personal -> dev@scheduler-systems.com), verification-link click that activates an account, and audio-CAPTCHA-or-unsolved-CAPTCHA handoff. Surface preview + cost + directory + credential to Growth Console; await Command(resume=approve|reject|edit).

execute_submit (node): only reached after approval. Call the existing dispatchDirectorySubmit() / Playwright submit() contract through a thin tool wrapper (run on the existing automation runtime, NOT inside the LangGraph container). Capture profileUrl, captcha info, screenshot path.

verify_email (node + interrupt-poll): start email-verification using the Gmail-poll pattern; instead of a blocking 30s loop, schedule a LangGraph Cron/webhook re-entry so the thread parks (interrupt) and resumes when findVerificationEmails returns a link or a max-wait deadline passes. Clicking an activation link is itself approval-gated via human_gate when it activates/claims an account.

write_result (node): writeRunResult(docId,{success,needsHuman,note,screenshotPath}) -> appends evidenceNote arrayUnion, sets metadataDrift=false on success, sets runState=needs_human when blocked; never sets runState=completed (reserved for human/GAL confirm). Mirror to Growth Console Firestore + Google Sheet (non-fatal).
summarize (node): aggregate succeeded/needsHuman/errors, post run summary back to Growth Console + PushNotification on items parked for approval; END.
impact: Removes the polling/orchestration toil that currently blocks the operational bot: durable email-verification polling (no wedged 30s sleeps), automatic resumption, and a single auditable run surface. Marketing keeps the live, working Playwright+2Captcha engine but gains HIL gating on every money/ownership/publish action, so it is safe to leave running. Revenue impact is indirect (SEO/backlink authority, listing freshness) — not a direct paying-user lever — which is why it is P1, not P0: protect Scheduler revenue and direct-cash work first, run this as background upkeep.
approval_gates: Human-in-the-loop interrupt() is MANDATORY before any irreversible/external action: (1) final directory submission/publish; (2) any paid listing — BetaList $29 Hobby / $299 Bootstrapped / $999 Funded, Microlaunch/ItsLaunched paid slots; (3) ownership transfer or account migration off personal accounts (scheduler.sa.dev@gmail.com -> dev@/support@scheduler-systems.com) — flagged in 5+ targets; (4) clicking a verification/activation link that claims or activates an account; (5) audio-CAPTCHA fallback or any unsolved/no-key CAPTCHA -> human handoff queue; (6) metadata-drift remediation that edits a live listing. Approvals arrive as Command(resume=...) from the Growth Console. Safe-background jobs (recheck/crawl/poll/balance/drift-scan, evidenceNote-only writes) run WITHOUT approval. The runner must NEVER write runState=completed autonomously.
tools_integrations: Firestore seo_backlinks (getScriptableRows / writeRunResult) — primary data plane
/data/backlink-targets.json — tier/difficulty/status catalog (100+ tracked)
dispatchDirectorySubmit() — the stable Playwright submission contract (invoked as an external tool, not run in-container)
2Captcha API (recaptcha v2/v3, hcaptcha, turnstile) via existing captcha.js
Gmail API (findVerificationEmails / clickVerificationLink) for email-verification polling
Growth Console (Firestore-backed canonical execution surface) for triggers + approvals + run summaries
Google Sheets (updateGoogleSheet) — non-fatal mirror
Anthropic Claude (orchestration / copy-normalization / category mapping only — NO model training/eval/distillation)
LangGraph Cron (scheduled upkeep + verification re-entry)
LangGraph Runs API + webhooks (Growth Console triggers, resume)
GCP Secret Manager (directory + OAuth + Gmail credentials)
deploy_notes: Target: LangSmith Deployments / LangGraph Platform (managed Cloud SaaS, api.smith.langchain.com). langgraph.json: { "$schema":"https://langgra.ph/schema.json", "dependencies":["."], "graphs":{"agent":"./agent.py:graph"}, "env":".env", "python_version":"3.12", "image_distro":"wolfi", "store":{"ttl":{"default_ttl":43200,"sweep_interval_minutes":60}}, "checkpointer":{"ttl":{"strategy":"delete","default_ttl":43200}}, "auth":{"path":"./auth.py:auth"} }. compile() the StateGraph to module-level `graph` and DO NOT pass checkpointer=/store= — the platform injects production Postgres checkpointer+store; passing a custom saver is ignored/overridden in the hosted runtime. Architecture decision: the LangGraph container does the orchestration, state, interrupts, and Cron re-entry; it does NOT run Playwright/Chromium in-process — Playwright submission is invoked through dispatchDirectorySubmit() running on the existing automation runtime (mirrors the workspace 'orchestrate-local/cluster, execute-on-engine' rule and keeps the heavy headless browser off the managed runtime). Persistence (checkpointer) is what makes the verify_email park-and-resume and human_gate interrupts durable across the multi-day approval/verification latency. Secrets injected via the Deployments UI/API, never committed (per security rules + AGENTS.md no-hardcoded-secrets). Compliance: this agent does orchestration + calls Claude for copy normalization ONLY — it does NOT develop/train/evaluate/distill any ML model, so it stays within Anthropic Consumer Terms (no gal-model involvement). Tie work to a documented GitHub issue before implementation per workspace policy. Idempotency: dedupe on Firestore docId (sheetName--rowNumber) + reuse distributed-lock semantics so Cron and Growth Console triggers can't double-submit the same directory.

process: SEO Keyword Research & Gap Analysis (Marketing)
trigger: Two triggers: (1) Manual/API run via LangGraph Platform `Runs` API or Studio with input {seed_keyword | competitors[], mode: "keyword" | "gap" | "full"} for ad-hoc research. (2) Scheduled weekly cron (LangGraph Platform Cron/Scheduler, Mon 09:00) seeded from the existing tracked keyword set (aso-keywords.json primaries + last week's gap intersect) to refresh trends — matching the current "weekly export capability". Cron runs auto-pause at the approval interrupt; a notification is posted and the run resumes only on approve.
graph_steps: START -> load_context: pull tracked keywords from store (BaseStore namespace 'seo/tracked'), last gap intersect, ASO cross-channel keywords (aso-keywords.json shape), and per-run config; decide mode (keyword | gap | full).

check_budget (conditional gate): read Moz monthly call budget from BaseStore ('seo/budget/moz', 5000/mo @ $250); read Semrush-free daily counter (10/day). If Moz budget exhausted -> route to degrade_mode (free-tools-only, no Moz calls) and flag in report; else proceed. Pure read, no interrupt.

discover_keywords (node, parallel fan-out): Google Autocomplete (free, unlimited) + AnswerThePublic scrape + Moz keyword metrics ONLY for high-priority seeds within budget. Dedup against tracked set. Tool calls wrapped with retry/backoff for Moz 429 rate limits.

competitor_gap (node, runs in gap/full mode): call Moz Links API v2 linking-domains intersect for positive_targets (getsling/wheniwork/deputy/homebase/7shifts) minus negative_target (scheduler-systems.com), mirroring data/moz-request.json; build competitor-vs-us coverage matrix.

classify_and_score (Claude synthesis node): classify search intent (informational/commercial/transactional), compute priority_score (volume*0.3 + difficulty_inverse*0.4 + intent*0.3) for keywords and gap_score (DR*0.4 + competitors_linking*30 + dofollow*20 + relevance*10) for backlinks; categorize Quick Win / Strategic / Competitive / Long-tail and Easy/Medium/Hard.

content_mapping (Claude node): map top keywords to content types + ASO cross-channel overlap (which web keywords also matter for App Store), addressing the 'content-to-keyword mapping not automated' pain point. Produce draft content-calendar + on-page checklist artifacts.
assemble_report (node): write structured JSON (keyword-research-{date}.json / gaps-{date}.json) and markdown (content-calendar, gap-report) to a STAGING area in state only — no external writes yet.

human_review_gate (interrupt): surface the full report + the exact pending writes (Google Sheet rows by tab DIRECTORIES/BACKLINKS/OUTREACH, Firestore docs) and ask approve / edit / reject. Run halts here (checkpointer persists). Resume via Command(resume=...).

publish (conditional, post-approval ONLY): on approve -> upsert to team Google Sheet, write Firestore submission/gap docs, persist new tracked keywords + decrement Moz budget counter in BaseStore, archive report to /data/seo/. On reject -> discard staged writes, log reason. No outreach/email is sent by this agent (outreach targets are emitted as action-items for the separate human-approved outreach flow).
END: return run summary (keywords found, gaps, budget remaining, approval decision, artifact refs).
impact: Automates a 75-automatable, currently Claude-Code-command-driven marketing process into a scheduled, resumable, auditable managed service. Directly attacks the stated pain points: (1) Moz rate-limit/$250-budget burn — a persistent BaseStore budget counter + degrade-to-free-tools routing prevents overspend; (2) incomplete long-tail discovery — parallel free-source fan-out (Autocomplete + ATP) widens coverage without extra Moz cost; (3) un-automated content-to-keyword mapping — a dedicated Claude node produces the mapping + on-page checklist. Frees ~weekly manual effort and gives a clean approval/audit trail. Indirect revenue link only (organic acquisition for Scheduler), so P2 — does not gate paying-user revenue; protect the $250/mo Moz spend is the main cost lever.
tools_integrations: Moz Links API v2 (lsapi.seomoz.com/v2 — linking-domains intersect, keyword/DA metrics; budget-counted, retry+backoff on 429)
Google Autocomplete suggest endpoint (free, unbudgeted)
AnswerThePublic scrape via headless browser/WebFetch (free)
Semrush Free (10/day, optional, daily-counter gated) — read-only metrics
Anthropic Claude API (claude-opus/sonnet) for intent classification, scoring synthesis, content-mapping, report drafting — ORCHESTRATION ONLY, no model training/distillation
Google Sheets API (team visibility sheet, write — APPROVAL-GATED) routed to DIRECTORIES/BACKLINKS/INTEGRATIONS/OUTREACH/APP_STORES tabs
Firestore / Firebase Admin (seo_submission_profiles + gap docs, write — APPROVAL-GATED)
LangGraph Platform managed Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore (auto-provisioned; do NOT pass custom checkpointer/store to .compile())
LangGraph Platform Cron/Scheduler (weekly trigger)
GCS / repo /data/seo/ artifact archive (JSON + markdown history)
secrets_needed: GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS / FIREBASE_ADMIN_CREDENTIALS (Firestore service account, project scheduler-systems-web)
SEMRUSH_FREE_PASSWORD (optional; only if Semrush-free browser path enabled)
SEO_SHEET_ID (team Google Sheet id — config, non-secret but env-injected)
approval_gates: Single mandatory human-in-the-loop interrupt() before ALL external writes (human_review_gate node). It blocks: Google Sheet upserts, Firestore writes, persistence of the new tracked-keyword set, and the Moz-budget decrement — i.e. nothing leaves the agent's staging state until a human approves. Resume model: Command(resume={decision}) where decision ∈ approve | edit(payload) | reject(reason). On scheduled cron runs the interrupt still fires — the run persists via the managed checkpointer and a notification is posted; it executes writes only after approval (never auto-publishes). Outreach/email is explicitly OUT of scope for this agent — it only emits action-items for the separate, independently-approved outreach process (honors workspace 'no send without approval' rule). Secondary safety gates: Moz budget gate (auto-degrade, no human needed) and Semrush daily-limit gate. All secrets via LangGraph Platform env injection — never committed in langgraph.json/.env.
deploy_notes: Deploy as a managed LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments / Cloud SaaS) Python deployment. langgraph.json: {"dependencies":["."], "graphs":{"seo_agent":"./seo_agent/graph.py:graph"}, "env":".env", "python_version":"3.12", "image_distro":"wolfi", "pip_installer":"uv", "store":{"index":{"embed":"openai:text-embedding-3-small","dims":1536}, "ttl":{"default_ttl":43200,"sweep_interval_minutes":60}}, "checkpointer":{"ttl":{"strategy":"delete","default_ttl":43200}}}. graph.py builds a StateGraph(State) with State holding messages (add_messages reducer), config (mode/seed/competitors), staged_artifacts, pending_writes, approval_decision; compiles with builder.compile() and NO custom checkpointer/store (platform injects production Postgres saver + store). human_review_gate uses langgraph.types.interrupt(); resume via Runs API Command(resume=...). Configure the weekly Cron in Platform after first deploy. Inject all secrets through the Platform UI/API (not the committed env). Constraint compliance: orchestration calls Claude only (no training/eval/distillation — satisfies workspace AGENTS.md Anthropic-terms guardrail; this is permitted application code). Real-process refs grounded in: /Users/scheduler-systems/Documents/scheduler-systems-ltd/EscalateLabs/marketing/.claude/commands/seo/seo-keyword-research.md, .../seo-competitor-gaps.md, .../seo-export-sheet.md; data shapes in /Users/scheduler-systems/Documents/scheduler-systems-ltd/EscalateLabs/marketing/data/moz-request.json, .../data/aso-keywords.json, .../data/seo/usage.json; live Moz integration + Firestore write pattern in .../seo-tools/scripts/refresh-moz-gap-artifact.mjs. v1 cut-scope: ship keyword+gap+full modes with Moz + Autocomplete + ATP and the single approval gate; defer the optional Semrush-free browser path and live outreach to v2.

trigger: Three entry points into the same compiled graph (`graphs: {"content_orchestrator": "./src/graph.py:graph"}`): (1) Scheduled cron via LangGraph Platform Crons API / Assistants `schedules` — a "calendar tick" run fires Tue 06:00 EST and Thu 06:00 EST (matching the verified Tue/Thu cadence in data/social-media/calendar-framework.md, NOT the aspirational 3x/day blueprint) to draft the week's due posts; a separate Mon 13:00 EST "calendar-compliance" tick audits whether the week's required slots were filled/published. (2) Webhook/API run (`POST /threads/{id}/runs`) from the blog pipeline when an approved blog post is published, to fan it out into platform-specific social + email drafts. (3) Human-initiated run from the Growth Console "draft these topics" button. All runs are thread-scoped so the Postgres checkpointer persists state across the human-approval interrupt.
process: AI-assisted content generation, cross-platform repurposing, and scheduling for Scheduler/GAL marketing across LinkedIn, X, and email — with a hard human-in-the-loop gate before any irreversible publish/send. The agent is an ORCHESTRATOR only: it calls Claude to draft/repurpose/fact-check copy and decides WHAT to publish and WHEN, but every actual publish/send is delegated to the already-built dispatchers (automation/src/social/publish-dispatcher.js for LinkedIn/X/Facebook via Playwright+GCP Secret Manager; the Brevo/SendGrid email sender) and only after a LangGraph `interrupt()` is resolved with explicit human approval. It does NOT train, fine-tune, distill, or evaluate any ML model — pure LLM-as-tool orchestration, compliant with the workspace Anthropic-terms guardrail. It replaces the manual "fill the queue, hand-write variations, remember Tue/Thu, chase Shay for approval" loop while preserving the existing approval-queue and credential-vault security model.
graph_steps: plan_calendar (node): load calendar-framework.md (Tue=product/technical, Thu=industry-insight, optional Sat=founder), content-pillars.md, and CONTENT-RULES.md from the bundled repo or BaseStore; determine which slots are DUE for this run and which monthly theme is active. For a webhook run, instead read the inbound approved blog post and build a repurpose plan (1 LinkedIn long-form + 1 X thread/single + 1 email blurb). Emits a list of `content_briefs`.

generate_drafts (node): for each brief, call Claude (claude-opus-4 / sonnet for cheaper variants) to produce platform-native copy: LinkedIn (long-form, 8-10AM EST slot), X (<=280 chars, single or thread, 9AM/12PM EST), and email blurb. Inject CONTENT-RULES.md as system constraints. Map post -> target slot datetime. Writes `drafts[]` to state.

fact_check_gate (node): self-critique pass — Claude re-reads each draft against the 6 absolute CONTENT-RULES (no fabricated stories, no unverified metrics/MRR/user counts, no false feature claims, no contradictory positioning, founder=Shay not John Rush, every claim verifiable) plus the verified product-facts block (per-user pricing NOT flat-rate, NOT offline-first). Also runs the dispatcher's hard checks locally: reject any caption containing cms.scheduler-systems.com (must be scheduler-systems.com) and any X draft >280 chars. Flags `violations[]`; clean drafts proceed, flagged ones are rewritten once then escalated.

human_approval_gate (node): call `interrupt({drafts, slots, violations, repurpose_source})` — execution suspends, state persists in the managed Postgres checkpointer. The Growth Console (port 3001) Social Approval Queue renders the payload; Shay approves/edits/rejects per-draft. A push/Slack notification fires. The graph resumes via `Command(resume=...)` carrying the human's per-draft verdicts + any edited captions. THIS IS THE ONLY PATH to publish — no auto-publish branch exists.

route_approved (conditional edge): drop rejected drafts; for approved ones branch on action — `publish_now` if the slot time has passed/is now, else `enqueue_scheduled` to persist to the content queue + register a one-shot resume (Crons API or queue row) at the slot datetime. Edited captions re-enter fact_check_gate's hard checks before dispatch.

dispatch_publish (node): for approved+due drafts, call the existing irreversible dispatchers as tools — NOT reimplemented in the graph. Social: POST to Growth Console which invokes dispatchSocialPublish({platform, draftId, caption}) (Playwright; creds resolved from GCP Secret Manager project marketing-automations-ss, never through the LLM). Email: hand the approved blurb to the Brevo/SendGrid sender. Each call returns structured evidence (postUrl, screenshotPath, success|needsHuman).

record_evidence (node): write per-post outcome (postUrl, screenshot ref, timestamp, slot, approver) back to the Growth Console as audit evidence and to BaseStore for the compliance audit. needsHuman/CAPTCHA/verification results from the dispatcher are surfaced as a fresh interrupt rather than silently retried.

engagement_monitor (node, READ-ONLY): on the compliance/audit tick, pull lightweight engagement + check which required Tue/Thu slots were actually published vs skipped; emit a weekly calendar-compliance summary (the explicit pain point 'weekly content calendar compliance not tracked'). Read-only — proposes catch-up briefs but cannot publish without re-entering the approval gate.

END: graph compiled WITHOUT a custom checkpointer/store (platform injects Postgres). For scheduled slots, the thread parks at a durable interrupt/queue row and resumes at slot time, keeping each publish individually human-gated.
approval_gates: MANDATORY human-in-the-loop via LangGraph `interrupt()` before EVERY irreversible action — no exceptions, no auto-publish branch. (1) Pre-publish per-draft approval in the Growth Console Social Approval Queue: Shay approves/edits/rejects each LinkedIn/X post and email blurb individually; resume via Command(resume). Mirrors the existing rule in publish-dispatcher.js ('MUST only be called after explicit human approval... Never publish without a human-approved draft'). (2) Edited-caption re-validation: any human edit re-runs the hard checks (280-char X limit, no cms. subdomain) before dispatch. (3) Email send approval is a separate gate from social — bulk sends to the list never piggyback on a social approval. (4) Dispatcher escalation gate: any needsHuman/CAPTCHA/identity-verification result re-interrupts for a human instead of retrying. (5) Scheduled-slot release: when a parked scheduled post resumes at slot time, it must still carry a prior human approval verdict; approvals are not open-ended — a re-approval is required if the draft was edited after approval. Read-only engagement/compliance auditing needs no gate; any catch-up content it proposes must pass through gate (1).
tools_integrations: Claude API (Anthropic SDK) — drafting, repurposing, self-critique fact-check; orchestration only, NO model training/distillation
Growth Console API @ http://127.0.0.1:3001 (callGrowthApi, x-api-key) — Social Approval Queue, content queue persistence, evidence store
automation/src/social/publish-dispatcher.js dispatchSocialPublish() — LinkedIn / X / Facebook publish via Playwright (existing, called as a tool, not reimplemented)
Brevo / SendGrid — email sequence + blurb sends (existing email layer in email-templates/ + sender)

GCP Secret Manager project marketing-automations-ss — platform creds (support-linkedin-password, support-twitter-password, support-facebook-password); resolved in the dispatcher runtime ONLY, never surfaced to the LLM
LangGraph Platform Crons / Assistants schedules API — Tue/Thu calendar ticks + Mon compliance tick + scheduled-slot resume
Managed Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore (auto-injected by LangGraph Platform) — thread persistence across interrupts, calendar rules/pillars, compliance history
Repo data sources bundled or in Store: data/social-media/{calendar-framework.md, CONTENT-RULES.md, content-pillars.md}, email-templates/, content queue JSON
Slack/push notification on interrupt (approval requested) and on dispatcher needsHuman escalation
OpenTelemetry tracing (per workspace evals/observability standard) + LangSmith run tracing
secrets_needed: GCP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON (or workload-identity to reach Secret Manager project marketing-automations-ss)
deploy_notes: Deploy to LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments) Cloud SaaS via `langgraph deploy` from a new repo (suggested: EscalateLabs/marketing/automation/langgraph-content or a sibling repo). langgraph.json: {"dependencies":["."],"graphs":{"content_orchestrator":"./src/graph.py:graph"},"python_version":"3.12","image_distro":"wolfi","env":".env","store":{"index":{"embed":"openai:text-embedding-3-small","dims":1536}},"checkpointer":{"ttl":{"strategy":"delete","default_ttl":43200}}}. CRITICAL: compile the StateGraph WITHOUT passing checkpointer=/store= — the platform injects production Postgres + Store; passing InMemorySaver would be ignored/overridden in the hosted runtime. State schema uses add_messages reducer plus typed fields (content_briefs, drafts, violations, approvals, slots, repurpose_source, compliance_summary). Inject real secrets via the Deployments UI/API, never commit them (.env in repo holds only non-secret defaults; satisfies workspace 'never hardcode secrets'). The Playwright publish-dispatcher runs OUTSIDE the LangGraph container (it needs a browser + GCP Secret Manager) — the graph calls it via the Growth Console API over the network, keeping the deployed agent a thin orchestrator and keeping heavy/irreversible execution on existing infra (consistent with the workspace 'orchestrate local, execute on cluster' principle). Always tie work to a documented GitHub issue (workspace rule) before building. v1 scope: LinkedIn + X + email only (Facebook supported by the dispatcher but out of v1 calendar); blog auto-publish stays in the existing blog pipeline and only triggers this agent's repurpose path — full blog-publishing automation remains a separate pain-point item. Phase the rollout: ship draft+approve+manual-dispatch first (highest safety), enable scheduled-slot auto-resume only after the approval loop is proven in production.
impact: Closes the documented pain points that are within an orchestrator's reach: (1) 'content approval workflow needs UI' — the interrupt() gate wires directly into the existing Growth Console Social Approval Queue with per-draft approve/edit/reject. (2) 'weekly content calendar compliance not tracked' — the Mon compliance tick + BaseStore history produce a weekly filled-vs-published audit against the Tue/Thu framework. (3) Generation + cross-platform repurposing (blog -> LinkedIn/X/email) automated with platform-native variants, replacing hand-written variations. (4) Enforces CONTENT-RULES factual guardrails programmatically (no unverified metrics, no false feature claims, correct per-user pricing, scheduler-systems.com not cms.) before a human ever sees the draft, reducing review time toward the blueprint's 5-10 hrs/week target. Does NOT fix the underlying API-access pain points (LinkedIn API limited, X elevated tier, blog publish not automated) — by design it routes around them by orchestrating the existing Playwright+Secret-Manager dispatcher rather than requiring official posting APIs. Revenue link: this is EscalateLabs marketing (a slow/indirect lever per workspace revenue-first guidance), so it is P1 not P0 — protect Scheduler's live revenue and the PLG wedge first; time-box this against direct monetization work.

process: Cold Outreach & Prospect Research
trigger:
Three entry points, all landing on the same compiled graph (`agent`) deployed to LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS):
1. Scheduled cron (LangGraph `crons` API) — daily Tue/Wed/Thu 08:00 recipient-TZ "campaign tick": picks up the next batch of due prospects/follow-ups for an active campaign and runs them to the approval interrupt.
2. On-demand API/SDK invocation — operator runs a campaign by thread (e.g. `{"campaign_type":"partnerships|reviews|guestposts","mode":"research|draft|followup"}`) from a thin internal UI or `curl`/SDK against the deployment.
3. Resume-after-approval — a human resolves the interrupt (approve / edit / reject) via the SDK/Agent-Inbox, which resumes the same thread from its checkpoint. No send ever happens without this step.
graph_steps: ingest_request — normalize the run input (campaign_type, mode, batch_size, dry_run default true) into typed State; load active-campaign config and per-channel daily counters from the LangGraph Store (BaseStore, namespaced by campaign). No network sends here.

load_prospects — read prospects from /data/outreach/* (mounted/synced) and Brevo contact lists (read-only GET) for the campaign; dedupe against the Store's already-contacted index; emit a candidate list. Marketing/research/enrichment only — never trains or scores any ML model (orchestration calls Claude only).

research_enrich (fan-out, mode=research) — for each candidate, a Send()/map step gathers personalization hooks via WebSearch + read-only page fetch + Brevo engagement logs (opens/clicks/replies). Writes enrichment + hooks back to the prospect record. Read-only; no outbound contact.

lead_score — deterministic, rule-based scoring node (NOT an ML model): ICP fit (company size 10-100, industry), engagement recency/frequency from logs, reply sentiment, source quality. Produces a 0-100 score + tier (A/B/C) used only to prioritize and route. Pure Python + Claude for sentiment tagging of existing replies.

compliance_gate — automated CAN-SPAM / GDPR / CASL precheck BEFORE any draft is shown: verify suppression/opt-out list (Store), require physical address + unsubscribe in template, block EU contacts lacking lawful basis, enforce per-channel daily caps and quiet-hours (no sends 22:00-07:00 recipient TZ). Hard-fails route the prospect to a 'needs_review' bucket, never to send.

draft_personalize — select template (A partnership / B review / C guest-post / D value-first, or follow-up step 1-4) and personalize with Claude using the enrichment hooks; produce subject + body + channel (Brevo email or LinkedIn-profile note). Output is a DRAFT object only.

human_approval (interrupt) — call `interrupt()` to pause the graph and surface the batch of drafts (recipient, channel, subject, body, score, compliance status) to the human via Agent-Inbox/SDK. This is the mandatory HITL gate for every send/publish/irreversible action. Operator can approve, edit-then-approve, or reject per item.

execute_sends (post-resume, only on explicit approve, only if dry_run=false) — for approved items: send via Brevo transactional/campaign API (email) with 2-5 min jittered delay; LinkedIn/social actions stay manual-assist (agent prepares the note + profile URL; human performs the action) to respect the stealth/automation pain point. Records send to Store + outreach-log.

schedule_followups — for sent items, compute the next follow-up step + due date (Day 5 / 12 / 20 email cadence; Day 3 / 7 / 14 social) and persist to the Store so the next cron tick picks them up. Updates prospect status (new -> contacted -> following_up -> replied/closed).

log_and_report — write per-prospect outcomes, campaign metrics (sent/opened/clicked/replied/positive/meetings), and decrement daily counters back to the Store and /data/outreach/reports/. Emit a run summary in State; END.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS) — runtime, auto-provisioned Postgres checkpointer + Store, crons API
Anthropic Claude (via langchain-anthropic) — the only LLM, used for personalization + reply-sentiment tagging; orchestration only, no model training/distillation
Brevo API — read contact lists + engagement logs; send transactional/campaign emails (gated behind approval)
WebSearch + read-only HTTP fetch — prospect research & personalization hooks
/data/outreach/* filesystem (prospects, templates, outreach-log, reports) synced into the deployment
LangGraph BaseStore — suppression/opt-out list, already-contacted index, per-channel daily counters, follow-up queue, campaign config
Agent Inbox / LangGraph SDK — human-in-the-loop approval surface for the interrupt()
LinkedIn / Reddit / Twitter — manual-assist only in v1 (agent drafts; human performs the action) to avoid the unsolved stealth-automation pain point
Custom Auth (langgraph.json auth.path) — restrict the deployment to the marketing operator(s)
approval_gates: Single mandatory HITL gate via `interrupt()` at `human_approval`, before any send/publish/irreversible action. Nothing in `execute_sends` runs until the human resumes the thread with an explicit per-item APPROVE (edit-then-approve allowed; reject drops the item). Defense in depth: (1) `dry_run` defaults TRUE so an un-resumed or mis-triggered run never sends; (2) `compliance_gate` runs BEFORE drafts are shown and hard-blocks opt-outs, missing unsubscribe/address, EU-without-basis, over-cap, and quiet-hours; (3) LinkedIn/social actions are manual-assist (agent never auto-acts on social) given the stealth-plugin pain point; (4) per-channel daily caps + jittered delays enforced in code, not by the model. Read/research/draft steps need no approval; only outbound contact and follow-up scheduling that leads to a send are gated.
impact: Automatable score 65: research, enrichment, deterministic lead scoring, compliance precheck, personalization, and follow-up bookkeeping are fully automated, while every actual send/publish stays human-gated. Directly serves the revenue-first priority by feeding the partnership/review/guest-post pipeline that drives Scheduler installs, and formalizes the currently-manual GDPR/CAN-SPAM checks and follow-up cadence. Realistic v1 win: collapses the per-batch operator effort to approve/edit-only, with the agent doing the prep. Net-new pain points still NOT solved in v1 (call out explicitly): LinkedIn/social automation remains manual-assist (no stealth plugin), and email reputation/warm-up is enforced only via caps + delays, not a managed warming service.
deploy_notes: Managed deploy, no model dev (Anthropic-Terms compliant — orchestration calls Claude only). langgraph.json: {"dependencies":["."],"graphs":{"agent":"./outreach_agent/agent.py:graph"},"env":".env","python_version":"3.12","image_distro":"wolfi","pip_installer":"uv","auth":{"path":"./auth.py:auth"},"store":{"ttl":{"default_ttl":525600,"sweep_interval_minutes":120}},"checkpointer":{"ttl":{"strategy":"delete","default_ttl":43200}}}. CRITICAL: compile with `builder.compile()` and assign module-level `graph` — do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= (platform injects Postgres versions; passing InMemorySaver is local-only and ignored in prod). Use interrupt()/Command(resume=...) for HITL, not a custom REST loop. Secrets injected via the LangSmith Deployments UI/API (env in repo is placeholder only) — never hardcode (security rule). Create the daily run via the crons API after first deploy; keep dry_run=true until a human has validated one full batch end-to-end. Suggested layout: outreach_agent/{agent.py,state.py,nodes/,tools/}, auth.py, langgraph.json, pyproject.toml. v1 scope cap: email sends through Brevo only; social stays manual-assist. Tie work to a documented GitHub issue in EscalateLabs/marketing before building, and develop in a worktree, not the live checkout.

trigger:
Three deployment-native triggers on LangGraph Platform (Agent Server), no local subprocess fleet:
1. API/UI kickoff: POST to the deployment's /threads/{id}/runs with input {campaign_name, product_profile_ref, channels[], duration_days, start_date}. This is the primary path — a human starts a named campaign from a dashboard or the LangSmith Studio UI.
2. Scheduled daily tick: a LangGraph Platform Cron (built-in scheduler, not local launchd) fires once/day at a configured hour to advance the active campaign's per-day plan (directory batch, social batch, outreach batch, PH warmup). Cron resumes the SAME persisted thread so day-N state carries forward.
3. Resume-after-approval: an external action (the human approving/editing a batch in the inbox/dashboard) POSTs a Command(resume=...) to the interrupted run. This is how every gated batch continues.
Idempotency: campaign_name is the thread business-key; the orchestrator is resumable and never re-submits an item already marked done in the tracking store.
process:
Campaign Blitz Execution — John Rush's 13-step launch framework, refactored from the one-shot /marketing-blitz Claude Code command into a long-running, resumable, multi-day LangGraph deployment on LangGraph Platform (managed/Cloud SaaS tier).

WHAT CHANGES vs the command: the markdown command is a single imperative run that does everything inline (Playwright submits, Gmail sends, Twitter posts) with rate-limit prose. The deployed agent instead (a) holds durable campaign state in the platform's auto-provisioned Postgres checkpointer keyed by campaign_name thread, (b) drafts every batch of external actions and HALTS on a human-in-the-loop interrupt before any send/submit/publish, (c) advances day-by-day via the platform Cron so a 14-day campaign is dozens of short governed ticks rather than one mega-run, and (d) writes structured tracking + attribution to the long-term BaseStore so the missing real-time dashboard and post-campaign analytics become queryable instead of manually compiled.

LLM ROLE (allowed): Claude is used only for orchestration and content generation — composing directory submission field values, ProductHunt tagline/first-comment/maker-intro, per-channel social copy, personalized cold-email first lines, classifying which subreddits/threads are genuinely on-topic, and summarizing daily metrics. NO model training/eval/distillation; this is pure application orchestration calling an LLM, which is permitted.

GOVERNANCE (hard): every node that would touch the outside world (directory form submit, PH launch post, tweet/LinkedIn/Reddit post, DM, cold email, review request) does NOT act — it emits a draft batch and raises interrupt(). The run sits in 'interrupted' status until a human approves/edits/rejects via Command(resume). This satisfies the constraint that any send/publish/irreversible action is approval-gated, and the user's standing 'no send without approval' + 'no delete without approval' rules. Rejected/edited items flow back through draft regeneration. Rate limits (3 submissions/hr, 50 emails/day, 30-40 Twitter, 20-30 LinkedIn, 2-3 Reddit/day) are enforced in-graph as state counters AND as the natural cadence of one approval batch per Cron tick.

STATE (TypedDict, persisted): campaign_name, day_index, phase, product_profile, channel_targets, per-channel cursors (directories_done[], directories_pending[], outreach_sent[], social_posted[]), today_drafts (the batch awaiting approval), approval_decisions, rate_counters {date->channel->count}, metrics_log[], ph_launch_date, errors[]. Use add-style reducers on the list fields so Cron ticks append rather than clobber.

OUTPUTS: structured tracking equivalent to the command's campaigns/{name}/*.json (directories-submitted, social-engagement, outreach-log, metrics, leads) but living in Postgres/Store and exposed via the deployment API for a live dashboard; plus an end-of-campaign performance + per-channel attribution report generated by the analytics node.
graph_steps: START -> load_or_init_campaign: read thread state from checkpointer; if new, init State from run input (campaign_name, product_profile, channels, duration, start/PH date); if resuming a Cron tick, increment day_index. Reject if required product assets/params missing (current_state requires campaign prep).

plan_today: deterministic router that, from day_index + phase + per-channel cursors + rate_counters, computes today's eligible work batch sizes per channel (e.g. <=10-15 directories, <=50 emails, <=30-40 Twitter, PH warmup vs PH launch-day vs post-launch). Pure logic, no LLM, no side effects.

draft_directory_batch: LLM composes submission field values for the next N pending directories from product_profile; produces a structured proposed_batch. No submission yet. (Skipped on days with no directory budget.)

draft_producthunt_assets: on PH-prep days LLM drafts tagline(<=60c)/first-comment/maker-intro/hunter-DM templates; on PH launch day prepares the launch post + coordinated cross-channel push payloads. Solves the 'PH timing coordination manual' pain by encoding the launch-day fan-out as one reviewable batch.
draft_social_batch: LLM composes the day's Twitter/LinkedIn/Reddit/IndieHackers posts + reply suggestions for on-topic threads it classified; tags each with target channel and rate-bucket.
draft_outreach_batch: LLM builds personalized cold-email / review-request / guest-post / integration-partner messages (first-line personalization) for the day's prospect slice, under the 50/day cap.
assemble_approval_packet: merge all drafted batches into one human-readable packet (channel, count, content, rate-budget remaining, links). Single review surface per tick.

HUMAN_GATE (interrupt): raise interrupt(approval_packet). Run enters 'interrupted'. Human approves all / edits items / rejects items / defers, then POSTs Command(resume=decisions). REQUIRED before any external action — this is the approval gate for all sends/submits/publishes.

apply_approved_actions: ONLY for approved items, call the side-effecting tools (directory-submit, email-send, social-post, DM, PH-post) sequentially with in-node sleep/rate-pacing; mark each item done in cursors; increment rate_counters; capture per-item success/pending/failed. CAPTCHA/bounce/suspension -> flag for manual + log, never auto-retry destructively.

record_tracking: append results to metrics_log, outreach_sent, directories_done, social_posted in Store + checkpointer (the live-dashboard data source). Handle edited/rejected items: edited -> re-queue to next tick's draft; rejected -> mark skipped.
advance_or_finish: if day_index < duration_days -> END this tick (state persisted; next Cron tick resumes); if final day -> route to analytics.

analytics_and_report: LLM-summarized end-of-campaign report — traffic/signups vs Rush benchmarks, per-channel attribution (the missing cross-channel attribution), top tactics, ROI per channel, lessons learned; persist + expose via API; archive campaign thread. END.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments / Agent Server) — managed Cloud SaaS deployment; Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore auto-provisioned (do NOT pass own checkpointer/store to .compile())
LangGraph Platform Cron — built-in daily scheduler that resumes the campaign thread (replaces any local launchd/cron; aligns with 'orchestrate local, execute on platform')
langgraph.json deploy config (graphs: campaign-blitz; python 3.12; image_distro wolfi; store.index for semantic prospect/thread dedupe; checkpointer.ttl; http app for the dashboard/approval API)
Anthropic Claude (via API key in deployment env) — orchestration + content generation ONLY
Playwright / browser-automation MCP — directory submissions, ProductHunt posting, social posting/DMs (side-effecting; behind HUMAN_GATE)
Gmail / email-send integration — cold outreach + review requests (behind HUMAN_GATE; respects 50/day)
Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Reddit, IndieHackers connectors — posting + engagement (behind HUMAN_GATE; per-channel rate caps)

Approval surface: LangSmith Studio interrupt UI for v1; optional thin FastAPI mounted via langgraph.json http.app to render the approval packet + collect Command(resume) decisions and serve the live dashboard JSON
backlink-targets.json / directory tier list — seeded as run input or Store-loaded prospect source
LangSmith tracing/observability — built-in run tracing for the missing real-time campaign visibility
secrets_needed: DASHBOARD_API_SHARED_SECRET (auth for the mounted approval/dashboard http app)
approval_gates: SINGLE mandatory human-in-the-loop gate per day-tick, plus an elevated gate on ProductHunt launch day. Mechanism: the assemble_approval_packet node feeds HUMAN_GATE, which calls LangGraph's interrupt() — the run halts in 'interrupted' status and NOTHING external happens until a human POSTs Command(resume=decisions). Decisions are per-item: approve / edit (text returned, re-drafted) / reject (skipped, logged) / defer (re-queued to next tick). Coverage — every irreversible/external action is behind this gate: directory form submissions, ProductHunt post + launch-day cross-channel fan-out, all tweets/LinkedIn/Reddit/IndieHackers posts, all DMs, all cold/review/guest-post emails. Non-gated (auto): reading targets, LLM drafting, rate-counter math, internal tracking writes. ProductHunt launch day requires explicit separate approval of the exact launch timestamp + the coordinated push payload (addresses the manual-timing pain without removing human control). No deletes/archival of campaign data without approval (honors 'no delete without approval'). Hard stop if rate budget would be exceeded — items roll to the next tick rather than auto-overriding limits.
deploy_notes:
v1 scope: ONE product (Scheduler Systems profile from the command file), human approval at every external boundary, daily Cron cadence over a 14-day window, single combined approval packet per tick. Source of truth for content/profile/benchmarks = EscalateLabs/marketing/.claude/commands/marketing/marketing-blitz.md (lines 200-301 hold rate limits, company info, Rush benchmarks).

Deploy path: keep langgraph.json at repo root; graphs map 'campaign-blitz' -> ./blitz/graph.py:graph; compile WITHOUT checkpointer/store (platform injects Postgres). Deploy via `langgraph` CLI to LangGraph Platform (Cloud SaaS, api.smith.langchain.com), inject all secrets via the deployment UI/API — never commit (honors security rules: no hardcoded secrets, read from env). Set image_distro=wolfi, python_version=3.12, store.index embed=openai:text-embedding-3-small for prospect/thread dedupe, checkpointer.ttl long enough to outlive a 14-day campaign. Register the daily Cron against the deployment after first run creates the thread.

COMPLIANCE: this is application/orchestration code only — it calls Claude for content/routing, it does NOT train/fine-tune/eval/distill any model, so it is permitted under the workspace Anthropic-terms guardrail. No gal-model involvement.

WATCH-OUTS for v1: (1) the marketing repo already carries real ToS/anti-spam landmines (G2 biased-collection, no unsubscribe/blocklist in the send path, fake FIRSTNAME merge per workspace memory) — the outreach/review-request batches MUST include unsubscribe handling and a suppression/blocklist check inside apply_approved_actions before any email send, and the human gate is the backstop. (2) Don't run heavy browser automation as a local subprocess fleet; the side-effecting tools should call hosted MCP endpoints from the deployment, keeping the Mac as conductor only. (3) Tie this to a documented GitHub issue in EscalateLabs/marketing before building (standing rule). (4) Confirm the live product claims before any copy goes out — the directory profile historically over-claims time-tracking/AI that Scheduler does not ship; the draft nodes must pull from the verified short/medium/long descriptions only. NOT production-until: a real product-asset prep step + suppression list + a per-channel connector smoke test are in place.

process: ICP & GTM Motion Definition — dynamic lead qualification + buying-trigger detection + marketing-to-sales handoff. Closes the four documented pain points (no dynamic lead qualification automation; handoff criteria not formalized in Brevo; secondary-segment outreach unresourced; buying-trigger detection manual) by turning the static ICP (model/icp.md) and GTM motion (model/gtm-motion.md, ADR-0001/0002) into an executable scoring + routing loop. The agent does NOT redefine the ICP/GTM strategy (that stays human/ADR-governed) — it OPERATIONALIZES the existing definition against live lead data. Automatable score is 45/100, so this is an assist-not-replace design: the agent does the read-heavy enrichment, deterministic ICP fit scoring, segment classification (primary product/eng teams 5-80 eng vs secondary founder-led SMB ops vs excluded), buying-trigger extraction, and drafts the Brevo stage move + sales-handoff note, but every irreversible write (Brevo deal stage change, contact attribute/score write, sales notification, outbound enrollment) is held at a human-in-the-loop interrupt for approval. Honors the documented exclusions (on-prem-only, heavy-regulated pre-pilot compliance, <3 operational owners, no-human-checkpoint expectations) as hard DISQUALIFY gates that short-circuit scoring. Preserves the load-bearing GTM rule: 'Brevo lead scoring/segmentation are automated/hybrid; qualification DECISION remains a human checkpoint.'
trigger: Primary: webhook from Brevo on new contact / contact-into-"Lead captured" stage (LangGraph Platform webhook endpoint -> creates a run with thread_id = brevo_contact_id for idempotency + resumable approval). Secondary: scheduled cron (LangGraph Platform Crons, e.g. every 6h) sweep of un-scored contacts in the "Lead captured" stage to catch any webhook misses and to re-score on new signal. Tertiary: manual invocation from the business-ops-admin operator for a single contact or a batch backfill. Intercom escalation/churn signals are NOT a trigger for this agent (those flow CS->Product per gtm-motion.md); they are read-only enrichment context only.
graph_steps: ingest_lead: normalize the trigger payload into LeadState (contact_id, email, company, role/title, segment hints, source group, raw firmographics). Validate required fields; if the contact is missing identity (no email/company) route to needs_human with reason='insufficient_data'. thread_id is pinned to brevo_contact_id so re-triggers resume the same thread instead of duplicating.

enrich_context: read-only fan-out to gather signal. Pull current Brevo contact + deal state (so we never re-process an already-qualified contact), optional firmographic enrichment, and read-only Intercom history for the domain (existing conversations = engagement signal). Load the live ICP/GTM rubric: fetch model/icp.md + model/gtm-motion.md + ADR-0001/0002 from the Scheduler-Systems/business-ops-admin repo via GitHub read so scoring always reflects the current human-governed definition (no hardcoded ICP).

exclusion_gate: deterministic hard gates BEFORE any LLM scoring. Disqualify if any documented exclusion matches: on-prem-only/isolated-deploy requirement, heavily-regulated buyer needing custom compliance pre-pilot, fewer than 3 operational owners, or buyer expecting full autonomy with no human checkpoint. On match -> set decision='disqualified', skip scoring, go straight to compose_actions (draft a 'Lost/Disqualified' note, no outbound). This protects ICP integrity and saves tokens.

classify_segment: Claude call (orchestration only — NO model training) that maps the lead to exactly one of {primary: product/eng team 5-80 engineers; secondary: founder-led SMB ops on Brevo+Intercom+Morning; out_of_profile}. Returns structured JSON (segment, confidence, evidence_spans citing the firmographic/source fields used). Low confidence -> flag for human in the handoff note rather than guessing.

detect_buying_triggers: Claude call that scans enrichment text (role, company signals, Intercom history, source context) for the ICP-documented buying triggers — repeated delivery delays / coordination drift / incident escalation drift (primary) or rising ops volume + inconsistent SLAs + decision-traceability need (secondary). Outputs a typed list of triggers with evidence + a time-to-value tag aligned to ICP (7-14d primary, 14-30d secondary). This is the 'buying trigger detection' that is currently manual.

score_fit: deterministic, auditable ICP-fit score (0-100) computed in code from the structured outputs (segment match weight, firmographic band fit, source-group fit, trigger strength, engagement). Bands -> action: HIGH = recommend move to 'Qualifying' + sales handoff; MED = recommend nurture/enrich, hold in Lead; LOW = recommend no-action/monitor. Score formula and weights are explicit and logged so a human can audit why — qualification stays explainable, decision stays human.

compose_actions: assemble a single ApprovalPacket: proposed Brevo stage move (Lead captured -> Qualifying, or stay), proposed contact attribute/score writes, proposed sales-handoff note (owner + escalation metadata per gtm-motion.md Sales handoff rule), and — only for the under-resourced secondary segment — a proposed nurture/outreach enrollment draft. All actions are drafts only at this point; nothing has been written.

human_checkpoint: interrupt() — HARD human-in-the-loop gate. Surfaces the full ApprovalPacket (score + evidence + every proposed irreversible action) and waits. Operator can approve, approve-with-edits (edit the note/stage/score), or reject with reason. This is the load-bearing GTM checkpoint: 'qualification decision remains a human checkpoint.' On reject -> record rationale, no writes, end. Resumable via thread_id (Command(resume=...)).

execute_approved: ONLY runs after explicit approval. Applies the approved writes idempotently: Brevo deal stage move + contact attribute/lead-score write, sales-handoff notification, and (if approved) secondary-segment outreach enrollment. Each external write is wrapped with retry + idempotency key = contact_id+action so cron re-sweeps never double-apply. Partial-failure -> route remaining actions back through a bounded retry, surface hard failures to the operator.

audit_log: persist the decision record to LangGraph store + append a structured trail (lead, segment, triggers, score, formula version, ICP/ADR doc commit SHAs used, approver, approved/edited/rejected actions, write results). Feeds the weekly KPI review (kpis/funnel-metrics.md Lead->Qualified conversion) and keeps the automated/hybrid scoring fully traceable for ADR governance.
impact: Directly attacks the highest-leverage documented funnel gap: baseline is 3 new leads/30d with 0 in Qualifying (target >=30 lead, >=10 qualified) and a live guardrail that alerts when leads exist but Qualifying stays 0. Today qualification + buying-trigger detection are 100% manual (owner: Kruti/Shay), so leads stall. This agent makes Lead->Qualified throughput scale without adding headcount (the ICP's own primary buying trigger), formalizes the not-yet-formalized Brevo handoff criteria into an explicit auditable score, and finally gives the under-resourced secondary segment a drafted outreach path. Because it is assist-not-replace (automatable 45/100) with a mandatory human checkpoint, it speeds the read/enrich/score work while keeping the qualification decision — and ICP/GTM strategy — human-governed. Revenue-relevant: faster, consistent qualification is the top of the paid pipeline. NOT a model-training/distillation system — pure orchestration calling Claude for classification/extraction only, which keeps it inside Anthropic terms.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS) — Postgres checkpointer + BaseStore auto-provisioned (do NOT pass own checkpointer/store to .compile()); webhook endpoint + Crons for triggers
Anthropic Claude (Opus/Sonnet) via langchain — classify_segment + detect_buying_triggers ONLY (orchestration/classification, no training/distillation/eval-for-model)
Brevo API — read contact/deal pipeline; write deal stage move (Lead captured->Qualifying), contact attributes/lead-score (APPROVAL-GATED writes)
Intercom MCP/API — READ-ONLY enrichment (existing conversations as engagement signal); not a trigger, no writes
GitHub (Scheduler-Systems/business-ops-admin) — read model/icp.md, model/gtm-motion.md, ADR-0001, ADR-0002 at run time as the live scoring rubric; capture commit SHA for audit
Optional firmographic enrichment provider (read-only) for company-size/role bands
LangGraph store — long-term decision/audit records feeding kpis/funnel-metrics.md weekly review
LangSmith tracing/observability for run + token + decision auditing
approval_gates: Single mandatory human-in-the-loop gate at human_checkpoint via LangGraph interrupt() — NOTHING irreversible executes before explicit approval. Gated actions: (1) Brevo deal stage change Lead captured->Qualifying, (2) Brevo contact attribute/lead-score write, (3) sales-handoff notification, (4) secondary-segment outbound/nurture enrollment. Operator options: approve / approve-with-edits / reject-with-reason; runs are resumable by thread_id=brevo_contact_id via Command(resume=...). Disqualified (exclusion-gate) leads still pass through the checkpoint before any 'Lost/Disqualified' note is written. Enforces the GTM rule that the qualification DECISION is a human checkpoint while scoring/segmentation is automated/hybrid. All external writes are idempotent (key=contact_id+action) so cron re-sweeps and webhook retries cannot double-apply. Secrets via LangGraph Platform env injection only — never committed in langgraph.json/.env.
deploy_notes: Repo layout: langgraph.json at root of a new deploy package (e.g. business-ops-admin/agents/icp-gtm-qualifier/), graphs:{"qualifier":"./qualifier/graph.py:graph"}, python_version 3.12, image_distro wolfi, pip_installer uv, store.index with openai/text-embedding (or anthropic-compatible) for decision recall, checkpointer.ttl set for thread retention. Build graph with StateGraph(LeadState) and .compile() WITHOUT a custom checkpointer/store (platform injects Postgres). Deploy via `langgraph deploy` CLI or the LangSmith Deployments UI against api.smith.langchain.com (EU region eu. if data residency required — note this is a marketing/CRM PII workload, prefer EU). Wire Brevo webhook -> Platform run endpoint (verify BREVO_WEBHOOK_SECRET in ingest_lead). Configure a Cron for the un-scored sweep. Inject all secrets via Platform env, not the repo. Compliance guardrail: this agent must NOT be extended into model training/distillation/eval-for-a-model — keep Claude usage to classification/extraction orchestration only (Anthropic Consumer Terms + workspace AGENTS.md). Tie work to a GitHub issue in business-ops-admin before building (workspace rule). Suggested rollout: shadow mode first (score + draft, auto-reject all writes / log-only) for 1-2 weekly KPI cycles to validate the score formula against human decisions, then enable approval-gated writes. Source files grounding this spec: /Users/scheduler-systems/Documents/scheduler-systems-ltd/Scheduler-Systems/business-ops-admin/model/icp.md, /model/gtm-motion.md, /decisions/ADR-0001-business-model-and-kpi-baseline-q2-2026.md, /decisions/ADR-0002-gtm-motion-q2-2026.md, /kpis/funnel-metrics.md.

process: Product Lifecycle & Distribution Lock
trigger: Three deployed entrypoints on LangGraph Platform (LangSmith Deployments), all hitting the same compiled graph as separate assistants/runs: (1) GitHub webhook -> Agent Server custom HTTP route (langgraph.json `http.app`) on PR opened/synchronize and issue opened/labeled in Scheduler-Systems/business-ops-admin and consumer product repos -> runs work-class + release-ready gate; (2) scheduled cron (LangGraph Platform Crons / external scheduler POSTing to the runs API) weekly to mirror the existing weekly-kpi-export cadence -> runs the operating-review evidence sweep across all products/status/*.json; (3) on-demand human run from LangGraph Studio / SDK ("validate evidence for <product>", "can we release <product>", "is work-class X allowed for <product>"). Input state carries {product_id?, work_class?, issue_url?, pr_number?, repo?, mode: gate|review|release_check}.
graph_steps: ingest_and_route: validate input against State schema (TypedDict, add_messages reducer); classify mode (gate | operating_review | release_check) and resolve product_id/work_class/issue context; reject malformed payloads with a structured error and END.

load_evidence: read the canonical sources read-only -- products/status/<id>.json (or all for review), products/lifecycle.md rules, products/status-page-components.json, data/exports/latest/product-status.json -- via a sandboxed git-read tool (clone/pull business-ops-admin at pinned ref, no write). No LLM here; deterministic load into state.

validate_current_problem: LLM-assisted evidence judge node. For each of the 5 required current_problem links (target user, painful workflow, current workaround, urgency-now, credible-answer) call Claude to assess whether the linked issue/doc actually substantiates the claim; emit per-field valid|weak|missing + rationale + cited link. Pure read/reasoning, no side effects.

validate_demand_distribution: same judge pattern over the 9 distribution_loop fields (segment, early-adopter/dogfood source, primary channel, positioning, conversion event, feedback path, weekly cadence, owner, continue/pivot/stop metric); allow internal-dogfood-only when operator+conversion+cadence are named.
validate_focused_release_and_integrity: judge focused_release (7 fields) and release_integrity (install path, smoke test, checklist, support loop, telemetry); flag waived items explicitly.

derive_lock_and_decision: NO LLM -- call scripts/product-status.mjs as the source of truth via subprocess: `decide --product <id> --work-class <class> --format json` for gate mode, `operating-review --format json` for review, full release-ready criteria for release_check. Reconcile the deterministic engine output (distribution_lock flag, allowed/blocked work classes, gate_flags, recommendation) against the LLM evidence findings; if they disagree, the mjs engine wins and the divergence is recorded as a flag for human review. This keeps the agent consistent with the existing CI gate.

check_override_validity: if an override is present or requested, verify class is in the approved set (production_outage, security_compliance, legal_compliance, infrastructure_integrity, customer_commitment, release_blocker), is time-boxed (expires_at in future), owned, and evidence-linked; reject invalid reasons (future scalability, cleaner architecture, broader agent power, preparing for scale, abstraction preference).

compose_verdict: assemble structured verdict {decision: allow|warn|block, decision_reason, distribution_lock_active, allowed_work_classes, blocked_work_classes, evidence_states, divergences, override_status, release_ready: bool, override_path} plus a human-readable summary. Read-only artifact in state.

human_approval_interrupt: for ANY write/publish/irreversible effect -- posting a PR status check or comment, commenting/labeling a GitHub issue, opening a PR that edits products/status/*.json, or marking release-ready -- call interrupt() (LangGraph dynamic interrupt / interrupt_before) and pause for human approval via Studio/SDK resume. Read-only verdicts returned to a human-initiated run skip this gate; automated webhook/cron runs that would mutate anything always stop here.

apply_effects: only after approved resume -- execute the approved side effects through scoped tools: GitHub App (issue comment, PR check-run/comment via gate, draft PR for status-JSON edits -- never direct push to main), and write the run record to the LangGraph store (BaseStore, semantic-indexed) for audit. Each effect re-checks the approval token; nothing fires without it.
persist_and_report: write audit entry (inputs, evidence findings, engine decision, divergences, approver, applied effects) to store with TTL per langgraph.json; emit final messages and END.
impact: Closes the four named pain points: evidence validation becomes an automated LLM-judge sweep over the same links the records cite; distribution-lock status is derived on every trigger from products/status/*.json via the canonical product-status.mjs engine instead of manual record updates; override validity (class/expiry/owner/evidence) is checked automatically and logged for review; and the work-class/release-ready gate runs in CI on every PR and issue so release-ready criteria are enforced at the dev boundary rather than after the fact. automatable_score is 50 because the hard judgment (is this evidence actually credible, is an override justified) and every mutation stay human-gated -- the agent removes the manual sweeping/cross-referencing toil and produces an auditable verdict, but a human still approves all writes and contested calls. Keeps the agent strictly consistent with the existing gate.yml/runner-policy CI by reusing product-status.mjs as the decision authority.
tools_integrations: LangGraph Platform / LangSmith Deployments (managed Cloud SaaS) - Agent Server runtime, auto-provisioned Postgres checkpointer + store

langgraph.json: graphs.gatekeeper -> ./agent/graph.py:graph (compiled WITHOUT custom checkpointer/store - platform injects), http.app -> ./app.py:app for the GitHub webhook route + HMAC verify, auth.path -> ./auth.py for run authz, store.index for semantic audit search, image_distro wolfi, python_version 3.12
Claude via langchain (Anthropic) - orchestration/evidence-judging LLM ONLY; no model training/fine-tuning/distillation (Anthropic terms + workspace AGENTS.md guardrail)
product-status.mjs CLI (Node subprocess in the image, dockerfile_lines to install node) - canonical decide / operating-review / export / issue-gate engine = decision authority

git read-only tool - clone/pull Scheduler-Systems/business-ops-admin at a pinned ref to load products/status/*.json, products/lifecycle.md, status-page-components.json, data/exports/latest/product-status.json

GitHub App (fine-grained, scoped to business-ops-admin + product repos) - issue comment/label, PR check-run + comment, open draft PRs for status-JSON edits; NEVER push to main directly; all behind the approval interrupt
LangGraph human-in-the-loop interrupt() / interrupt_before - approval gate for all writes
Existing CI surface (.github/workflows/gate.yml, runner-policy-check.yml) - the agent's PR check-run plugs in alongside these, not replacing runner-policy
approval_gates: Single hard human-in-the-loop interrupt before any side effect (LangGraph dynamic interrupt(), resumed via Studio/SDK Command(resume=...)). Gated actions: posting/updating a GitHub issue comment or label; creating/updating a PR check-run or PR comment; opening any PR that edits products/status/*.json (no direct main push, ever); and any flag that marks a product release-ready. Read-only verdicts requested by a human in an interactive run return without interrupt; automated webhook/cron runs that would mutate state ALWAYS stop at the interrupt and never auto-apply. The approval token is re-checked inside apply_effects so a resumed run cannot fire un-approved effects. Override approvals are surfaced for human decision, never self-granted by the agent. Aligns with workspace rules: no send/publish without approval, no delete/overwrite without approval, tie work to a documented GitHub issue.
deploy_notes: v1 deploy path: 1) Repo layout at root: langgraph.json, pyproject.toml (langgraph, langchain-anthropic, langsmith, PyGithub/githubkit, pydantic), agent/graph.py (StateGraph -> .compile(), exported as `graph`; do NOT pass checkpointer=/store= -- the managed platform injects production Postgres), app.py (Starlette/FastAPI with /webhooks/github HMAC-verified route that creates a run on the deployment), auth.py (Auth instance). 2) langgraph.json points graphs.gatekeeper at agent/graph.py:graph; image_distro=wolfi, python_version=3.12; add dockerfile_lines to install Node so product-status.mjs runs in-image (or shell out to a pinned business-ops-admin checkout). 3) Deploy via `langgraph deploy` (CLI) or LangSmith UI to Cloud SaaS; inject all secrets through the platform secrets UI/API, never in env file committed to repo (security rule: never hardcode secrets). 4) Configure store.ttl + checkpointer.ttl in langgraph.json for audit retention. 5) Wire GitHub App webhook (PR + issues) to the deployment's custom HTTP route; keep the existing gate.yml/runner-policy CI intact and add the agent's check-run as a non-required status first, promote to required after a shadow-mode period. 6) Run in shadow mode initially (verdicts only, approval interrupt always pauses) to validate LLM-judge vs product-status.mjs agreement before enabling any auto-applied effects. Constraints honored: managed LangGraph Cloud target; Claude used only for orchestration/judging (no model dev/training/distillation per Anthropic terms + AGENTS.md); every irreversible action approval-gated; secrets via platform env only.
